### chocolate — 2012-05-04T23:33:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctor-diabetes-leptin-rx-oh-my.618/post-21433>

[http://www.nature.com/news/remote-controlled-genes-trigger-insulin-production-1.10585](http://http://www.nature.com/news/remote-controlled-genes-trigger-insulin-production-1.10585)

Friedman and his colleagues coated iron oxide nanoparticles with antibodies that bind to a modified version of the temperature-sensitive ion channel TRPV1, which sits on the surface of cells. They injected these particles into tumours grown under the skins of mice, then used the magnetic field generated by a device similar to a miniature magnetic-resonance-imaging machine to heat the nanoparticles with low-frequency radio waves. In turn, the nanoparticles heated **the ion channel to its activation temperature of 42 Â°C. Opening the channel allowed calcium to flow into cells, triggering secondary signals that switched on an engineered calcium-sensitive gene that produces insulin.

Does anyone know what is special about this temperature? It seems to be 107 degrees F, and that is the hot tub reset temperature. I don't know what that means. But anyway, hot tubs are supposed to lower insulin and improve sleep. Now it seems the iron and the temperature have something to do with it...Maybe?

Read more: [http://wiki.answers.com/Q/What\_is\_the\_proper\_temperature\_for\_a\_spa#ixzz1tw27zfB3](http://http://wiki.answers.com/Q/What_is_the_proper_temperature_for_a_spa#ixzz1tw27zfB3)

A comfortable temperature will be between 90 and 105 F. Experiment and find what is comfortable for you. For public spas many states have laws regarding a maximum temperature, usually 107 F,** so check local regulations if this is for public use.

does any one have any thoughts to share?

### chocolate — 2012-05-06T01:07:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctor-diabetes-leptin-rx-oh-my.618/post-21700>

[http://www.diabetes-book.com/articles/hot\_tub\_therapy.shtml](http://http://www.diabetes-book.com/articles/hot_tub_therapy.shtml)

Dr. Bernstein Objects to "Hot Tub Therapy"/ Articles

Article: "Hot Tub Therapy" Helps Diabetics,

Reuters Health, September 15, 1999

Sitting in a hot tub helped improve blood sugar readings, sleep, and general well being in a group of patients with Type 2 diabetes, reports a Colorado researcher.

But a diabetes expert cautions that "hot-tub therapy" can be dangerous for diabetics who have lost feeling in their feet, and increase their risk of skin injuries.

The new study, in a letter published Thursday in The New England Journal of Medicine, looked at a group of 8 people with Type 2, or adult-onset, diabetes. Such patients are often told to engage in regular exercise to control their condition, but not all are able to exercise. The researchers conducted the study to see if sitting in a hot tub offered an effect similar to the beneficial effects of exercise for these patients.

The research team, led by Dr. Philip L. Hooper from the McKee Medical Center in Loveland, Colorado, found that sitting in a hot tub for 30 minutes a day, 6 days a week for three weeks improved the patients' condition, helping them lose weight and lower their blood sugar or glucose levels.

"As the study progressed, they reported improved sleep and an increased general sense of well-being," Hooper writes.

After 10 days of such hot-tub therapy, one study participant reduced his daily dose of insulin by 18%, the researchers report.

"Our results suggest that hot-tub therapy should be further evaluated as a therapy for patients with Type 2 diabetes," Hooper concludes, suggesting that the benefits "could result from increased blood flow to skeletal muscles."

But one diabetes expert, Dr. Richard Bernstein, director of the New York Diabetes Center in Mamaroneck, New York, warns people with Type 2 diabetes to think twice before jumping into the hot tub.

"The heat will help dilate blood vessels, which improves blood flow and helps the body use insulin better, but hot tubs can be dangerous for people with diabetes," Bernstein told Reuters Health.

"For one thing, people with the condition often have nerve damage in their feet which means they can't feel their feet, so they are likely to get burned in hot baths yet feel no pain at the time," he explained.

In addition, soaking in water can harm the skin and make it more susceptible to injury. "For diabetics, sitting in the hot tub is just asking for trouble," he adds. "There are much better ways to lower insulin resistance. Exercise is fabulous, weight loss is great, and several medications are also helpful," he adds.

Type 2 diabetes afflicts about 15 million Americans; most of whom are older than age 45 and overweight. People with this type of diabetes do not make enough insulin or do not use insulin properly. Without enough insulin, sugar builds up in the bloodstream. Type 2 diabetes is typically controlled by eating a healthy, low-fat diet, maintaining a normal body weight, and engaging in regular exercise. Sometimes medication is necessary to help the body use insulin. (Source: The New England Journal of Medicine, 1999; 341:924-925.)

### chocolate — 2012-05-09T03:41:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctor-diabetes-leptin-rx-oh-my.618/post-22438>

[http://www.mendeley.com/research/vitamin-d3-restores-altered-cholinergic-insulin-receptor-expression-cerebral-cortex-muscarinic-m3-receptor-expression-pancreatic-islets-streptozotocin-induced-diabetic-rats/](http://http://www.mendeley.com/research/vitamin-d3-restores-altered-cholinergic-insulin-receptor-expression-cerebral-cortex-muscarinic-m3-receptor-expression-pancreatic-islets-streptozotocin-induced-diabetic-rats/)

Nutritional therapy is a challenging but necessary dimension in the management of diabetes and neurodegenerative changes associated with it. The study evaluates the effect of vitamin D(3) in preventing the altered function of cholinergic, insulin receptors and GLUT3 in the cerebral cortex of diabetic rats. Muscarinic M3 acetylcholine receptors in pancreas control insulin secretion. Vitamin D(3) treatment in M3 receptor regulation in the pancreatic islets was also studied. Radioreceptor binding assays and gene expression was done in the cerebral cortex of male Wistar rats. Immunocytochemistry of muscarinic M3 receptor was studied in the pancreatic islets using specific antibodies. Y-maze was used to evaluate the exploratory and spatial memory. Diabetes induced a decrease in muscarinic M1, insulin and vitamin D receptor expression and an increase in muscarinic M3, α7 nicotinic acetylcholine receptor, acetylcholine esterase and GLUT3 expression. Vitamin D(3) and insulin treatment reversed diabetes-induced alterations to near control. Diabetic rats showed a decreased Y-maze performance while vitamin D(3) supplementation improved the behavioural deficit. In conclusion, vitamin D(3) shows a potential therapeutic effect in normalizing diabetes-induced alterations in cholinergic, insulin and vitamin D receptor and maintains a normal glucose transport and utilisation in the cortex. In addition vitamin D(3) modulated muscarinic M3 receptors activity in pancreas and plays a pivotal role in controlling insulin secretion. **Hence our findings proved, vitamin D(3) supplementation as a potential nutritional therapy in ameliorating diabetes mediated cortical dysfunctions and suggest an interaction between vitamin D(3) and muscarinic M3 receptors in regulating insulin secretion from pancreas.**

Author-supplied keywords

acetylcholinesteraseacetylcholinesterase metabolismanimalsblood glucoseblood glucose analysiscerebral cortexcerebral cortex drug effectscerebral cortex metabolismcholecalciferolcholecalciferol pharmacology

### chocolate — 2012-05-10T06:21:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctor-diabetes-leptin-rx-oh-my.618/post-22770>

[http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2012/04/120419132604.htm](http://http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2012/04/120419132604.htm)

ScienceDaily (Apr. 19, 2012) — Cardiac researchers at the University of Cincinnati have found that a certain cellular pathway is linked to obesity-related disorders, like diabetes, heart disease and fatty liver disease.

These findings, being presented at the American Heart Association's Arteriosclerosis, Thrombosis and Vascular Biology (ATVB) 2012 Scientific Sessions in Chicago, April 19, 2012, could lead to a potential molecular target for metabolic diseases in humans.

Building on previous research, Tapan Chatterjee, PhD, and researchers in the division of cardiovascular diseases at UC found that genetically "deleting" the enzyme histone deacetylase 9 (HDAC9) completely protected mice against the health consequences of high-fat feeding, like elevated blood sugar, cholesterol levels and fatty liver disease.

### chocolate — 2012-05-12T05:23:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctor-diabetes-leptin-rx-oh-my.618/post-23337>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19730809](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19730809)

Aspirin attenuates insulin resistance in muscle of diet-induced obese rats by inhibiting inducible nitric oxide synthase production and S-nitrosylation of IRbeta/IRS-1 and Akt.

Abstract

AIM/HYPOTHESIS:

High-dose aspirin treatment improves fasting and postprandial hyperglycaemia in patients with type 2 diabetes, as well as in animal models of insulin resistance associated with obesity and sepsis. In this study, we investigated the effects of aspirin treatment on inducible nitric oxide synthase (iNOS)-mediated insulin resistance and on S-nitrosylation of insulin receptor (IR)-beta, IRS-1 and protein kinase B (Akt) in the muscle of diet-induced obese rats and also in iNos (also known as Nos2)-/- mice on high fat diet.

METHODS:

Aspirin (120 mg kg-1 day-1 for 2 days) or iNOS inhibitor (L-NIL; 80 mg/kg body weight) were administered to diet-induced obese rats or mice and iNOS production and insulin signalling were investigated. S-nitrosylation of IRbeta/IRS-1 and Akt was investigated using the biotin switch method.

RESULTS:

iNOS protein levels increased in the muscle of diet-induced obese rats, associated with an increase in S-nitrosylation of IRbeta, IRS-1 and Akt. These alterations were reversed by aspirin treatment, in parallel with an improvement in insulin signalling and sensitivity, as measured by insulin tolerance test and glucose clamp. Conversely, while aspirin reversed the increased phosphorylation of IkappaB kinase beta and c-Jun amino-terminal kinase, as well as IRS-1 serine phosphorylation in diet-induced obese rats and iNos -/- mice on high-fat diet, these alterations were not associated with the improvement of insulin action induced by this drug.

CONCLUSIONS/INTERPRETATION:

**Our data demonstrate that aspirin treatment not only reduces iNOS protein levels, but also S-nitrosylation of IRbeta, IRS-1 and Akt. These changes are associated with improved insulin resistance and signalling, suggesting a novel mechanism of insulin sensitisation evoked by aspirin treatment.**

### chocolate — 2012-05-12T06:42:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctor-diabetes-leptin-rx-oh-my.618/post-23349>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21088567](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21088567)

Is protein metabolism changed with obesity?

Guillet C, Masgrau A, Boirie Y.

Source

UnitÃ© de Nutrition Humaine, Clermont UniversitÃ©, UniversitÃ© d'Auvergne, France. [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted])

Abstract

PURPOSE OF REVIEW:

Although it is well established that obesity is accompanied by various degrees of metabolic impairments especially in the regulation of carbohydrate and lipid metabolism, the influence of obesity on protein metabolism is not clearly understood. The purpose of this review is to present data describing the modification in protein metabolism that have been reported in the clinical setting of obesity.

RECENT FINDINGS:

Recent findings suggest that protein metabolism at the whole-body level is less sensitive to insulin action. Impairments in skeletal muscle protein synthesis rates in the postabsorptive state and in response to anabolic factors are reported in obese human. **Finally, chronic excessive energy intake and increased adiposity in rats, without the appearance of other metabolic disturbances, do not induce any changes in tissue protein synthesis rates.

SUMMARY:

Body composition in obesity is characterized by elevated fat mass but also lean body mass which is considered either increased or decreased (in the case of sarcopenic obesity). Thus protein metabolism as reflected by changes in protein synthesis and breakdown might be modified in obese individuals but it is still largely debated. Only a few studies have investigated muscle protein kinetics during obesity and do not lead to the same conclusions prolonging the controversies. Indeed, obesity is associated with many metabolic disturbances which might constitute confounding factors differently affecting muscle protein metabolism.**

### chocolate — 2012-06-29T06:42:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctor-diabetes-leptin-rx-oh-my.618/post-34323>

[http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2012/04/120417080431.htm](http://http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2012/04/120417080431.htm)

> Liquorice Root Found to Contain Anti-Diabetic Substance
>
>
>
> ScienceDaily (Apr. 17, 2012) — Researchers have discovered a promising anti-diabetic substance in the amorfrutin class of natural substances.
>
> It provides the raw material for liquorice candy, calms the stomach and alleviates diseases of the airways: liquorice root. Chosen as the "Medicinal plant 2012," the root has been treasured in traditional healing since ancient times. Researchers at the Max Planck Institute for Molecular Genetics in Berlin have now discovered that liquorice root also contains substances with an anti-diabetic effect. These amorfrutins not only reduce blood sugar, they are also anti-inflammatory and are very well tolerated. Thus, they may be suitable for use in the treatment of complex metabolic disorders.
>
>
> The substances, which have a simple chemical structure, are not only found in liquorice root, but are also in the fruit of the Amorpha fruticosa bush. The new anti-diabetic agents were named after this plant, which is native to the US, Canada and Mexico. As the researchers demonstrated using diabetic mice, the amorfrutins not only have characteristics that reduce blood sugar, they are also anti-inflammatory in their effect. Moreover, they also prevent fatty liver -- a common disease caused by excessively fat-rich nutrition.\*
>
>
>
> **"The health-beneficial effects are based on the fact that the amorfrutin molecules dock directly onto a receptor in the nucleus called PPARγ," explains Sascha Sauer. PPARγ plays an important role in the cell's fat and glucose metabolism. The binding of the amorfrutin molecules activates various genes that reduce the plasma concentration of certain fatty acids and glucose. The reduced glucose level prevents the development of insulin resistance -- the main cause of adult diabetes.**

\*I know the cause is incorrect, but maybe the prevention is accurate.

Is there any sense from you guys that becoming cold adapted is shifting the make-up of your gut bacteria without other intervention (probiotics)? Are you getting better absorption? Immunomodulatory effects?

I can't help feeling out gut bacteria must have been more optimal along with our good selves way back when....  Better at making B vitamins and goodness knows what other V. important things...

Thanks for being here!

Freesia.

\*I'm only three days into Leptin Reset (loving it) and CT is still but a twinkle in my eye (lots of reading to do yet) but I can't waut to get started.

### KA24 — 2012-04-25T21:53:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adapted-people-and-gut-bacteria.619/post-18816>

I can't give you a direct answer, but anecdotal evidence from my CT has made me consider this same thought.

Prior to CT, I felt great about my gut. Digestion was good, I've dialed into pretty much anything that gives me issues. I'm in good shape, and only started CT to really see what impact it might have since everything else was dialed in.

Since starting, I've noticed even better bowel movements and what I can feel, improved digestion. I was using digestive enzymes before and have tapered off substantially since CT.

For about a 3-4 day period about 1-2 weeks in, I had an inconsistent, but nagging stomach-ache during the day. It subsided after only a few days, but this is very uncommon and hasn't ever happened before. The only thing I can think of was the CT had something to do with it as it was the only thing I really changed.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-04-25T22:10:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adapted-people-and-gut-bacteria.619/post-18825>

I can now drink coffee without any after effects. That never happened before.

# Thread: I can stop the shivering

### hellojtm — 2012-04-03T02:37:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-can-stop-the-shivering.621/post-10584>

So last nite when asked by my SO if I could stop shivering I realized that I could... I simply relaxed and I stopped shivering and would feel comfortable and warm. I know exactly what it feels like to do "it" and can do it now while typing this. However I can't maintain it for more than 10 seconds or so... maybe a limitation of attention span? Perhaps it needs some conditioning? This is preliminary stuff for me, things may change, but for now it is associated with a soft breath hold. When I breathe again I will start shivering, inhale or exhale...

I will keep playing with it. Along the lines of another thread I glimpsed there may be value in inducing non-shivering thermogenesis, but for all we know shivering triggers a faster adaptation.

# Thread: Best Study Design to gather evidence about CT's efficacy?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-study-design-to-gather-evidence-about-cts-efficacy.622/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kalibex — 2012-04-03T14:00:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-study-design-to-gather-evidence-about-cts-efficacy.622/post-10726>

For a lot of skeptics, "is there evidence" will be the bottom line. So...**is** there a 'high in the evidence hierarchy' type of clinical study design that can be done to start gathering _some_?

Can't be a randomized controlled trial (for obvious reasons). Could even a cross-over design work? Jack's deliberate return to SAD eating long enough to re-gain weight to give him his excuse for surgery was an example of going from one testing condition to another, but you can't even say there was a 'washout period' when he returned to his 'baseline'...(though he's now gathering more of his own personal data as he returns to his preferred healthy ways to lose the extra weight he still had after his surgery). And who's to know right now what effects of eating this or that way might or might not be permanent?

Or are case histories/ case series (our anecdotes we share here could be argued to be a sort of informal case history) the best we're going to be able to do right now? Other than observational studies, with the usual issues with participants' less-than-perfect memories and the various confounders that complicate things when studying something that involves diet, in an attempt to see correlations between behaviors and health outcomes...

That said, there are doubtless many who'll say, "Scr\*w the 'evidence', let's just each do our own personal n=1!!" Just sayin'... there has to be a logical level to which this can ultimately be 'tested'...with labs, telomere testing, weight gain/loss as the various types of gatherable data...

Though I doubt anyone here would want to be the SAD eating 'control' at this point...

### Shijin13 — 2012-04-03T15:12:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-study-design-to-gather-evidence-about-cts-efficacy.622/post-10728>

I think it would be very easy to set up an experiment using mice or monkeys. two separate rooms one held at a temperature of close to freezing, and one held at 75 degrees (average indoor temperature year round in houses/builidings etc) - set each group up w/a double blind - then test seasonal eating vs SAD eating in each environment, circadian cycling, and CT. you would also need to have 1 control group in each environment that got the same bland food, no change in circadian cycling etc for the control group... once you've established the baseline - then you cans start playing w/the experiment...

### kalibex — 2012-04-03T15:30:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-study-design-to-gather-evidence-about-cts-efficacy.622/post-10733>

> **Shijin13;10797 wrote:** I think it would be very easy to set up an experiment using mice or monkeys. two separate rooms one held at a temperature of close to freezing, and one held at 75 degrees (average indoor temperature year round in houses/builidings etc) - set each group up w/a double blind - then test seasonal eating vs SAD eating in each environment, circadian cycling, and CT. you would also need to have 1 control group in each environment that got the same bland food, no change in circadian cycling etc for the control group... once you've established the baseline - then you cans start playing w/the experiment...

True...could just focus on the temperature instead of the keto vs SAD...

### Shijin13 — 2012-04-03T15:32:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-study-design-to-gather-evidence-about-cts-efficacy.622/post-10736>

> **kalibex;10802 wrote:** True...could just focus on the temperature instead of the keto vs SAD...

but inorder to activate the pathway you need to be keto or in hibernation...

### kalibex — 2012-04-03T19:12:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-study-design-to-gather-evidence-about-cts-efficacy.622/post-10832>

> **Shijin13;10805 wrote:** but inorder to activate the pathway you need to be keto or in hibernation...

Which might make doing an animal study more challenging - are mice or monkeys ever in ketosis in their natural environments? There are cold weather primates who might be suitable... but the golden snub-nosed monkey, for example, even though living in a sometimes very cold area (mountains in China) is an herbivore...

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-03T19:22:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-study-design-to-gather-evidence-about-cts-efficacy.622/post-10836>

I think you all need to re read CT 6......the experiments I mentioned there were pretty stiff proof.

### Adriana — 2012-04-03T22:13:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-study-design-to-gather-evidence-about-cts-efficacy.622/post-10905>

> **Jack Kruse;10905 wrote:** I think you all need to re read CT 6......the experiments I mentioned there were pretty stiff proof.

# Thread: Welly, well, well, well, look what FINALLY happened!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welly-well-well-well-look-what-finally-happened.623/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 34
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cody — 2012-04-03T16:30:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welly-well-well-well-look-what-finally-happened.623/post-10760>

Don't give up hope, friends, Romans, countrymen.

I froze my ass off for a month.

Did I see progress?

Of course. Strength gains, better sleep, feeling better, thicker skin.

Were there hiccups on the way? Yep. I've had periods of the most god awful nausea you can imagine. I've gotten to the point where I never thought I'd warm up again. I've experienced sleepless nights, as well. Where I just lay in bed shivering with a few hours of fitful sleep at best. Cold showers have only just become bearable. And I'm guessing the temp is only down to 70 degrees!

But I look at it this way, I damaged my body over 38 years. Quick healing means acute symptoms. Price of entry.

Well. I woke up this morning and what did I see? Coming for to carry me home.

It was weight loss. Oh yeah baby! Down 8 pounds this week. This is on top of muscle gain and strength gain.

And appetite is going away. It just happened. As if by magic. Magic I say! Started on Saturday. I only had bulletproof coffee yesterday. Same for breakfast today. I'm gonna eat some meat for supper though. Unless I'm not hungry. LOL

Yeah, yeah, I hear you, put down the crack pipe and stay away from your keyboard you crazy SOB.

I'm just ecstatic. Because for a freaking change, something actually works the way its supposed to!!!

### Cody — 2012-04-03T17:47:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welly-well-well-well-look-what-finally-happened.623/post-10789>

> **Clabbergirl;10846 wrote:** Congrats on the progress!
>
>
>
> Is this the first sign of progress you've had or were there other tell-tale signs that things were happening for the better?

Yep. Muscle mass improved noticeably the first week. Workout recovery also sped up massively. I also actually felt like moving. Really moving. I can do perfect full pushups now, before, I could only shakily get down about half way for 5 reps.

Improved sleep has been a mixed bag, though. About half the time, I sleep like a baby. Really well. The other half, it's fitful at best.

Sex drive has improved as well, although, this seems to come and go. I have a lot of weight left to lose, so I expect that I will keep experiencing negative symptoms, off and on, until I get optimal.

### Jim — 2012-04-03T19:21:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welly-well-well-well-look-what-finally-happened.623/post-10835>

OK now I am motivated. I have been slacking on the baths and just taking cold showers. They haven't been long but they are very bearable given my process (legs first, then wet head & shampoo, then wash upper body, then rise hair, then rinse back of legs and then back into the shower to get the back). Water down the spine is the worst for me but by this time with everything else wet I am easily able to handle it. Water temp is about 55-60 deg F. I have also been out working in the yard and walking at night with temps in the 50's in only shorts and a t-shirt. I also am driving around with the a/c on full blast and keeping the house at about 65 deg F. At night I have been slowly removing the layers of covers. Last night I slept with just the sheet and a very thin blanket. I have noticed some strength gains but I recently changed my workout so it's tough to say why. My weight and measurements are still fairly constant. I am getting compliments at work about the weight loss however so that is motivating too. Thanks for your report.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-03T19:23:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welly-well-well-well-look-what-finally-happened.623/post-10838>

awesome Jim.....keep it up.

### caroline — 2012-04-04T03:27:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/welly-well-well-well-look-what-finally-happened.623/post-11070>

> **Cody;10829 wrote:** Don't give up hope, friends, Romans, countrymen.
>
>
>
> I froze my ass off for a month.
>
>
>
> Did I see progress?
>
>
>
> Of course. Strength gains, better sleep, feeling better, thicker skin.
>
>
>
> Were there hiccups on the way? Yep. I've had periods of the most god awful nausea you can imagine. I've gotten to the point where I never thought I'd warm up again. I've experienced sleepless nights, as well. Where I just lay in bed shivering with a few hours of fitful sleep at best. Cold showers have only just become bearable. And I'm guessing the temp is only down to 70 degrees!
>
>
>
> But I look at it this way, I damaged my body over 38 years. Quick healing means acute symptoms. Price of entry.
>
>
>
> Well. I woke up this morning and what did I see? Coming for to carry me home.
>
>
>
> It was weight loss. Oh yeah baby! Down 8 pounds this week. This is on top of muscle gain and strength gain.
>
>
>
> And appetite is going away. It just happened. As if by magic. Magic I say! Started on Saturday. I only had bulletproof coffee yesterday. Same for breakfast today. I'm gonna eat some meat for supper though. Unless I'm not hungry. LOL
>
>
>
> Yeah, yeah, I hear you, put down the crack pipe and stay away from your keyboard you crazy SOB.
>
>
>
> I'm just ecstatic. Because for a freaking change, something actually works the way its supposed to!!!

So great to hear - very happy for you!

### Cody — 2012-04-04T16:18:55+0300

I think there are better examples of success with CT than me, so far. Maybe when I've got visible abs!

I just want to provide a little light for the people who are struggling.

Change is hard. Damn hard. The first thing we need to change is our mind. The rest falls in line. And we all get to a point where we feel overwhelmed. And I'm hoping that by sharing our positive stories, we can keep each other going.

# Thread: Vasper training
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vasper-training.624/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Pardus — 2012-04-03T17:37:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vasper-training.624/post-10779>

Hi all,

I've been reading a little bit about the recent success of Vasper training (being researched by NASA!!!) in accelerating muscle and strength gains while training less.

For those who aren't familiar, you wear ice-pack compression cuffs and a tight ice vest to cool and compress your body parts while you train. Apparently, this traps lactic acid in the muscles, 'tricking' your body into thinking it's working harder than it actually is. The result is an increase in the HGH response, allowing you to build more muscle with less effort.

see [http://www.vasper.com/](http://http://www.vasper.com/)

Combined with an already brief, intense routine like Body By Science, I would guess that Vasper will probably enhance and complement it very well.

Occlusion training is a similar concept, just without cooling vests/cuffs: [http://www.exercisebiology.com/index.php/site/articles/how\_does\_occlusion\_training\_works\_in\_increasing\_muscle/](http://http://www.exercisebiology.com/index.php/site/articles/how_does_occlusion_training_works_in_increasing_muscle/)

Has anyone ever tried Vasper, occlusion training, or a do-it-yourself variation?

What are your thoughts?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-03T17:38:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vasper-training.624/post-10781>

I have done it......in 20 minutes your ready to drop.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-04-11T04:37:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vasper-training.624/post-13598>

Will try it...wanting to do Body by Science. It looks good and efficient. I dropped swimming since March 18. The water was 84! SO HOT! (Plus I wanted to experiment and see the effects of CT alone. I only did the swimming for 3 weeks prior to that anyway).

I know I can find a nice cold lake nearby and swim in that instead...much better! I already look muscular, but would like to have ab definition on my now flat stomach. (Yes, it returned to flat again...yay!) (Obviously b/c of CT and keto seafood.)

### Glamorama — 2012-04-11T05:07:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vasper-training.624/post-13615>

I saw this on the news here in OZ last month, very fascinating, as it was about 3 weeks into my first taste of CT aka JK. I know that CT alone has given me very good gains in the gym... today, while doing my biceps/back/core WO, I caught a glimpse in the mirror and was shocked by how muscly my arms/shoulders are becoming. I think I almost looked a bit too manly!! \*horror\*

I can imagine CT while you exersice must do amazing things!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-11T05:09:13+0300

In two months I replace 25 lbs of fat with 27 pounds of muscle....using deep CT and compression/HBOT/Pressure

This is probably being saved for the book, but I still have to ask!

# Thread: So let's try this ... DarleenMB
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1771
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DarleenMB — 2012-04-03T18:19:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-10801>

It feels as though I've tried everything and over the years just got fatter and more unhealthy. Let's start with the reader's digest version, shall we?

I am a master at running away from things and burning the candle at both ends. Or I used to be. Back in the early 70's when I decided to go back to college I worked a full time job as a cocktail waitress (40+ hours a week), commuted 60+ miles (round trip) a day to get to classes, did 15 hours a week work/study AND carried an 18+ hour course load each quarter. I barely ate. Didn't have time and besides I needed to stay thin! Of course, pictures from then show a thin body with a volley-ball belly.

The running didn't stop when I graduated. I just found other ways to keep myself busy. A few years later when we'd moved to Wyoming I was working 20 hours a week as a DJ (that's like 40 hours at a desk job), selling Avon on the side (cause I was bored), singing in a rock band (I always wanted to be singer, for as long as I can remember) and anything else I could think of to keep moving.

And I starved myself to stay thin.

Then, in my early 30's, the weight starting piling on. And, although I didn't know it at the time, I was already in menopause. Sleep deprivation became the norm, my periods were gawdawful, my weight continued it's upward climb until I hit 203 and about had a nervous breakdown. (I'm 5'9.5" tall when I stand up straight). I NEVER EVER in my wildest dreams thought I would ever be that fat. Then came the parade of "remedies." I tried the diet service that had me eating a couple of ounces of protein and a couple of crackers twice a day. Then came Nutrisystem where I wound up being a counselor and telling other people that eating fat makes you fat. The scale kept moving upwards.

Then I read Enter the Zone and tried it. It worked! I lost 2 pounds a week for 2 months and kept it off for over 3 years. Actually I was probably closer to Atkins than the Zone because there was NO way I could eat that much veggie matter at one sitting. I finally got sick and tired of chewing and focusing on what to eat and just slowly wandered back to my same old eating habits.

Then came the total hyst. And more weight. 6 years ago I topped out at 241 pounds. I was so ashamed of myself I didn't want to leave the house. We were due to travel to Washington state to see friends and I had an emotional breakdown sobbing to hubby I was ashamed to let our friends see me looking like this.

I love my husband. he looked at me as though I had two heads and was popping a 3rd. "Well," sez he, "why don't you do the diet Kirsty Alley is doing?" But it's SO EXPENSIVE I wailed. He didn't care. So I ordered it up.

18 months and several thousand dollars later I had dropped a less than exciting 58 pounds. EIGHTEEN FREAKIN' MONTHS. My counselor kept asking if I was cheating. NO I was absolutely sticking to the 1200 calories a day and not eating anything that wasn't on the plan. I was tired. I was beyond hungry (it's your typical SAD ... heavy on the carbs, light on the fats and esp the protein). I was completely incapable of working out.

I finally gave up. Quit. Began researching on the net. Discovered my basal temp was down to an average of 95.6! Talk about a slowed metabolism. I began eating coconut oil. I gained back 20 pounds. Went on HcG and after about 9 months of starvation finally got that 20 back off.

It came back. Brought friends. I tried to do Jenny Craig again and just could not. Tried to do HcG again. Just. Could. Not.

Then in January of 2011 I read Gary Taubes's "why we get fat" and had an epiphany. I went low carb. But I was still eating small amounts of grains. Last September I found Dr. William Davis's "Wheat Belly" and threw out all wheat containing products.

From there I found Jack's blog and in October attempted the leptin Rx.

Trying to eat 50g of protein for breakfast made me want to puke. I had to FORCE it down and some days just could NOT do it. In two weeks I dropped 10 pounds. Why? How? I couldn't eat anything after breakfast. Just. Could. NOT.

Then those 10 pounds came back. All of a sudden I was HUNGRY and feared gaining more weight. I quit.

But I kept trying to plow through Jack's blog postings as he was now off on CT.

Interesting.

I dithered through the holidays being really naughty with a what the hell attitude on.

January rolled around. I closed my eyes and got on the scale. Well it wasn't as bad as I'd feared. Maybe I should try that BAB again.

So I"m dithering with it a bit and finally decide to add face dunking to the mix. Arguing with Jack about the cost of all the blood tests. grrrrr. If I had HIS income blah blah blah

Ok excuses excuses.

I KNOW i'm very low in vitamin D ... I NEVER GO OUT IN THE SUN. I'm a strawberry blonde (or was ... age and gray hair aside) with the old "peaches and cream" complexion. Do you know how many times and how badly I sunburned myself as a child and young adult? I got tired of it so I stay inside most of the time. My regular CRP is GOOD, practically zero. OK OK I'll get the HS CRP checked when they check the D again in a few weeks. (insert exasperated sounds here)

I bought a compression shirt.

Filled up a 2 gallon zip lock baggie with ice from the ice maker.

I'm up to 30 minutes today. Yesterday was HELL. The boiler for our heating system is on the fritz again, it's been cloudy so the house is COLD and I got chilled. Lay on the floor for 25 minutes shivering away. Onward.

I gave up my beloved HOT showers weeks ago because of what Jack said to me. darn it. NOw they're tepid followed by a quick ICE rinse cause our cold water is 43 right out of the tap.

I have given up decaf. And HWC! Found an awesome home-made creamer recipe (I think it was over on MDA ... 1 can of Thai Kitchen coconut milk with 1 egg, 2 TBS CO \[Tropical Traditions ... the ONLY CO I will ever use it is just SO awesome\] and 1tsp vanilla (SF of course) whipped to a froth in the blender). First batch came out nice and thick. OMG it is delicious! I do not miss the HWC. 2nd batch is kinda runny. Still tastes great.

Dug out my Mr. Coffee espresso maker, bought a Mr. Coffee burr grinder and have discovered that Cafe Americano does not give me caffeine jitters! I'm using Starbucks. sue me.

bought a HUGE hand rolled batch of Amish butter for bullet=proof coffee. Sorry but they only had the salted. Haven't tried it yet but darling hubby has and I think he liked it.

My weight, which I forgot to mention earlier, has stabilized. I weigh exactly what I weight in October. 5 months without a gain. AMAZING! UNHEARD OF! And that deep visceral belly fat seems to be disappearing. hmmmm.

Oh and just for the record I'm at 223.3

I'll try not to be humiliated by that confession.

I am slopping through the leptin Rx. But I keep trying and keep trying to be GOOD. I'm slowly getting there. A lifetime of really poor eating habits has a choke hold on me. OK thats an excuse.  I'm weak. So every day is a new day with a new attempt at commitment.

Maybe keeping this very public journal will make me stick with it.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-04T18:34:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-11329>

Yesterday's 30 minutes with the ice bag didn't happen. It was FREEZING in here because the boiler is on the fritz and by the time I got to my shower I was way chilled. I shivered the entire time I was in there. Which brings up a question I had while freezing my tushie off. Why is it that tepid water makes me feel colder than cold water? One thing I've noticed so far is that when I get out of the cold (shower or ice bag) my face has a LOT of color in it. Rosy cheeks (think scandahoovian milk maid) and deep pink lips. Huh.

Ate very little yesterday. Had my usual BAB and about 5-6 hours later was wandering through the pantry and fridge looking for something. Didn't know WHAT but something. Finally had a slice of ham. Ok, I'm good.

Dinner was a tad late as I was at the torch and actually WORKing.  Had some rotisserie chicken heated up in the nuwave. And a cup of coffee. Done.

This morning had my first ever cup of bullet proof coffee and OMG I think I have died and gone to heaven. YUMMMMMY!

Downside, my left ear is plugged up and I feel like crap. Feels like a cold trying to worm its way in for a snuggle. sigh. And after dodging that EPIC cold DH had a few weeks back. bother.

Well shoot. It's 9:30 and I haven't eaten. Don't feel like it but guess maybe I should. OTOH maybe I'll just down another cup of BPC. Or maybe I"ll go back to bed.

thanks Jack! And Non ... my goal is to start spending 10-15 minutes each morning out on the patio having my bullet proof coffee. I hope to work up to twice a day within a few weeks.

Yesterday was my monthly massage and she worked me HARD. Woke up feeling sick so I am definitely ON with the ice bag this morning.

Sipping my BPC and feeling headachy and just plain icky. I thought I drank enough water but evidently either didn't or have let myself get dehydrated again.

I do NOT understand why I'm not drinking enough water. 6 months ago I was routinely going through 100+ ounces of water a day.

Well, even though I'm feeling like homemade doo-doo it's time to go eat.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-06T19:02:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-12185>

boy my tail is dragging this morning.

Did 30 minutes yesterday with the ice bag and the compression T. time to double bag as I got some moisture (probably condensation) on my shirt. Was trying to relax with a meditation track but 10 minutes in started shivering. 10 minutes later I switched to Lady Gaga and rocked the rest of my time.

BAB yesterday (and today) ... 5+ oz of good Wyoming grass fed ground chuck, 3 farm eggs with coconut milk (carton, not can) and a couple of slices of nitrate free bacon. burp. ANNNND a mug of bullet proof coffee. Made with espresso of course.

Skipped lunch and had the rest of the rotisserie chicken last night. Managed to drink lots of water for a change but am evidently holding onto it for dear life as the scale was up FOUR POUNDS this morning. Damned water retention.

Am so tired ... don't get this. I've ben wearing my blue blocking sunglasses in the evenings starting at sunset. Am sleeping really well but last night my stomach was giving me fits. That's 2 nights in a row and I suspect it's the magnesium which prior has not bothered me. ???

Been kinda of "off" all week. Sometimes feel as if I'm fighting a bug but maybe it's detox? who knows. Just keep on truckin'.

### MartiD — 2012-04-06T19:35:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-12194>

> **Nonchalant;11568 wrote:** Darleen, since you now are eating more animal fats, you may be able to get much more sun exposure now without burning. I was pleasantly surprised, myself.

I agree, I just returned from Mexico for vacation and was almost disappointed at how I didn't get any color the first few days. I tan nicely but do burn if not cautious. I started out with 45 spf with about 4 hours in sun on beach and at pool...no lines from suit. Next day did 30 spf...still nothing. Decided to go drastic and went with a 8 spf oil based....faint lines but seemed to be getting some color just not what I had hoped. Stayed with 8 next day and saw a difference but no red, just tan. Last day, or last 3 hours in Mexico sun I went totally without anything. Finally, at the airport my hubby said I looked like a got a little red! Whoo Hoo! LOL...next day had a very nice rich dark tan.

I can't believe changing eating habits can make such a difference with sun exposure but I am a believer now!

Good luck with your adventure...I am a believer in HCG, Leptin RX and CT so I think you have to find what combination works for you and just go for it! Look forward to following your progress!

### Marsha — 2012-04-06T20:33:00+0300

Love your journal, Darleen! Besides being informative, it's very entertaining and encouraging!!!

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-07T18:22:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-12473>

Good to know about the dietary fats and sun exposure, Thanks MartiD! Marsha, thanks I aim to please and come on in, the water's fine!

I, however, feel like home made doo-doo this morning. I woke up in the middle of some very entertaining dreams to a screaming, claw my face off headache. At first I thought maybe caffeine w/d? but that can't be it. Then I realized I was HUNGRY!!!! Holy cow, is THAT what that feels like? So I did what any sane person would do. I pulled the covers over my head and went back to dreamland.

The cats got me up a little bit later and that's when I realized my neck and upper back were ICE COLD. duh. Muscular tension = SCMFO headache. Still no heat and it's been in the high 20s here at night. Kitchen was a balmy 55. I'd yell at our plumber who left us in this fix but he's not back from Florida yet. I will refrain from name-calling.

ANYhoo. Staggered out of bed holding my head and mumbling 4 letter words. Did the usual AM routine (brush teeth, apply hormones, clean out litter boxes, feed whiny little fur faced babies). Remembered I was HUNGRY so got breakfast started. The usual BAB routine and a mug of BPC.

Guess I should mention that last night I fixed some yummilicious bratwurst (the excellent organic variety) and batch of my patented hubby-pleasin' cole slaw. Glad to hear pineapple is ok but I was a few hours early with it (go visit Jack's FB photos and check out the crawdad gumbo/stew whatever with the huge hunka pineapple in it!). Here's my recipe if you're interested:

slice up some cabbage with a mandoline or save your fingertips and use the food processor

dump in some chunks of pineapple (canned in it's own juice is good, fresh is way better)

a FEW raisins (like a couple of TBS ... they're just for flavor and hubby won't give 'em up so ... use at your discretion)

chop up some raw walnuts or pecans or throw in a handful of pine nuts

Add whatever else trips your trigger.

Dressing: plop some good mayo in a bowl, sprinkle liberally with raspberry infused balsamic vinegar (our fave but fig-infused is also FAB), add a touch of stevia or a pkt of splenda (yeah yeah I KNOW!). Stir till it's a nice smooth consistency. Taste to be sure you have the right balance of acid/sweet. Pour over the above and stir ... ENJOY!

So back to the headache. I don't know if THAT'S why my stomach is upset or if I'm doing more detoxing. I tried to drink plenty of water yesterday and have my honkin' tumbler of ice water right at hand. I'm also looking longingly at my now empty mug of BPC and thinking I maybe oughtta have another.

The big question is whether or not the headache will keep me from the (what the heck do I call this?) ice bag today. Man it is BAD. So bad I may have to take an aspirin. And since I tend to eschew taking drugs of any sort (ick ick ick) you know it's bad 'un.

Maybe I'll go lie down.

later.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-08T17:46:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-12692>

Yesterday was a total wipeout. The headache finally left last evening but I"m still feeling rocky. I cured the detox though ... ate a bunch of carbs (popcorn, one of my 4 favorite food groups and the last one I'm having trouble letting go of). That stopped it but I still feel like crap. I suppose that's from the carb overload.

Didn't get to the market yesterday so am having a power shake for breakfast ... 3 scoops of protein powder, a cup of ice, a cup of almond milk, 3 TBS coconut milk (the can, not the carton), a TBS of cocoa powder and a touch of sweetener. BPC is up next.

I was pondering this morning while brushing my teeth why Jack says to be sure and have a meal with plenty of protein and fat before doing the face dunk (and I'm assuming any other form of CT). Yet another DUH moment. The last several times I've iced down with the compression T (maybe I'll shorten that to ComT) I've gotten RAVENOUS afterwards. Being as how I'm so good at ignoring hunger signals I really haven't paid much attention to it. Don't know what prompted that thought this A.M. but there it was. Mayhap eating well prior to CT keeps the hungries at bay?

Have a lovely chuck roast in the fridge and although hubby thinks it's going to make soup I have other ideas. Guess whoever gets to the kitchen first wins, eh?

Today's plan is 35 minutes with the ice bag and ComT. And A LOT OF WATER afterwards. Wonder what lovely symptoms the detox will give next. Oh, on that subject I suspect I threw off a BUNCH of estrogen the last couple of days. Yet another reason to stop the ketosis for a bit. gee this is just SO much fun.

### .lina — 2012-04-08T18:53:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-12724>

oooh, if you want to substitute popcorn for something, try this seed cracker recipe, it has some slight popcornish flavour! and everyone i serve these to just love them, even the carb-eaters

> \- mix together 1 cup each of your favourite seeds (flax, sesame, sunflower, pumpkin etc)
>
> \- add a couple of tablespoons of psyllium husk and a pinch of salt
>
> \- boil a couple of cups of water, add a few spoons of olive oil
>
>
>
> stir the liquid into the seed mixture gradually until you get a texture where it's not too wet but also not too dry, you want to be able to pour this mixture onto a sheeted oven pan so it is as thick as you want the cracker to be (too little liquid and it'll have problems sticking together as a "dough" when you spread it, too much liquid makes it too loose and drippy).
>
>
>
> as a last touch, sprinkle some flakes of sea salt on top of it and bake it in the oven for 45 minutes on 150Â°c/300Â°f. then take out, let cool and then eat them as they are, or with butter and cheese, or dipped in really dark chocolate. SO yummy! this is my instead-of-bread, instead-of-crackers and instead-of-popcorn
>
>
>
> there are variations of this recipe where the flax seeds are mixed with the liquid and left to gel up for 30 minutes before baking, and then you can omit the psyllium husk, or you can add a whisked egg as the binding agent, but i haven't tried that yet
>
>
>

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-09T21:14:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-13091>

Rocked 35 minutes with the icebag/ComT yesterday while listening to fatburningman's interview with Jack. Have enough of it left to finish out today's ice torture.

Decided to do a face dunk this morning. Cold water straight from the tap (about 43Â°). Lasted a whopping 30 seconds before my entire face yelled "UNCLE." Entire face was red which faded rather quickly. guess I"m not ready for the tub just yet. Thinking I'll do another face dunk this afternoon before the icebag.

More loose stools this morning as well as a slight headache so the detox is back. Going to the health food store for bitter melon extract (which they probably don't have ... one of the pitfalls of living out in the wilds of Wyoming) and some activated charcoal.

Woke up really late this morning (8:30) then tried to go back to bed as I was quite sleepy. No go. Didn't get my breakfast (another power shake) until about an hour ago. Just feel yuk.

ETA:

Didn't find the bitter melon extract but did buy some krill oil and some activated charcoal AND some epsom salts.

Just had a salmon "burger" for lunch with a couple of spoonfuls of coconut manna for dessert. Going to go see Mirror Mirror in about an hour so best hustle if I"m going to get in my 35 minutes of icebag/ComT.

Getting real tired of the extra estrogen coming out. My engine is revving like mad, if you know what I mean and it is the LAST thing I need. grrrr. Not even the start of the progesterone phase of BHRT is cooling things down.

Well, maybe the ice will help.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-10T18:03:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-13368>

Got in 30 minutes yesterday with the icebag/ComT before I had to give it up and head to the movies. I did get to finish fatburningman's interview with Jack. If you haven't heard it it's well worth the time.

We had Aidell's chicken sausages for dinner and I had about 1/2 cup of nuked sweet potato with lots of butter.

My Blu Ray sunglasses showed up so I put aside the Serengeti's I've been using which weren't nearly as effective at blocking the blue light as the BR's are.

Fell asleep about 10:30 only to be awaked about 40 minutes later by Missy SCREAMING at Three (my cats). Got everyone settled down and then couldn't get back to sleep for at least half an hour. woke up this morning about 7ish with pain in my lower back, right side. Don't know what that was.

Cool shower in a bit and maybe, just MAYbe, I might actually get off the computer and go make some beads today. And aiming for 45 minutes today with the ice. Am seriously considering doing the bath with 65 degree water instead. brrrr.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-11T01:24:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-13524>

OOOkay. Did 45 minutes this afternoon, rockin' with Lady Gaga again. Started shivering ... deep, visceral shivers, about 7 minutes in and didn't stop the rest of the time. Had several weenie-out moments but managed to ignore them. I keep telling myself this is all good. It means my body is reacting as it should to the cold.

Then I thought about the one hour mark and that means starting over with the ice directly on my skin. eeeeeeeeek.

am also gearing up mentally for the first time in the tub. Our tub is really cold anyway because it is, ahem, a "sunken" tub. That means whoever installed it cut a hole in the floor, put up some supports in the crawl space and dropped the tub in. It is a nightmare to clean. Only about 4" above the floor so I have to practically lie on my stomach to clean it. On the plus side it's BIG and it's your basic old tub ... metal with porcelain finish. ON the negative side it is harvest gold. gag.

Ok, digression aside. Not really hungry since BAB so am having a mug of BPC and few spoonfuls of coconut manna. Jack will be so proud  Trying to decide what's for dinner. Maybe some turkey mignon ... Schwan's turkey wrapped in appelwood smoked bacon. And some asparagus for hubby.

I had to try the BP coffee, it was in my mind since I read your message this morning  Had one with my lunch made with CO, butter and raw chocolate...Sooooo good!! I found it so easy to come off coffee, now I'll have to go back (with deca for the moment)!!

Have a great day, keep rocking!!

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-11T20:31:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-13830>

NONONONO NO DECAF!!! BAD. I have been on decaf for probably 20 years because caffeine makes me insane. After reading the posts on coffee over at bulletproofexec.com I realized the problem was the coffee, not me.

I have a (don't laugh) Mr. Coffee espresso maker and having read somewhere the espresso has far less caffeine than a cup of brewed coffee decided to make an Americano and make it bullet proof. I can have a mug of that at 7pm and sleep like a baby.

And your English is far better than my long lost year of college French. No apologies necessary. And I write like I think only not as fast, LOL. My writing tends to be staccato because I'm already a mile down the road from the thought I'm typing so have to keep it brief. You should see my handwriting! On second thought, maybe not. It might scare you!

OK, BAB over and done and a big steaming mug of BPC. Quite full for now. I browned up some icky 80/20 ground beef (was out of fine grass fed wyoming ground chuck) with 3 eggs stirred in. 2 pieces nitrate free bacon. urp. I managed to get it all down somehow.

Aiming for 50 minutes today with the ice. Probably should go face dunk NOW while the breakfast is still working its magic.

Been urging my sister to get over here and start reading up on all this. She's 3 years younger but physically about 30 years older than me. She's had a hip replacement, her knees are shot, her ankles, and a host of other issues. She finally FINALLY listened to me (WHY won't people take my advice? It's so GOOD!) and went for disability retirement last year. Big surprise she got it. She was just so sure they would think she was gold-bricking. Now I'm trying to help her see the light and change her eating and thinking. Sometimes I think it would be easier to teach a pig to fly. And I"m not just talking about her but about other members of my family. They all tell me how smart I am and then when I try to talk to them about this act like I'm a brainless twit. sigh. No respect from those closest to you.

ANYHOO. Louise if you wander in here, finally, and read all this, know I love you and want you around for a long time to come.

Guess I'd better go dunk the ol' face. brrrrrrr.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-12T01:52:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-13986>

Just finished 50 minutes with the 20# icebag over the ComT. Yes I finally remembered to buy the bag of ice while I was at the store. And oddly, it seemed LESS cold that the 9# bag I've been using. Odd. anyway, shivers over for today. Two more days and then it's off with the shirt and start over again at 5 minutes. Then into the tub when I get back up to an hour. Did a face and hand dunk this morning. Cold water only so it was about mid-40ish. brrrrrrrr. Decided to stick my hands in to see if it will help with the osteo in my fingers.

No lunch but finally starting to feel a bit hungry. Not sure what to fix. Am having some coconut manna right now.

Not seeing any detox the last couple of days except for this odd pain in my lower back when I wake up. Don't get that as we have one of those sleep number air beds and have had for about 12 years. I NEVER have a backache unless I don't have enough air in my side. Oh and my vision is kind funny. Not too sure if my computer glasses feel too strong or what. Having a hard time focusing. I'm sure it will pass. Most stuff does.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-13T02:41:09+0300

I don't know where today went but it's about gone. Took my usual cool/cold shower. Started shivering so must have had it down to a decent temp. No CT other than that. Just not in the mood.

BAB was 5oz of steak, 3 FF eggs with coconut milk (carton) and couple of slices of NF bacon. Getting pretty good at chowing that down. Got a wee bit hungry about 2-ish so had a slice of ham rolled around a cheese stick (made the mistake of grabbing the "low fat" ones ...blech. So the ham and a little mustard make it a little more edible. Thinking about fixing some shrimp for dinner as I'm really working on putting more seafood in our diet. I LOVE seafood. Wondering WTF factor X is. My science background is a long time in the past and was in chemistry not biology so I guess I'm going to have to wait for the big reveal.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-13T23:39:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-14868>

so glad you are amused and not angry!

I cannot ... CANNOT .. quit coffee. I love it. I asked my sister if she drank coffee and she said no. I told her she could not be a member of this family!

when our brother was born she watched as Mom nursed him. She asked why she was doing that. Mom told her that he was getting milk (or something ... I mean I was 7 and she was 4. My memory isn't that good). Louise turned to our Grandmother and asked her "Does coffee come out of yours?" Still makes me laugh.

So I'm sorry if coffee is a no-no for you. I LOVE the stuff

today was another blah day. The fur babies woke me up way too early then demanded that I get out of bed like right NOW and feed them. Never mind that kibble is ALWAYS available. They want the good stuff. NOW MOM.

Just a bit ago fell asleep in the massage chair and I think I hurt myself snoring. At least I woke myself up 3 times.

Had planned a cold bath today but it's gray, chilly, and I don't wanna.

Oh dear. I see my inner child is loose again and running things.

### janagram — 2012-04-14T21:11:13+0300

ha! I have one of those! (inner child who runs things...or tries to)

I am so impressed with you CTers!! I tell myself I'll get to that.....

### Lanie — 2012-04-15T02:20:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-15169>

Hey Darleen - It's me from Against the Grain. I did it - I started CT today. Let's do this together!

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-15T19:47:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-15338>

Hi Lanie! Yay! for you! I'll be doing the bath tomorrow ... cool/cold shower today (gotta wash my hair, it's sticking out in every direction. I look like I stuck my finger in the socket, LOL).

I kinda fell off the ol journal-wagon the last couple of days. I have fallen back into that dark hole I just climbed out of. It's not clinical depression but I AM depressed. The whole thing started about 2 1/2 years ago when we had to put our dog down. I was sad and weepy for several months and felt so guilty. No reason, it's just there. I no more than started to get over it when something happened that dumped me back into depression ... cannot talk about it in public as it's a legal matter. That situation is near complete resolution and I started to realize just how depressed I have been about it. I actually went out into the studio and made a few beads and cleaned up my work table!

Then a few days ago my sister called to tell me our 58 yo brother was in the hospital and probably near death. Neighbors had called the police because they hadn't seen him for several days. He was found, unconscious, in a chair in his house. The police told the neighbors another 12 hours and he would have been gone. He's drinking himself into an early grave. I have tried and tried to help him, so has my sister, all to no avail. The last time I ever spoke to him (last summer) he was angry, drunk, and threatening. His living conditions can only be described by an episode of Hoarders. I still say he isn't a true hoarder but simply too drunk and too lazy to clean up after himself. I might be wrong. I don't know.

All I know is I just feel like I'm crawling back into my mental cave and shutting down again.

OK enough of the pity party. I'm going to stay on top of this and am NOT going to give into it.

BAB this morning after sleeping in WAY late. Had ground beef mixed with a glob of bacon fat sautÃ©ed up with 3 FF eggs. added 3 strips of nitrate free bacon and a mug of BPC. I'm a happy girl. Oh and just so you know what a "pig" I am I'm using about 2 ounces of THE best Amish butter in my 2 cups of coffee. oink. I do NOT know where all that fat I'm eating is going but I sure do like it.

I am going to start alternating a cool/cold shower with cool/cold time in the tub. Today is shower day (see above about my HAIR). For the tub, tomorrow I'm started at 65Â° for as long as I can take it. When I get to a full hour I'll drop the temp 5Â° until I get to the optimal temp.

There's another thread over in the CT forum about hot showers. Got me to thinking about when I started going HOT. That would have been in high school. I'd fill the tub with water as hot as I could stand it, climb in and sink down (and usually fall asleep) and stay until the water cooled down. My skin would be bright red. So for me to go even tepid in the shower is a major achievement. But enough pussyfooting around. I may couple the shower days with the icebag on the ComT still.

Thinking about baked tilapia for 2nd meal today. Just LOVE it that way!

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-15T20:12:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-15345>

So sorry about your brother Darleen. So sad to be so young and be destroying his live like that.

Please don't crawl back in your cave. See all the good things you are doing to yourself to feel better, younger and optimal. The depression feeling will fade away as your health improve, even if at first sight it doesn't seem to be link to your health condition. Once your health, your sleep and your hormones are in tunes, you'll be able to handle whatever happens way more easily.

Have a good day. And btw, that coffee you made sounds delicious!!

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-17T18:20:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-15904>

After 3 days of near total badness I am determined to knock off the self sabotage and get with the program.

had my BAB ... 8oz of ground beef, 2 slices bacon and 2 eggs plus a mug of BPC AND my vitamins. Yes, I finally remembered to take the darned things. In about 10 minutes I'm headed for the tub. Have my humongous glass of ice water ready to chug down. We'll see how long I last.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-17T19:18:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-15915>

HOLY CRAP!

The water was 63Â° ... I"m using our digital meat thermometer. We have a nice sized tub. It's big enough for me to sit with my legs fully extended. However, it's not as deep as I thought (I only ever shower in it until now). Filling it to the overflow drain only gets the water to my hips.

Sitting down was a joke. Every inch brought another GASP and a few more 4 letter words. I finally got SAT but omfg. I honestly tried to lie back. I tried about 6 times but every time the water hit my back I rose up about 3 inches out of the water. I finally gave up and just sat there trying to think. The timer finally marked 4:45 minutes of agony. By then I actually was able to ease my back down a bit without swearing at Jack. My feet were starting to complain and I just wussed out.

Then there was the joke of trying to get OUT of the tub. Ever watch a 50 or 60-something try to get up off the floor? Having one knee that won't bend completely thanks to two surgeries didn't help. I wondered for a moment if I would have to sit there and shiver until DH got home from coffee with the guys. I finally got rolled over onto my knees and was able to get up and out. Grab bars are less than useless in this stupid tub.

My legs were cherry red in spots (yeah, seriously) but they have been feeling warm since I got out. The skin isn't warm to the touch, but they feel warm to me. I don't know how else to explain it.

And now I'm rethinking the every other day in the tub thing. Yeah yeah, insert chicken squawks. I'll be dragging out the bag o' ice for the next step (no compression T, just the bag of ice on bare skin OMFG). Mayhap every THREE days for the tub.

This all reminded me of the time my Mom took me to the city pool. I must have been 7? I remember she made me get out of the water because I was literally turning blue. I stood there on the hot concrete apron, wrapped in a towel and shivered uncontrollably. I couldn't seem to get warm. No wonder I love my hot showers so much. I sure do miss 'em.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-04-18T07:54:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-16160>

I totally hear you re: a love of hot showers!

Re: your bath, two things that have been helpful to me: 1) try the "Croak soak" method... Fill the tub with an inch or two of warm water and get in and sit down. Get something to read/listen too and then turn on the cold tap at a slow rate and let it slowly fill. My husband swears by this method for every bath and finds it easy. It still gets very cold in the end. 2) keep your feet (and hands!) out of the water, and don't forget the hat!

Good luck.

### Lanie — 2012-04-18T14:09:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-16178>

Way to go on the bath!

There's another tub method that seems intriguing (in addition to the Croak Soak that KiwiLauren describes)...that's to gradually lower the temp of baths by 1 degree per day over time. I saw someone on here did it that way. Anything to minimize the shock.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-18T17:23:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-16207>

Thx KiwiLauren and Lanie.

My original goal had been to start in the mid-60's and lower the temp gradually as I became acclimated. hahahahaha silly me. I'll try the "croak soak" and see if that works. Trying to get it fine tuned to 1Â° per day might be a little more than I can manage at this point.

### shilohman — 2012-04-18T17:44:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-16211>

Darleen, you might want adapt by starting with a warm shower, then after a minute turn a little more of the warm water off and let your body adapt to that, then keep doing that until you are able to go all the way with no warm water. Once you are adapted to that, then go for the croak soak. That is what worked for me. My goal was to become cold adapted without any of the agony that others on this forum seem to have in their process of becoming cold adapted.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-04-18T20:11:35+0300

I stood there in about an inch of cold water thinking I would get seated first & then add water...not a chance in hell that was happening! I ended up adding some warm water in order to get seated & then added the cold water.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-18T20:24:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-16280>

Oh Shilohman I loves me a little hurt (kidding, just kidding!).

Today's shower came after a wee bit of exertion. DH is getting ready to put down our new flooring (well it was new when we bought it over 2 years ago). So the ugly carpet is coming up and I have to move all my collectibles (aka "crap") out of the living/dining room. Now that most of it's gone I'm thinking it needs to be permanently GONE. I wander off into uncharted territory for a mo'.

SO off to the shower. I set it to where it was yesterday during my near abortive attempt at a cold bath. Two quick gasps and I was in. Mmmm. Not too bad. Turn around. YEEGODSANDLITTLEFISHES as my late Aunt Mary used to say. OK, I can breathe again but the girls may never be the same. whew.

Bottom line is once I adapted it wasn't bad at all. It quickly stopped feeling like icy fingers up and down my spine and actually felt kind of warmish. I finished with about 10 seconds (probably 2 but it felt like 10) of COLD. brrrrr. You know how a dog will shake when it gets out of the water? That's what I felt like doing. BRACING to say the least.

BAB was shelved for a BAS (big ass shake). Protein Power style power-up shake. 1 cup of chopped ice, 3 scoops of bioplex whey, 1 cup of (carton) coconut milk, 3 heaping TBS of (can) coconut milk and a touch of sweetener. Plus a mug of BPC. I'm ready for more coffee btw. But I need to polish off the ice water first.

Have some boneless "country style" pork ribs lined up for dinner. And a fresh pineapple. oh boy.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-19T19:51:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-16645>

Actually behaved myself yesterday. Had chicken for dinner. Cup of bpc. then got nauseous. ???

Slept well. woke up about 3:45 just cooking. Fell back asleep until 7:30. Did the usual A.M. routine and had a BAB of 6 oz ground chuck, 3 eggs and 2 slices of bacon. 2 mugs of bpc.

Now my right ear HURTS. Same one I used to get earaches in as a kid. You know, the screaming kind? Back then the "cure" was warming up mineral oil, soaking a cotton ball in it and stuffing it into the ear. shudder. I think I'm gonna go lie down with the heating pad for bit. My jaw even hurts.

LOL, I guess everyone loves a little hurt now and then  One advantage that I found to pushing the comfort level just a little is that your detox is much less. I have had days where I did a cold bath and earlier that day did spot CT on my waist and the next day I was wiped out from all the detox. So easy is best for me.

One question, does the label on your Protein Power powder say how much sugar it has. If its too high it may be causing an insulin spike that can interfere with your leptin reset.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-22T03:46:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-17616>

Not sure where I've been the last couple of days.

Took a cool shower this morning, couldn't take the cold. Really need to get back to using the bag o' ice.

Was looking over my old blood tests and it appears the local hospital does test HS CRP and the last one I have shows mine is 0.09.  Pretty darn close to ZERO. Oh and that test was a year ago so I will be VERY interested to see what it is now. Since I need to have my vitamin D levels checked in two weeks I'll just ask them for the full deal again. That's the panel they do for the annual health fair but you can have it done anytime.

I have noticed recently that a lot of things I thought were OK seem to be bothering me. I got into those canned nuts recently and they clogged me right up. I don't know if it's the nuts or the oil they use but I won't be eating them again. Raw OR roasted. And I think I"m having similar difficulties with chocolate. I had a pice of 90% dark with lunch and a little later got some heartburn. It didn't last very long but still ...

I think I've had it with the BAB. It's just too much. I can eat it but then I don't want anything else the rest of the day. By evening I might be a little hungry but it's nothing I can't (and usually do) ignore. So today I trimmed back to 3 soft boiled eggs and 4 slices of bacon with a mug of BPC. mmmm. Had a leftover jalapeÃ±o sausage for lunch and am getting ready to have paleo stew for dinner. Not real hungry now although I WAS about an hour ago. I gotta stop waiting on hubby.

Sleep has been excellent and I should have mentioned I got a pair of blu blocker sunglasses (from their website ... they were on sale) and I'm telling you it makes a HUGE difference. I start dimming lights at sundown and put on those sunglasses and I'm yawning in no time. I fall asleep quickly and unless the cats are making a ruckus I STAY asleep. I'm getting in a solid 8 hours. YAAHOO!

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-23T20:57:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-18133>

Behaved admirably yesterday in spite of the fact BIL wanted popcorn at 10PM. I didn't so much as put ONE KERNAL in my mouth. I'm so proud.

Yesterday's menu:

B: 3 eggs scrambled with coconut milk (from the carton not the can), 3 strips bacon, mug of BPC made with 4 TBS buttah. Yeah. OINK OINK>

L: leftover paleo stew which was mostly broth, 2nd cup BPC with the aforementioned piggy amount of butter

D: leftovers ... rotisserie chicken back (the rib parts), small green salad (like maybe a cup) with homemade balsamic vinaigrette, big handful of medium cooked shrimp. Didn't take much and I was full.

No CT. House is in an uproar as DH and BIL were replacing the flooring in the main part of the house. They finished late last night except for cleanup and replacing the trim. It's VERY NICE! Now I have to put everything back in place.

Took a cool shower this morning with cold flashes when DH turned on the hot water in the kitchen. brrrrrrr. Maybe I'll get the ice bag today, maybe not. Hope to get back on track after things get put right and BIL returns home. Thinking of hopping on the scale in the next day or two to see where THAT is. Bracing myself already.

Sleep was good until the cats woke me up at 3:30. Lay awake for about an hour feeling really sorry for myself. Discussed depression over brother's situation with DH and feel a lot better. I think I have decided that I am NOT going to be depressed but deal with things and move on.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-23T21:13:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-18139>

Forgot to mention that my sense of smell has been trying to make a comeback. I hadn't realized how far gone it was until the last week or so when I started getting whiffs of things. VERY weird.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-24T17:51:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-18373>

Boy I'm really out of step. Yesterday's menu:

B: Protein Power super shake (3 scoops bioplex whey, 1 cup coconut milk from the carton, 3 tbs coconut milk from the can) and a mug of BPC

L: bowl of leftover paleo stew, this time with lots of meat, mug of BPC

D: What was probably just 3 oz of "country style pork ribs" with sauerkraut and green beans.

Became ravenous a couple hours later so ate the last 2 TBS of coconut manna (it's been REALLY warm the last couple of days and the stuff was mooshy soft). I know, I know. A little later the hunger was WORSE so ate a hunk of Troyer (Amish brand) colby cheese. That fixed it.

This is the second time in a couple of weeks I've had this major hunger problem after 8PM. And I mean it's HUNGER. I tried drinking a big glass of ice water. Nope. Hunger to near nausea. ????

No CT yesterday except for the shower so doing a face dunk this morning and hopefully the bag o'ice thing this afternoon. BIL still here so chaos (i.e. finishing up the flooring job) continues.

### DarleenMB — 2012-04-26T18:56:19+0300

Crap on toast, as a book character I "know" is fond of saying.

I got on the scale and am up 7 pounds. AM now back to within 10 pounds of my all time highest weight. crap.

Yesterdays menu:

B: 3 soft boiled eggs, 2 slices NF bacon, mug of BPC with 4TBS butter

L: 1 leftover ground beef & herb patty (a la Juliet Child and it was 4 oz of raw meat), handful of veggie chips, mug of BPC with 4 TBS butter

D: 1/2 of tuna salad (can of WP tuna, 1 HB egg, minced celery and onion, blob of mayo) on small Hass avocado

The urge to snack is gone. As long as I eat, that is and stay away from nuts and chocolate.

WTF am I gaining??? STILL?!

grrrr.

Time for my cool shower and the bag o'ice.
