I want to bitch slap every one of them._ Been there, done that I complained of not being able to sleep and all they offered is an antidepressant. The drug of choice - that and Ambien!!
>
> FYI, girls, years ago I read "The Wisdom of Menopause" by Dr. Christine Northrup. Amazing book with a wealth of information. It helped me a lot, and she recommended BHRT but of course nobody around here worked with it. They even looked at me funny up at Mayo when I asked about it. That was an endo in 2005! Not like 50 years ago!
>
> Darlene, will you tell me how cold, how long you do your showers? I have to figure something out because my tub isn't big enough to get immersed, and I would have to stop and buy a bag of ice every time I did spot CT'ing, which I may do! Did you start with face dunking? Do you do it only after a high fat meal? I can do showers although our tap water is 70 degrees! Better than nothing, I figure and it would be a start. You're my hero, babe, so can you tell me about your showers?

Well Joan, let's see. Yes I started with face dunking and progressed to the big bag o'ice on the belly. Worked up to an hour with the compression tee on then did a couple of times with it on my bare skin but not for more than 15 minutes. Then I kinda fell off the ice wagon.

The cool showers have been ongoing since, I dunno, last Feb? when Jack made me rethink the whole "long, hot, steamy shower" thing. I've gradually been lowering the temp until yesterday when I put it on full cold. And our cold tap water is still REALLY cold. Now I'm starting to have dreams about cold baths, LOL! Seriously! So I think it's time.

Since your tap water is so much warmer than mine I would offer the opinion that it's a great way to get (pardon the pun) your feet wet. Start with the water as cool as you can stand it and just try dropping it a little more each time. Just follow your comfort level. If you go too cold and are standing there turning blue and shaking like a leaf then I'd think that would be a bit much. I definitely think using the ice bag is a GREAT way to ease into it all and I'm glad I did it. I still have that 20# bag in the freezer and keep thinking I should go back to the bare skin bit.

HTH!

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-09T20:50:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30303>

It now appears that eggs are definitely a problem for me.

We had friends from Washington stop by for a brief visit on their way home and we joined them for breakfast yesterday before they left. Without thinking I spooned out a bunch of scrambled eggs from the buffet and it wasn't until I finished them I realized I shouldn't have done that.

I was OK for most of yesterday but was feeling tummy upset by last night and again this morning. And then it hit ... they don't call it the "runs" for nothing. I had to RUN down the hallway to the bathroom and barely made it. Good thing our house isn't all that big!

so I need to engrave NO EGGS on my brain.

I did weigh this morning and was up 0.2 pounds. CRAP.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-09T21:14:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30306>

I read (can't remember where) that sometimes the so-called scrambled eggs at some restaurants/buffets have some questionable ingredients. Maybe that could explain part of the problem. Or you just verified your n=1 egg hack means no eggs for you!

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-09T22:21:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30314>

Nope. Eggs at home do the same thing. I have to stay away from them.

also I forgot to mention an interesting little tidbit I found. All this diarrhea has resulted in dehydration, leg cramps and major fatigue. I've been trying to remember to take a potassium tab but kept forgetting. Last night I remembered reading something about potassium loss in the Drs. Eades's last book **The 6 Week Cure for the Middle-Aged Middle. It states:

> ... This prescribed regimen wil replace critical electrolytes and minerals, such as potassium, magnesium, and calcium, that the body may lose at a greater rate during this metabolic switchover period; it will boost levels of iodine and iodide, which are substances clinical testing has found to be low in 90% of tested subjects and critical to normal metabolism ... Magnesium helps to maintain the potassium balance, so look for a product that contains both in near equal amounts -- aim for about 400mg of each one ... We implore you to heed this advice; _unless you replenish the potassium you'll lose every day in the first two weeks as you lose fat and shed excess retained fluid, you may become very tired and could suffer muscle cramping or even heart rhythm disturbances._

**emphasis is mine

So potassium loss from all that diarrhea could explain a lot of what I have been through in the last several weeks and am STILL suffering with. Now I know to match the potassium to the mag and hopefully that will put an end to this strange fatigue I have been having off and on. It's been quite bad the last day or two with my legs feeling like lead. Last night I could barely move around the house. After I took 4 potassium tabs with one mag capsule I began to feel better.

### justjoan060 — 2012-06-10T18:49:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30422>

I have the same problems, Darlene. I sweat a lot when I'm active - always have. When I did the big bike ride across Iowa, I'd have to take salt pills and two bottles of water between towns. (95 and no shade on that ride) Lately I've been overly tired and getting cramps in my feet at night, AND my little heart arrythmias have been worse lately. So what he said makes sense. I haven't been doing that diet, but do think I get dehydrated easily. I recently started taking Potassium again, but don't always remember. I'm glad you posted that advice!

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-10T19:45:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30427>

JustJoan I have to say I feel much better right after taking the mag and the 4 potassium tabs. I only had one of the potassium yesterday with my neuromag and by 8pm was just dragging. I took the 4+1 before bed but also took them again this morning at breakfast. I'll to the neuromag with dinner and take another potassium then. We'll see how I'm feeling at bedtime.

The lethargy really scares me. It's like my legs are lead weights and I can hardly move. If it doesn't improve with the potassium I guess I'll have to make another trip to the doc. darn it!

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-11T17:40:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30525>

I decided to skip the cow this morning and had 2 Schwan's salmon filets. It wasn't as bad as I thought it would be ... having fish for breakfast ... and I did mange to get most of it down but now my stomach is upset. Just great. Now I suppose I'm either allergic to salmon or to coffee. Or maybe it's the bile acid factors capsule I take before every meal. A few unladylike belches and now it's not so bad. geez.

the weather here has turned cool/cold again. I suppose we're going to have one of those summers ... hot and cold and cold and hot and cold again. The joys of living in Wyoming. One of many too numerous to count. Makes it less inviting hopping into that cool shower.

Made a quite tasty shrimp salad for dinner last night, even DH commented on how good it was. Must have been all that mayo.

I know I keep slipping into ketosis ... cat pee indeed. Sure wish I'd stay there long enough to LOSE SOME DAMNED WEIGHT!!!

Still tired and was twitchy last night while trying to go to sleep. Had a hard time of it ... most unusual these days ... and woke up early. So I"m still taking the extra K with the Mg. Now if I can just figure out what I'm reacting to so I don't have all those lovely symptoms.

I think I need a nap. Already.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-11T20:19:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30559>

ANNND it's looking like I am allergic to fish. Same symptoms as from the Great Mushroom Incident (where I had Dover sole) ... upset tummy bordering on nausea, increased difficulty breathing, sneezing (a lot) and generally feeling like home made dog doo-doo.

(insert choice of 4 letter word with many exclamation points)

And NOW to add insult to injury, I;m reading [this September blog post from Jack](http://http://jackkruse.com/msg-your-gut-and-your-brain-post-trauma/) and am wondering if I have destroyed my hypothalamus. When aspartame first came out I was literally living on diet soda. I drank the darned stuff for 10 years, at least, before I finally just quit altogether. Then I read an article about how bad aspartame was by a doctor and I tossed EVERYthing in my pantry that contained the stuff.

I have NEVER (except inadvertently) used any food with MSG (or so I thought. had to rethink that after seeing the list of pseudonyms) because it gives me a 24 hour migraine=like headache (and it's 24 hours nearly to the minute), nausea and diarrhea. So I really REALLY make a major effort to avoid it at all costs. Much to the consternation of relatives and wait staff.

But maybe all this (leptin reset, etc) is going to be of no avail. Maybe I have permanently damaged myself and am now stuck.

I'd bang my head on the keyboard but I already don't feel so good.

I think I'm going to go cry.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-11T20:49:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30565>

Darleen, I just re-read that post because I used to use diet soda years ago. Although the post wasn't encouraging to past users of aspartame I didn't get the sense that we're totally screwed (for lack of a better word). Maybe I'm missing the bigger picture but this is the line from that blog that gives me hope that we didn't permanently damage ourselves...

"So if these neurons become damaged or rendered non functional your hormones will never be optimal with diet alone".

Which is why we need help via hormones and such??? IDK, a few years ago I'd be right into the science of all this but I feel like it's over my head lately, which I mainly blame on the brain fog!

### justjoan060 — 2012-06-11T21:35:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30576>

Well, Darlene, just DANG IT!!! I feel for you. I couldn't eat fish for breakfast if I had to - I generally don't like fish. I like shrimp, tuna, salmon, but not for breakfast!! Doesn't seem like it's a typical allergy, but what do I know! Maybe it is something else like ealachan says. I did look over the weekend, and my Potassium's only < 100 per capsule, so I started taking more of that. I did have more energy this weekend. I have 22 windows on my first floor and got them all washed!

I also have started back taking my Juice Plus capsules (you can Google it) which always seem to help my energy level and I don't get the colds/flu when everybody else at work has them. Haven't asked Dr. K an opinion on that.

I just went and read that September blog post (printed off the MSG list so I can have it with me shopping). I didn't read anything that sounded like your hypothalmus is "destroyed". It says it "may not be able to be managed with food only", unless I read it wrong. I do believe the body has amazing healing capabilities, so hang in there! Thanks for passing along info. It helps other people!

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-12T01:20:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30622>

OK maybe I over reacted ... maybe "damaged beyond repair" is more accurate.

and the only thing I ate this morning was the salmon. Had another mug of BPC a bit ago and NONE of the symptoms I had earlier appeared. I googled food allergies and it turns out an allergy to fish is quite common.

Resupplied myself with kombucha from the health food store and this time they had L-glutamine but no FOS. Ditched the liquid Splenda (i quit using the packets long ago because of the maltodextrin) and bought a bottle (ye gads it's pricy!!) of liquid stevia. Tossing the Truvia as it is erythritol. I HATE sugar alcohols.

So I'm slowly trying to get my leaky gut healed (because I'm pretty sure that's BIG issue for me). And it's back to big burgers for breakfast.

Thanks ladies. I appreciate the input!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-12T01:45:43+0300

### justjoan060 — 2012-06-12T05:14:59+0300

Crap! I just read what Dr. K posted! I've used Sweet N Low in my coffee for years! I've FINALLY gotten used to Stevia, so maybe I can get less and less dependent on sweeteners at all. And yes, liquid Stevia is pricey!!

Also, was going to tell you I had bought some frozen Wild Alaskan Salmon patties at Sam's Club, read the label and it looked pretty legit! By the way, Darlene, I don't know if I've mentioned before, but I've been getting some therapy from a Physical Therapist that does some cranio sacral and other treatments that aren't real mainstream. I've been sleeping better since and have been able to wean down some on the dose sleep med I take. I did go grain and sugar free about the same time, so it's a little hard to tell what's helping, but I'm pretty sure the cranio sacral therapy has been helping me sleep better and stay generally calmer through this divorce. We do Reflexology, and some other alternative therapies at the clinic where I work and I had good luck with that also. You might try it. I believe in it! ### DarleenMB — 2012-06-12T17:55:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30706>

JustJoan, my monthly treat is a visit to my massage therapist and she has done a lot of cranio-sacral work on me. I'm very open to alternative treatments and have tried acupuncture (now THAT was weird!) but cannot afford to continue that on a regular basis along with the massage. I used to go to the chiro on a regular basis (ours is a PEACH! He has a master's in sports med and his doctor of chiropractic or whatever you call it) but again, it's just too much with the massage. If I'd get back to making and selling beads I could afford both or even all three. And would NEED them! LOL

Ealachan, I hear ya on the hubs thing. Mine is still eating the occasional DQ blizzard. I did get him down to sugar free popsicles but now have to take THOSE away. He is not going to be happy. Although his weight, unlike mine, is easily fixed. He quit all things wheat in January and has lost (effortless, the butthead!) 20 pounds. He nibbles his way past dinner, crunches on nuts until I have to leave the room or else smack him with a heavy object and he keeps losing. It is so frustrating I cannot believe it.

Jack, thanks for the input. NOT. just kidding. This could explain why the hcg diet did not work very well for me. That and all the food allergies I am starting to uncover. I keep a list of current no-no's on the whiteboard on the fridge. Just in case I forget. Doesn't always help because yesterday I had a yen for some raw almonds. they don't seem to bother me as much as the processed ones. gee. I wonder why THAT is. duh.

So off to read through more of Jack's blog postings to see if I can uncover what I need to be doing. I'm starting to think that I need a phone consult with him. Better start saving my money because I AM going to do the 23andme thing first and I'll need all the blood tests done. This may take a while.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-13T22:24:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30966>

I snagged this little template from paulalynn. Thanks!

**Breakfast within 30 min of waking: as close as I could get

**Breakfast 50 grams of protein: 8 oz ground sirloin 44g?? is that right? 2 slices ultra thick applewood smoked bacon mmmm

**No Snacking:

**5 hours between Breakfast & Lunch:

**5 hours between Lunch & Dinner:

Dinner within 4-5 hours of bedtime**:

Scale of 1 to 10, 10 high**

Energy?** 3

Mood?** 4

Cravings?** 0

I haven't said anything about this because, frankly, I've been too damned tired. It has been SO bad that a couple of nights ago I could literally barely get up off the couch and walk to the bedroom. I was starting to think something was really really wrong until 2 nights ago I was having the same thing happen and went and lay down for about 30 minutes. I didn't sleep, just closed my eyes and lay there. It helped. I realized I might not have been sleeping very well and I know I've been waking WAY too early the last week. So now I think it's been a lack of sleep although it has never ever affected me like this.

I also wondered if it was one of the new supplements I've added to the pile. Yesterday I skipped all of the supps except the Mg and the K. I take my supps twice a day. Once with breakfast and the remainder with lunch. Last night I got so bone weary AGAIN I had trouble moving around.

So I'm thinking maybe it's the extra potassium and I skipped THAT this morning and took all my regular supps. Had my breakfast and I'll be darned if 2 hours later my stomach didn't start to REALLY hurt AGAIN. (insert sound of creaky rusty mind trying to turn over)

hmmm

Could it _possibly_ be the cold water omega capsules? Well DUH you dummy! If you are, indeed, allergic to fish and esp salmon! WTH are you taking fish oil for???????

This might explain a lot. I'm ditching the fish oil (I'm also taking krill oil so we'll see if these nasty symptoms continue with that) for now. I have an appt with my doc next week who specializes in allergies. I'm scheduled for a blood draw friday for what our local hospital calls a "wellness screening." I'm confident almost every city and town does an annual health fair where they check your now-standard tests and tell you how close you are to dropping dead of a coronary because your cholesterol is 2 points over the latest low mark. Our local hospital now offers that test every week on Friday, same price as the health fair ($45). It's been over a year since I had this done and thought I'd like to see if there have been any significant changes.

I'm also MAKING myself drink lots of water because the last several months I've really fallen off the ol' wagon. I am STILL having horrible muscle craps. I woke up early this morning with a cramp on the side of my right leg that felt like it was pulling my foot sideways. A few hours later my left leg cramped the same way only higher up. My feet have been cramping (at night while I'm sleeping) as have my calves. WTh? The only other thing I can come up with as a cause is dehydration. Hence the water police.

Now I've worn myself out. And I'm feeling that stupid mouth thing that comes with the stomach ache. It's so weird. The only way I can describe it is to say it feels like ... tsk ... you know when you pour soda over ice and you get all those eensie bubbles that jet up off the surface? THAT'S what my mouth feels like. It's kind of tingly, kind of a little bit hurting and majorly annoying. I keep feeling like I need to scratch my tongue or the roof of my mouth. It's not bad right now but it's there. UGH!

It's not bad right now but it's there. UGH!

Darleen, are you getting enough SALT? Potassium and magnesium are electrolytes, but so is sodium. If one of them gets out of whack it can throw the whole balance off and make you feel craptastic - tired, muscle cramps, the whole nine yards.

Just a thought. Hope you're feeling better soon!

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-14T00:10:45+0300

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-14T03:53:31+0300

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-14T05:09:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-31068>

Thanks paulalynn. I'm pretty sure it's allergies. I couldn't figure out why I was feeling crappy again this afternoon and DH insists it must be some sort of issue with my supplements. Thank you, dear.

THEN I remembered I decided I could eat some raw almonds. um. Maybe that wasn't such a good idea. It IS on the top of my list followed by eggs, fish and mushrooms.

I suspect that by eating clean for so long the allergies which have always been there are now really beginning to flare. Enough that I now have to pay attention. At least, that's my theory and I'm stickin' to it for now.

By eating the almonds, btw, my appetite completely disappeared and I had to force myself to eat 2 thin but large slices of Amish deli ham, 6 beanitos (couldn't help myself, blood glucose levels be damned) and a tiny handful of sweet potato chips. It was all I could manage. That and a cup of plain kefir. I still feel crappy. but my stomach is getting better even though my tongue is still itching.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-15T19:31:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-31382>

**Breakfast within 30 min of waking: Not today. Had to go to lab for blood draw

**Breakfast 50 grams of protein: 8 oz GF ground sirloin, 2 THICK slices bacon (50ish??), mug BPB (2TBS GF butter, 2TBS CO)

**No Snacking:

**5 hours between Breakfast & Lunch: Hungry, just. ham, avocado, terra veggie chips, kombucha

**5 hours between Lunch & Dinner**:

Dinner within 4-5 hours of bedtime**:

Scale of 1 to 10, 10 high

Energy?** 3

Mood?** 5

Cravings?** 0

I notice a definite difference when I stay away from trigger foods. The change in feeling is like throwing a switch. Happened last evening so all the crap must have finally been cleared out of my system and the inflammation from eating the almonds fell below some threshold. Went to bed at 9pm as I was TIRED. Woke up about 6 but still need more sleep.

Am keeping my fingers crossed that my next weigh-in will show a drop since I'm really paying close attention to what I'm eating and how I feel.

3 days ago I was RAVENOUS. Ate lunch AND dinner.

next day I ate raw almonds and my appetite evaporated.

Felt better yesterday and was STARVING for lunch. At the 5 hour mark I was so hungry I could have eaten the cat. Raw. Good thing I had some ham in the fridge. FAMISHED again in about 4 hours.

So what I think is going on is that the food allergies have kept my real appetite at bay. Here I thought it was ME. I'm curious to see what will happen today but be assured I will be near the kitchen about 1 which will be closing in on the 5 hour mark.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-18T18:18:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-31938>

My stupid allergies have had me laid low for days now. It's so bad even HUBBY is complaining about itchy eyes and runny nose. My energy level is about 1 and I am sick and tired of hacking and hawing. I sound like a 90 year old man with sinusitis.

Am beginning to discover exactly what I should NOT be eating ... like anything with sugar alcohols in it no matter how minute. They wreak havoc with my blood glucose levels. When I eat ONLY proteins and fats and tiny bits of carbs my fasting BG tests out perfect. It was 79 the other morning. Last night I had 3 of those tiny sugar free Werther's and this morning my BG was 99. Guess what I'm giving up.

I have also stopped all supplements with the exception of the bile acid factors. If I don't take THAT I simply do not digest any fats I eat and probably don't get all the benefit of the protein. I cut way back on the butter and CO in my coffee. Not because I don't want it but because if I'm a pig about it then I'm just STUFFED at breakfast. Anyway, still having a little "tongue tingling" again this morning and am wondering WHAT is causing it. It just might be my seasonal allergies because they are SO bad right now that even medication isn't helping. But something I have been taking has been making it worse.

And on the TMI front, I was NOT paying attention again and am once again constipated. Stupid frakking allergies. GAAAAH! This is just MADDENING! No wondering I feel like crap. Yeah yeah, I know. I'm full of it. pfffbbbbbbtttt

I was hoping to get to the torch today so if I'm going to do that before the temps get too high I'd better skedaddle. Will try to remember to update:

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-19T17:14:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-32140>

Nothing new here. Same old same old. Went to see Prometheus ... good movie, fantastic visuals. Ate popcorn. Now here's the interesting part. Eating popcorn leaves me with a FBC the next morning of (this time) 86. Eating 4 or 5 of those tiny SF werthers spikes me up to as high as 123. Yeah yeah I shouldn't eat either. I'm having a tantrum. I eat the same BAB every day (going on 8 months now). Eat clean 99% of the time. have I lost anything? Only my temper. It's not like I'm only 10 pounds over weight. I'm frakking 90 pounds + over weight.

Never mind that I have discovered that food allergies either make me gorge on the trigger food or lose my appetite altogether. That's was a surprising revelation. sigh. I guess I'm getting PO'd over what few foods are left that I CAN eat. Which isn't really the case but it sure feels that way. I mean, nuts, eggs, dover sole, salmon all for sure. Chicken? Not sure about that one yet. Having Moroccan chicken tonight from one of my paleo cook books. We'll see how I tolerate that. Mushrooms. Short list but I'm in a whiny mood. Pass the cheese (to go with my whine).

Then he mentioned "diet & exercise." LOL. I had given him Jack's intro page and the leptin RX pages that I had printed out. I sure hope he will go to Jack's blog and start reading!

Noticed the last couple of days my skin is unbelievably DRY. It looks like it belongs on a 90 year old woman!!! EEEEEEEK. I'm all dry and crinkly! Super deluxe moisturizing lotion didn't do much. WTH???

Had marinated chicken breast fried in CO for dinner with a nice fresh crunchy green salad with some of the last of my Suzanne Somers' ranch dressing on it. yum!

{{hangs head in shame}} ate popcorn again today. I am so bad. AND I had a mini milky way bar. Which made my stomach hurt although I don't know if it was from guilt or the chocolate. haven't had CANDY candy in 2 years. tsk! shame on me.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-23T04:33:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-33101>

Destiny I explained that in the post where you copied this from. Each number represents a line on the little plastic syringe. One is how much estradiol and the 2nd is how much progesterone. And yes ... twice a day.

Are you on the Wiley Protocol? Because if you aren't the numbers are meaningless in comparing to yours. The WP is a very specific program who's purpose is to put a woman's hormones back at the same level as when she was in her 20's. Which means I'm using a LOT of hormones. Most docs would probably come unglued at the numbers. But the whole point is to restore the protection provided by the hormones at their optimal level. Oooh. I used Jack's favorite word, LOL!

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-24T18:27:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-33296>

Why would i stop my BHRT?

And thank you AbetterMe I hope it will work this time too. Actually I hope I GET my order so I can get started. I bought the [book](http://http://amzn.to/KGCh2v) mentioned in another thread and already have more hope this will work this time.

The first time I did hcg was about a year after coming off that stupid jenny craig diet. I was still not eating right (paleo or low carb) and I'm hoping that means that my body just wasn't either leptin sensitive OR willing to give up any weight. I've said before how the last 6 months on that diet I only lost 8 pounds. EIGHT POUNDS in 6 months on 1200 calories a day. Something was just not right. I still had another 50 to go to goal. 40 if I was being reasonable. As it was it took me a year to drop 50 pounds. That's less than a pound a week when, in my 20's when everything was still working pretty well, that amount of caloric intake would have jettisoned 30 pounds in a couple of months.

SO. We will see. I know I'm sick to death of eating so much at breakfast and this morning cut back on the ground sirloin to 4 ounces (raw). Still had the 3 slices of bacon and the 4TBS of butter in the BPC. BTW I way miscalculated on the butter when I first started drinking BPC. I wonder if I did it on purpose. I was putting HALF A CUP (4 oz) of butter in a mug. And drinking it that way 3 times a day for about a week. Then I guess I topped off the ol' tank because my desire for BPC has dwindled to just one mug in the morning and then I'm done.

On another note, I got a sick headache last night (maybe it was that stupid movie DRIVE that I watched) and was up for a couple of hours in the middle of the night just plain sick. I felt clammy and a bit nauseous. Have NO idea what's going on but the stupid headache is still there. I took 2 8-hour tylenol about 4AM and I STILL have a headache. PLUS poor old Sammy cat had a nasty seizure about 2AM so I spent a couple hours worried about him. He really had a hard time coming back from it. That's 3 in about 6 weeks. WITH meds. Might be time to talk to the vet again. Geez.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-25T18:09:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-33464>

The heat is on and my appetite has withered to near nothing.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-26T19:42:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-33705>

And yet another boring post. Went to see BRAVE yesterday and I'm SUCH a sap it made me cry. Got to hubby too although he'd never admit it. heh heh.

AND... NO POPCORN. Actually it was more because there were too many people at the counter but I didn't really miss it so however/whatever.

Made a nice green salad for dinner with homemade balsamic vinaigrette and lots of cooked shrimp. mmmm. And some thawed berry mix with a touch of HWC for dessert. That and breakfast were all I had (well, except for a handful of sunflower seeds).

I'm still waiting to hear on my order for hcg. I think it got lost in their system.

AND on that note I have some comments about all the flap about the protocol. First of all Simeons wrote that thing in the 1950s. For those of you who were not there here's a few thoughts on various subjects.

1\. Tuna back then came oil packed. I don't think water pack showed up for at least a couple of decades. I plan to eat it on protocol if I'm not allergic to it.

2\. Lotions and creams were different. And honestly I don't think you absorb fat or calories via their use.

3\. Given what I'm reading about hcg's effect on leptin I'm starting to think the whole strict food list is pretty much nonsense. The fact that hcg might assist the body in reading leptin levels and thus stave off hunger means you only need to restrict your calorie intake and only worry about getting adequate protein. Fat is avoided because THAT'S WHERE ALL THE CALORIES ARE. Although given how much butter I have in my coffee everyday and have NOT GAINED AN OUNCE I'm thinking that's probably a non-issue as well. But I'm not going to risk it.

Skip the breadstick! Wheat is off the menu! See [Dr. Davis's blog](http://http://www.wheatbellyblog.com) to find out why if you haven't read his book (and if you haven't, WHY NOT?). Watch the fruit. Apples in the 50s were SMALL compared to the gargantuan things in the market today. Broccoli ... why wasn't it on his list? Well as near as I remember broccoli didn't show up in the stores out in the boonies of Colorado where I grew up until the 70's. I have a Betty Crocker cookbook from the 40s or 50s and it doesn't even LIST broccoli! EAT it if you want it. It's good and has very few calories. Ditto with green beans. Filling up on salad (with zero calorie dressing) or steamed NONstarchy veggies is a good thing on this protocol.

Ok /lecturemode

Loving my cool showers in this heat. Have not gone back to using the bag o'ice although I keep thinking I should. thought about going to the public pool and soaking in the water for an hour IF it isn't heated. I need to call the rec dept to find out. Don't know why I didn't think of this sooner.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-27T16:57:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-33936>

I found a good app for tracking calories for my iPad. No judging the food with letter grades (fatter, of course being given an F), no judging about (lack of) exercise but they do have ridiculous calorie limits. I put in my weight, height, age and that I wanted to lose 2 pounds a week. It says I should limit my calories to 981 a day. hahahahahaha. Pretty funny. But it tracks the food I eat with protein/fat/carb amounts as well as the calories. So I'm ready for the 21 days of hcg. which I hope jump starts the weight loss.

Breakfast this morning was 4 ounces (weighed raw) ground sirloin, 3 slices bacon, 1 mug BPC with 4 TBS butter. 731 calories.

that's what I had yesterday and was pretty much fine until about 5pm. We had garden salad again this time with diced ham and a sprinkling of pine nuts.

It's been gawdawful hot here ... up into the 100's the last few days. Today is supposed to be "cooler" and only the high 80's low 90's. Break out your fur underwear. There's a big fire up in Montana and the smoke has drifted down here so we got a lovely deep red sunset last night and an equally red sunrise this morning. ### DarleenMB — 2012-06-28T20:48:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-34209>

Cut my breakfast down even more, or so I thought. I had 2 eggs scrambled in CO with no tummy issues after. hmmm. 4 slices bacon and a mug of BPC w/3 TBS butter.

I should say here that I use my Nu-Wave oven to cook my bacon and steaks and burgers. Most of the fat goes into the pan below the rack but that's ok because I add in CO and butter to other stuff. >
> I sure wish my old Palm pilot hadn't decided to go on the fritz. I had this GREAT little program for tracking calories ... it tracked and totaled EVERYTHING and I could add new stuff.

What app are you using for calorie counting? It's not an app, but I really like Paleotrack.com. It shows you all kinds of different tallies of stuff - calories, O6, O3, carbs, protein, vitamins and minerals...it even lets you create entries for your supplements and vitamins so you can really fine-tune stuff. It's neat.

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-28T21:28:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-34219>

Hi darleen. Have you considered leaky gut as a reason for your new food allergies? The way it was explained to me is when you have a leaky gut, food passes through gut into immune system (especially if you have low stomach acid and food is not as digested as it should be) and then your immune system marks that food as a foreign substance and now you're allergic to it. Clear up leaky gut and it might help your allergies. Just a suggestion. Good luck to you. I admire your spirit and sense of humor!!

LOL Thanks vkiernan!

The good news today is I have no food allergies according to the RAST. so I really must have had some kind of bug thingy goin' on for a week or so.

And now I look like a pincushion but I am (still) allergic to trees (russian olive being a big offender and we have a whole LINE of 'em up by the road so when the wind blows it carries all that lovely pollen right straight to the house. ahhchoo. Thanks), molds, grass (gotta love that we live on the grasslands) and dogs and cats. Sorry. I'm not giving up my cats for anything.

So I'll be starting on drops as soon as they get them formulated and back to the doc.

I spent my waiting time continuing to read Weight loss apocalypse. Interesting take on how leptin actually works and how hcg might fit into that equation. Don't like all her rantings about emotional eating. I'm sure a lot of folks ARE emotional eaters to some degree or other and yes, until you get that part fixed you will not succeed with any eating plan. But it's boooorrrring. Give me the science please.

And if I hear one more person yap about "sticking to the protocol."

Am I the only 60-something out there who remembers the 50's? Yes I was a wee lass but I remember.

Just eat the egg WHITE? What? Are you making a meringue? Where's the pie?

broccoli? What the heck is THAT?

etc etc.

Try to remember when the protocol was written. Everything today was not the same as in the 50's. Try to remember that.

With that in mind I laid out a little eating plan that includes egg white omelets made with extra lean ham (50's again ... WTH is that??) and lots of green salads made with ZERO calorie dressings. again, WTH is THAT?

We have so many newfangled things and notions and ideas that weren't even the beginnings of thoughts 60 years ago.

Try watching "Back to the Future" if you don't get it.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-06T20:59:41+0300

Man I am just plain STUPID from the heat. It's been 104 for several days (very unusual but not unheard of for this time of year) ... the landscape is just PARCHED (that's why UW colors are brown and gold). No fireworks this year anywhere because of all the fires in surrounding states (which are sending us lots of smoke, thanks a lot) august and Sept will be a nightmare at this rate.

Also, my appetite simply disappears in the heat. I mean ... I just do NOT want to eat. So why aren't I losing weight? hmmmm?

Still waiting on the hcg. Skimmed through that book on hcg and finally found a bit worth paying for ... seems researchers have determined that hcg "stimulates leptin" which I take to mean it forces fat to release leptin thus shutting off your appetite. So perhaps the problem I had the year I used it was that (wait for it) my leptin resistance was so intense that it didn't make any difference. We'll see if the nearly 9 months of eating a BAB and not much else has changed that. If not then I'm going to have to really look at my hormones.

FINALLY got some sleep last night. About 10+ hours worth. It cooled down with some cloud cover and a tiny bit of rain during the night and I was able to shut both curtains and actually needed a blanket. I'm still tired (from the heat and lack of sleep again) but not feeling nearly as brain dead. Between my seasonal allergies, that strange bug I picked up and now the smoke from all the fires I am a mess.

Ordered up that ice vest from Amazon and a bandanna and ice pack refills. Since I'm NOT doing cold baths and NOT using the bag o' ice I figured I'd better do something besides the cool showers. Maybe this will get me motivated. If nothing else I can wear both while I torch (if the outside temps ever fall back to sane levels again).

And finally, back to my usual BAB ... 8 oz ground beef and 3 slices bacon and a mug of BPC with a little over 4 TBS (2 oz) butter. AND I just realized I didn't take my bile acid factors. I'll pay for that. Man I hate being sleep deprived. I just cannot think straight.

Time to catch up on my leptin reset.

I did make a tuna salad last night even though I wasn't feeling hungry. Ate it w/o a problem. And then REALLY wasn't hungry. If I thought of food the thought left before it even got formed.

Woke at 4am with a HUNGRY tummy but fell asleep again and woke at 8 not hungry at all. huh.

The problem, I am beginning to think, is my hormones. I'm still on the Wiley Protocol (one year now) but even though the doc I went to told me I didn't need to supplement testosterone I think he's full of beans. So it's back to the PA for a frank discussion (cause I simply CANNOT face talking about THIS with my doc ... this has to be a "girl's" frank discussion) and getting my testosterone levels checked. I KNOW my estradiol and progesterone need to be checked but we don't know what the correct levels are. I'm hoping I can get Deb to sign up for the Wiley certification class. THEN we have a reference point. Otherwise I"m going to have to seek out another Wiley certified doc or go back to the old one (whom I do NOT like because a. he won't take medicare patients and b. I think he went into medicine to help himself, not others, but that's just MY opinion and worth what you paid for it).

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-11T18:41:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-36848>

Darleen,

Yup, I'm on day 29 of my first ever HCG round. I would call it a success. Prior to undergoing, my weight was stable, and I have been eating paleo and epi paleo long enough that I don't worry about slipping back into SAD.

My approach was to really listen to and monkey those who have been successful with this on a long term basis. I look at journeys and behaviors of those who lost and kept it off for long periods of time.

What I found out was they were all very strict with WHAT foods and ate on protacol. Meaning no veggie mixing/swapping/messing.

I feel I'm in the groove on it now, and here is what I'm doing:

Coffee and Tea, no artificial sweetners, lots of lemon and vinegar.

Lunch: small serving of either spinach, lettuce, cucumber, asparagus and the remaining calories in protein.

Dinner: same, but never same protein or veggie as lunch.

Afternoon snack: about 40-80 calories in a fruit.

I want my protein going to protein things my body needs, not converting to glucose so I included a fruit.

I do exactly (or as close as one can get) 500 cals of protein. THis is way more than 100 grams weighed raw.

ONLY SIMEON FOODS. NO MIXING. NO DUPLICATING PROTIEN.

My protiens have been: chicken, cod, crab, shrimp, beef and once had buffalo.

I am not weighing daily. Last time I checked I was losing .08 lbds a day on average.

I messed with doses quite a bit. Lower, lower, lower works best. I'm doing about 125-150 iu's and adjusting as needed. I skip a day . .and my skip day is usually quite enjoyable. More than the other days!

The other advice I've seen from successes is don't do a short round. Harder to become stable after.

I plan to do the whole steak day thing.

I am all fat free, nothing, not a cream or a lotion and wash my hands immediately when preparing stuff for others. I planned ahead and brought all my food for vacations. I'm not taking any chances and I am doing this as perfectly as I can. I've gone through this much trouble, I don't want to risk anything by taking a chance a restaurant really didn't use oil - or accidently be too far off on calories.

I don't bother with "cooking" or preparing anything delish. Just eat it. It's a hormonal therapy not a real meal.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-11T19:45:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-36877>

Thanks for the input Cavemam. the original protocol calls for 21 days, period with SIX WEEKS between rounds 1 and 2. EIGHT weeks between 2 and 3, TWELVE weeks between 3 and 4, TWENTY weeks between 4 and 5 and SIX MONTHS between 5 and 6 if that many rounds are called for. I really think those who do double or more of the 21 days or do short time between rounds are asking for trouble in the long run. ANd it seems they are the ones who are hollering the most about sticking to protocol.

I also KNOW that some foods not on the original protocol were not even in the grocery stores in the early 50's when the protocol was written so the whole "broccoli" or "no broccoli" thing is a total non-starter. Beef was NOT LEAN back then. Ranchers would have laughed their backsides off had you asked for lean beef. Egg whites were used to make meringue for pies not omelets. Again, you'd have been laughed at had you asked for such a weird thing. times have changed, formulae have changed. Body lotions used to be made exclusively with mineral oil. And the only artificial sweetener around was saccharin. And I'm not sure it exactly when it became available.

/rant

Having said all that, I'm glad to see you're doing so well. I want to do a normal 21 day round to see if I can jar my body into proper action. I'm finding little pillows of fat on my legs (the 2nd one appeared on my left leg recently). ARGH. I've been icing the original one which was 3" x 2" when I first measured it and quite fluffy. I've got it down to about 2 x 1.5" now.  Now I have to start icing the OTHER leg. WHY is my body doing this? Is it because I"m not properly metabolizing the fats I'm eating? Geez. Bad enough to be FAT now I'm LUMPY fat. (insert swear words of choice)

My appetite is still nonexistent. AND I managed to get myself dehydrated enough that I had had a moment yesterday AM when I nearly blacked out. I was sitting at the computer (where else?) and took a deep breath and ... whoa!!! Remembering I was getting muscle cramps AGAIN the last several nights I smacked myself HARD on the forehead and went and filled up my water pitcher. I use it to track how much water I have or have not been drinking. I drank ALL of it plus part of another one PLUS a liter bottle in the car. Feel much better, TYVM

Had lunch which I don't normally do because a friend was here (she had her right knee replaced a couple of weeks ago and is now recuperating by coming out here to watch last season's GAME OF THRONES eps. So I fed her and ate as well so she wouldn't feel like I was doing something extra special. Then I didn't want dinner but did eat some junk (popcorn, what else).

Cut back on the ground beef this morning because 8 oz just seems too much. Cut back to 6 and changed the butter in my BPC to see if THAT makes a difference on the itchy tongue. If not then I'm looking at the coffee. NOOOOOOOOOOO

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-11T23:25:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-36950>

Dear god lumps of fat and no coffee? I'd rant too. I think it's great you are not wanting to eat! That's good right? Good luck on spurring the loss. I don't plan on doing another round any time soon, but I am eager to see what experience others are having with different stuff. Ultimately for me I just let go of the idea of the food being a pleasurable thing, and It's like medicine so I don't much care if it's broccoli or asparagus or salad. They all run together - no pleasure in them. That IS the idea though right?

For discussion (because I like exploring things to the Nth dgree, not because I'm contrary), the protocol says no more than 40 objections for the obese, and if you need to lose 15 or less do 23 injections. Or a max of 36 lbds. I wish!

You are probably right with the this/that veggie as long as calories and type of fiber are same. One thought I had on the meat, is wouldn't the beef in the 50's in italy have been normally raised grass fed types - thus lower in fat that we would have had here? Ultimately I'm sure your right it a little more fat in one meal probably won't RUIN all progress.

For me, it comes down to OCD + VOODOO. Since I don't really care much about the food anyways, I see no reason to NOT do it by the book. Although, come to think of it, I am not doing any melba and doing much more protein than suggested. I also have been skipping my daily "bow down to the pounds and inches book and dance by the light of the moon and summons simeon fairies" suggestions so we will see.

I think it's premature to feel "successful" on this yet. I think I need a good 6 months of no gains following to see. After looking at my specifics, doc thinks it's going to be tough to keep the weight down. It's probably good he said that as I'm going to be extra vigilant. I hate being told I can't do something

I have not weighed daily, as I really question if you can even judge if some combo or some specific food was the cause of a fluctuation. So, I may or may not weigh next week. I like the "try on skinny clothes" methods.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-14T03:59:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-37666>

I'm trying to get this whole leptin thing straight in my head.

HcG stimulates leptin which lowers your appetite.

CT causes cell apoptosis which lowers the amount of leptin available to circulate in your blood. I'm assuming here that when the fat cells are disappeared (obliterated?) that the leptin goes with?

So if one is obese, then why wouldn't the two, in conjunction, work better than either alone?

with those questions ringing in my head I got out the old bag o'ice and did 15 minutes on my bare belly which has waaaay too much fat on it. I'd have done longer but I haven't used it in about 2 months. I figured since I'm roasting from the AWFUL heat we're having it would be most pleasant. I was right ... after the first 2 or 3 minutes. Belly is a nice cherry red. Cool/cold shower for the morning.

SUGAR, corn syrup and maybe coconut. They weren't as good as the Mexican coconut ICE CREAM bars I got into a while back. (insert mumbling about scandahoovian ancestry and other assorted northern european types ... thanks a bunch)

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-16T16:48:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-38353>

Day 5 Hcg protocol

today's weight ... 223.6

change ... -2.0

total ... -4.2

I did NOT expect that big a drop again. figured 1 pound would be great.

Having some detox symptoms. got a headache last night and it's still here this morning.

Yesterday had shrimp and green salad with 0cal dressing and a small slice of melon (cantaloupe) for meal 1 and a halibut and about an oz of leftover shrimp with 1/2 small sweet potato (a tad less than 3 ounces but didn't eat the skin so probably 2+?) with a small slice of cantaloupe.

Drank a LOT of water. The entire pitcher and then some. So well over 100 ounces which is good since we're still up into the 90's here. Missy announced her displeasure at them around 1 so had to drag her into the bedroom to shut her up. For such an old and tiny cat she has a BIG VOICE. And she's not afraid to use it.

Off to the PA today to see about my hormone levels. again.

Maybe chicken for lunch today for something different or maybe I'll do that tonight. DH isn't fond of fish.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-17T17:25:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-38719>

Thanks PaulaLynn!

Yesterday was little rough. I don't know if it was caffeine withdrawal (shouldn't have been) or if we had managed to pick up a bug (we aren't eating the same things so pretty sure it wasn't the food). I had a bad headache for over 24 hours, some nausea and loose stools (tmi?) Hubby had the runs. shrug.

So I caved early in the day and had a decaf mug of coffee with 2tbs butter. Well, there goes the protocol out the window. Stuck to it through lunch (diced chicken breast in a green salad with 0 cal dressing and a thin slice cantaloupe). Mid afternoon I was still feeling really lousy so fixed a regular cup of coffee with 2TBS butter. Dinner was a Costco/Trident salmon patty cooked in a scant tsp of coconut oil with a handful of Ranier cherries for dessert.

Sleep came late. Might it have been the coffee? And an hour long thunderboomie RAIN storm that had me up several times checking windows and covering up stuff in my studio where the roof has leaked recently. So another day with my butt draggin'.

Day 6 hcg protocol

weight ... 222.8

change ... 0.8

total ... 5.0

See that? I still dropped a little. 5 pounds in 3 days is awesome from where I sit. I'm actually surprised it dropped at all given how much has come off in the first 2 days of VLCD. Not to mention "cheating" as I did with all that FAT. So I"m pumped and I am having a mug of BPC with 2TBS buttah and 1TBS coconut oil for breakfast. Will continue the regular menu of 2 meals, no/low fat high protein with a kiss of carbs. And a cool/cold shower when I finish the coffee.

argh I just checked the weather widget. A "cool" 89 for today, 101 tomorrow and high to mid 90's the remainder of the week. My scandahoovian ancestors are whining.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-17T22:52:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-38832>

Ya know, Cavemam, even on 750 calories a day anyone will lose weight, hcg or not. I honestly don't believe that the list of approved foods makes one bit of difference as long as you keep your protein up, the carbs down and the total calorie count down. We'll see. I think the reason he banned fats from the diet is that's where all the calories are. But we all know that all calories are NOT equal. Right?

IF hcg stimulates leptin which then cause your hypothalamus to say ... oooo we;re full stop eating now! ... then keeping to 500 calories of protein keeps you from munching on your own organs and muscles -- and you. will. lose. weight.

Now I could just be full of it and it wouldn't be the first time. Being smart is no guarantee of having the right train of thought. I do know this. I have started a round of hcg, lasted 7 days, came off and just started eating and did not gain an ounce. So much for THAT hypothesis. Of course I wasn't eating lots of carbs or sugar or bread or pasta either. Had I been eating the "approved" SAD I probably would have PILED on the pounds. But I didn't.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-18T17:23:29+0300

weight: 222.6

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-20T22:48:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-39791>

If I had any lingering doubts that CT was effective in removing fat cells they have been blown away like dandelion fluff.

I have been taking cool/cold showers for several months now. I start with tepid water (which now feels too warm) so I can wash my hair without turning myself into a popsicle. Then I turn the water temp down and start washing. I continue to turn it down until it gets just this side of too chilly. When I've finished washing I stand with my back to the shower and just let the cold water run onto my back. It feels GREAT, btw. And given our balmy temps it's even better.

OK. I bought this handy dandy doodad for putting lotion on your back. I was putting the cream on my back and noticed it wasn't quite getting absorbed. So I got out my mirror to see where I had missed and there in the middle of my upper back was this rather large diamond shaped FLAT spot. WTH????

I looked again. Yup, definitely a flat spot in that FAT back of mine. What the heck is going on? Then I realized it is exactly where I let the cold water run on my back. Double checked it with hubby. I am not seeing things. Looked again. Holy cow. This WORKS! Time to get real and submerge the rest of my fat self in cold water.

Oh yeah, spot iced my fat belly yesterday for 30 minutes. aiming for 40 today and another 20 on my upper thighs which are now sporting LUMPS!!! What is going on with my body that these stupid lipomas are cropping up?! And they are relatively new (as in within the last couple of years).

I'm thinking tomorrow I'm just going to fill the tub with the temp set where I shower and just GET IN and get it over with.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-21T18:14:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-40008>

Day 9 HCG

Weight 221.6

Change: -1.2

total: -6.2

Yesterday I had hunger starting about 7 and lasting until I went to sleep. Shouldn't be happening. So I'm trying yet another experiment (which is what this round of hcg has always been about). I'm going to do the leptin reset while using the hcg and see how that goes. Although I'm going to try to keep the fat intake under strong control because, again, that's where all the calories are.

so I had pseudo-BAB this morning, i.e., I had about 6 ounces ground beef. Cooked in my nuwave cooker that works out to about 4 ounces cooked ... all the fat (sob) falls through the grill into the pan at the bottom. DARN. It's GREAT for cooking bacon and then you can save all that lovely bacon grease for other things, but I wander. I also had a mug of BPC made with 2 TBS butter (can you believe the restraint?) and 1 TBS CO.

One hour later I checked my BG which was 88. So there's proof that putting fat in with the coffee keeps your insulin from spiking. Not good enough? OK I can check it again in about 30 minutes if you like.

Now then, I started having HUNGER issues again so grabbed my new jar of Artisana coconut butter (which yesterday was LIQUID from this heat) and gobbled a few spoonfuls of the now hardened stuff. And now I'm feeling hungry still AND a bit light headed. So much for keeping the fat intake under control.

What I'm learning from this little personal experiment is that I have been overeating while on the reset. I was using the reset as a license to eat a LOT without regard for true hunger. Granted I have not gained any weight but that wasn't the point. I mean, I've been on the reset for MONTHS now. Something is a bit hinky and I don't think it's necessarily my metabolism (although we'll see once the test results come back on my hormones. I still think my testosterone is flat lined and THAT is part of what is keeping the weight on).

But I really need to step back and assess where my hunger is before I start eating. I have a difficult time with noticing and interpreting hunger signals. I'll find myself cruising the kitchen or pantry and not really FEEL hungry but in fact, I am. I sometimes confuse thirst with hunger. I guess all those years of overriding my body's hunger signals has left me unable to read them at all any more. Which is why doing the hcg has been good for me. Limiting my caloric intake has forced me to notice true hunger.

Well anyway, that's my story and I'm stickin' to it. We'll see how the rest of today goes. So far it isn't looking promising. Maybe I should have had that bacon after all.

### chocolate — 2012-07-21T18:26:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-40010>

I'm glad to know that coffee doesn't do that with fat. I was seeing that its a great source of boron. Its one of the simplest pleasure in life. Thanks for setting me straight and sharing. I guess I was using butter instead of ghee and I'm going to blame it on my genes that popped up out centuries past and conflicting information.

Just curious, though.... If you ate only fat, wouldn't the body scavenge for bad proteins first and eat the carbohydrate shells? That's kind of my goal when we get stable around my house. I think we may have the same issues, only mine caught up on the early end because of no fat storing capacity. I'm stuck ready to bury myself in the mud and then come out and lay eggs whilst pooping mucous, the frog. Maybe you're the penguin.

### Lanie — 2012-07-21T18:37:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-40012>

That's really cool about the cold showers! Wow.

Re: insulin spiking after drinking coffee... if your blood sugar is 88 that doesn't mean your insulin didn't spike. It means your blood sugar didn't spike. Insulin actually brings blood sugar DOWN...so it may indeed be dropping your blood sugar and contributing to your hunger.

I'm enjoying the experimentation. Good for you for putting these different things to the test to see what works for you.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-21T19:33:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-40018>

Hmm. Good point Lanie, as usual. Some days my head just doesn't get put on straight. But wouldn't there have been a rise in BG first? It had barely been an hour since I had finished breakfast and the coffee.

Well, hmmm.

chocolate, I'm not going to do a fat fast, LOL. That was Dr. Atkins method of last resort if his two week induction didn't work. and he did not recommend it lightly. He had many caveats against it, AAMOF. There isn't much food on his fat fast ... you'd be surprised how little there is in 1,000 calories of fat.

I'm trying to find a balance. The leptin reset is a great starting point but I must be doing something wrong (i.e. eating too much) or my hormones are blocking the road (zero testosterone for one thing -- my number one suspect). Or a little of both.

Oh yeah, I looked at my back in the mirror again this morning and there is that flattened kite shape surrounded by fluffy me just as plain as day. It is just WEIRD.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-21T19:51:00+0300

> **DarleenMB;40195 wrote:** Day 9 HCG
>
> Weight 221.6
>
> Change: -1.2
>
> total: -6.2
>
>
>
> Yesterday I had hunger starting about 7 and lasting until I went to sleep. Shouldn't be happening. So I'm trying yet another experiment (which is what this round of hcg has always been about). I'm going to do the leptin reset while using the hcg and see how that goes. Although I'm going to try to keep the fat intake under strong control because, again, that's where all the calories are.
>
>
>
> so I had pseudo-BAB this morning, i.e., I had about 6 ounces ground beef. Cooked in my nuwave cooker that works out to about 4 ounces cooked ... all the fat (sob) falls through the grill into the pan at the bottom. DARN. It's GREAT for cooking bacon and then you can save all that lovely bacon grease for other things, but I wander. I also had a mug of BPC made with 2 TBS butter (can you believe the restraint?) and 1 TBS CO.
>
>
>
> One hour later I checked my BG which was 88. So there's proof that putting fat in with the coffee keeps your insulin from spiking. Not good enough? OK I can check it again in about 30 minutes if you like.
>
>
>
> Now then, I started having HUNGER issues again so grabbed my new jar of Artisana coconut butter (which yesterday was LIQUID from this heat) and gobbled a few spoonfuls of the now hardened stuff. And now I'm feeling hungry still AND a bit light headed. So much for keeping the fat intake under control.
>
>
>
> What I'm learning from this little personal experiment is that I have been overeating while on the reset. I was using the reset as a license to eat a LOT without regard for true hunger. Granted I have not gained any weight but that wasn't the point. I mean, I've been on the reset for MONTHS now. Something is a bit hinky and I don't think it's necessarily my metabolism (although we'll see once the test results come back on my hormones. I still think my testosterone is flat lined and THAT is part of what is keeping the weight on).
>
>
>
> But I really need to step back and assess where my hunger is before I start eating. I have a difficult time with noticing and interpreting hunger signals. I'll find myself cruising the kitchen or pantry and not really FEEL hungry but in fact, I am. I sometimes confuse thirst with hunger. I guess all those years of overriding my body's hunger signals has left me unable to read them at all any more. Which is why doing the hcg has been good for me. Limiting my caloric intake has forced me to notice true hunger.
>
>
>
> Well anyway, that's my story and I'm stickin' to it. We'll see how the rest of today goes. So far it isn't looking promising. Maybe I should have had that bacon after all.

LOVE LOVE LOVE experiments, may I design some for you since I'm too chicken? hah!

### Lanie — 2012-07-21T20:59:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-40047>

> **DarleenMB;40205 wrote:** Hmm. Good point Lanie, as usual. Some days my head just doesn't get put on straight. But wouldn't there have been a rise in BG first? It had barely been an hour since I had finished breakfast and the coffee.

There can be a rise in insulin without a rise in BG. For example, I tested my blood sugar before and after eating an almond-flour cookie that was artificially sweetened. BG dropped 30 points in one hour. And of course, I was then hungry too.

I see two possible expeiments for you that might be telling. First, try no coffee or tea for a few days...see how your hunger feels then. If caffeine withdrawal is a problem take half a no-doz. Same amount of caffeine as a cup of coffee. Second experiment possibility: only eat to hunger for a few days, and only eat enough to feel satisfied but not full. I've envied your lack of appetite - lets put it to good use!

### Lanie — 2012-07-21T21:09:14+0300

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-22T20:35:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-40253>

So what would happen if you combined hcg with the leptin reset? Ever wonder? Me too. I know that the CW says eating "normally" can cause massive weight gains either coming straight off the protocol or while on the hcg but I do not believe that. "normally" as defined by the "protocol" being the current SAD then yes, you would gain. But not because of the hcg but because the SAD causes you to gain. Or, as defined in the 50's/60's going back to eating starchy carbs. Which I no longer eat anyway.

I have, in the past, stopped in the middle of a round and gone right back to eating "normally" and not gained an ounce.

But what happens if you eat a seasonal ketogenic paleo diet (say that one 3 times fast)?

Let's find out.

Oh yeah, and I was back up 1.2 today. Gotta love fluid retention. I snacked. Shoot me. I'm trying to normalize today to 2 or 3 meals, plenty of protein, low carb (hard to do as it's summer and those cherries have my name on 'em AND the strawberries AND that honkin' huge pomegranate), MODEST (are you listening to yourself, Darleen? MODEST) good fats.

The ground beef I took out of the freezer yesterday was still a brick this morning so I wound up having a 3 scoop (60g) protein shake made with ice, water, cream and a coupla TBS of Ghiradelli cocoa. And a mug of BPC with (I am so proud of myself) only TWO, count 'em, TWO TBS butter and 1TBS CO.

Shoulda had the beef or something else. Man that shake blew through me so now I'm spooning out Artiana coconut butter. I really liked when it was liquid the other day. I may set it out on the porch this afternoon to get it nice and soupy. Now it's firmed up. Still good but I liked it soupy better.

OKOK it's technically a snack. But I needed to shut my stomach up.

Lunch: (cause I know I'm gonna want/need it) small green salad with small can of water=packed tuna (cause that's the only kind I every buy)

Dinner: Scallops and shrimp. ???

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-25T18:55:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-41021>

Well I've wandered way off the "path" ... I got tired of sticking myself. The stuff does NOTHING as far as I'm concerned. I really truly believe it is all mental. My weight has stayed put no matter what I eat. So much for the fear of gaining massive amounts of weight if you stray from the VLCD. My hunger levels are exactly the same as when I wasn't using the hcg. So what's the point. What a waste of money.

I'm really pissed I missed yesterday's webinar. I got the email yesterday afternoon and went to sign up only to find that my internet was out. CenturyLink/Qwest/USWest/MaBell or whateverthehellthey'recallingthemselvestoday had yet another "outage." It was STILL out at 9:30 when I went to bed. stupid phone company.

Been yet another boring week.

Saw the PA yesterday and yeah, my testosterone is flatlined. So she's ordering up some cream from my friendly local compounding pharmacist to add to my BHRT. Maybe that will give me some energy which I am sorely lacking. Never mind my flatlined sex drive. It drove off a cliff when they yanked my parts. OKOK TMI but too bad. I'm in a MOOD.

Showed her AND my massage therapist the spot on my back which they both plainly saw. So I am not imagining things. I tried to take a picture using my iPad but it didn't show very well. My flab sure did. pfbbt.

My new resolution is to spot ice my big fat belly twice a day. I'll do the bath thing after I shower when I'm already adjusted somewhat to the cool/cold water. After I'm clean my plan is to cork the tub and wait till it's full enough to lie back in then switch off the shower, lie down and let the tub finish filling up. Next stop will be to have a bucket of ice ready and have hubby dump it in the tub with me but that's a bit down the road. First things first.

Man I wish this weight would go somewhere. Like DOWN.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-27T04:19:52+0300

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-28T18:39:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-41673>

this mornings discussion falls under the heading of TMI or EWWWWWWWW.

Everybody here talks about input (diet) constantly but I never see anything about output unless it's about an unexpected case of "the runs."

Well I have noticed a definite change in my bm's of late. Where the output used to be light to medium tan it is now deep, dark brown almost black. does this herald a change in the machinery? I suspect the answer is "yes" but I'll be durned if know what it means.

Fasten your seat belts so you don't get whiplash as I'm changing the topic now to something COMPLETELY off subject.

Anybody else watching Longmire on A&E? Of COURSE we're watching it ... the setting happens to be smack dab where we live in Northern Wyoming. I really REALLY like the character so I finally decided maybe I oughtta read one of them thar books the character is based on seeing as how Craig Johnson lives over in Clearmont (pronounced CLAREmont for the uninitiated) which is just a hop, skip and half a jump away from Sheridan.

So I mosied on over to Amazon and had a WTF moment when I saw the price of the Kindle edition. $12.99!!!??? for a 10 year old book? The friggin' paperback is TWO DOLLARS CHEAPER. I made a side commentary about the publisher's mothers and wandered over to the local library figuring I'd just read it for free. Well hell's bells it is naturally CHECKED OUT because the whole flappin' town is watching the series and I'm not the only one who hasn't read the books. poop (and that is not intended to signal a change back to my original topic).

I grumbled a bit more. Chewed on the price a bit more and finally decided WTH I could damn well afford to put a couple extra bucks in Johnson's pocket and if the book was as good as all my friends said it'd be worth it.

Ladies and gents I am here to tell you to RUN, do not walk, to Amazon and BUY [THE COLD DISH](http://http://amzn.to/N3zWeH).

Not only will you get a GREAT character in Walt Longmire, you get snappy patter (which I adore), a head-scratching who-done-it, giggles and grins along the way and a mashup of the state in which I live. OK OK that last part needs a bit of 'splainin. Craig took a mixmaster to our fair state. Which leaves me scratching my head in consternation about every third page. An example is his blithely moving Lame Deer from the Northern part of MONTANA down to Wyoming. At moments like that I want to revoke his poetic license but it's all good in the end.

Regarding the tv series my only complaints are (1) it's filmed in freakin' New Mexico (which I tipped to the instant they showed a motel with a Thunderbird on the the stepped wall of the exterior. Wyoming is way to proud of it's "Bucky" logo to use anything else.) and (2) the cast does not know how to pronounce ABSAROKA. It's pronounced abSORka just in case you wondered. Now you can sound like a native too! I"m waiting for them to mispronounce "Arvada." It is properly pronounced arVAYda here in the wild and wooly state of nowhere. Oh yeah, forgot there was a (3) the writers need to come take a guided tour of the area thus staving off any more horse laugh type mistakes like the one in the last episode whereupon our intrepid deputy pronounced that "room service at the hotel in Ranchester reported ..." BWAHAHAHAHAHAHA. ROOM FRACKING SERVICE? IN **RANCHESTER**??? We have ONE ... count 'em ONE motel in Sheridan which offers room service. But RANCHESTER? hahahahahaha

Ranchester is about 20 miles north of Sheridan and is on one of the prettiest drives (if not the most frightening) into Yellowstone which is "over the mountain" from us. Population is somewhere around 800 give or take (not counting cows and/or deer) and while it IS a tad bigger than Dayton (further up the same road) I don't believe that the MOTEL there offers anything approaching "room service."

whew. I think I hurt myself on that one.

OK. Can you tell I am bored with my non-movement on the leptin rx? It's been MONTHS. I'm still tired all the time. Although I am sleeping a lot better and people keep asking me if I've lost weight. No.

I finally got my testosterone cream and started it yesterday. In about 2 weeks I have decided to spring for the cortisol test to check that. Other than that I don't know where else to turn.

And that is that for today. Nothing else new to report. Hope you all enjoyed the book/tv report. I'm gonna go finish my book.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-28T18:50:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-41676>

Boy howdy don't you just love how surgeons love to yank your parts telling you that you can live just fine without 'em? Really, doc? Then why did I have them to begin with?

bother.

### vlynnb — 2012-07-28T19:37:14+0300

This is my first time reading your posts. Shoot, I should have been working on something this Sat. morning but I couldn't stop reading. Skipped a lot of the middle because grand kids need breakfast but the last few on Wyoming kept me chuckling. Husband grew up in Lovell/Powell area, live in S. East Idaho now. ### DarleenMB — 2012-07-28T19:49:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-41694>

Vlynnb welcome! Always good to have someone else in my own little world who "gets" me!

I tend to avoid town altogether during rodeo weekend. A) it's just plain nutz with all the flatland touristers wandering the streets and b) it's usually hotter than the proverbial firecracker. Which, this year, it WAS. Sorry I missed the "carry your wife" race. Must have been a hoot given the size of most folks around here.

You know I "did" the protocol about 3 years ago and it took me nearly 9 months to drop 20 pounds. You'd think I'd have learned from that. But I am impatient. although I whine about not "getting" why I'm not losing, a part of me understands that the underlying mechanisms in me are so broken that it's just either going to take a lot more time or it may never happen at all unless I just keep my caloric intake low enough to force the weight loss. Since I am no longer a fan of starving myself thin I think I just need to learn a little patience and tough it out.

And I think you will LOVE The Cold Dish. Can't speak to any of the others because I haven't read them yet although my good friend and Sheridan native, Ariel, assures me I am now hopelessly hooked and might as well just lean back and enjoy the ride. Oh and if you get A&E they're rerunning a bunch of episodes this weekend including the pilot. Check your listings. Robert Taylor (not of old Hollywood but new Australia) is FAB in the role. He's rumply, like an unmade bed, has the Wyoming accent down (except for his continued mispronunciation of "Absaroka" which is an easy tenderfoot mistake and one I made many years ago much to the amusement and consternation of my radio audience) and is just plain "cowboy sexy." and I am NOT, in the parlance of the book, anywhere near being a "buckle bunny." But his easy manner, his entire demeanor, just says honest, hard working MAN. Enjoy!

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-29T19:24:58+0300

Still waiting to feel the effects of the testosterone. OK it's been 2 days. I told you I was impatient. A little more energy, is that too much to ask?!

Other than that, nothing new to report. Except I ate a bunch of fruit last night for dinner. I mixed fresh blueberries and the last few strawberries with 4% Daisy cottage cheese and served it up on a slice of cantaloupe with a side wedge of watermelon. mmmmmm mmmmm mmmmm.
