### DarleenMB — 2012-07-30T17:57:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-41945>

ungh .... I don't think my heart has started yet this morning. woke early and put on the sleep mask since we have to leave one of the curtains open for fresh air at night. Slept until a little past 8 and am feeling like a zombie.

Nothing new going on. Breakfast was my usual sortaBAB. GF ground beef patty and bacon. I don't think I'm likely to change anytime soon because I really like it. Fish for the other 2 meals is acceptable but I just cannot get down with it first thing in the A.M.

Lunch was microwaved pepperoni (like crispy little meat crackers) with a touch of humus (shhhh don't tell Jack!) and a double handful of cherries. Gotta be the last bag I buy 'cause I am such a little pig! Oh yeah and a tiny wedge of watermelon. Hubby has eaten most of it ... and it was a good sized one, not those tiny "just enough for 2" jobbies.

dinner rolled around and I wasn't particularly hungry (so I thought ... gotta watch that "thinkin'" stuff) but I made a small tuna salad and had most of it on lettuce and half an avocado. Hubby got the rest plus the leftover shrimp salad from 2 days ago. Way to force him to eat leftovers, Darleen!!  And more watermelon. 2 tiny wedges this time.

So that's it. Dentist this morning for teeth cleaning so need to be sure and get all the bacon out beforehand. And now I'm on the 2nd Walt Longmire book ... [Death Without Company.](http://http://amzn.to/MWIplh) Boy I sure do like that guy! Best character since Lucas Davenport. Gotta say though it's cuttin' in to my other reading. I'm almost finished with [The Dark Volume](http://http://amzn.to/MOtdKH) (you want STRANGE? Read the first book [The Glass Books of the Dream Eaters](http://http://amzn.to/OtO9GG)), just started James Rollins [Blood Line](http://http://amzn.to/T0SQHq) (and I swear everytime I start reading one of his books it will be the last, yet here I am again. argh) and have 3 books I picked up at the library the other day AND 2 bookcases FULL of stuff I haven't read yet.

You know what I want? I want to be able to press a book against my forehead and have the contents INSTANTLY transferred into my brain. Is that too much to ask???

### vlynnb — 2012-07-30T22:58:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-41966>

"So many books, so little time!" Do you really want it all transferred instantly? I think I wouldn't mind picking up pure knowledge or fact that way but I would miss the actually slow reading of a good book, the kind I really don't want to end. I haven't even convinced myself that I would love an e-reader. Something about having a library full of books and feeling it in my hand as I read.

I am wondering how you get along with all that fruit. Are you already Leptin Sensitive? I am craving some fruit right now myself.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-31T06:02:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-42019>

vlynnb, YES! I love my kindle (which DH is currently using) AND my iPad because I can have a library full of books in something the size of a double postage stamp! I still have physical books and I still buy them but I LOVE ebooks. They save space, trees AND with my iPad I can look up words just by touching them so when I'm reading something and run across a word I don't know ... bingo! There it is!

ETA that plus all that my elibrary is ALPHABETIZED by author or title which means I can find that book that I know I had ... physical books require a major expedition. Not so with ebooks.

I'm not eating THAT much fruit. when I say a small wedge of watermelon I mean just that. I quartered the melon, then cut each quarter into slices about 1" thick. Don't know if leptin sensitivity has returned or not but I'm not passing up cherries OR watermelon or cantaloupe as they are in season for just a short time. I do know now, however, not to gorge myself. Not always easy.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-02T21:22:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-42546>

had out of town company last night and took them out to dine but it was way late. 9 or so before we actually ate. grrr.

Dinner was lovely though. Had mussels for appetizer (YUM YUM) and I had halibut with a mango/strawberry confit. The fish was beautifully cooked though a bit salty and I wasn't too crazy about the confit. Also had a cup of seafood chowder made with crab, lobster, cream and butter. mmmm mmmm mmmmmmmmmmmmmmm

Felt like the Hindenburg all day yesterday. Every time I caught a glimpse of my butt in a mirror I thought I was looking at the back end of a buick. Dont' know what that was about because all my clothes still fit as usual. Guess I'd better jump on the scale for a quick weigh-in tomorrow just to double check.

Missed taking my bile acid factors for 2 meals and didn't digest ANY of the fats I ate at those meals. BOTHER.

Am still on my "Longmire" reading kick. Am now on book 4 and am going to be majorly bummed when I finish the last one in the series so far. I think that's about 4 books away. Best darned books I've read (devoured) in ages. Can't believe I'm reading them this fast but cannot put them down.

We're still wilting from the heat but we are supposed to get a reprieve tomorrow. Weather widget says high of 76. I'll believe it when I see it.

### vlynnb — 2012-08-03T20:14:55+0300

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-04T18:44:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-42853>

Well I had a real pity party yesterday. I fixed popcorn and ate a HUGE bowlful. OK so it was Troyer purple popcorn not the nasty stuff from the local bigmart. AND I cooked it in lots of CO. But still. Had cottage cheese and blueberries & strawberries for dinner with a sprinkle of sliced almonds on top.

Oh yeah, I weighed yesterday. Still the same just FEELING fatter than ever.

Slept really well since it turned coolish yesterday and last night got down right cold. Had to put the winter blankie on the bed.

My resolution today is to get back on the reset track and EAT what and when I need to. My plan from a week or so ago fell apart the minute I thought about it. I still think if I have a SMALL green salad for lunch with a BIT of protein and then a bit of protein for dinner with a veggie I will do better than I have been. Plus I keep 'forgetting' to do the spot icing.

This summer has just sucked. I'm stupid from the heat, LAZY from the heat. I just can't make myself do anything. Except either read the Walt Longmire series or play on the computer. My house is pit. My brain left the building last June. I'm turning into Jabba the Hut.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-06T19:50:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-43244>

Got ambitious yesterday and made a quiche with ham, eggs and raw milk cheddar. Had two slices for breakfast with 3 pieces of bacon and BPC. Wasn't sure I could finish it but managed to get it all down.

Did a whey protein shake for lunch and will do it again. Doesn't make me feel too full and I'm thinking now that I am still not eating ENOUGH. Had pork chops and asparagus for dinner. If I get my bills done and my shopping I plan on doing a spicy shrimp recipe I tore out of a magazine. It's full on paleo and looks/sounds yummy. Will probably do caulimash to go with. Or better yet a nice crisp green salad.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-07T19:20:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-43449>

Lunch AND dinner yesterday was a protein shake. Lunch was a SF chai "latte" which was AWESOME. Chopped a cup of ice in the blender, added in one cup SF chai tea, 4 TBS Thai Kitchen coconut milk, 2 scoops vanilla cream whey protein from Bioplex. Just yummy!!

Went to see Batman Rises ... gotta say I wasn't all that keen on seeing Anne Hathaway as Cat Woman but she did an AMAZING job. Movie kinda drug in spots but when it reached the finale it was well worth the wait. And yes, I had popcorn ... the small bag (which in our neck o' the woods still means a 4 cup serving (almost)). We always go to the 4PM showing but Batman was quite long ... got out at 7:10 so I gulped another protein shake for dinner since I was already past the witching hour.

Off to the store today so looks like I'll be making that spicy shrimp for dinner.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-08T20:47:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-43709>

Weighed in today. No change. As I've said before this is somewhat of a miracle. I cannot believe I'm not gaining given all I'm eating.

Breakfast was a slice of quiche, a GF ground beef patty, 3 sausage links and bpc with butter and CO.

Lunch will be a 2 scoop protein shake.

Dinner? Not sure yet. It's gonna be 99 again today and yesterday I spent what little energy I had going to the store, dragging in all the groceries and putting them away. I HATE the HEAT!!!

Speaking of which, I have discovered in the last couple of weeks that when I first get up in the A.M. I need to splash cold water on my face. And our tap water is still 60Â° ... love that snow melt. It just feels so goooooood. I guess because I'm HOT when I first wake up. This isn't like night sweats or hot flashes. I'm just HOT. Not that kind of hot either. Just overly warm. And that cold water is so soothing. I'd face dunk but don't have the patience to wait for the sink to fill. It's a deep sink.

Well, that's it. stupid from the heat. tired from the heat. Have sheets to hang out and ironing to do. Actually it's "pressing" but it's still hot. If I don't get to it now it won't get done for another day.

and then I'd have to sit around reading the last and latest Walt Longmire novel. It's already off to a great start!

### ealachan — 2012-08-08T20:55:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-43714>

I feel your pain, Darleen! I can't stand waking up to it already being hot. Ugh! It just sets me up to be a miserable bitch all day long. More miserable than usual, anyway...

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-09T05:23:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-43808>

Yeah but it did do one thing ... I just crawled out of my 2nd attempt at CT. Lasted about 10 minutes. Water was 61 when I went in and 64 when I came out. I just sat for a bit getting acclimated and then held my breath and lay back. WHOO! I'd been planning on doing this all day and after I brought hubby home from the weekly Legion "meeting" I just said to heck with it and ran the tub full. Started reading the last Robert Crais novel so that helped. His work is always engrossing. Three (my feral adoptee) did NOT like the sound of the water going into the tub and he REALLY didn't like it when I climbed in, LOL. I heard about it the entire time I was in there.

Well, the computer just informed me that it's sunset so I have to put on my blu-blockers. Means it's time to shut the 'puter down because I can't do much of anything at this point. boo.

Oh yeah, had a 2 scoop protein shake for lunch AND for dinner. Hubby was at the legion and it was just too damned hot to even think about food.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-09T20:37:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-43930>

Out of quiche and need to go get some good sausage from Sackett's so I can make another. Had 3 XL eggs scrambled in coconut oil, hunk o'ground beef, 2 slices bacon and 2 link sausage and a mug of BPC with CO for breakfast. I can't believe I ate all that.

Odd thing about the protein shakes. about 4-5 hours after i have one I get HUNGRY. I mean actual hunger pangs. If I skip lunch, or eat "food" instead of the shake, I don't get 'em. weird.

It was overcast this morning so didn't wake up until 8:45! HOLY MOLY. OTOH I did wake up a couple of times in the middle of the night. Last one was about 2:30 and I was HOTHOTHOTHOT. Wondering if it's the testosterone. NO NOT THAT KIND OF HOT. although I sure wouldn't complain too much about that. Temperature HOT. And again this morning splashed my face with COLD water. OOOOH it felt so good. I came real close to filling the tub with cold water and climbing in. Sure would have woke me up. Taking a cold shower in a bit instead. I am running WAY late today. Also, computer is acting up and it's something I can't figure out. grrrrrrr. That doesn't happen often but it pisses me off when it does. Evidently something (I'm thinking OpenOffice) has squirreled the works. Can't locate the missing piece. DAMN

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-12T19:34:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-44554>

YAY!!! we got a break from the heat! I mean a REAL break. Like 70 instead of 99. What a relief. It's going to climb a little the next few days but Wed forecast is for 67. WHAOO!

Have been late getting my blu-blockers on the last several nights and have paid for it by not being able to fall asleep as easily. gotta watch that.

Nothing new on the reset front. Still eating the BAB. 2 scoop protein shake for 2nd meal and am honestly not hungry past that. Forgot all about fixing dinner because of it and at 8 hubby was banging around in the kitchen looking for food. Had to go rescue him (which I'm sure was his plan all along). Me? Naw. Not hungry. I mean NOT HUNGRY. Don't even want to think about food. Maybe I have finally topped off THAT tank.

Jumped in the shower early yesterday morning instead of waiting until mid-late morning as usual. OH MY that felt good. Cranked the water all the way over to COLD (which is still 60Â° -- love it!) and just let it pour over me. Barely noticed it was cold. So WHAT is it with early morning overheat? Again, it doesn't feel like hot flashes (which I never really had, just bouts of power surges where I was uncomfortably warm) ... I don't even really notice I'm hot until I splash cold water on my face and then it just feels RIGHT. How did I go all those years taking hot showers? I can't even stand tepid water any more. I'm sure my scandahoovian ancestors are laughing.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-15T20:33:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-45050>

I sure wish I could come here and report awesome and life changing news but it's still just the same old same old. With a couple of interesting additions.

1\. My skin has suddenly become incredibly soft. At first I thought it was the body cream I've been using, but nope. It's just wonderfully silky.

2\. My upper chest has lost a LOT of fat. My breasts used to extend up toward my neck in a sort of "foothills" if you will. That's almost all gone now. Very strange. I used to be able to grab a big handful of flab but not anymore. too bad I can't say the same for my belly.

3\. I don't seem to need or want much food anymore. Doesn't mean I don't occasionally eat just because, but to have that constant need gone is pretty amazing.

and 4. Boy howdy am I sleeping good. Except when my allergies flare which they've done AGAIN because of all the grass fires in the region. I cannot wait for it to snow and END this.

So I continue to slog forward telling myself to stop beating up on myself because of my weight. NOT my fault. Hormones. HORMONES. Try to keep that in the forefront. Menopause hit me way WAY early and the weight gain, lack of sleep, fibroid tumors that bled me dry for YEARS was a direct result of that. The stress from my childhood and adolescence coupled with own stupidity in my early 20's started all of this. Now I know better.

I plan to have my cortisol levels checked once my allergies are under control. I know they're stressing me because my blood pressure yesterday 110 over 65. That was up from the 100 over 60 it was the last 2 times. Yeah yeah. I know that's low for most peeps my age. But both my sister and I (unlike other family members) have LOWER blood pressure even though we're both overweight. OK both obese. There. whatever.

I did get my allergy drops yesterday. FINALLY. I've been waiting for weeks and weeks. I suspect they were at the office all this time and no one bothered to call me. WhatEVER. I'm also going to add in a daily teaspoon of local unpasteurized honey. I cannot take this anymore. It has been AWFUL. My mouth itched yesterday to the point I thought I'd scream! I washed my sinuses, took my OTC allergy meds and STILL was going nuts. I'm tired and yesterday I hated the whole WORLD. Which tells me I wasn't sleeping well the last few nights and am dead tired again.

blech

Just when my energy levels were showing signs of finally going up a bit. crap

So.

BAB was 5.5oz ground beef (measured raw), 4 slices bacon (the paper thin kind) and a mug of BPC with plenty of buttah and a heaping TBS of CO. Am now having another cup of decaf with cream. We'll call it lunch.

Speaking of which ... probably a protein shake about mid afternoon.

And that may be it unless I get super hungry. All that fresh fruit keeps calling me ... I have 5 Colorado peaches (OMG THE BEST!!) that are fully ripened and in the fridge along with another bag of cherries (I KNOW I KNOW) plus some watermelon and cantaloupe. OOOOO the temptation. Maybe I'll throw a peach in the protein shake. mmmm.

DH is off on a 3 day road trip with a friend. They're headed up around Salmon ID in said friends convertible Mercedes (vintage which he got way cheap). Hope it doesn't break down. So I don't have to worry about fixing his meals for a few days.

It's cool today (again) but the smoke from the fires is keeping me pretty much housebound. At least I can keep the windows closed without expiring from the heat. It's supposed to be 45 tonight YAY!!

### vlynnb — 2012-08-16T00:11:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-45083>

Sorry you are not feeling well. I know how that fruit can call to you. Maybe another good book will take your mind off of it.

My allergies have been so good after going off of wheat, sugar, milk etc. that I only take an over the counter anti-histamine if I am going in for an allergy shot. The new diet is doing what allergy shots never have accomplished. I know because if I cheat and eat wheat etc. I am stuffed up or itching the next day. Hope the epi-paleo starts giving you this kind of relief soon.

Fires here too, close to Salmon, by Idaho standards anyway. We are getting a lot of smoke from the Salmon fires and a lot of others. Hope your hubby finds a canyon that is not affected. (effected? I never can get those two right!) Which reminds me, I think you write very well and descriptively. Maybe you should write a book.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-16T17:37:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-45203>

Thanks vlynnb. My allergies are all grasses, trees, molds and mildew. I'm really allergic to timothy grass and since we live in the grasslands ... oh yeah and russian olives. Of course we have a whole LINE of the damned things up by the road so when the wind blows it scatters all that lovely pollen from those trees right straight at our house. pfbbbt.

Once upon a time I thought I wanted to write. Until I figured out I'd have to reveal too much of myself in my work. Oh well. I keep thinking I should write about my life because I really believe what I went through and survived might help someone but, again, I'm not real good at letting too much of my real self show. Which is probably why I love the internet so much. I can let fly and not worry too much. Love that anonymity.

REALLY off day yesterday. Just felt plain crappy all day. Wasn't all that hungry until way late. Had that peach protein shake for "lunch" and was good until about 8 or so. I sorta wanted something but nothing sounded good which probably means I should have left well enough alone but NOOOOO. I had a small bag of dried fruit and nuts and did a face plant in it. Didn't QUiTE eat it all but came close. Good thing it wasn't one of those horse sized jobbies.

Had a real sneezing fit a bit ago and now my mouth is feeling itchy. Not too bad. This awful summer just has seemed endless first with the non-stop heat and smoke and now more smoke. Maybe we should move to Antarctica. I hear they're always hiring down there.

vkiernan, if you can try to screen them over the phone before you invest your time and money. I had 3 doctors and the endo was the worst - the most dogmatic doctor, I've ever met (TSH needs to be over 10 to be of a concern; treats with T4 only; doesn't test 4 irons; my elevated FBG, thyroid, and low sex hormones are not connected - you get the drift...). So ask them what they treat, how they treat, etc. Good luck!

Destiny, Have you tried the Wiley P? Just trying to garner as much info as I can before making the final decision. ### DarleenMB — 2012-08-17T22:36:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-45420>

I just picked one of the providers in Loveland. He seemed nice enough and was quite ready to give me what I needed. Since I have no parts and was 64 when I saw him there were no tests done at that time. However, AFTER I started on the BHRT then he did test to see where my levels were. Honestly, I wouldn't choose based on anything OTHER than what you're seeing them for which is to be put on the Wiley Protocol. One thing at a time.

Back to me.

Breakfast yesterday was 5 oz (weighed raw) ground beef, 4 slices bacon and a mug of BPC with butter and CO.

Lunch ... a bit of fruit

Dinner ... 2 nice slices of cold roast beef with cream cheese and avocado slices and the last of the cherries (sob).

TODAY: forgot to thaw any more ground beef so had 4 eggs and 4 slices bacon and a mug of BPC with butter and CO.

Lunch ... 2 slices of cold roast beef (I'm easy) w/cream cheese & avocado slices. And a peach. mmmmm. Only 2 left. sigh.

Dinner ... shrug. dunno. Probably should have fish.

Speaking of fish I had a tin of smoked baby clams for lunch the other day. It's OK as an appetizer but the smoke thing gets to be too much after a bit. Won't do that again. Am pining for mussels.

Waking up at 5:30 the last few mornings and am real TIRED OF IT NOW! It means that by 7:30 or so I'm starting to nod off and by 9 my body is FORCING me into sleep. I mean I can barely move/breathe/think. I crawl into bed and WHAM it's lights out. I did wake up a couple of times during the night ... just enough that my bladder decided it wanted attention. geez. NO problem dropping off again though. I'm back to sleeping to hard that I could probably sleep through a bomb run.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-18T18:55:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-45555>

My personal opinion is that your hormones are not something you should just blithely mess around with. This is something that absolutely needs medical oversight. Makes me think of that adage ... which I am going to twist a bit ... about those who medicate themselves having a fool for a physician. Plus the only way to get the real deal is to ... wait for it ... see a physician. When it comes to your sex hormones, taking supplements cannot restore what time has taken away.

And that's my opinion and I'm stickin' to it.

breakfast: the usual

and I have been bloating up like the Hindenburg every morning when I drink my BPC. I know it's that TBS of coconut oil but I'll be durned if I can figure out why. I've been using CO for several years now and thought I was well past that bit of nastiness.

today I am going to MAKE myself fix a small green salad for lunch. I seem to do better all around if I eat a few raw veggies daily. I hate to admit to being lazy but I can't think of any good reason why I refuse to do this for myself. I LIKE salads. I know that my appetite is way down for some reason (OK so I think I'm constipated again and I blame those whey protein shakes I was downing last week. I manage to forget every single time that they do that to me. argh) but salads "move" me so I'm going to FORCE it today. I'll add a small amount of canned fish and call it good.

dinner? sigh. who knows. Last night I finally felt a little hungry about 6:45 so had some Daisy 4% cottage cheese and 1/4 of a cantaloupe. Then later ate a humongous handful of raw almonds. BAD DARLEEN!!!! Where's that rolled up newspaper when I need it?!

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-19T18:02:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-45701>

I decided yesterday to start tracking everything I eat, my weight and my fasting blood glucose.

Day 1:

weight 227.2

FBG: 95

Breakfast, 8:30am

6.5 oz raw/4.5 oz cooked ground beef

mug of BPC with 1.5 oz butter and 1 TBS CO

4 slices bacon

total: 1012 calories!!!, 44g protein, 0g carbs, 75g fat

judas aitch.

Yesterday I didn't eat again until about 3:30 and by then was famished. Had a small green salad with a handful of cherry tomatoes, avocado, yellow pepper and small can of water packed tuna. Dressed with 0 calorie dressing because I was out of my homemade, and about 4 big green olives and a pepper on the side.

Late (like around 8) was hungry so had a handful of raw almonds.

Took half a bottle of mag citrate yesterday afternoon. Cleared the pipes and am feeling somewhat better.

Am starting to think about ditching the bacon and the ground beef and eating white fish for breakfast. Something has got to give on my weight. This just has got to stop. I am not prepared to just give up and accept that I am FAT/obese. I HATE the way I feel and the way I look.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-20T00:07:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-45750>

TSH is "normal" and doc wouldn't do a reverse T3. Yes, sex hormones checked 2 weeks ago and within normal parameters. Don't know how that matches up with the wiley protocol but the PA was happy. And that's why she put me on testosterone ... it's flatlined which I already knew.

I had my cortisol checked a couple of years ago and it was ok. Been thinking about doing it again.

And yeah, most people would lose weight eating what I'm eating. Except for 30 years worth of ED ... NOT eating, then eating crap. We didn't have kids so when DH was off working (on the railroad and gone more than he was home) I simply didn't eat.

I have no proof but the evidence suggests I went into menopause in my late 20's -- early 30's. THAT'S when my periods became insane. I suspect I lost all my eggs at once because you do NOT develop a kilo's worth of fibroid tumors in just a couple of years. No eggs, no progesterone. No progesterone ... estrogen dominance anyone?

It's beyond frustrating. Which is why I intend to carefully monitor what I'm eating as well as watching my hunger levels. I still don't really know when I'm actually hungry.

(insert huge whiny sigh)

3 years ago I was down to 182. (see my avatar pic ... that's when I took that one) I felt better and looked OK for a change. I could wear "regular" sized clothes. I gave away ALL my "fat" clothes and now that I've gained back 40 pounds NOTHING fits. crap crap crap

Change of subject.

I'm baking tilapia for 2nd meal and nuking broccoli. Total calories after I eat will be about 1300. Unless I have a cup of decaf. Am giving up on the bpc for now and going back to just a couple of TBS of cream. That saves 300 calories right there. poop.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-20T18:06:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-45852>

Well I figure that if I log everything for about 2 months then take it to the doc/PA they will HAVE to listen. Of course, if I start losing weight that may not be of help.

I went back and looked at my records (which are hit and miss ... I"m not THAT anal) and in February of 2010 I weighed 197.2 pounds. I was still trying to stick to the hcg protocol. I had lost 20 pounds the previous YEAR doing that but had put 10 pounds back on. By the end of 2010 I was up another 10 pounds. Since I went "low carb" last year in January I have gained ANOTHER 20 pounds. Pardon me while I go shoot myself.

Last night about 9:45 I did get hungry and I know it's against the leptin reset "rules" but I did it anyway. I had 1/2 cup fresh blueberries (the last of them) and 2 TBS of HWC. It was enough. Total calories for yesterday approx 1600. I say approx because of the way I cook the beef and the bacon -- most of the fat stays in the drip pan. So I'm not sure I'm getting THAT many extra calories.

Day 2 ...

FBG 88

**Breakfast** was 3 7/8 oz ground beef (weighed cooked), 3 slices thick cut bacon, coffee with 3 TBS HWC for a total of 649 calories, 38g protein, 40g fat and 0g carbs. Cut the calories in HALF from yesterday. We'll see how long I can go on that. I know that yesterday by 3 I was hungry. No hunger pangs but knew I needed to EAT. I'm planning on having fish again. I'm sure DH will like that. NOT.

Oh and Lanie, thanks for the lab info. (and I've been reading your blog, just haven't had anything to say back!)

Then what?

The real question is: Is your health improving for going low carb?

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-21T17:02:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-46036>

Beats the hell out of me, Cavemam. I just know I'm sick of being FAT.

So to complete yesterdays log:

Had a venti SF latte bout mid morning. Later on had some raw walnuts and raw almonds. Went to the movie (dear god save yourself ... RUN AWAY if anyone tries to make you go see The Expendables 2) and had a small popcorn.

Got home a little after 6 and fixed bone-in pork chops for dinner. I had that plus 1/2 cup green beans and about 1/3 cup sweet potato with 2 tsp butter along with 2 TBS unsweetened applesauce.

Totals were 1498 calories; 94g protein, 71g fat, and 70 whopping grams of carbs. The result? this mornings FBG was 103. Insulin resistance anyone? Which means I am STILL leptin resistant. Big surprise there. So what am I to do? Keep those carbs under 50 and STOP EATING POPCORN. Not that eat all that much but even one little handful is now out of the question. So I think I can be a grownup about this.

Day 3. FBG 103 (the horror!)

Breakfast was the usual for a net 613 cal, 35g protein, 50.6g fat and zero grams carbs. good girl.

Hubby is off to the hills for a camp out with friends and to test drive his "new" little camper. He's been working hard to get it whipped into shape and after robbing the kitchen of half my utensils thinks he's ready for a shake down cruise. I get to stay home and babysit the cats and perhaps make a voodoo doll of myFATself and stick pins in it.

Switched my coffee from full on leaded to half and half. I've noticed the last couple of days/weeks I tend to be a bit shaky a couple hours after I drink my morning mug. since the weather is cooling off and I'm wanting to get back to work, shakes are NOT allowed. When you're working in a 1700Â° flame on a bit of glass the size of a pea it's best to have steady hands.

lunch/dinner: probably a bit of salmon and a green salad.

Am going to get the thyroid panel from DirectLabs but will have to drive 2+ hours to Casper to get it done. Oh well. Maybe I can do it on the way to colorado ... I'm 2 years out from my last visit to the outlaws and my sister. It's not that I don't trust hubby to take good care of the cats. I mean just because the last time he locked Three in the crawl space aND LEFT HIM THERE FOR NEARLY A WEEK with no food or water ("honest honey I don't know how he got out") ... grrrrr.

sigh

Men. Can't shoot 'em. Can't live with 'em. Can't live without 'em. Talk about an approach/avoidance conflict.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-22T16:07:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-46293>

The Rest of Yesterday:

Had a mug of decaf w/hwc late morning.

Raw almonds early afternoon.

Dinner was at 5:15 and consisted of scallops sauteed in ghee (tsp) and CO and some canned green beans. Got hungry around 9-ish and yes I was BAD BAD BAD and ate some raw almonds.

Totals: 1406 calories; 94g protein, 104.9g fat, 19g carbs.

Day 4 -- no change in weight

FBG: 82

Breakfast is going to be a serving of sausage quiche I made yesterday and a mug of BPC made with just TWO TBS of butter instead of my usual 4. That would 1 ounce instead of 2. OK.

I've been trying to reread Jack's posts on reverse T3/leptin and am starting to wonder if that isn't, indeed, a big part of my problem. I used to be able to lose weight easily. BUT I have had thinning hair since I was 17 when half of it fell out from stress. Now it's so thin I have to wear it "pixie" short. I have NEVER EVER had good fingernails. They are thin, soft to brittle. They either bend and peel or snap off. And I am TIRED. I am finally sleeping well. No I haven't been tested for sleep apnea but I don't think that's the problem. I might be wrong. I just have NO energy. I'll get a spurt of energy occasionally but when I run out of gas I'm DONE. So I'm off to order the full thyroid panel from direct labs. Won't be able to get it done until first of next month but the August sale price is just too good to pass up.

Have no idea what I'll have later today. Need to get some fresh greens for salad. shrug. just dunno.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-23T00:51:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-46427>

Waving at Lanie ... aren't you moving???

vlynnb ... yes it's frustrating. I was just over at wheatbellyblog.com and there was guy who just gave up wheat and sugar and dropped like a hundred pounds. He looks AWEsome btw. My DH gave it up in January and has lost 30 pounds. grrrrrrrr

So I was back rereading some of Jack's blog posts and right there it was ... I should NOT be skipping meals. I know he says when you can you probably should but I'm working from an ED and skipping is the last thing I should be doing. I think I'm getting the reverse T3 connection to leptin resistance. Maybe. Sort of.

Oh yeah. I cannot EAT that much for breakfast. This mornings quiche had a nice lot of protein in it if I did my arithmetic correctly and THAT was easy to get down. So I may make some sort of quiche a breakfast staple for a while. But I need to go back and double check.

Had a ground beef burger for lunch with a small green side salad with 1/2 a small Florida avocado on it. 0cal dressing cause it was handy. Got a tub of pico de gallo at the store so had that on the burger with a slice of colby jack (small slice). Found some fish I've never tried/heard of so have a fillet thawing for maybe dinner, maybe not. We'll see how hungry I am about 6. If not very may just have a bit of CC with melon.

Can't believe how good my blood glucose is when I don't eat many carbs. I'd like to keep it there permanently.

So off to reread more of Jack's posts. Gotta find my brain first, though. I think it's here somewhere.

ETA I don't crave popcorn. I just LOVE it ... crunchy, buttery, salty. yum. I dont' usually snack either unless I don't eat enough at a meal. Which I have obviously been doing (not eating enough). Gotta watch that.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-23T18:43:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-46547>

Catching up here ... yesterday:

breakfast was 1 slice of the sausage quiche and a mug of bpc with 7/8 oz butter

lunch was ground beef patty topped with a thin slice of colby jack and a small green salad with 1/2 avocado and 0cal dressing plus another mug of (decaf) npc with 2tbs butter

dinner was 1/2 cup Daisy 4% cottage cheese on half a medium cantaloupe topped with sliced almonds.

totals: 1862 cal; 86g protein, 139.4g fat and 56g carbs

**Day 5**

FBG: 102 OK I don't know if it was the melon or the CC or both. No more fruit after 3pm and no more cottage cheese for a while. Woke up feeling puffy as the marshmallow man.

Breakfast: 2 slices of the sausage quiche and a mug of bpc with 2tbs (approx) butter.

Have a fillet of barramundi in the fridge. have never had it and there's a yummy sounding recipe on the package so will have that for next meal with a small green salad.

Since I'm going to get the full thyroid panel done in a few weeks I have decided to stop fussing over calorie totals but am still going to log what I eat. I refuse to accept that I'm stuck being obese and I AM going to figure this out long before it's time for me to shuffle off this mortal coyle. Besides I just ordered a spectacular suede jacket from the King Ranch catalog online ... it's dark brown and heavily embroidered across the sleeves and shoulders with stunning blue flowers. It was ON SALE for $88!!!!! Shoot they had cotton tees "on sale" for more than that. Can't wait to get it although I'd bet the farm that I can't get into it just yet. I LOVE leather ... I have 4 simply gorgeous leather jackets that I bought -40 pounds ago. I want to WEAR THEM!!!

whew. OK that's enough ranting and raving. I need another cup of coffee but don't really want it if you know what I mean. Guess I'll finish off the glass of ice water.

I'm still plowing through the stuff on reverse T3 on Jack's blog and still can't make much sense of it all. I can't wait to get the results (even though I know I won't have them for at least a month). SOMEthing is not right. I'd sure like to figure it out.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-24T17:15:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-46719>

Had the barramundi last night, pan fried it in some ghee.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/47129=433-barramundi.jpg)

topped it with some pico de gallo and added a side of green salad with avocado, greek olives and almost the last of the 0cal dressing.

The fish was tasty but pan frying is a little hard on it. It's delicate with a very slight sweet taste, nice texture but not a lot of flavor. Maybe next time I'll sautee some garlic in the ghee before I add the fish.

ANYway. This morning FBG was 80!!! I think besides tracking my food intake this is the most important thing I've done because I"m really seeing what I should and should NOT be eating.

Ordered the thyroid panel from DirectLabs. Won't get to the lab in Casper for a couple of weeks though. Am doing the salivary collection today for the cortisol test. yay me.

Ate the last of the quiche yesterday so am having ground beef for breakfast with 3 link sausages I found in the fridge. Hubby loves to bury stuff. then blames me when it turns into a science experiment.

later tomaters

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-29T18:21:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-48098>

It has been gawdawful hot again and my brain is just fried. I hadn't been sleeping well either for the last couple of weeks and finally figured out that I didn't have enough air in my mattress. It felt way to hard a couple of weeks ago so I tried to let out some of the air. Nothing happened so I kept pushing the button on the remote and then all of a sudden I was flat on the platform. When I refilled it I didn't get enough air IN so my back and neck have been loudly complaining. sigh. Not the first time I've been through this.

Have finally figured out via my morning FBG that I need to eat dinner about 5 and then NOTHING ELSE AGAIN. Then it stays in the low 80's, otherwise it's low 90's.

Flathead cherries are in. Around here everyone waits for this time of year when some enterprising individuals bring in a couple of truckloads of the cherries and sell them from roadside stands out on Coffeen Avenue. Every year I swear I want to go to Flathead lakes and pick some myself. I'd probably waddle off the rez. Anyway, I finally caved and bought some. They are the size of a QUARTER and beyond delicious. A couple of big handfuls will clean the pipes but good! I'm trying not to overdo since the last thing I need is to put on more fat. But it's not easy. They also had some Ranier cherries, same humongous size and just as good.

Did some reading (very little) on reverse T3 and promptly went and bought some milk thistle. Unless I'm supposed to be living on 800 calories a day on the leptin reset there HAS to be some underlying condition that keeps me from losing weight unless I starve! I keep coming back to thyroid issues.

Of course, I do not have a medical degree so I really am just flying blind here. Hopefully the results of the cortisol and thyroid panel will shed some light on this.

On another note, my sister just got some not so good news. She is a mess, physically having had a hip replacement, surgery for spinal stenosis and she needs to get both knees replaced. She is retired now on disability but won't sit still. She fell off a ladder a while back and finally went to the doc. She has a bone splinter in her spine. That was as clear as she could make it to me. Her doc told her she needed to see a specialist but the soonest she could get in was sept 12. And it's a long drive from where she lives. She was planning on doing the driving. I suggested she might need to call the doc and see (a) if that long a wait was advisable and (b) if she should be driving at all. I know she's scared. But she won't do anything to help herself. I'm thinking of posting in the Ask Jack thread to see exactly what he prescribes for his patients prior to surgery. Is it full on CT and paleo?? Anything else. Not that she'll listen.

crap.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-31T15:56:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-48559>

Here's my WTF moment for today ... FBG is 112. I repeat. **WTF**?!!!

Yesterday's menu:

breakfast: the usual 6oz (raw)/4oz (cooked) ground beef with 3 slices thick bacon and a mug of BPC with 2tbs butter

lunch: leftover shrimp scampi (about 3/4 cup med shrimp), a slice of braunschweiger and a handful of almond nut thins (yeah yeah I know they're made with rice and I shouldn't eat them)

dinner (about 6-ish) 4 oz (weighed raw) salmon with a small green salad (greens, radishes, handful of cherry tomatoes and 1/2 small avocado) with homemade vinaigrette (vinegar and oil, period), glass of kombucha.

that was IT.

#1 suspect is the kombucha. although WHY it would raise my BG is beyond me. It's kombucha and ginger juice with 7g carbs per 8 oz crap crap crap

On another note, I went back through my wellness tests and here's something interesting. My TSH:

8-31-98 ...1.78 (yeah my file drawers are like an archeological dig)

8-22-04 ... 0.42

6-23-06 ... 1.73

12-12-08 ... 0.61

4-15-11 ... 1.85

8-22-11 ... 1.50

6-15-12 ... 2.29

I had come off the Jenny diet in February of '08 (18 months of starvation beginning in September of '06). and I wondered why I wasn't losing weight on 1200 calories a day and why I was "tired" all the time. judas aitch. The increase this year is "suspiciously" timed to when I started the leptin reset. hmmmm.

I'm thinking I'll go ahead this month (september) and do the annual wellness thingy again just to see if it's gone up any more since June. I've been pretty good about that BAB.

In the meantime, back to my @\*#&%(#) blood glucose levels.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-31T18:06:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-48581>

Well ladies, that's good to know in that I will stop those drinks. Good thing I only have a couple left in the fridge.

also, happy to amuse!  If you met me in real life though, you wouldn't know I'm the same person. I was listening to "There goes rhymin' Simon" yesterday (one of my all time favorite Paul Simon albums) and the song "Somethin' so right" came up. Brought tears to my eyes AGAIN with the lines:

"they got a wall in China,

it's a thousand miles long.

To keep out the foreigners they made it strong.

And I've got a wall around me,

that you can't even see.

It took a little time to get close to me."

I'm always surprised at how I can just let the walls down here on the 'net and just be me. Of course, it may well be that this is just another invisible wall.

And that is my TMI for today.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-02T01:27:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-48820>

Woke up feeling like home-made doo-doo. It was really smoky again yesterday ... could barely see the mountains through the haze. I've been taking allergy? or would that be anti-allergy? drops for nearly 3 weeks now so the whole tongue itching thing has pretty much disappeared. But I have had major sneeze attacks the last several days and first my left ear hurt and this morning my right ear felt plugged up and achey. I had gawdawful earaches as a child and that was long before they figured out a fix. I still have problems occasionally.

ANYhoo. Rinsed my sinuses and about passed out the salt water HURT!!! Did it again a bit ago and it wasn't quite as painful so evidently shoulda been doing it all along. geez.

FBG was still elevated at 94 this morning. would really like it to STAY around 80, permanently!

Am feeling like a beached whale and am terrified to get on the scale but really need to do it. So will weigh tomorrow. Man I knew I shouldn't have started eating those damned cherries.

Am still logging meals, nothing new to report.

Did a wee bit more reading/scanning on reverse T3 and am now back to where I started several years ago. I have a LOW basal temperature. When I quit the jenny diet it was hovering just below 96. The doc told me that that was fine, 98.6 is "just an average." unhuh. Don't see him anymore, either.

So I printed out a graph set up to record daily average. 2 readings today because I didn't find it until long after I'd been up for 3 hours. So 2 readings and the average is 97.7. 2nd reading I took after bussing around the house vacuuming the floor and then it was 97.6 so really I think the whole issue is my stupid thyroid/adrenals.

I mean, not to sound like a broken record (do any of you know what that means??? god I feel ancient some days) BUT when you go for 18 months eating 1200 calories a day (ok ok it was 1200 SAD calories) and you're 110 pounds overweight to start AND you only lose 52 pounds in that time with the last 6 months only dropping EIGHT FRAKKING POUNDS and the whole time you have the energy of a cooked noodle and when you quit your basal temp is hovering around 96 on a REGULAR BASIS, your nails are paper thin, you hair has begun to reach 'time to buy a wig' thinness what the heck else do you suppose is wrong???

I mean ... anybody? Anything???

grumble grumble

Waiting for the results of the spit test for cortisol and anxious to get the blood draw for the thyroid panel. I don't know what else to do.

sorry for the rant. I'm having a bad "I am so fat I hate myself" moment.

### Endless — 2012-09-02T05:32:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-48891>

> sorry for the rant. I'm having a bad "I am so fat I hate myself" moment.

I have those, too....they suck. Luckily it's only a moment, though, right?

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-02T17:35:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-48950>

Morning, LinD! Yes. We can.

Got on the scale and it made me laugh. I was expecting to see it had gone up 5 or more pounds because my belly just feels like it's out to THERE. I'm up 1 pound. ONE pound. That was the cherries. Good thing I sent the last of them off to Colorado with hubby a few days ago.

FBG 91. No more pistachios for a while. I had baked tilapia with pesto and green beans for dinner last night. With pistachios for dessert. salty/crunchy is my downfall. And get this. I've lost my craving/desire for chocolate! I have 4 bars of dark chocolate in the cupboard. shrug. don't care. aMAzing.

OK, first-thing-in-the-morning basal temp ... 97.8 Given that a year ago it was 96.8 I think this is pretty incredible. Eating as much protein as I can choke down IS turning up the furnace. Of course I still can't hit the 50g mark unless I do 2 slices of quiche and then I still have to force the last couple of bites, but I'm getting there. Fat seems to be easier because I can just throw a couple extra TBS of butter in my morning cup. Which I did because 5oz(weighed raw)/3+oz cooked ground beef and 3 eggs is only 37g. DAMN. But I keep trying.

My plan is to go jump in the tub in about 15 more minutes so guess I'd better go turn the water on.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-02T19:04:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-48979>

Just a quickie ... filled the tub with the setting I normally use in my daily shower. Turns out its only 77Â°. And here I thought it was way colder. So I hopped in anyway figuring I'll drop the temp a little more tomorrow ... it was chilly but quite comfy. Watched (or should I say rewatched) S1E1 of True Blood on my iPad. I KNEW there was another good use for that! Stayed in about 23 minutes. will aim for 5-10Â° colder tomorrow and 30 minutes. I need to get some sort of cushion for the back of my neck. And I will definitely put some ice on my jelly belly partly so I don't have to LOOK at it rising up out of the water like some ivory island. Maybe I'll get some little plastic palm trees and stick 'em on there while I soak. And a tiny hula dancer.

### Endless — 2012-09-02T19:25:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-48991>

### vlynnb — 2012-09-03T18:04:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-49187>

Darleen, You continue to make me laugh - hula dancers should help pass time! I find it hard to believe that you wouldn't be somewhat the same in person, but I will take your word for it. But, do keep writing, laughing at your posts helps keep me healthier. Especially keep writing on those days you hate yourself. I have those days too though I am not fat anymore, just very unhealthy. Years of trying very hard to read and study and follow everything that was suppose to be healthy (eat lots of whole grains, etc.) helped to ruin my health and it is very discouraging at times, especially when my best friend, the dietician, just died at age 58. What more could we do but try to do our best then and keep trying to learn and do our best now. My brother's advice, "Do your best and then don't worry about it." How does anyone not worry about it? Partial answer, read Darleen's posts.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-03T20:27:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-49204>

LOL vlynnb you give me far more credit than I deserve.  My sense of humor is apparently stuck about age 9 or so. I still love the Three Stooges. I am such a geek.

Yesterday's menu:

Breakfast, ground beef patty, scrambled eggs and BPC (was out of bacon, sob)

Lunch: can of pink salmon, green salad with homegrown TINY ITTY BITTY heirloom cherry tomatoes and home made vinaigrette

Dinner: pork chop, sauerkraut, green beans

prebed snack of raw almonds. OK I know I'm not supposed to do that but I got hungry. Just a few almonds shut my stomach up.

This morning's FBG 82. Musta been the fishy.

Breakfast: ground beef patty, bacon (went to the store) and BPC

Have a screaming headache. Still don't have enough air in the bed so my lower back is tied up tighter than a tick's ass. It's now causing nausea it's so bad. Guess I'll go sit in the massager for a bit and see if it loosens up. Just took some aspirin hoping if I knock the headache and I can relax enough to get the back to calm down. wonder if hubby still has any muscle relaxants left from his back surgery ... they'd be kinda old but should still work.

### Endless — 2012-09-08T05:18:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-50189>

Hope your headache was easily tamed, and your bed fixed!

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-09T21:49:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-50556>

I'm still working on both. DH doesn't call me "the princess & the pea" for nothing.

Other than that the only new thing I have to report is a slight uptick in energy. Ever since I started the milk thistle I find I have the energy to do small things ... make the bed, clean the kitchen, sweep the floor ... one chore right after the other, without needing to go take a short nap.

But nothing else is happening. Still don't have my cortisol results. thyroid panel will be taken when I go to visit the outlaws and my sister in Colorado in a couple of weeks.

OUt of sorts by a bunch yesterday. The day before I had a glass of malbec with dinner. It's been in the fridge for several months and I finally decided to crack it open. Was singularly unimpressed. not only that it made my face flush really BAD for several hours. We're talkin' bright red and a lot of heat. It's from Redwood Creek. And yesterday the flushy thing happened AGAIN for no apparent reason. I went and lay down for bit and felt petty icky. Had had a salmon fillet and some leftover cole slaw for lunch. Had NO appetite at all until, of course, 9 or so and even though I now know WHY I shouldn't eat anything that close to bedtime I knew if I didn't I'd have a hard time falling asleep and wouldn't sleep well because I was hungry. So I had a double handful of raw almonds.

I need to find something I can keep at hand for times when I don't feel like eating but need to ... like last night. sigh

### jib — 2012-09-09T22:07:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-50559>

"I need to find something I can keep at hand for times when I don't feel like eating but need to ... like last night. sigh"

When ever I feel like this my absolute "go to" is King Oscar TWO LAYER finest brisling sardines in extra virgin olive oil. Oh my goodness....they are tasty...and it takes the edge right off.

### Lyndra — 2012-09-10T05:21:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-50645>

Hi Darleen, I just read about you taking milk thistle and having the flushing experience you related to the wine. I wanted to mention that I have been taking MT for a few weeks now and just developed an allergy. It first showed up as a migraine after I took the MT on an empty stomach (as directions indicated). I didn't think too much of it, figuring the migraine came from something else though nothing else really added up. Then a couple of days later, I got hives shortly after taking MT again. I suspect I built up enough in my body to start reacting. Don't know if that is an issue for you at all but wanted to mention it so you could be on the lookout.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-10T06:23:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-50659>

Lyndra, sorry to hear that but I'm sure it's not the MT. I have had a similar reaction to wine in the past so I'm pretty confident that was it. I've taken the MT twice today with no recurrence of the flushing. Oh and my bottle of MT says to take with food.

### ealachan — 2012-09-10T16:42:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-50712>

> **DarleenMB;51214 wrote:** Lyndra, sorry to hear that but I'm sure it's not the MT. I have had a similar reaction to wine in the past so I'm pretty confident that was it. I've taken the MT twice today with no recurrence of the flushing. Oh and my bottle of MT says to take with food.

Did you check your wine to see if it has sulfites? Sulfites can cause flushing and headaches, among other things, if you're sensitive to them.

### Endless — 2012-09-10T18:49:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-50734>

I had wine last night....I don't get a bad flush right away, but the next morning I run hotter and feel flushed. I also sleep like crap. Thinking its not really worth it!

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-11T05:44:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-50900>

Nothing really exciting to report the last several days BUT yesterday I only ate 2 meals, my usual ground beef patty and bacon for breakfast with a mug of BPC and about 2ish I had a green salad with a small can of tuna and homemade vinaigrette. I wasn't really hungry so skipped dinner but about 9 I felt a little hungry. I decided to just ignore it because it wasn't really much.

My temperature this morning was 96.6 at 10:15 and again at 1:30. So instead of skipping lunch I had a small protein shake, just one scoop, and then we ate dinner about 5:45... Boulder Sausage brats and cole slaw. Now the really interesting thing is that I took my tempt at 4:30 and it was 98.4!!

I am more convinced than ever that my issues are related to thyroid, specifically RT3.

### vkiernan — 2012-09-11T14:51:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-50938>

Darleen,

Been thinking about you lately and your trying to figure out what is wrong. My own n=1 is that had it not been for having a few consults with Jack, I still would be here completely struggling and not having a damn clue why. He has directed me with a few labs at first and then more specific ones. I have had 3 half hour consults with him and now I am finding my problem. Money usually is the issue for not testing but I can tell you that without it you will struggle forever most likely. I had what is called a GI Effects test by Metametrics, Jack recommended. It is a test to tell you exactly what is going on in the gut. Remember the gut and brain speak to each other and if something is off they can't speak correctly (**side note: you can have specific bacteria that inhibit weight loss no matter what you eat**). I more than likely have hashis and celiac according to my results as they were high. They are autoimmune diseases and ANYTHING I eat that I would have a reaction to causes imflammation and then disease. I also have fat malabsorption which is an indicator of celiac. This means I can eat all the good fat in the world and it won't do me a damn bit of good and will never reach my brain and I will continue to deteriorate. Jack has gotten this message thru to me loud and clear. He even told me things that my test results show and I answered yes, he was totally right. Amazing!

As for the rt3, yes, I have that too. I also have the pg/e2 imbalance. Until I fix some of these things, I will never get better. You have done the right thing to balance your hormones but that is only 1 step in this process. A consult with Jack is priceless; completely worth every penny you have to spend. I'm not saying you should go get these tests, all I am saying is you can quit guessing once you actually figure it out and Jack will help you get there and tell you which labs to do. Everyone is different and context is really important. I have a pretty clear path now. Completely sucks everything I cannot eat but at least I know now I will be slowing my own death down if I comply.

Ok, not saying anything really except that if you haven't had a consult (which I don't remember you saying) that it would completely benefit you if truly are serious about figuring this out.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-11T21:04:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-51015>

vkiernan,

you're right, of course, about the consult. It's been on my mind quite a lot lately and yesterday I even went looking for the link. But isn't he sort of changing things right now? I know he's so DARN busy. I just don't see how he does all he does and stays sane.

Well. Today's basal temp is already looking much better although my FBG was 105 this morning. Hmm. I had the usual for breakfast, a one=scoop protein shake for lunch with some coconut butter, then I was bad and had about 8 almond nut thins which are made with rice flour. I really should NOT BUY THEM. And one little date thingy rolled in chopped walnuts. Then dinner was brats and cole slaw with apple & raisins. hmmm. We ate before 6 and I had nothing else prior to going to bed.

Oh yeah. Temp ... first take was 97.2Â° YAY!! If I can EVER get it back to 98.6Â° I think my metabolic issues will be well on the way to being fixed.

I have been looking at the Fall fashions and am, as usual, devastated I am too fat to wear them AND look good. I hate this. I miss being slender and being able to dress nicely and look good. So shallow, I know, but when I look good I feel good. crapadoodle.

Having itchy mouth and tongue again so am going to stop the milk thistle for a while and see if it stops. Crap redux. I swear it is what has been giving me some energy. Now I can go back to being a door stop.

### vkiernan — 2012-09-12T02:01:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-51085>

I wonder if you should start with the e-mail consult. Don't know if it is expired or not(might be). You write up a very detailed history and he will tell you where to begin via e-mail. That was a great deal for $99. Otherwise, book a half hour to get you going in the right direction. Then do what he suggests to the best of your ability and then have a real consult and download vodburner recording software, if you have skype, so you can record it (this is a 1 month free subscription that actually works quite well). Recording it is the best. He will pretty much not stop talking the whole time and it is extremely difficult to catch it all and attempt to take notes. Good Luck!

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-13T20:41:17+0300

Just got the results back from my cortisol tests. I thought I was getting the 4x done but for $70 I only got AM and PM. But they are both LOW. AM was 6.84 (normal range listed as 18 to 35) and PM was 0.44 with normal range of 0.9 to 9.2

uhhuh.

Body temp still down. Was 95.6 the other day for 1st AND 2nd reading.

No wonder I can't lose weight.

### vkiernan — 2012-09-14T02:31:08+0300

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-14T03:10:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-51561>

Thanks vkiernan, I did but, like most of his posts, about 2/3rds went right over my head.

Got my new, more potent allergy drops today, took one drop of each and about an hour later my face looked like I had a sunburn! My lips hurt and felt like they were blistered. Checked in the mirror and they were fine. Wow.

Heard from my sister. The fall she took broke off those "spiny" protrusions on 2 of her cervical disks and she has a large bone spur as well. The doc scheduled her for surgery in a few weeks and she's now wearing a cervical collar. There's other stuff going on but this is the worst of it. Doc cautioned her about infection since she is prone to that anyway. Years ago she finally got someone to listen to her and they discovered her gallbladder needed to come out and it wasn't just that she's obese. Well boy howdy it needed out ... it was gangrenous.

ANYway. I have BEGGED her to show her doc the preop post Jack wrote. I cautioned her that she could be facing some horrible horrible pain from the surgery and if she starts NOW eating as much fish as she can choke down along with fat and doing some CT she might come through this ok.

If I won the lottery tomorrow I'd send her to TN. I'm scared witless for her. We had been estranged for nearly 20 years and just have reconnected. That nasty assed childhood and adolescence I mentioned wasn't suffered alone. We're both reaping the whirlwind as far as THAT goes. We both suffer from dissociative personality disorders AND we're both so out of touch with our physical selves that, when injured, it takes 3 days before either one of us notices. True.

crap on toast. To quote my favorite witch.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-14T15:48:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-51631>

Boy did I sleep great! The automatic pet feeder I ordered arrived yesterday. Sammy, my 16lb male cat with cataracts and seizures needs his meds 3x a day. It's easiest to mix it with his wet food. But by 4 or 5 AM (even leaving out kibble) he wants me to get up and feed him. Sooooo. Last night I fixed his last medicated meal about 9:30, put a little something in one side of the feeder to tide him over until it was time for his regular meal and his A.M. dose/meal in the other. OMG they left me alone until sunrise! I never woke up once either. I'm a happy camper right now.

Meant to say I've been using Quelson's powerup shakes the last couple of days. We'll see if they can kick my lazy metabolism into gear.

gotta go fix my BAB ... later.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-15T20:14:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-51832>

Sunrise today was a deep red ... there's a huge forest fire West of Buffalo which is about 40 miles South of here. The fire is up in the high country and is totally inaccessible so it's just ... burning. I don't understand why the smoke is drifting this way but the sky has been so hazy for a week now that you can barely see the mountains. It's really bad today. I steppd out onto the porch to open the windows and the door and now my mouth is driving me crazy it itches so badly. I've had the house completely shut up for a week and the air cleaner in the bedroom going 24/7. Just washed my sinuses in the hope it would calm things down.

Basal temp yesterday was 97.2!! Those power up shakes seem to be doing something. Sure wish the coconut milk wasn't so darned expensive. Oh well.

Worked on my medical history last night. That, coupled with talking to my sister yesterday, gave me a lovely little nightmare this morning. More forbidden territory that I"m not supposed to look at. At least bears and/or lions didn't show up to scare me off. This time it was a barbed wire fence topped with razor wire. hmmm.

My sister remembers a LOT more than I do about our childhood/adolescence. I'd rather she not remind me about it but she is insistent. darn her.

I'm trying very hard not to focus on my fat these days and am instead try to focus on healing my body. I stopped taking the milk thistle because I thought it was causing allergy issues (stupid forest fire smoke) and now because I"m getting the blood draw on Monday (FINALLY!) for the full thyroid panel. I didn't want it to be skewed. I'll start taking the MT again when I get back from visiting the outlaws and my sister next weekend.

I'll be leaving Monday (not looking forward to the 6 hour drive) so will probably not post anything at all until I get back home.

Positive thoughts and energy for my sister would be greatly appreciated. Her doc is, she said, confident he can FIX her neck but he's very worried about the possibility of infection. I sent her the link to Jack's post about pre/peri op and begged her to show it to her doc. I've sent her lots of other links and talked to her until I"m blue in the face. Right now she's so frightened she's like a deer in the headlights. She has NO time to get ready but I told her the least she can do is start icing the back of her neck with a gel pack inserted between her skin and the cervical collar and to start eating LOTS of CO and fish.

They're so broke they can't even pay attention and she doesn't have any credit cards so she's stuck waiting for payday next week. He's a retired school teacher and she just went on disability retirement last winter. I'm just beside myself. and trust me, that's a sight no one wants to see.

She's 63 and a physical wreck. Worse than me because her joints are all shot, she's riddled with arthritis, she's been morbidly obese her entire adult life, she lost one adrenal and part of the kidney years ago when they found a baseball sized cyst on that kidney. They finally did a nuclear scan on her and discovered her thyroid is almost nonexistent. I'm pretty sure we're all prone to liver issues ... I do know our brother is slowly dying of liver failure brought on by alcoholism. Mine has been giving me fits since I was in my late 20's and I'd bet dollars to holes in donuts I have non-alcoholic fatty liver syndrome and so does she.

Ain't we all just a big fat mess.

### Destiny — 2012-09-16T14:12:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-51913>

Darlene, I never realized that Milk Thistle should not be taken before thyroid tests. Do you know why?

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-16T18:40:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-51937>

I'm not taking it because I don't want the results skewed. MT as I understand it, helps the liver to clear itself. What I read about RT3 is that it is made in the liver and if your levels are too high then taking MT helps clear it from the liver. THAT's why I have stopped it until after the test. don't know if I'm on the right track or not, just guessing from what I've read.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-23T00:03:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-53491>

Chocolate, LOL we have, gasp, electricity! And propane! and natural gas! Oh yeah and a lot of coal.

All snarkiness aside we have hot water heat that is propane fired. We don't live up in the high country. We live 7 miles outside of Sheridan which is a small town. BUT we have a Home Depot, even!

Just got home about 30 minutes ago. Got my thyroid results and I'm not sure the doc is even going to look at them.

I'll post 'em over in the hack my labs thread...

### chocolate — 2012-09-23T02:33:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-53523>

Oh, I wasn't being citified, I just wondered if you burned wood to keep warm. I use wood in the desert. I'm jealous of the hot water radiator. REALLY! Its so expensive for utilities in CA you wood think we were the South Pole.

I hope you get to getting better soon.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-23T16:01:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-53581>

chocolate, I hear you on the cost of utilities. when we bought this place in 1996, propane was 25CENTS a gallon. 10 years ago it shot up to $2.25 and has stayed there. This year the CoOp introduced "balanced billing" where they took your previous year's usage, divided by 12 and you pay that amount monthly, year round. They just came out and added more propane to our tank (which holds 1000 gallons) and the cost was $1.45!! so they must have been able to get a sweet deal from THEIR supplier since they knew ahead of time how much they were going to need. So no more $500 bills from them for a lousy 200 gallons. We regularly go through 1200 gallons annually in spite of all the weatherization improvements we have made to this place. And it's NOT that big ... around 2000 sq feet. I think what really hurts is the floor is not insulated. The house is on a 4' crawl space. Oh well.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-24T06:56:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-53754>

You might have noticed I'm back. My trip to Colorado was not good. First off I was sick by the time I got there. It felt like I was getting an ear infection, I was hoarse and just plain felt icky. Slept most of Tuesday, woke up looking and feeling feverish.

Wednesday my "SIL" dragged me off shopping. She likes to not eat all day long. I didn't fare so well. thursday I got a pedicure (LOVE the candy apple red color!) so my feet are nice and almost soft again. Poor Doen, she really had to work to get all that dead skin off my feet. Then I got a facial ... first one ever. I was late for both appointments. HEY! All those backroads did NOT exist when I was a kid! Thank god for GPS. The facial was quick because I was late but nice. Turns out I really didn't need it as I had very few clogged pores. But she got a bunch of those danged chin hairs. They're hard for me to see because they're blonde AND very thin. Plus I got to relax a bit. The vaporizer thingie sure helped my allergies. I forgot to mention to smoke from the forest fires went all the way from Sheridan to Denver and beyond. It was SO bad on Wed that you couldn't even see the foothills!!

My sister came over Friday and hand delivered the bad news. I'm still reeling. She has spinal stenosis so bad, and has had it for so long, that her spinal cord is dying. she has signed a DNR for this surgery.

I can't talk about it ...

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-27T00:27:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-54589>

So now I have infections in both ears. My brain feels as if it slid sideways. I hate taking antibiotics. And my sister, who is having surgery on her neck to repair the damage from her fall last summer, has signed a DNR. She is 62. I know she's terrified of becoming a paraplegic because of the damage and the spinal stenosis but I think the DNR is a mistake at this time. Her son and daughter are not so happy with her either, nor is her husband. I'm just plain scared out of my wits. We have been estranged pretty much since I left "home" at 17 and just this past year have reconnected.

Got my thyroid labs back. Did I say that already? I know I posted over in the optimal labs section. Turns out, big forking surprise, that my RT3 is about twice what it should be. Coupled with my low basal temps and low cortisol levels IT'S NO FREAKING WONDER I'M OBESE. Sometimes I just want to bitch slap the entire medical profession. Well, except for Jack. He gets it.

So now I'm shuffling paper to get ready for my consult. After that I will go see my doc with all this new info and may need to take a foam rubber bat with me to just smack him a few times for telling me that "RT3 isn't checked unless they suspect disease" and that regarding my weight "we need to talk about calories."

Did you all read the latest blog about iodine? It's AWEsome. I sent the link to my sister and told her if she wants to avoid infection (which is a real concern given her history) she needs to read it AND give it to her surgeon. I'm going to wait to order the liquid potassium iodide from Amazon until I've had my consult. AND I'm really trying to get into the whole "eat more SEAfood thing" .. which, living in landlocked Wyoming, is pretty forking expensive.

We went to Billings to Costco on Monday and bought a lot of meat. I really wanted to get some shrimp etc but at $45 for the bag I had to let it go. I did buy TWO of the packages of canned pink salmon and one of the packages of tuna. We hadn't been since early last spring so it was a very expensive trip. I'm hoping the weather will stay nice enough I can get back up there the first of November. That'd be the fish trip.

I guess I'll take my cranky lil self off to the kitchen now and make a mug of decaf. I gave up the leaded stuff when I got my cortisol results. I was only drinking a mug a day but figured even that probably wasn't a good idea. I've gone most of my entire adult life without caffeine so it wasn't to difficult to give it up again.

Here's to clear skies, a little RAIN and happy eardrums.

### chocolate — 2012-09-27T00:41:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-54598>

Darleen, real sorry about everything going on.

On a note that might help you, Trader Joes and WF are a third of costco's prices. Seriously. IDK why. I've heard that the restaurant suppliers are pretty good also.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-27T00:55:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-54606>

Thanks but I think the nearest Trader Joe's is probably a little more than 2 hours away. WF? 6 hours down to fort Collins. We live in the middle of nowhere.

Costco has great prices and great products plus they treat their employees like gold! We love 'em. That $45 bag of shrimp? It was a LOT of shrimp and they were the big ones. But we were already way over budget so I had to either put something back or skip it for now. As it was we spent WAY too much. Because we only go about every 3 months (and not at all in the winter) we usually spend around $300 per trip. We spent double that this time. I'm still reeling. But we were out of everything plus there's the usual, "oh look at this!" ... I must have spent nearly $80 on raw nuts alone and about that much on the meat. But it was 10 boneless pork loin chops (the big ones), 4 big strip steaks, 4+ pounds of chuck "ribs" which I cut up for a fantastic paleo beef stew AND 2 huge bags of sirloin burgers (which at $3.99 a pound already saved me a bunch over what I've been paying). When you consider how much you're getting, it's well worth it. I keep raw almonds, pecans and walnuts in the freezer. And I was all out of pine nuts. Plus EVERYTHING has gone up this summer. What I had in that cart would have been $500 last Spring. I'm trying not to get too depressed. At least we can afford decent food and a roof over our heads and heat in the winter. Not everyone is so lucky.

Darleen, I have a home remedy that works within hours for relieving the pain (and infection).

Take some warmed olive oil, crush a bunch of garlic in it for about 10 mins, then drip the oil in your ear. Let it go down to the drum. It can get in there quick and alleviate things within hours.

### ealachan — 2012-09-27T18:00:33+0300
