Hope you're feeling better soon, Darleen, and will be keeping your sister in my thoughts. When is the actual surgery?

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-27T19:06:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-54766>

> **ealachan;55573 wrote:** Hope you're feeling better soon, Darleen, and will be keeping your sister in my thoughts. When is the actual surgery?

surgery on her neck is October 2nd. The speed with which they scheduled it has me freaked. But they're not done. She has a "lump" on her side which no one would take seriously. She's concerned it's filled with infection from a previous surgery. I don't know how many years ago it was they found a baseball sized lump on her left kidney. When it was finally removed, along with half her kidney and her adrenal gland, it was found to be an OLD hematoma that had become a hard shell. She said the interior was lined with gills like a mushroom and it was FULL of infection. There's more to it but she's convinced they didn't get all of the infection cleaned out and that's why she's had other problems, including last year's episode with an infection in her jaw that came out of nowhere. It took weeks of IV antibiotics to clear up. Dr. Chung is very concerned and has already booked an appt for her with another doc to take care of THAT.

Also, her hip replacement has been really bothering her. Dr. Chung says it could be a loose screw which would have been pistoning up and down and causing her grief and if that's what it is it's easily fixed.

thanks for the good thoughts ealachan. I'm worried sick about her.

As for me ... I'm feeling somewhat better but my ear is hurting again. Guess I need to go wash out my sinuses again. It almost rained yesterday. sigh. I could spit more moisture than that. The smoke continues to create a horrible haze over Sheridan and my allergies are still driving me nuts. I swear I'm getting a mask and wearing it from now until it clears up.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-09-27T21:08:18+0300

I tried to lie down for a bit because my ear is bothering me again. stooopid catz. Missy (who's 19 and I swear has lost some of her hearing) marched up and YELLED in my face. Then she tried to sit on my head. Well to be honest, she was trying to sit on the heating pad which I had gently laid over my aching ear. Then Three showed up. She doesn't like him. I figured there was going to be a beat down right then and there. But she finally decided she'd go under the blankets along my back while Three settled for pining me down in the front.

Then Sammy showed up.

bother.

I'm rethinking the "love potion" for my ear.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-29T19:49:36+0300

I see the mad spammers are hitting this forum REALLY hard. jerks.

Was feeling a little better when I finally got up this morning. Three woke me up at 3am talking to himself up and down the hallway. I was awake for 2 hours then finally dropped off again. Had truly strange dreams (like that's something unusual for me). Now my right ear is kinda bothering me again. You'd think it would get better once I'm up and it can drain. Thinking about putting some warm OO and garlic in it and lying back down for bit.

Reading other people's journals to see how everyone is doing ... I guess my cravings left a long time ago. Now if I could just figure out why my appetite signaling seems to be out of whack. ### DarleenMB — 2012-10-01T17:16:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-55569>

I'm back. didja miss me?

My ears are much better today. finally. Good thing I was taking the antibiotics because Three hooked my arm a couple of nights ago and it developed a little infection. The redness got about as big around as a penny but the swelling went down quickly and now it just looks a bit like an old bruise. Live with cats, plan on getting hooked now and then.

So, still wading through BG13. Read BG12 for the 3rd time and it's slowly starting to make sense. I think I'm going to go find a good beginning text on cellular bio and start studying. I'm getting tired of this. Computers are much easier.

Nothing new on the diet front. I still don't "feel" hungry but do get slightly nauseated. Wonder if that's the antibiotics working. Had shrimp and OMG FRENCH brie last night. First bite of cheese was a little mouthgasm it was soooooooo good. And I'm surprised to report I did not get hungry again. Didn't eat all than many shrimp either. Planning on having the baramundi fillet for dinner tonight. Still having the ground sirloin burger from Costco for breakfast and some bacon. I know it should be fish. I'm workin' on it.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-02T19:29:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-55866>

My sister is, right now, going under the knife. We spoke last night and I had a good, calm feeling about this. I think she's going to come through fine. She's worried about her attitude after. I kept reminding her she is 62, not 22; that not doing what the surgeon says after is being selfish as it could deprive those of us who love her of her company ... and on and on.

I'm trying to get myself motivated to get back in the studio. It's been way too long and I'll probably set fire to something I shouldn't. I am just so fatigued. man I'm sick of this.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-03T02:38:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-55985>

I'm so relieved about my sister! So it's time for a bit of humor. DH's SIL sent me this:

Ladies, this has to be read, laughed at and passed on. There is not a woman alive who won't crack up over this!

I was due for an appointment with the gynecologist later in the week. Early one morning, I received a call from the doctor's office to tell me that I had been rescheduled for that morning at 9:30 am. I had only just packed everyone off to work and school, and it was already around 8:45am. The trip to his office took about 35 minutes, so I didn't have any time to spare.

As most women do, I like to take a little extra effort over hygiene when making such visits, but this time I wasn't going to be able to make the full effort. So, I rushed upstairs, threw off my pajamas, wet the washcloth that was sitting next to the sink, and gave myself a quick wash in that area to make sure I was at least presentable. I threw the washcloth in the

clothes basket, donned some clothes, hopped in the car and raced to my appointment.

I was in the waiting room for only a few minutes when I was called in. Knowing the procedure, as I'm sure you do, I hopped up on the table, looked over at the other side of the room and pretended that I was in Paris or some other place a million miles away. I was a little surprised when the doctor said, 'My, we have made an extra effort this morning, haven't we?'

I didn't respond.

After the appointment, I heaved a sigh of relief and went home. The rest of the day was normal ... some shopping, cleaning, cooking.

After school when my 6-year-old daughter was playing, she called out from the bathroom, 'Mommy, where's my washcloth?'

I told her to get another one from the cupboard.

She replied, 'No, I need the one that was here by the sink, it had all my glitter and sparkles saved inside it.'

Never going back to that doctor. Ever.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-03T22:53:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-56115>

I made clam chowdah yesterday ... used 2 cups 1/2 and 1/2 and 1 cup HWC (thank you Costco!) Boy it is tasty. Had another SMALL bowl for lunch just now and added in the last handful of cooked shrimp. yummy!!!!!

Looking at fixing baked cod for dinner. Got the cod and some flounder on the last outing to the store. Am going to Albertson's fish counter tomorrow and see if they have any fresh oysters and/or mussels. I figure I can at least TRY raw oysters. Maybe if I hold my nose and shut my eyes. Mussels, OTOH, I could eat by the bucket full. mmmmmmmmmmm so good! And clams! ersters are just ugly looking.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-04T19:29:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-56255>

Missed the cod, forgot it was DH's night with the boyz so he's never home for dinner. I had a chilled small can of crab meat which I dumped onto some lettuce and dressed with a combo of mayo, seafood sauce, lime juice and a few drops of worcestershire for flavor. The crab was just boring. Last time I buy it that way.

had a cup of decaf a bit later and then had that stupid flush on my face AND my mouth started itching. Don't know what caused it.

Meat day today with the usual BAB. Which is never BIG enough. I think I'm still skirting the 50g of protein but I have lots of butter in my morning decaf so that should take care of it.

Lunch will probably be a small bowl of the clam chowdah.

Dinner is going to be meat loaf. I've thawed some of Sackett's yummy home made breakfast sausage and have a pound of ground sirloin sitting there. Gotta use it. so meat day today.

Had trouble falling asleep last night. Finally got up and put on some socks and fell asleep before my head hit the pillow again. When my feet are cold I cannot sleep. They didn't FEEL cold but yesterday's temps never got above 38 and it was about 62 in the house when we went to bed. It's AGONY crawling between icy sheets (I gave up pj's and other bed "clothes" when I was 19) but man I warm that bed up fast these days. But my feet need a little help. Somewhere in the middle of the night I shucked the socks. And slept until 9:30!!!!

gotta run ... the timer on my breakfast is beeping...

Got another call from my sister last night. She's already home! She said all of the pain she'd been experiencing is gone and only a small amount from the surgery. They put her in one of those electrical stimulators to stimulate bone growth. My BIL has one from his neck surgery last winter. Back to sis ... she's supposed to take it easy for 6 weeks. No NOTHING. HA that should be interesting.

so one hurdle down, 2 more to go. AND she said the CT for the concussion showed no damage so we both think her eyesight problems probably stemmed from the bone spur and the stenosis in her neck. Hopefully that means THAT should improve and when it does she has my old Kindle now with about 300 books on it to keep her busy.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-07T03:54:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-56663>

Thanks Diane! But they're in a jar! I think I'm gonna bake 'em.

That's ok though. Dinner tonight was mussels ... I sauteed some onion and garlic in a bit of ghee and CO, took it out of the pan and added in the mussels (they were already cooked and on the half shell) until they warmed up. Put the onion/garlic mix back in the pan and stirred a little bit longer. Had I some dry white wine I would have finished it with that. Had to settle for half a lemon (and not a very fresh one at that but it was still good). I steamed the crab legs while I was working on the mussels and that was dinner. OOOOOOO doggies! That was some fine eatin'!

I have to say those were also some big assed mussels. My taste buds are a little warped right now (probably from the antibiotics) but they still tasted gooood. And yeah, I chewed them puppies up good. Couldn't finish my crab so I have a bit left for tomorrow.

Jack would be so proud.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-10T23:43:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-57438>

Plowing through BG13. Now I'm a kinda "woo-woo" gal here but sometimes Jack out woo=woos me.

Have been getting "trembly" late morning/early afternoon for the last several weeks. YEsterday it happened again and I'm thinking WTH because I'm back on decaf. Then I ate a little lunch (braunschweiger and a small handful of sweet potato chips) and the trembling stopped.

crap.

Back to square one. I may be obese but in my heart of hearts I know that I am not eating ENOUGH. And you know what? I dont' wanna. Eating is a pita. There. I said it.

Today I was out running errands (voting and grocery shopping) and here came the tremblies again. Well fork.

Got home, unloaded the cat food and litter (yes my darling little furbabies eat well and on time as opposed to moi) and got Sammy his lunch/meds. Still trembling. okokokokokokok Fished (ahahahaha) the pack of water-packed sardines out of the fridge and spooned 'em out into a dish. BLEH! Oh. "no salt added" Well WTF

Salt.

Better

Am now finishing up a small handful of salted/oven roasted almonds. And my decaf latte AND my big glass of ice water.

There. Don't I feel better now?

pfbbbbt.

ETA my face has flushed bright red and my temp has spiked to 98.8!!! Holy fishcakes. could the sardines have had something to do with this? Do ya suppose??? Wonder what it all means.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-14T02:52:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58025>

I know better than to say "never" on any forum. It's been a long, stressful day but I'm still here. Sorry for the little snit I tossed earlier. sometimes the 5 year old gets hold of the controls and runs amok. I've sent her to her room to play with her crayons.

Just got done with my phone consult and have oodles and oodles to ponder. Good thing the recorder thing was working. Now if I can just get it to playback because I was so nervous I'm sure I missed half of what he said.

And then to add to the stress (pity my poor wrung out adrenals) I had a short visit with hubby about the consult. whew. I finally managed to share some things with him we've never discussed in over 35 years of marriage. My face is burning and I want to burst into tears but it's out. I cant' say I feel any better right now but you have to start somewhere.

Now that you're all scratching your heads I guess I had best explain just a little. And that's all it's going to be... a little.

My childhood and adolescence were marked by continued sexual, emotional and physical abuse. Parents divorced when I was 6, and I lost my Mother to a car accident when I was 13. The impact that part of my life had on me is not easy to even think about. BUT the good news is Jack believes that if I'm willing to pursue it I can fix my poor beleaguered brain. that the problems I am now experiencing are due to the fact the proper neuronal connections were not made when I was child. I think I got that part right. So if I rewire using epi-paleo, increase hormones and test test test as well as work on releasing lots of oxytocin I should be able to fix what's been broken for so long.

I'm excited. Yeah that's the neanderthal genes expressing delight at something new to tackle. I do SO love a challenge.

### Lyndra — 2012-10-14T02:56:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58027>

Glad to have you back and hearing you sound optimistic. Go Darleen!

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-15T02:56:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58223>

Sneaky mussels.

I've never cooked fresh ones before. I got them home, tucked them in a nice bowl with damp paper towels over them and a tight lid. Took them out a bit ago and a bunch had opened. Well hell. Into the trash.

Getting ready to fix seafood stew and thought to look and see how to clean the darned things.

Sneaky mussels.

I had to fish all but 2 out of the trash. TAP TAP TAP anybody home? errrrrrrk as they closed up tight.

bwahahahaha you're mine now you tasty little thangs.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-15T03:46:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58230>

so the fish stew is done. I tossed in oysters and shrimp. Just a simple base of tomatoes, onion, garlic, parsley and organic chicken broth.

It looks and smells yummy.

so why am I not hungry????

Had my usual BAB ... Costco ground sirloin patty, 3 slices bacon, mug of BPDC

About 3 I had 3 small slices of smoked cheddar with a bit of deviled ham on it and a handful of sweet potato chips. and some cream cheese stuff. I have no hunger right now. But I'll bet about 9-ish I get hungry again. foo.

Well hubby can test the stew ... he loves oysters and mussels and shrimp. I hope I got all the beards off the mussels.

Oh and I'm working on a rather long journal post. Man my head has been buzzing all day long with images and thoughts flitting around like fireflies on a hot summer night. sheesh.

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-15T04:31:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58238>

Darlene,

I love your posts! I think it's ok to be yourself and now and then actually even communicate negative things with people. I've never been accused of looking through rose colored glasses. Sometimes things actually suck, and sharing the suck with someone can be a great way to process and let it go.

On our call last night, Dr. K mentioned that there are some things that talk-therapy just makes worse. . .when you focus on negative things and go over them and keep them alive and keep dwelling on them. . . and I agree with this. Sometimes you just have to move on - but there is a place for sharing and letting the support you get help you jettison the bad juju.

I also have great respect for people who can go back and change their minds and adjust rather than sticking to some statement made in high emotions. I have a long history of cutting off my nose to spite my face, so I appreciate examples of slightly more mature behavior!

It must have been so difficult to talk to your husband after so long.

Good luck with re-programming!

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-15T20:44:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58373>

I checked out some of the programs listed on the Skype site. The one I bought (it was $20) is eCamm recorder. Real easy to install (but then I drive a Mac so that kinda goes without saying) and use. When the call came in I pressed the button with the red dot on it and it started recording. I haven't looked at it yet but it appears to have gone straight to the correct folder.

Just a word of caution... be sure you have NOTHING ELSE RUNNING while you're on that call. I forgot that in my earlier panic that day I had set my email program to check every 5 minutes (I normally check it manually because I go through a filter program first to take out all the spam). so I had drop outs every 5 minutes and I did miss a lot at one point because evidently there was mail being d/l. STUPID STUPID STUPID But I was pretty much running in panic mode from the time I got up.

The things Jack discussed with me were very difficult for me to deal with because, I think I said this earlier, I have never EVER talked about them with anyone. And to discuss them with a man (even though he is a doctor) just made it about a billion times worse. I haven't even told my own physician (who I really really like and trust) the things I told Jack. It showed on my face too because it was bright red the whole time.

I'm seeing the PA today because (again) I just can't bring myself to tell Chris (my doc) the things I need to. I'm more comfortable talking to Deb about it because I've already broached part of it with her and survived to tell the tale.

god I am such a coward.

OK. I wanted to share my new journey and yesterday tried to write in my personal journal about all the thoughts banging around in my head. It was like trying to listen to Beethoven, Lady Gaga, Led Zepp, Dvorak and every horrible pseudo C&W song ever written ... all at freaking once.

Jack made me face some unpleasant truths. Unpleasant because I have been avoiding them for a very long time. So score a few points for the good doc.

What I distilled out of yesterday's mindmash was this. I have allowed fear to inform every single decision I have ever made and I am where I am today because of those choices and decisions. I have to own them if I'm going to fix them. My marriage is where it is partly because of actions on dh's part but I need to take responsibility for part of the sad state of affairs we have going on. It was choice I made. Now I have to work on changing it.

And that's as much as I can deal with right now.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-15T23:50:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58407>

> **DarleenMB;59885 wrote:** I
>
> god I am such a coward.
>
>
>
> What I distilled out of yesterday's mindmash was this. I have allowed fear to inform every single decision I have ever made and I am where I am today because of those choices and decisions. I have to own them if I'm going to fix them. My marriage is where it is partly because of actions on dh's part but I need to take responsibility for part of the sad state of affairs we have going on. It was choice I made. Now I have to work on changing it.
>
>
>
> And that's as much as I can deal with right now.

When a person grows up with constant abuse, fear is intelligent. Fear is essential to survive. To not have fear is to get more abuse, or even be killed. Fear is not cowardice in this situation, it is survival instincts.

What I hear you saying is that your fear no longer serves you. In fact, it is colouring your ability to see clearly when things are no longer abusive.

The same intelligence that helped you to see you needed to be afraid can help you to see what you need now. We all do this step-by-step. None of us do it all at once. If you treat yourself harshly, you are recreating the same thing that was done to you. Treat yourself kindly. Baby steps.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-16T00:15:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58422>

Thank you both for that.

Now, I sure wish I could rename this journal:

**Unleashing the Rock Goddess**

And that will take a bit of explaining but I'm not there yet.

Just thought I'd toss that out for grins and giggles.

The PA is prescribing cytomel for my thyroid. I gave her the 411 on my consult but we're taking one thing at a time. I gave her the list of suggested labs so we need to sort that all out. Just remembered I forgot one. I really need to watch the recording of the consult and make better notes.

AND I got almost as flustered talking to her as I did to Jack which makes me wonder if something else is going on. Like the deeply buried threat of what would happen if I ever told anyone. Life with a psychotic monster can be fun. /sarcasm

One step at a time. But now my brain is going haywire on me again.

Easier stuff ... breakfast was salmon, shrimp and bacon with a mug of BPDC

Just had 2nd meal which was tuna salad with celery, onion, HB egg and a bit of mayo and mustard on half an avocado with just a touch of black lumpfish roe (which I LIKE! but it's way salty and I do not care for the dextrose they added. ugh). Side of baby peppers and tiny dill pickles and a bit of dark chocolate with chili pepper in it. Love that kick.

### ealachan — 2012-10-16T23:54:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58602>

Well see, there's two schools of thought there: you can spend a bunch of money for something state-of-the-art that does the trick, or you can spend little to nothing on something low-tech that does the trick. Either way, trick's getting done! I think a lot of women go the expensive route on their first foray into BOB's, because they maybe don't know quite what they want / need. Once you've gotten one and gotten a feel for it (sooo punny, I KNOW), you'll be able to then shop a little more efficiently. You'll know that the rabbit ears don't do it for you, or WHOA BUDDY bigger is better, or nope you just want a little $8.99 pocket rocket that gets you where you need to go quick fast and in a hurry.

Also, for what it's worth, there are cheaper versions of the one that you linked to. There are lots of them out there nowadays in that shape and style, not least of all because they look a hell of a lot more discrete than some of the big anatomically correct jelly monstrosities.

### Lanie — 2012-10-17T08:51:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58684>

I recommend the Hitachi Magic Wand...a bit on the pricy side, and not the quietest or the tiniest of toys, but other than that it's the best! Nothing else has come close. I tried a less expensive knockoff first - just as effective, but the product itself didn't last as long. Good luck, and I second the motion to have fun!

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-17T20:21:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58743>

Thanks for the recs ... I'm still trying to narrow the field down.

FInally gave up waiting for my sister to get on her computer so we could talk via skype and I just called her. I was telling her some of the things I learned in my consult and the next thing I know she's sobbing. crap. She is just beaten so far down there isn't any more up for her. I can't talk about it any further than that because it's her story, not mine. But it just makes me feel so damned helpless.

and before the lectures start, let me share this with you. When I was 3 and Mom was pregnant with Louise, Mom went about getting me ready for the arrival of "MY baby." Now mind you I was 3 when she was born. She (my sisiter) got really really sick right after she was born and they kept her in the hospital for a week. She almost died. And I have a vivid memory of the day she finally came home. They made me sit on the couch and I can still see my Mother walk through the door with this huge bundle in her arms.

It was March so lots of blankets and Louise was a good sized infant ... she weighed almost 9 lbs when she was born.

Mom walked over and put that big bundle in my lap and I got to hold MY BABY for the first time.

I promised Mom I would always take care of MY BABY.

and here I am 62+ years later still trying to take care of her. And I can't. I can't fix her life. I can't fix what's broken in her because I'm having a hell of time fixing what's broken in me. And it's tearing me apart.

### vkiernan — 2012-10-17T20:32:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58753>

A whole lot of responsibility and guilt for a 3 year old!!!!

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-17T20:35:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58755>

> **vkiernan;60289 wrote:** A whole lot of responsibility and guilt for a 3 year old!!!!

Nothing compared to the guilt heaped on me a couple years later by my dad. Doesn't matter. I love my sister and would move heaven and earth to make her life better. If only that would work.

Hugs to you.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-18T00:01:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58817>

Jack my intellect knows that and agrees. My emotions just want to hold her and make her well. I know I can't do it for her but it doesn't stop making me feel like I should. And that is precisely why I laughed when you told me that people close to me might say I'm a PITA.

I'm working on it.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-18T17:48:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58941>

Yesterday I only took 3/4 of a tab of the cyclomet. I had my usual bab of bacon, ground sirloin patty and mug of BPDC. Lunch was a BUNCH of shrimp. That was it. Later on I had a handful of almonds. I never got hungry again. I waited because usually if I eat a late lunch I don't get hungry again until about 8 or so. Nothing.

Got up this morning, not real hungry but I started breakfast and then checked my BG ... HOLY CRAP. It was 182! That is TWICE what it usually is.

I know I've read somewhere in here that someone else was having a similar problem with their BG bouncing all over the place. Anyone have any answers? Or do I need to go back to the PA? Why on earth would bumping up my T3 cause my BG to go nuts? And yes, I'm going to check it again in about an hour.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-19T20:46:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-59212>

so just what IS a rock goddess anyway?

This has been rumbling around in my head the last several days so I figured it was time to lay it all out.

First of all, the very fact I actually managed to get up on a stage once-upon-a-time and belt out rock tunes while fronting a band is pretty amazing in and of itself. My primary directive in life has always been to avoid the spotlight. Drawing attention to myself always brought punishment. Easier to hide. I learned chameleon-like behavior very early — mimic the predator and you stayed safe. I wonder what part of the reptilian brain controls THAT behavior.

ANYway. I saw a new album yesterday by Van Morrison entitled “Born to sing — no plan B” which pretty much sums me up. I wanted to sing for as long as I can remember. I sang to myself mostly. When I was 8 or 9 I conned my Mom into letting me take the lime green Crosly radio that was in the kitchen up to my room. I set it on the window sill next to my bed where, when I lay down, the speaker was right next to my ear. I would turn it on really low late at night and listen to the rock jocks on KIMN out of Denver. That was pre-head banging days btw. But it was still some great music. And Pogo Pogue was my fave DJ. Baby rock queen.

Later I dreamed of singing on stage with a band. At 30 I finally grabbed hold and went for it. It lasted 2 whole years and I was just getting comfortable with it and my voice was showing vast improvement (like any muscle, exercise strengthens) and then BAM it was over. (I just want to interject that a regional talent scout had already started booking gigs for us and we hadn't even signed the contract yet ... we were GOOD). I’m not going to go into it except to say it was like being divorced 5 times all at once. I shut down the music in my head and my heart. Turned my back on the thing I loved.

BUT (cue Joan Jett and the Blackhearts) I (STILL) LOVE ROCK ’N ROLL … my heart has a 4/4 backbeat. Give me pounding drums, screaming guitars and I’m a happy camper. Sex, (I don’t’ do drugs) and rock ’n roll. yup. That’s the life I live in my head.

When I read TS Wiley’s “Sex, lies & menopause” I found within the first couple of pages a very interesting tidbit. At least I found it interesting. Don’t know about anyone else. It’s called the sacculus and is part of the middle ear. It likes loud noise. Channels the vibes right through to (where the flock else?) the hypothalamus.

side note here … I only had one quarter of neuropsychology way back in my college days. I’m thinking I must have forgotten most of that class because although I remember gross anatomy of the brain I do not recall the hypothalamus getting a lot of attention. Why? Here’s this quote from Wiley, “ … the part of your brain that controls drives like hunger, sex and more than a few other hedonistic responses — the" (say it with me brothers & sisters) "HYPOTHALAMUS." CAN I GET AN AMEN? But then, it was only an introductory class.

Where was I?

Oh yeah, TS Wiley. She goes on to talk about how activating the sacculus causes feelings of movement or floating. Um. Well, maybe in YOU darlin’ but my reaction is a whole lot different. (see previous reference to sex, (I don’t do drugs) and rock and roll)

There you have the first half of the equation. Or at least a brief peek at it. Rock Queen + sex goddess = ??????? Will I get there? Is there any point. Well, other than feeling I finally pulled all the disparate parts of myself back together I guess not. But as long as I’m still breathin’ I’m not giving up.

### janagram — 2012-10-20T06:15:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-59293>

this friend of mine who is a really shy older gal played a tape of herself singing with a band back in the day.........Sounded. just. like. Grace Slick.

It was awesome!!!!!!!!!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-20T12:59:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-59306>

Baby rock queen.

BUT (cue Joan Jett and the Blackhearts) I (STILL) LOVE ROCK ’N ROLL … my heart has a 4/4 backbeat. Give me pounding drums, screaming guitars and I’m a happy camper. Sex, (I don’t’ do drugs) and rock ’n roll. yup. That’s the life I live in my head.

When I read TS Wiley’s “Sex, lies & menopause” I found within the first couple of pages a very interesting tidbit. At least I found it interesting. Don’t know about anyone else. It’s called the sacculus and is part of the middle ear. It likes loud noise. Channels the vibes right through to (where the flock else?) the hypothalamus.

side note here … I only had one quarter of neuropsychology way back in my college days. I’m thinking I must have forgotten most of that class because although I remember gross anatomy of the brain I do not recall the hypothalamus getting a lot of attention. Why? Here’s this quote from Wiley, “ … the part of your brain that controls drives like hunger, sex and more than a few other hedonistic responses — the" (say it with me brothers & sisters) "HYPOTHALAMUS." CAN I GET AN AMEN? But then, it was only an introductory class.

Where was I?

Oh yeah, TS Wiley. She goes on to talk about how activating the sacculus causes feelings of movement or floating. Um. Well, maybe in YOU darlin’ but my reaction is a whole lot different. (see previous reference to sex, (I don’t do drugs) and rock and roll)

There you have the first half of the equation. Or at least a brief peek at it. Rock Queen + sex goddess = ??????? Will I get there? Is there any point. Well, other than feeling I finally pulled all the disparate parts of myself back together I guess not. But as long as I’m still breathin’ I’m not giving up.

If you’re not prepared to be wrong, you’ll never come up with anything original.........I think your thoughts here are quite unique and I like what I am reading. What did I learn about my own health IPO 7 years ago when I re built me? I realized that if knew where I was headed, I wouldn’t do what it took to get it done. I realized why doctors fail with patients is because we focus too much on a treatment plan. When I can predict or plan it, I just don’t do it. So instead of focusing in on the destination I enjoyed the ride of the creativity in my head as I filled with all this evolutionary medicine. The more I learn about Evo Med, the more I realized my imagination was no competition for Mother Nature. She brought ideas from my minds margins and now you are seeing those ideas in my words on a blog.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-20T18:41:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-59352>

gah... Seeing a response from Jack made me feel momentarily as if the teacher was about to yank me outta my seat and send me to the principal's office. whew.

Talked to my sister last night. She sounds soooo much better. Her voice is back to normal, her attitude sounded better. She saw the PA on Wed and got her muscle relaxants changed so that must be helping. The bad news is she now thinks she's ready to go and was up cleaning etc. It's real difficult not to act like the scolding parent. She knows she is supposed to take it easy for a few more weeks. But the guilt thing won't take a break.

Better news ... my FBG this morning was 77. yeehaw. I ate 3 meals yesterday. Granted lunch wasn't much more than a fat fest. I had 3 slices if braunschweiger, a bit of Danish soft cheese (omg it's like solidified cream) and a couple of tiny bites of cheese hubs found at Costco. Sausages and peppers & onions for dinner.

Called the doc's office to get my scrip changed to slow release cyclomet. I've been taking 1/2 a tab when I get up then 1/4 about 7 hours after that and another 1/4 before bed. I can feel it wear off and I start to slow back down. Oh yeah, my average temp yesterday was almost 97Â° ... yippeee!!!! At this rate I might actually get back in the studio and start melting glass again.

And speaking of that. (here's the reader's digest version) About 3 years ago I got caught up, along with a lot of other bead makers, in a nasty little mess created by some nut job. Without going into too many details she posted some vicious lies about us on the internet. A small group sued her to try and get the blog taken down. We ran out of money so had to drop the suit. A year after that (2 years ago) SHE sued about 30 of US making all kinds of bogus claims. We suspect she thought we'd never have the money or the guts to fight back and that she was about to get a near $3 million payday. 4 of the people got released from the suit but the rest of us were stuck because we had sued her and thus put ourselves under the jurisdiction of that particular state's courts. bah humbug.

TWO LONG YEARS, over $100,000 later (we did a lot of the equivalent of bake sales to raise funds) we were awarded NO EVIDENCE summary judgements. In two years she never presented ANY evidence to back up her snark filled claims. Why? Well first of all she had no evidence. What she did have was gall in that she was demanding that we turn over all our tax returns for a 10 year period so she could prove her "case." The mere fact that our tax returns had nothing to do with her lawsuit never entered her head. We said no way and the judge backed us up.

Her suit was a pack of lies from start to finish. Along the way her lawyer quit on her. So in March the judge put the hammer down. She filed an appeal with the circuit court of appeals. Two days ago THEY put the hammer down. so that is no longer hanging over my head which is further incentive to get back to work.

She did succeed in one thing though ... she broke the creative spirit in most of us for nearly 3 years. There's a lot of private celebrating going on in our little corner of the 'net today. And I'm not the only one contemplating dusting off the ol' torch.

HOT stuff. comin' through.

### ealachan — 2012-10-20T18:51:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-59356>

I've always, always wanted to learn to do glass-melty stuff. I hope you dust off the torch and make some super cool stuff.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-20T19:03:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-59359>

Darlene while you are getting to optimal and reclaiming your own reward tracts in your head you can see between your words you are beginning to re tap the giant pharmacy in your head. Your dopamine surge will be back soon and so will your creativity. It will be like when you were a teenager again. When your brain is approaching optimal you just ponder things and act. Why? If you just think and do not act what do you wind up with in life? As teenager's we didn't take more chances because they suddenly downgraded the risk they put us at. We took those risks because we gave more weight to the payoff. What would happen if you did this again in midlife? Darleen seems to ready to find out. I bet this ride is going to rock.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-20T21:03:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-59375>

> sez Jack: I bet this ride is going to rock.

Well I hope so cause right now it's mainly BUMPY.

OK... This is one of those TMI moments I am evidently becoming famous for.

I have a major crush on a [Minnesota Viking](http://http://www.nytimes.com/2012/10/20/sports/football/punter-chris-kluwes-voice-is-heard-in-same-sex-marriage-debate.html?_r=1&ref=sports). The "crush" thing is way beyond embarrassing because he's half my age and married with 2 little kids. Can't help it. I have this thing for really really smart guys. Plus he rocks (literally), is a computer game geek AND has an absolute WAY with words (that perfect score on the SAT verbals IS impressive). If you didn't read the original letter/rant that catapulted him to national fame you'll find the link to it in the above linked article. It is a major jaw dropping polemic.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-21T20:22:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-59498>

too much salt. My eyes are so puffy today I can barely open them. BUT the good news is my FBG was 76 this morning. Pays to eat, even tiny meals.

Yesterday:

B was the usual

L was packet of sardines (had to fight with the cat over who got 'em), a tiny bit of cheese, some sweet potato chips and a couple of squares of dark chocolate.

D was clam chowdah --yum

Feeling really tired this morning. Cats had their usual wrestling match last night and I wound up not really falling asleep until after midnight. I know I dozed prior but it wasn't sleep/sleep. The little brats woke me up again at 5:30 so I got up, staggered around a few minutes then fell back into bed. Didn't wake up again until 9:30 so my day is just off to a rip roarin' start.

Looks like tuna salad for lunch and clam chowdah for din. I can live with that.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-21T22:28:48+0300

um.

It kinda wore off today. Now I feel like hiding. Maybe I'll just settle for wearing a brown paper bag over my head.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-22T02:30:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-59555>

My issues have issues.

I made a mistake.

It started because during my consult Jack called me on something. He thought I was "ok" with a situation in my life. Well that was partly true. More accurate to say I was resigned.

I should have known better.

Suffice to say I should have left that door closed. I had gotten used to it.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-23T17:08:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-59817>

Day 6 on the cyclomet. PA told me that she can't find a timed release version and that I'd have to go to the local compounding pharmacy. Don't get that one at all. If it hasn't been approved then how can the CP make it. so I'm stuck taking 1/2 a tab on awakening, 1/4 tab twice later in the day. Seems to be working ok but I sure miss that AWAKE feeling I got the day I took an entire tablet at one time.

Anyway. Am really bloated from all that salt I took for a couple of days. My eyes are still puffed up really badly and my weight is up 6 pounds. crap.

My FBG behaved for 3 whole days. It was under 80 until this morning. I had a feeling I hadn't eaten enough last night as I felt just a tiny twinge of hunger right about bedtime. I was right. 101 this morning. Temp when I got up was 95.3.

So there it is. My body matches my mood.

I pay about $25 for my sr T3 for a month. Not too pricey at all and you can ask for a filler that is good for you like a probiotic and not some chemical.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-23T20:50:13+0300

She can but then I have to pay out of pocket. I'm already paying $70 a month for BHRT and another $36 about every 6 weeks for the testosterone. My scrip for the regular cytomel costs me $8. I think I can continue to whack tabs into 3 pieces.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-24T20:45:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-60014>

I have to go run errands in a bit and am still not dressed! Woke up about 9:30 ... gotta love those overcast mornings.

Got up feeling and looking like the goodyear blimp. WHAT is with the puffy face?!!! I drank lots of water yesterday, too. I decided I need to go back to taking the whole tab of cyclomet instead of chopping it up. Maybe that's been part of it, I dunno. I'm also wondering if we don't need to do some testing like right now instead of waiting 3 months. I'll be talking to Deb (the PA) about it.

b.o.b. arrived yesterday.  My cat likes it. sheesh.

ANYhoo. I have been really in a bad place the last couple of days. Yesterday as I was driving to the massage therapist I realized something important. The last several years my libido has just gone missing. I thought it was related to (lack of) hormones but the BHRT I've been on for over a year now has not changed anything. Then I thought about the contretemps DH and I had a couple of days ago. And I realized ... She has gone back in hiding. So I spent most of yesterday arguing with myself about why She should get her ass back out here.

Without going into a lot of detail I do want to share something. Most adults who were abused as children will often tell you that when the abuse happened they would float away out of their body like a balloon on a string. Not me. I invented a tiny sound proof and light proof closet. I would go inside and shut the door and stay until it was safe enough to come back out. And that little cellar room is where She fled. It's safe there. No one can hurt Her or me. You can only take so much rejection before that room becomes a permanent home.

Ok that enough crap for now. gotta run.

oh yeah, FBG was 89 this morning. Down from yesterday's 101. I had the last of the clam chowdah for dinner and about 9ish realized I was HUNGRY again. So rather than face another SPIKE in the A.M. I had a couple of slices of braunschweiger and the last 2 bites of the Danish cheese.

### vkiernan — 2012-10-25T14:53:01+0300

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-25T19:31:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-60164>

I constantly get those two things confused. WHY would they name them so closely. Yes, vkiernan I'm on cytomel (syn. T3).

Janagram ... I'm 65 as well. The BHRT didn't bring Her out of hiding which the DO who originally prescribed them said would happen. The added testosterone (because mine is flatlined) didn't help either. My issue is my marriage. Can't fix it all by myself. But I CAN fix me. Since we don't have kids and I'll probably outlive him I need to make sure my health is absolutely optimal. I am NOT going to end up in the back room of some nursing home all alone.

Endless ... loss of estrogen I'm pretty sure can play havoc with your libido. Since I'm so FAT i've probably had enough to keep things alive but there's that other component that Janagram mentions ... the emotional side that we all need.

Honestly I am so broken I sometimes wonder how in hell I'm going to fix myself. But the liothyronine (syn T3) is helping. I think. I woke up AGAIN feeling and looking like the goodyear blimp. Maybe it means my adrenals are kicking back in. Won't know until I get my cortisol levels checked again and am not planning on that for another month or two.

Man I am getting tired of this. I haven't been puffy likes this ever.

yesterday's menu:

B -- the usual ground sirloin patty, bacon and BPDC

L -- sardines, couple slices of braunschweiger and half a handful of sweet tater chips (last of the bag), handful of raw almonds

D -- smoked pulled pork with a tiny bit of sauce, green beans

plenty of water.

Can't face the scale. Not yet. last time it was up 6 pounds. At least I didn't kick the scale when I saw the number.

Oh yeah. Sleep has continued to be wonderful. Unless there's some emotional upset going on. I swear I haven't slept like this since my 20's. I honestly close my eyes, roll over and it's morning. WOW. That is just the BEST.

### janagram — 2012-10-26T02:30:53+0300

### Endless — 2012-10-26T05:12:46+0300

### Inger — 2012-10-26T09:40:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-60276>

Darleen.. I am just suggesting this now, I know it might sound crazy. But I see you suffer and want to loose weight.

What if you try one week all raw? First one week and the check how you feel about it. I had a point in my life when I was feeling heavy, was tired, did not felt good. I tried the all raw thing and loosed fast and tiredness went away fast! I think it must have to do with BG series about our guts needing bacterias? In raw food they are present in cooked not. IDK but at least it worked and still works for me! I am not eating all raw anymore but mostly still. But if you want to get real fast results do 100%! I almost know, you will loose real fast!

You could make delicious raw salmon tartar, chevice, beef tartare, carpaccio.. salads, there are lots of nice thing to eat!

You know.. If I ate sausages and such I would swell up too... IDK I am just that broken I guess.

I very much understand if you do not want to try this out, because it is not very mainstream for sure. But if you one day would like to, I would be so happy to hear how it worked for you!

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-26T19:13:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-60334>

Inger, I appreciate the suggestions. Raw seafood is out of the question since we are completely landlocked and about 2000 miles from the ocean. We're also in a very small, isolated community so even though I CAN get "fresh" seafood and fish it isn't all that fresh and most of it has been previously frozen. Although I guess that wouldn't make a big difference. I'm still leery of doing raw when I don't know how long it's been since it was caught.

Raw meat? Well, oddly enough I was thinking about how DH's SIL and her daughter both love steak tartar. And I found myself thinking it actually sounded pretty good where before I just went Ewwwww. Maybe it's time to try it.

But I do have to say eating raw isn't going to fix my RT3 issue. At least I don't think it would. I'm still trying to make sure I eat ENOUGH. I know that sounds stupid given how overweight I am but it is a huge part of the problem. I can get up to 2000 calories but only if I eat a LOT of fat. My normal daily intake is well under 1800 and probably closer to 1500. I know. I've tracked it several times over the last year or so.

Jack .. short but sweet. and how sweet that sleep is.

Nothing new to report except that the puffy eyelid thing seems to have gone down a bit this morning. Hard to tell given how swollen they have been. I'm still feeling fatigued so am going to try adding in a tiny bit more of the cyclomet (syn T3 just in case I got that wrong AGAIN) about midday to see if that helps.

Am off to the pharmacist and running errands. Maybe they'll have some mussels at Albertsons again.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-26T23:18:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-60365>

Back from town. Forgot the pharmacist is in Florida. Checked my blood pressure at the grocery store. It was 126/60 (I'm pretty sure that bottom number is correct. KNow for sure the top one is). Pulse 80. So I'm not going to add in any extra T3 just yet. My temp, however, is another story. It was 96.8Â°. that's up but I really want to see it STAYING in the 97+ range not dropping back down to 95.

Breakfast: the usual

midday ... just had the rest of the salmon salad I made yesterday and most of 1/2 an unripe avocado. DAMN I hate crunchy avocados. A few (less than a handful) sweet potato chips and a handful of raw almonds for dessert.

dinner ... haven't a clue. Am thinking shrimp scampi.

### Endless — 2012-10-26T23:19:56+0300

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-27T19:14:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-60455>

I'm landlocked as well, and stumbled thru wallyworld on a hunt for socks for a Halloween costume,and lo and behold, frozen mussels and scallops. I just had scallops for breakfast. How incredibly indulgent is that!? Next up is scallop ceviche.

Ad yes, beef tartar is good...I made it for a while when I could get good burger. Mix in an egg yolk, some salt and Pepper, garlic, onion, and wrap in a romaine leaf or yellow bell pepper.

No real help with the puff, other than this...I had low aldosterone AND low potassium. Supposedly, the two can't exist together (or so several endos have told me). What helped my poof was potassium chloride, aka nuSalt, in juice of some kind. Red cabbage juice is good, tasty, even, (I have been avoiding fruit juice for some time). DO NOT just put it in water. You WILL regret it. It tastes horrendous.

I needed to use between 1/4 and 1/2 tsp, am and pm, and got to where I could feel whether I needed sodium or potassium. Not sure that that is your problem, but it may be something to look at. (FWIW, my potassium labs were usually low but in range...3.9-4.1ish while sodium ranged from 135-141) low aldosterone is pretty common with low thyroid, and in my case at least, it resolved itself after a while.

Just another variable to consider, I hope it helps. Thyroid stuff is a beggar to sort out, but keep at it. You will get there.

One more thing...have you tried EFT for the emotional stuff? Sounds very woowoo I know, but it does seem to help. I shied away from it because of mercola...and he's a snake oil salesman in the first degree I think, but I've heard too much anecdotally for it to be mere placebo.

{hugs} been down a similar path with the thyroid stuff, and it's hard. As my friend Sam would say, chin up and tits out, girly, fake it til you feel it

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-27T20:26:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-60458>

LOL, great advice especially that last bit.

Yes, I know about tapping. As my once-in-a-while therapist explained to me it is NOT woo-woo stuff. It has a basis in neurobiology/chemistry.

I'm going to explain this badly because she gave me a quick recap and that's all the further it went. When you have a traumatic event it does not get stored properly and the memory will then loop on you (keeps replaying). By using tapping while simultaneously evoking the emotions from that event, you interrupt the loop and allow that memory to be stored thus breaking the loop permanently. Evidently some well respected Harvard(?) professor has studied it extensively and has the research to back this up. I think it's fascinating stuff but my poor worn out brain just isn't up to the task of hunting down and reading all the info.

I have some potassium tabs so I'll try on of those and see if it helps. I was puffy again this morning but not so bad as before.

Got hooked up with an RT3 group on yahoo. Have printed out a couple of things to give to my PA. From what I've read so far it can take up to 12 weeks to clear the RT3 from your system which is about the timeline the PA gave me for my thyroid to respond to the medication.

In looking at my last labs from June I see that then my NA was 140 (range of 136-145mMol/L) and K was 4.0 (range of 3.5-5.1 mMol/L) so everyone could be right in that my K is now a bit low.

Last nite's dinner was smoked pulled pork again from costco and some chocolate cream cheese. It wasn't enough. About 8pm I got what felt like an upset stomach but it turned out to be hunger. I ate 4 small slices (about an ounce) of smoked cheddar and it went away. Which tells me I am NOT eating enough, still.

This part is difficult for me because I am used to just not eating. My entire adult life has been spent shying away from food because of the message I internalized in my teens and from poor nutritional habits in my childhood. Food is the enemy. To be slim you have to NOT eat. I've been aware of this for some time and have been actively working to overcome it. This RT3 therapy feels like it may be the way to end that nonsense once and for all.

I'm also having issues again with digesting fats. I'm getting nauseated after BAB and that meal is the one where I get the most fat so I'm thinking it's time to dust off the HCL again.

Other than that the sun is shining YAY!!! and I"m hopeful.

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-27T21:39:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-60462>

Well, it's good to know that it does have basis in act. Seems too simple.

Hey, have you ever made your coconut creamer and had it separate? I used a can of co milk, a tb of co oil, and a justbeenlaid egg, and it separated into this watery stuff on the bottom, and thickish coconut stuff on top. No idea where I went wrong...any thoughts?

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-27T23:07:34+0300

Sorry I don't use the coconut creamer. I"m using BUTTER.  Like 1/2 a stick in 2 cups of coffee. But only once a day. That's all I want. mmmmmmmm good.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-28T18:19:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-60593>

Overcast again this morning. I want to go back to bed. I'd never survive in Seattle. I need sunlight.

So my brain is sluggish, my body is sluggish ... having trouble formulating thoughts this AM so responses to a couple of threads elsewhere on the forum have to wait.

I guess I'll go hop in the shower which these days has gone from cool/cold to tepid/warm because it's like 65 in the house and hubby is complaining big time. I LIKE it cooler. 65 isn't all that cold either.

OTOH maybe I'll go back to bed.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-28T19:20:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-60601>

I opted for a warm to cold shower instead of going back to bed. Got dressed and decided to walk out to the mailbox. Now that may not sound like much but our house sits back from the road by a good 150-200 yards. That's longer than a football field. the sun decided to come out, it's cold but not unbearably so. I feel much better.

Last night I made up some data sheets to track stuff while I"m on the cytomel. I'll be showing them to the PA and then giving them the filled out ones for my file if she wants them. I just want her to see how seriously I'm taking this because as far as I can see it is my last hope for ditching all this excess flab I've been lugging around for 25-20 years.

Confession time. I am not doing CT. And now that's it's cold I'm having even more trouble trying to motivate myself to do it. My big excuse right now is my basal temp is under 97. I'm ALREADY COLD. Then the voice of reason chimes in with "but if you DO the CT it should light your internal furnace and MAKE YOU WARMER IDIOT."

Boy the way I talk to myself about myself.

Tap water is still 55Â°. And I'm still too fat to fit in the tub comfortably. So why don't I make myself do the ice on the belly thing? wah wah baby pants. It's coooold and drippy and boring. judas aitch

ok I guess I'll just claim "lazy" and stop beating up on myself. toodles.

### Lyndra — 2012-10-28T20:27:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-60614>

I'm with you about the sun - I feel better and my mood is brighter when the sun is out. Tourism folks here claim we have 300+ days of sunshine per year but it hasn't felt like that this year. Maybe I'm just more sensitive or aware. I want my morning sun and feel deprived when I don't get it.

Good luck with the CT. I have been doing cold showers for the last 6 weeks and they are always easier than I anticipate they will be. I'll be re-testing hsCRP & thyroid in the next week or two (before I get back in the cold tub) so I have a good baseline measurement to see what effect CT has.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-28T21:45:14+0200

My hsCRP is way low. It's my RT3 that is the issue. I'm hoping that once I get that cleared up then all bets are off!

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-28T23:10:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-60640>

brrrrrrrrrrrrr Just did 30 minutes or so with 20#'s of ice on my belly. Skin temp 67... that noise you hear is my teeth chattering. body temp 96.2 ... so it went down.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-29T18:38:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-60739>

GAH!!! Woke up at 8:15 and took 1/2 a tab of cytomel. Went back to bed for about 30 minutes cause I thought I could go back to sleep. Didn't get breakfast in until 9:30 so now I'm vibrating like I've had 3 cups of regular coffee. eeeeeeeeee

Eyes still puffy this morning. Need to get my BHRT from the CP so will talk to him (if he has a minute) about what that might mean.

Headed out to Billings tomorrow for another Costco run. This time I'm getting seafood.

Oh yeah, finally got to talk with my sister via Skype yesterday. Glad I decided to just leave it running in the hopes she'd finally get on the computer ... my nephew gave her some great news. He's living and working in London. His supervisors told him last week that they have noted his work ethic and the great job he has been doing and are recommending a very substantial raise for him. He has a masters in computer programming. And he's been working his backside off for months. YAY! Had he stayed here in the States he would have been given a new job title and told to work more hours. Glad he went to London even though it's hard on my sister to have him so far away. He IS coming home for xmas so that'll be good.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-29T19:44:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-60756>

jitters have now abated. I think it might be my new vitamins. They're the Andrew Lessman complete and are micronized powder in capsules instead of pressed tabs so they're easier on the stomach. I think they just gave me a WHACK upside the head.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-29T23:17:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-60799>

Jitters came back. Went to get my BHRT and talked to the pharm... they did a quick muscle test on me and decided it was my parathyroid and sent me home with DSF formula which contains a few vitamins & minerals and

adrenal (bovine) 25mg

thymus (bovine) 70mg

spleen (bovine) 20mg

stomach (bovine) 10mg

parotid (bovine) 80 mcg

grape seed extract 1mg

lemon bioflavonoids 225mg

L-tyrosine 175mg

chlorella 50mg

(shrug)

So I'm not taking any more cytomel today.

Will skip the first dose tomorrow and then take 1/4 tab for the next 3 doses as usual and see how I do.

I don't know if the whole muscle testing thing is so much woo-woo or not. If this vibration thing doesn't quit I'll be going back to the doc later in the week. I'd do it tomorrow but we're going to Billings.

The weather is supposed to be really nice and this time of year you have to start dodging snow storms. 130 miles of nothing but empty miles and miles gets a bit tedious when you're driving on black ice or can't find the road for the snow.

In vino veritas. And all that.

### Kiera2 — 2012-10-31T05:21:15+0200

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-31T18:28:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-61106>

No Coriander, it's my turtle neck. And I wasn't cold I was just messing around.

On the personal front I seem to have gone from hypo- to hyperthyroid in the blink of an eye. Monday I noticed that when I took my first dose of cytomel (1/2 tab), about an hour later I got all vibrate-y (that's a step up from trembly). It went away after a couple of hours. I took the 2nd dose (1/4 tab) about 4 hours after the first and an hour after that got all vibrate-y again. 2 hours later it stopped. So I stopped the cytomel.

The last 3 or 4 nights I have had great difficulty falling asleep. I just lie there, eyes wide open unable to drift off for about 2 to 3 hours.

Next clue. Normally when I drink that much wine (2 glasses) I'm under the table and sound asleep. not last night. And I was exhausted from the drive to and from Billings (a little over 2 hours one way) and the trip around Costco. Drank the wine, got giddy and giggly and by bedtime was sober again. WTF? Where'd my buzz go?

got up several times before I finally fell asleep (which was like getting hit by freight train) because my heart gave several loud thumps (the joy of sleeping on an air mattress is every little sound is amplified if you sleep on your side like I do). My pulse, which is usually pretty low had jumped to about 92 but it quickly fell back to around 80. That happened twice.

Woke up at 9:30 and was vibrating like crazy. Got up and couldn't get that BAB fixed FAST enough. We'll see how long before I get hungry again because the last several days I'm looking for something else to eat within a couple of hours. Wasn't paying attention to that too much until now.

Now if only the FLAB will go away.

Boy am I warm, too. Wonder what my temp is ... ok 97.6. That's up from the usual low 96, high 95.

On another subject, the Costco trip was a bit of a disappointment because the seafood was just the usual salmon, shrimp, lobster tails and crab legs. They did have HUGE containers of fresh oysters but I'm not ready to do the small ones yet. So I bought 4 big cans of sea clams, more pink salmon and a couple of nice sized chuck roasts, one of which is awaiting the oven for tonight's dinner.

I'm out of sardines so it's off to the store to grab more and veggies for the roast. May stop over at Albertson's to see if they have any mussels. I honestly think I could eat those about every other day with no problem.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-31T21:35:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-61146>

Still vibrating and going about 60mph. It's like being on an entire pot of coffee. me no like.

### Endless — 2012-11-01T04:51:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-61211>

I don't blame you...is that a normal part of taking T3? I hope not as I will most likely be on it soon and I already have palpitations....that kind of stuff would give me a panic attack for sure.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-01T05:31:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-61217>

Endless, I'm not sure what happened. I've been trying to get into a schedule only to find on the STTM website that isn't necessarily a good approach. And it also appears that the buzz has finally worn off. So starting tomorrow I'm going to take a single dose of 1/4 tab when I get up and then wait to see how I feel. I'm only going to take more if I feel like I need it.

It may just be that I wound up taking too much too fast. Even though I never exceeded the one tab per day dose. Anyway, I'm back to feeling like I usually do and I don't like it because even though I was jittery as all get out earlier, my mind actually felt like it was working the way it used to. THAT part I loved.

Food report...

BAB this morning as fast as I could chow it down. REALLY needed the food.

Lunch was a can of salmon, drained and dumped on a plate with a squeeze of fresh lemon and big spoonful of roe on top. yummy. Ate almost all of it, too. And it stopped the jitters.

Ate the rest of the salmon pre dinner. dinner was a small bit of steak ... thought about eating the whole thing but decided to be nice and saved 1/2 for hubby's dinner. Wed is his night with the boyz down at the Legion so he usually doesn't get home until about 7:30. Or I should say when I BRING him home. I finally convinced him that on Wed I should play designated chauffeur so he doesn't risk getting stopped. I eat when I get back from taking him to town.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-01T18:58:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-61318>

Well my thyroid high has dumped me off a cliff and I'm back to 'normal.' ugh. took 1/4 tab this morning and that's all I will take today. I'm going to feel my way along for a bit. But right now my energy is about -1. Gotta run back to town to get mushrooms. sigh. Making a list is useless when you forget to put something on it, or worse yet, leave the damned thing on the counter.

I know I forgot somethings else because I remembered it a bit ago but have since forgotten it again. I liked myself better yesterday when my brain was firing on all 12 cylinders.

Overcast again this morning which doesn't help. Maybe a coolish shower will wake me up. Oh yeah, I fell asleep last night like I'd been hit on the head. Woke up at 3:30 and lay there for about 2 hours before I conked out again. Then woke up at 9:30. Where'd my wonderful sleep go? COME BACK!

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-01T22:09:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-61396>

Okeydokey. The chuck roast is in the slow cooker, a la Jack's boeuf bourguignon recipe. I was going to use the rest of the Aussie wine I had but evidently DH drank it when I wasn't looking so had to use most of the rest of the large bottle of Malbec which \*I\* have been drinking. Not sure how it's going to turn out because the Malbec isn't anywhere near as hearty as a burgundy.

ANNNNND on the thyroid front my T3 buzz finally showed up but is already fading. No more until tomorrow so I guess I'll be slipping back into my near comatose state. bah.

Since breakfast was really late I'm having a mug of BPDC for now because dinner should be done around 6-ish (slow cooker on high).

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-02T19:41:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-61581>

The boeuf was wonderful. The wine gave it just enough flavor but didn't overpower everything.

Took 1/4 tab of cytomel this morning and am now having just a tiny amount of tremblies going on. My temperature, OTOH, is back down in the basement at 95.6Â°! CRAP. ON TOAST. So now I'm thinking I was taking too much cytomel for my poor tired adrenals. For now, it looks like minimum dose daily of about 6mcg of the cytomel until the trembling stops and/or my temp goes up a little.

I wasn't going to take any at all today but was draggin' my backside around after I got up. Yesterday it took about 3 hours before it really kicked in and then I was able to actually DO stuff most of the day without collapsing into a heap. So I guess it's a good thing even though it isn't even a baby step.

Not sure what's for dinner tonight. I'd haul that sea bass outta the freezer but DH makes that _FACE_ whenever I serve fish. Maybe he can do leftover roast and I'll eat some tilapia.

Still had a little trouble falling asleep last night but then slept like the dead until 6:30.

My life is just so dull compared to everyone else in here. The joys of living out in the country in the least inhabited state in the Union and having NO social life whatsoever. Most excitement we had around here was 3 years ago when the mountain lion showed up on our deck looking to dispatch Three to the great beyond. Oh well.

Nothing new to report. Just a little trouble falling asleep last night but, again, slept like the dead until about 5:30 when the cats decided to wake me up.

Now Three is flopped across my lap making it extremely difficult to type so this will be really short.

1/4 tab of cytomel again today. I noticed a tiny amount of trembling yesterday so I think that's a good sign (that it's diminishing). Will stay on this dose for a few more days then add another 1/4 tab and see how I do.

Thanks, me too. they haven't shown up at all today so I'm thinking I just dosed too fast, too high on the cytomel. It took 3 weeks to finally slam me against the wall but I've learned my lesson. Easy does it. I am just so hopeful that THIS is the piece I've hunted for for 25+ years. Having been doing the leptin rx for about a year I'm hoping that when the T3 finally comes back online and the RT3 gets under control that the extra poundage will GO AWAY.

And with that I'm off to nosh on sardines for lunch.

stupid FBG was 117 this morning. WTF?! Let's see, small can of sardines for lunch yesterday. check. Baked pork chop and broccoli for dinner. check. small handful of raw almonds for dessert. check.

Am I STILL not eating enough? What the heck?!

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-05T19:31:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-62220>

I get so bored posting the same crap day after day. Not much changes so I can only hope that things are going on under the hood that will eventually cause my motor to turn over and my life to get back on track. (love those mixed metaphors)

Am halfway through Jack's latest blog post. Either his writing is improving or my brain is working better because I actually understand almost all of it. So far. I haven't hit the geek bit yet.

I suspect I am far more broken in that [area](http://http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-16-adrenal-fatigue-rx/#comments) than I realize. My temp goes DOWN when I do CT of any sort. I'm pretty sure that's not the way it's supposed to work.

I'm thinking of trying ealachan's BAB for a bit because the beef just itsn't keeping me going like it once did. I'm becoming OK with canned salmon so why not try it for breakfast with some bacon & eggs. Couldn't hurt.

Am really tired this morning. Don't know why because I slept really well again last night. Took my 1/4 tab of cyclomet but so far it hasn't really kicked in.

Got bad news saturday. My Doc is closing his Sheridan office but I can always drive all the way over to Gillette to see him. Something about the hospital and I don't care about those politics. DAMN. I want to go kick him in the butt. This is the 2nd time he's done this. And the last time he'll do it to me. Which reminds me I need to call his office and find out what to do about my records and where his PA is headed. Chris was at least willing to work with me on most stuff. Now I have to start all over. AGAIN.

Oh and that trip to Gillette? It may only be 90 miles but it's 90 miles of literally empty space and in the winter I don't leave town because I've spent too many hours slipping around out on the lonely interstate on black ice and wondering if I would ever get home. EMPTY SPACE. As in no inhabitants for miles and miles except for deer, antelope and assorted small critters. grrrrrrrr

Now that I've put myself into a bad mood I guess I'll go stand in a cold shower for a bit and see how long it takes for sticky-outy parts to turn blue.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-06T18:58:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-62378>

Ok I'm back.

BAB ... bacon, ground beef patty, mug of BPDC

lunch ... probably salmon with lemon juice and pepper and maybe a few sweet potato chips

dinner ... probably roast beast of some sort.

CT ... not yet. In about 30 minutes plan on filling the tub and whining a lot

FBG 98

7 drops of iodine/potassium iodide (hereinafter referred to simply as "I" ... with 3 drops = 150mcg I) with 1/2 tsp celtic sea salt in a big glass of water

1/4 tab cytomel

selenium

Waiting to take vitamins and assorted other supps with lunch and the remainder at dinner. Picked up maca, chlorella, the selenium, more silymarin (which also has curcumin with it) and some liquid astragalus. ouch. There goes the food budget for the week.

So now my sister and I are trying to visit and skype keeps cutting in and out on her end. Gotta run.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-06T20:30:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-62398>

Well shoot. She does have to keep reminding me of stuff I'd rather FORGET. In brief ... our Mother died in a car accident when I was 13 and my sis was 11. We were adopted by our Mom's aunt and HER psychotic husband (Bob ... I try real hard NOT to hate the very name itself).

Sooooo. going over possible brain injuries we both suffered, here's my list:

1\. I was 7? maybe 8? playing football with my two cousins, one just a year older than me the other a good 7 years older. The older one tackled me midsection and stood up. The momentum carried me over his shoulder and I landed SMACK on the top of my head and then onto my back. I remember getting up, screaming as I ran but blacking out as I got to the hedge. I evidently kept running, tripped and fell and hit my chin on the cement cistern near the door. When my Mom found me I was convulsing in the gravel.

2\. (thanks for reminding me. NOT) Bob used to shove us up against the hallway and scream at us and bang out heads against the wall. He pounded my head into the wall so hard one time I nearly blacked out.

3\. Car accident my senior year at CSU. The moron who was taking me home was DRUNK which I hadn't realized as he was talking and acting perfectly normal. He slammed his brand new corvette into a light pole doing about 70mph from a standing stop. I had my seat belt on (no shoulder belt at that time) and was thrown forward (big surprise) then as I was thrown back my head was slammed into the passenger door window. thank god the car had the brand new breakaway engine mounts or I would have lost my legs.

fun times.

So I can use those incidents as an excuse as to why my brain is fracked, right? didn't think so.

No haven't CT'd yet. Was yakking with my sister being reminded of all that crap. So now I'm drinking another mug of BPDC and thinking as soon as it's done I'll chug the glass of ice water sitting here on my desk and go fill up the tub.

yeah. unhuh.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-07T18:33:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-62578>

Cavemam I wish I could help. The closest I get is those cold showers and right now they are DARNED cold. I start slightly warm then keep turning the temp down until it's almost all cold. Then I make myself stand there for at least 5 minutes. I keep telling myself that I can do the tub every other day. And then I chicken out. So maybe I'll make myself use that 20 pounds of ice in the freezer and just do my bare belly.

Fish? I eat it every day now for lunch. Either sardines or a can of salmon. I got the pink salmon from Costco. Pricy but yummy. I squeeze a 1/4 of a lemon over it, add some pepper and yum. I'm out of roe ... put a thin layer of that on top of the salmon and even better. Adds a nice bit of salt.

Stayed up way too late last night waiting to hear the President's acceptance speech but just couldn't make it. Finally gave in a little past 11 and zonked right out. Slept until about 7 then managed to get up about 45 minutes later. Plenty of bright sunlight streaming in the windows this morning (and if DH doesn't stop watering them in the summer with dirty creek water I'm going to smack him -- guess what I'll be doing later today) so got some of that going.

FBG 89

1/4 tab of cytomel about 8:30

10 drops of I (equals 750 mcg if I'm doing the arithmetic correctly. Some mornings my brain just refuses to work.) with 1/2 tsp celtic sea salt. Still drinking that.

**BAB the usual. Today's mug of BPDC made with that yummy Irish butter. They had it at Costco so I bought two packages. sigh. I really want to go back to Ireland. What a beautiful beautiful place.

**Lunch** ... most likely salmon or sardines. It's easy and I don't have to think about it.

dinner** ... aye there's the rub. THAT I do have to think about. Requires planning and forethought something I have been incapable of doing for a while now. My brain is just so sluggish. Plus it's DH's night at the legion so I'll be fixing two meals. But at least I get a little alone time while he's down there solving the world's problems with the rest of the guys.

Gotta do the bills today ... outer limits of putting it off so whether my brain is ready or not ... time to get 'er done.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-07T18:40:17+0200
