good morning sunshine!

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-07T19:55:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-62609>

In reading through some conversations this morning I found myself feeling vaguely uncomfortable at what I found. The more I thought about it the more I thought it was time to really "out" myself.

I was born, grew up, and educated in Colorado. Fort Collins is my home town, now and forever even though it has changed to something I no longer even recognize and loathe intensely. I graduated from both FCHS and CSU with stints at Loretto Heights College back when it was still women only and not owned by Regis, and Aims Community College in Greeley. I have spent half my life living in Wyoming. Both are extremely red states although I have hopes that my home state is starting to see the light.

When it comes to spending my tax dollars I am unrepentantly conservative. When Congress is spending my hard earned tax dollars I expect a LOT of accountability.

I am, OTHO, socially liberal. I don't think the government has any business telling me how I may or may not deal with my body, who I may or may not marry and whether or not I can smoke a little weed or drink a little wine. None. Of. Their. Business. And just as the government has no business going into anyone's church, synagogue or mosque, those same places have no business meddling in the government.

OTHO I believe firmly in government regulation of business and industry. When it comes to money people will do and say anything to make a buck. They need to be watched and corralled if necessary. Witness what has happened to health care costs since 1980 if you don't believe me.

I believe that we need to take care of our environment. It is NOT ours to do with as we please. We should be caretakers not users and abusers.

Most of the time I keep my mouth shut. Well, except here on the internets where I feel safe. It's a great mask. And yes, I know it makes me more than a bit of a geek. I accepted that a long time ago. Anyway. today I felt compelled to open up and show a bit more of who I really am behind the mask. Just a little peek. Maybe to help you understand more of who I am when I write in here and why I say the things I do.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-08T01:32:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-62755>

That was part of what prompted it. My adopted Grandmother was a recycler way back before anyone knew what that was. Anyone who lived through the depression was a recycler. You didn't just throw things out (which means they get buried in the ground somewhere outside of town) you used them again for something else. Nowadays it's also called repurposing. But it isn't anything new.

As an example, Grandma Dot would take empty tuna cans, wash them thoroughly, cut off the bottom and turn the metal hoop into a xmas decoration. She did a lot of stuff like that.

But it wasn't just the recycling thing. There were other conversations going on and I just decided to let people know where I'm coming from.

onward

Just got an email from hubby's niece who is preparing to have a double mastectomy tomorrow because she's had "2 pre-cancerious cells removed 1 from each beast, one not aggresive and one that was " and I'm freaking out because she doesn't understand what she is getting ready to do to her body. I'm trying to talk her into postponing the surgery until she can get more info (aka get her ass over here and start reading up on Jack's stuff and get onto BHRT like yesterday).

I don't know if she'll listen to me because I got the party line about the average woman having a 12% chance of "getting it" blah blah blah de blah blah. She's had enough crap in her life this is just not something she needs to add to the pile.

This is the hard part about being out here on the bleeding edge with Jack. I'm not a surgeon, don't have a medical degree and can't talk to any of my loved ones with any kind of authority on the subject of optimal health. OTOH even if I were a surgeon they'd probably ignore me. Nobody can diss you like family.

shoot.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-08T18:08:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-62913>

> **Jack Kruse;64502 wrote:** I just thought of Roger Daultry screaming his ballad to Darleen.......Who the f#$% are you...............from the Who's next album! Now we know

An apt quote, Jack. In order to _change your thinking_ you first have to know who you are. You have talked at length about how changing your thinking can change your DNA. But if you don't really know who you are how can you effect any kind of change?

Childhood shapes you and your attitudes and beliefs define you. That is your road map. How can you reach a destination if you don't have a clue where you are at the start?

Yes, I'm big on self-exploration. The study of human behavior is endlessly fascinating. And here is a little bit of what I learned during my long stint on the couch. What I learned of my own behavior I could see reflected in society and humanity as a whole.

Micro Macro

I read an interesting book about 15 years ago entitled [The Holographic Universe](http://http://). Did you know (most of you probably do) that if you have a holographic image and you cut off the tiniest corner, all the information contained in the original is maintained in the tiny piece as well as the larger piece. Nothing is lost. So considering the universe as a hologram you find all the information contained within the universe also contained in every discrete "piece." That would be basically all matter. That would be you. And me. And the cat and the dog. And on and on.

I love the idea that quantum physics and Eastern philosophies tend to blur together at the outer edges. Talk about woo-woo stuff.

So by letting you peek behind the curtain, as it were, and get a glimpse of who I am you will, hopefully, begin to see where my road map leads. Optimal is off on the distant horizon but like the first glimmers of dawn I can see it. I just can't see the road quite yet.

Jack is slowing leading us all with bread crumbs but until I can shed some light on the path I can't find them. Does that make any sense?

So here's another piece of who I am. And please make The Who stop screaming in the back of my brain.

My childhood was spent in poverty. My Mother had to work before she divorced my dad because he wouldn't. Women didn't make much back in the early '50s. And he blew through everything before she even got it. After she divorced him we moved in with her parents. My grandparents were dirt poor. That's another story that shaped who I am but it's for another post.

I wore hand-me-downs from the neighbor kid who was in my class at school. I was the youngest in my class and also the smallest until about 7th grade. My sister remembers this with shame because she remembers being teased unmercifully about it by school mates. I deny.

My grandmother worked as a cook at sororities and fraternities around town. My sister (with her blasted good memory) remembers how we ate the plate scrapings she brought home. I deny.

See, I can't even find the way out of my own past. So when I tell you that I live in my own little world? I'm not kidding.

This, too, is part of who I am.

### Inger — 2012-11-09T13:16:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-63097>

Darleen.. that all sounds quite painful... I am so sorry.

> So by letting you peek behind the curtain, as it were, and get a glimpse of who I am you will, hopefully, begin to see where my road map leads. Optimal is off on the distant horizon but like the first glimmers of dawn I can see it. I just can't see the road quite yet.

This was so beautiful to read...

Look, that you can see the first glimmer of dawn is all that matters!!! Because that is when you can see the direction you are heading in Darleen!!! That is all that matter me thinks.. I cannot see the road eather at times, it is darn black.. but I know the direction for sure. And that makes me never despair.

We just need to be patient, the answers will all come to us when we just remind open. Or.. they are already there, we just need to see them. But we will. I know this Darleen because often I could see nothing.. but then all of a sudden I could...?!

The beautiful thing about seeing the glimmer of dawn is, you can not ever give up anymore. Because the glimmer is too beautiful.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-09T14:17:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-63107>

Darleen no matter what we lived through and what we remember (your sister) and forget (you) the power to alter your reality is within the atoms that make up your neurons.......You can change your reality with thoughts. A thought is an endocrine secretion for neural circuits that alters its function. The more good and optimistic we think the more success we begin to find. This is call optimism bias of the neural circuit.

Whatever happens in your life, whether you succeed or you fail, people with high expectations and optimism always feel better, because how we are designed to perceive feelings — when we get dumped upon or we win employee of the month — depends on how we interpret that event. That is an EPIGENETIC action.

We have that control if you teach ourselves to use it. Some of us never get that message. The path to truly new, never-been-done-before things always has failure built into it along the way. We can never understand good without sensing the bad. You and your sister have sensed more bad than good. It is time to think all good thoughts.......to balance your circuits. For every bad thing you must force yourself to think optimistically. Then your life will change, I promise.

We often don't know who we are until you see yourself in interaction — with people that are close to us, people that are enemies of ours, people that are agnostic to us. A traumatic childhood event doesn't doom us to suffer indefinitely, unless we believe it. Instead, we can use it as a springboard to unleash our best qualities and lead happier lives. That is precisely what I did. My sister did 180 degrees the opposite. She never got the message I am sharing with you here.

The only difference in us........was how we saw the events as kids. I refuse to fail. She cant help but fail. This ability is all built into our neural circuits. Humans evolved the attributes of a large brain and the ability to speak, in order to form intricate social networks that can use many aspects of technology. **Our biggest attribute is the ability to think.** This allows us to radically change the environment that we are ideally adapted to and how we think about things that happen to us. It has allowed us to dominate all habitats and create havoc in most of them as well. You have felt some of that havoc for sure. The real human miracle of our minds is not that we can see the world as it is……but that we can see it as it is not, and then change it forever and create a reality that we want. If we think and act incorrectly, we can quickly recalibrate and overcome it.........if we allow it to happen.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-10T04:36:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-63288>

Where I was and where I'm going. I hope this makes sense because it's a couple of bits from my private journal.

The following is a dream I had during the first years I was in therapy. Try to hold the freudian commentary down to a dull roar please. Believe me I see the significance of a lot of the elements in this dream.

_I dreamed I was at a raucous party in a room filled with people. At some point someone came rushing in, breathless and excited because they had found X but needed someone to go rescue them. For some reason I wound up volunteering (maybe because in the dream I felt that X was a dear friend of mine). Anyway. I was escorted, with some ceremony, over to one of the walls where there was a circular hatch, like on a submarine. The hatch was opened and revealed a pitch black tube. When I looked over the edge of the hatch I saw rungs attached to the wall leading down into the darkness.

I clambered through the hatch and onto the ladder as everyone wished me well. I began the downward climb and the open hatch with itâ€™s bright circle of light slowly disappeared from view. At about the same time I became aware of the barest hint of light below me. Emboldened I kept moving downward.

Eventually I reached the bottom and as I walked out and away from the tube I had just climbed down I found myself on a busy street crowded with people going about their daily business. I spied a sidewalk cafe and decided to rest a bit. I was so absorbed in my new surroundings I lost track of time. Then I remembered my â€œmissionâ€ and got back on track.

(I love the way you can jump around in dreams. Why bother with the boring part of travel when you can just GET there by simply thinking about your destination.)

Now Iâ€™m out in the country, surrounded by rolling, grass covered hills. I have two shadow companions with me and we are observing a house situated on the crest of a distant hill. We know there are soldiers patrolling the area and that we have to be very careful not to get caught or the rescue will not succeed. We creep ever closer to the house.

Now weâ€™re nearly there and I bolt for the house. My companions have disappeared and I am alone again. I wander into the kitchen where I open the refrigerator. Inside there is an old, aluminum ice cube tray. I pull it out and discover to my total delight a KEY frozen in one of the compartments. I am overjoyed. I can now release my friend!

I take the key and dash outside. I move around the house until I find what I am looking for. There it is. Doors into the cellar. But wait! Theyâ€™re already open. Whatâ€™s going on? I approach the opening and, bending down to see better, look into the cellar. I see a wall on the right and path across the room which makes a right angle turn to the right. I can see light coming from there.

I move down into the cellar, knowing I am about to free my friend. I walk down to the corner and turn to my right. And there, sitting with head bowed down and in their hands, is

**ME**

I am furious.

â€œWhy are you sitting there!â€ I all but shout. â€œThe doorâ€™s NOT LOCKED!â€

_

God I love my subconscious. Go ahead, smack me with the sledge hammer again. It just felt so damn good the first time.

Cellar as metaphor for my own personal dungeon. Not very original but it works. Thatâ€™s where I hid all the bad stuff over the years. Then came therapy and remembering and it emptied out. All the demons flew away with most of them destroyed in the light of day. The truth almost sets you free but what they donâ€™t tell you is there is always a pile of cleanup work to do. (cue Jack)

However, thereâ€™s still one resident of the cellar. Someone I am trying to let out.

(cue Rolling Stones â€œGimme Shelterâ€ â€˜cause as far as Iâ€™m concerned itâ€™s aural sex)

I admit to being girly. But Iâ€™m not one of those sylph-like woodland nymphs pirouetting across a flower-strewn glade and singing delicate melodies to the birds and butterflies. Naw. Not even close.

SHE is all tawny hair, emerald eyes, muscles rippling under the skin as she moves. SHE is fearless and bold. SHE knows who SHE is, what SHE wants and what her place in the world is. SHE doesnâ€™t walk, stroll or strut. SHE _prowls_. Her entire body throbs with every beat of her heart. SHE is as untamable as the wind and just as free. SHE takes what she wants, when she wants, accepting it as her due.

Frankly SHE scares the crap out of me.

And BTW â€¦ Raquel Welch? Really? She can eat her heart out.

bold enough for ya?

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-10T20:09:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-63397>

Well you can't say I never warned everyone.

Mundane post alert.

I've skipped the cytomel for 3 days now. Was shaky again yesterday morning and last night felt like (when I went to bed) my heart started to speed up a bit. I blame it on the iodine. Not sure where'd I'd put my energy level. It may have gone up by about 0.05.

Re: the iodine, some positive signs. I developed a cyst under my tongue earlier this year and it just sat there. since I started taking the iodine it has almost disappeared. Yay!

Yesterday I realized that I am running really warm first thing in the morning and that is when I really need to CT.

My breakfast yesterday was delayed because I had put a pork roast in the fridge to thaw (chili verde!) and had a real DUH! moment because I only put a wad of paper towels under it instead of putting it in a pan. You can imagine the mess. I had to dismantle 2/3 of the fridge to clean it out. An hour later I was sweating away and thinking NOW is when I should climb into a tub of cold water. It was shower day so I excused myself that way.

BUT. This morning I heated back up again since I fixed breakfast for 2. DH usually goes to town on Sat for breakfast with friends but it snowed yesterday and the wind was blowing and and and (candy ass ... hahaha but I don't blame him. All those years of getting called to work at all hours of the day and night and in hot weather and bouts of the Arctic Express) so he stayed home.

After breakfast I went right to the bathroom and filled the tub.

Starting water temp: 52.7Â°

Ending water temp: 55.4Â°

Starting oral temp: 98.2Â°!

Ending oral temp: 97.7Â° ???

20 minutes

skin temp after 62Â°

I didn't start shaking until I got out. Now I'm having a hard time typing. brrrrrr

Oh yeah, almost forgot. While doing the archeological dig in the fridge I found a jar of home canned fish a friend had sent home with hubby a while back (cough cough). I was going to toss it but figured I'd check the seal first. It was sealed tight. Opened it, sniffed it, took a bite ...YUMMMMMY trout! So we had a bit of that to go with the bacon and eggs I fixed this morning.

And now for your viewing enjoyment:

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/65268=547-nov2012.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/65268=548-nov2012-2.jpg)

Mundane alert

Had a really difficult time falling asleep last night. Don't know what was going on. I finally drifted off only to jerk awake about an hour later and then lay there for a good 90 minutes while my mind spun at about 1200rpm. woke with the light and couldn't get back to sleep because now the cat herd wanted attention and Three (little devil) proceeded to assault first Missy and then Sammy. Missy just screams at him, while Sammy pins him to the floor where they thump around and Three squeals.

Feel SO much better having taken just that first dose of D-ribose yesterday that I took 1/4 tab of cytomel this morning. We'll see how I fare.

Made the mistake of stepping on the scale ... forgot I've been downing 1/2 tsp of salt in a glass of water every morning and now am retaining fluid again. Up 2 pounds. In as many days. When will I learn.

Breakfast: usual

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-63837>

Well good morning to the spammer.

Slept like a rock. Getting ready to jump in the tub but need to finish my BPDC. Feeling fine this A.M. but the jitters started before I could get breakfast finished so gulped down some D ribose caps and then breakfast and am now just a wee bit trembly.

FBG 108

Cytomel: 1/4 tab

Breakfast, the usual

lunch: maybe salmon

dinner: chili verde

Monday is usually movie afternoon (we go to the 4:?? showing always ... few people so we get the theater mostly to ourselves and then DH can be home in time to watch .... FOOTBALL.  today's selection? Skyfall ... not only do I like the James Bond series but I happen to like Daniel Craig. As long as he keeps his mouth shut. I have little patience with people who achieve a level of popularity only to disparage the very people who put them there.

OK. Enough about me. How's YOUR day going so far?

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-12T20:30:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-63855>

CT update

30 minutes

water temp: start 52.8, finish 55

skin temp: start 91.7, finish 65

oral temp: start 96.5, finish 97.1

yay me.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-12T22:54:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-63867>

Back from running errands. It was 25 when I left the house, still shivering from the CT. Wore my boots, heavy sweatshirt, gloves, regular pants but no coat. Shivered all the way into town. Just before I got there I caved in and turned on the heat ... just to the midway point where it goes from cold to warm. turned it off a couple minutes later.

Shivered the whole time I was in town.

On the way home I realized I felt like I was going 120mph. Like everything had suddenly switched on and my motor finally turned over and caught ... whoo hooooooo. Man I felt so good I thought "I could run a marathon." and then that snarky little voice in the back of my head laughed at me and said "Yeah, you'd go 10 feet and have to take a nap!"

Well ok yeah. But I still feel like I'm roaring down the highway of life with the top down on the car and my hair blowing in the wind.

Man I had totally forgotten how good this feels, it's been so long.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-12T23:41:16+0200

I am so excited! It's probably a frankenduck because it's about 4 pounds but we're having it anyway. Can't wait to thaw it and cook it up.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-14T00:03:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-64130>

### Shijin13 — 2012-11-14T00:18:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-64135>

> **DarleenMB;66087 wrote:** I got one of those point & click thermometers at Home Depot. I don't know if it's super accurate but I'm not doing nuclear physics here just checking the bath temp. I aim it at my back before and after I get in/out of the tub. I discovered it needs to be pretty close to said object so I do try to keep that in mind.
>
>
>
> Ducky still in the freezer. I'm going to have to go search foodtv.com and probably Alton Brown's recipes because he's just got the best ones. IMNSHO.

I've got a recipe for duck...

Balinese spiced duck

8 portions of duck (butchered into breasts, thighs etc) fat/skin removed

1/4c dried coconut milk

1/4c coconut milk

salt/pepper

blemd the following together :

onion sliced

2 cloves garlic

ginger root peeled/sliced

1/2 tsp turmeric (I'd use more b/c its good for you!)

4 macadamia nuts

corriander seeds or cumin if you don't have seeds

red chilies (assuming you can tolerate night shades)

render fat/skin (reserve for later use)

toast dried coconut

spread spice paste over duck and let marinate for 3-4hrs

pre-heat oven to 235 shake off and save spice paste and transfer duck to oiled roasting pan \*use small amt of rendered duck fat here\*

cover w/double layer tinfiol and cook for 2hrs - remove pan from oven, drain duck juices from pan, turn oven upto 375,

heat reserved duck fat in pan, add spice paste, and fry up for 2 min, add coconut milk. cover duck w/ spice mister, sprinkle w/ toasted coconut - cook for additional 20-30 mins uncovered... serve over cauliflower rice....

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-16T00:38:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-64590>

This may well need to be filed under TMI ... be warned.

I posted some very personal information over in the sex thread this morning. I've been thinking about it for most of the day. What I left unsaid was, I think, important and I need to share it. Especially since someone else has said to me that they felt sex should only be with another being. Well ok. BUT.

There is an adage out there ... use it or lose it. The following is just my opinion and based on nothing more than personal observation.

If you shut the door on any sex at all whether it's with another person or just with yourself you run the risk, over time, of not being able to experience that pleasure at all.

This is what happened to me (all other sexual issues aside). When I had my hysterectomy about 15 years ago the relief from the constant sexual obsession was wonderful. But over time I kind of lost all interest all together. So much so that I found, much to my consternation several months ago, that I was unable to achieve an orgasm. My first thought was "what now? Do I need viagra?"

I saw my PA about it because I couldn't even begin to face my doc (a man). It was difficult to even talk to her but I got it out. She tested my testosterone which I thought was the issue and it was flatlined so she added it to my bhrt.

Still nothing.

I thought it was a hormonal issue but didn't know where to go with it. My consult with Jack centered around this and a couple other things I'm still not ready to make public.

Now I have said I love a challenge and he really threw down the gauntlet. So I did what he suggested which was get a vibrator, USE it and involve hubby. And part of the problem is being resolved ... vaginal orgasms galore. Clitoral --hit and miss, mostly miss and boy do I. Miss them. I strongly suspect had I continued down the path of self denial I may well have found myself in a sad sad lonely place where pleasure was just a vague memory.

My point to all of this is that even if you feel that b.o.b. is an unsatisfactory substitute for the real deal it is still far better than the alternative. Besides, no matter how you get there, an orgasm puts a smile on your face, the sparkle back in your eyes, and a bounce to your step. What man wouldn't find that attractive?

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-16T22:36:04+0200

The sky isn't actually colored at all (not blue or yellow or red or green). Rather, it's your mind that's colored. The world around us is physics devoid of meaning, whereas our perception of the world is meaning devoid of physics.

\-- Beau Lotto

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-18T01:44:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-65032>

**GEEZER ALERT**

It's been such a fun Saturday. Dh insisted we attend the AARP driver safety program for geezers. yawwwwn. 7 hours I'll never get back.

I barely got in my breakfast, no CT and now I'm COLD and I forgot to take anything out for dinner so I guess it's going to be steak because I can cook it to perfection in my nuwave oven from FROZEN in under 30 minutes. Lunch was tuna salad salad at the Palace (a local hole in the wall watering hole) which was edible but that's about all I can say for it. Shoulda had the chicken salad.

What else can I whine about.

Oh yeah. About 3 days ago I was taking the plastic wrap off the bowl of green beans I had nuked for dinner and I steamed my left index finger.

now mind you, I play with fire for fun and profit but the KITCHEN is where I have burned myself most often and most badly. This was no exception. Yesterday the blister that had resulted, which I hadn't even noticed, tore open. I finally had to put a bandaid on it because I kept scraping it on everything I touched. The broken blister is about 8mm across. It's BIG. And it hurts. wahwahwah. I know. took the bandaid off this A.M. came home and immediately scraped it on the mailbox as I was digging out the mail. OW!

Oh yeah, went to bed early last night hoping to avoid the 2 hour layover before I could fall asleep. DH immediately! hopped into bed (no such luck ... don't even go there) so he could read. 30 minutes later I heaved a loud enough sigh he finally got the message and turned out the light. I dropped off only to be awakened by Missy screeching at Three who had DARED to get on the bed.

tick tock tick tock tick tock

I think I finally drifted off again about 1A.M. and then woke at first light and had to get up to get ready for the aforementioned stupid waste of my time SENIOR DRIVER SAFETY PROGRAM.

The only accident I have EVER had was over a year ago when I had to go to town. The streets were a mess and the stupid city crews had NOT sanded the intersection at Main & Loucks which is one of the busiest ones in town. I was doing under 15 as I approached the stop light. I was behind a big ol' utility truck. I put my foot on the brakes and proceeded to slide, or should I say SLAM, into the truck. He suffered NO DAMAGE but the front end of my van looked like Thor had popped it one on the snoot. I had to drive the van of shame for almost a month before we could get the damage repaired. And the accident was an ACCIDENT. I didn't even get a ticket.

But he insisted I go to this stupid class so we could get a 10% discount on our car insurance. MEN.

I think I need to have a date with b.o.b. I'm in a real gritchy mood. And THAT is bordering on TMI for me so I guess I"ll post and just go take a nap.

### dcrawford — 2012-11-23T13:00:19+0200

Let me know if you come across specific forums that are not letting you in.

For example:
http://www.jackkruse.com/forum-2/meet-greet/happy-anniversary-dr-kruse-and-all-starfish/page-3263/#p65257 doesn't work.

but...
http://www.jackkruse.com/forum-2/meet-greet/happy-anniversary-dr-kruse-and-all-starfish/#p65257 but that does. The page-3263 that gets added throws that.

That is because the old forum and the new forum use different layouts for the databases. We knew there would be a few oddballs and that they should work themselves out over the next few days as members post updates.

I'm sure I had lots of things to share but have managed to forget them all nearly as fast as I thought of them. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. dinner ... wait and see

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-24T14:52:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-65507>

Just getting ready to go hop in the tub. Am currently re-reading CT2 since Jack announced he was headed into a cave yesterday for a biohack. There's that mad scientist again.

ANYway. I'm finding reading these posts for a 2nd (or 10th) time they are slowly beginning to make some sense. Or maybe it's just because my brain is coming back online.

I mentioned to a friend yesterday that it feels as if I have been living underwater for the last decade or so and I'm slowly breaking the surface. It's WONderful. If only I could convince DH to keep the bedroom temp on the COLD side I'd be happy. I may have to spend the winter in the guest room where it's nice and cold.

Anyway. ABsolutely nothing new to report. Weight exactly the same as it's been for weeks now. At least it quit going up. Now if it would just go DOWN. Still taking 1/4 tab (6mcg) of cytomel. Had to get up last night right after we went lights out because I could feel myself starting to tremble. Tsp of D-ribose stopped it. That was a 3rd dose yesterday, btw. Because of that I'm very hesitant to up the cytomel any further. I think the combo of the CT and the cytomel is starting to do something (I hope it's wiping out all the RT3). We'll see.

Turkey day was spent at friends. I had turkey, sweet potatoes with far too much brown sugar on them, a bit of the Waldorf salad I had made and some green bean casserole. I did eat a small piece of pumpkin pie sans crust just to be a good guest. Nancy doesn't cook much but she did make the pie so I felt obligated.

OK so I just wanted it. happy? It wasn't that it wasn't good (she doesn't use a lot of sugar) but it just wasn't. You know what I mean?

After 2 days of not being very hungry I got hungry for lunch yesterday so had some canned salmon. Pork chops for dinner tonight. Got up way late (another overcast day so I tend to sleep an extra hour or two ... that cave thing is sounding pretty good right now. Except for bats. And the fact I'd have to hike halfway up Tongue River Canyon to get there plus I hear the entrance is way small so maybe I'll just pull the covers over my head. hell. I wandered way off track. Where was I?

Head for the tub. right. Better go turn on the cold water.

Later...

I just love feeling like I'm talking to myself. Â ;P

Â 

So, no CT today. Â I'm doing every other day now. Â Boy did I get cold yesterday. Â Shivered for a couple of hours and didn't think I'd ever get warm again. Â Tap water hovering around 50Â° maybe a tad less. Â 

Am also feeling incredibly FAT today. Â That little voice in the back of my head starts whining about my weight but the sane part of me tells it to just STFU because starving won't get me where I want to be. Â Patience. Â Patience!

It's snowing right now. Â DH is watching the Broncos and I'm wandering the net instead of making some beads or at least cleaning up the studio. Â Which is a major mess.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-26T12:37:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-65631>

Okeydokey. Â Lovely morning out ... all that pristine snow and bright sunny BLUE sky. Â I love my backyard.

Â 

Â 

Today is CT day so have a few more minutes before I need to go fill the tub. Â It doesn't get any easier but I Â have learned to grit my teeth and just park my backside in the water. Â I'm at 10 minutes from the waist down and that's about all my legs can take. Â Then 30 minutes on my back. Â Try not to visualize, it's ugly.

Because I got up so late yesterday I only did two meals. Â Dinner was steak and I realized I wanted more than just half of one. Â So I ate the whole thing. Â Hubby thinks he has a delicate appetite so only had half of his. Â Then he ate about 6 SF popscicles (no I cannot get him to stop eating them. Â He's a big boy.). Â The plus for me is my FBG was 87 this morning. Â Lowest it's been in a couple of weeks. Â So there I have it. Â I need to eat enough at dinner. Â More protein, more fat. Â Less whining.

Planning on going to see the Life of Pi this afternoon. Â I listened to the audio version a couple of years ago and it was wondrous. Â I LOVE Ang Lee's work so am excited to see this. Â From the previews it should be gloriously breathtakingly stunningly beautiful. Â Can you tell I am highly visual?

Oh yeah. Â Cytomel ... took 1/4 tab yesterday because Saturday I took 2 doses and had trouble falling asleep. Â I"m going to try another double dose today and see how it goes. Â May be that 1/4 tab (6mcg) is all I can tolerate right now. Â shrug. Â I would love to get back to feeling as I did that very first day when I took an entire tab ... it was miraculous. Â Like I was suddenly AWAKE after stumbling along half asleep. Â darn.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-27T13:22:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-65700>

Another snowy but bright morning here in Wyoming.Â 

Â  10Â° but headed for a balmy 41Â° and supposed to be low 50's by Saturday. Â My kind of winter -- because winter starts early here in the wild and wooly West.

No CT today but am tempted. Â Now how weird is that?! Took 2 - 1/4 tab doses of cytomel yesterday and felt almost human. Just a little bit of difficulty falling asleep but nothing major. Â  Woke early because clear skies means too much light, darn. Â Finally hauled my backside outta bed around 9-ish. Â Lazy lazy lazy. Â Hubs left for coffee with the guys around 8 so I only had to fight the cats for the covers.

Dinner last night was a couple of big ol' homemade by Sackett's Market Bohemian sausage ... oooooo so much GARLIC! YUMMMY

BAB this AM with double amount of butter in my BPDC. Â sweetÂ 

Bought more water packed sardines yesterday ... they FINALLY got some more in so may have that for lunch as a change from the usual salmon.

Dinner ... dunno. Â Thinking right now more about the pile of laundry that awaits, the cats' water fountains that need cleaning and sheets and towels to change. Â all that boring "chop wood, carry water" kinda BS that has to be done. Â bleh.

didn't get to the movie yesterday. Â DH announced with a whine he doesn't want to go see Life of PI so I have to go alone. Â bother. Â Maybe today.

sure wish everyone would get themselves back. Â The echo from the empty halls is beginning to annoy me.

Actually I DO have a website and an Etsy store. And I used to sell exclusively on ebay. I haven't done anything for about 2 years ... the whole time I was involved in a massive bullsh\*t lawsuit brought by some maniac down in texas. We finally beat her sorry backside into the dirt and were all given NO EVIDENCE summary judgements. She tried to appeal (which was a horse laugh because her attorney quit on her months ago and she had to represent herself) but the Appellate Court basically told her to take a hike. The cost to all of us was enormous both in terms of emotional stress and actual money. Now I can't decide if NOT working has just become a habit. Also my studio space looks like a hurricane hit it. sigh. I need a maid.

ANYhoo. I keep having ideas I want to try out but can't seem to get myself back in front of the torch. I don't know if I'm afraid I've lost all my passion for melting glass or if I won't remember how to do it ... dunno. Something always manages to get in the way.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-28T12:59:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-65775>

Didn't get the movies again. Â I told myself it will be here another week so I'll go next Monday. Â I just don't really like going alone. Â You'd think I'd be used to it by now.

Â 

Ate lunch way late yesterday. Â I kept telling myself I was hungry and needed to eat. Â Finally sat down around 3 so was not hungry at all until nearly 8:30. Â boo. Â I had the rest of the turkey leftovers (which was probably 2 whole ounces), a bit of mayo on the side and a couple of bites of cream cheese with figs & walnuts. Â Was certainly enough to quell the hunger but my FBG was up to 107 this morning. Â WHEN will I learn?!

Â 

Took 2 - 1/4tab doses of cytomel yesterday with no problem. Â I'm thinking of adding a 3rd today. Â We'll see. Â I'd like to get it back up to a full tab because this is just gonna drag on forever if I don't. Â Had 2 doses of d-ribose as well. Â The trembling thing seems to have cleared up which is nice.

Â 

today is CT but DH just got out of the bathroom so I'm running late on that. Â Had a humungous amount of butter in my decaf this morning so I'm ready for the cold.

Â 

It's take-hubby-to-the-Legion Wednesday so I'll be having tilapia for dinner and he can have leftover steak when he gets home. Â I HATE driving back and forth after dark. Â The damned deer are all over the place. Â Week before last I had 3 near hits. Â grumble grumble.Â 

Â 

Well time to go fill the tub. Â

I just looked on a map. I didn't realize how close you are to MT and how much further north Glacier is. Sheesh. Maybe we fly into Calgary and rent a car. When I was a kid, we had many a summer vacation starting in Vancouver and ending in Calgary. Lots of fond memories there - encounters with black bears included. If we drive, we'll have to ring you up as we're passing through.Â

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-28T22:49:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-65813>

I was born and grew up in Fort Collins. Â My inlaws still live in Loveland, my sister and her husband in Brush. Â I positively HATE what has become of my once beautiful home. Â Where there was once open fields between Â Fort Collins and Denver there is now mile after mile after mile of subdivision. Â It literally makes me weep.

Â 

Once upon a time I wanted desperately to move back. Â I miss the front range. Â At least I always knew what direction I was facing, LOL, unlike here in Sheridan where we're huddled along a spur of the Rockies known as the Big Horns. Â It's pretty but I miss the foothills and the splendor of the mountains thrusting up behind them. Â Here you're either out on the flats or up on the mountain. Â No in between. Â Makes driving to the top very interesting, I'll say that. Â You get half way up to Sand turn and I swear you can see all the way to the South Dakota border. Â Just don't look down!

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-29T13:18:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-65843>

It is a giant PITA living with someone who just will not listen and doesn't want to get it.

Â 

DH complains constantly that the house is cold (I keep it around 68 just to be nice). Â At night he watches TV in the bedroom because he can close the door, turn up the little oil heater to max sweat Â and roast. Â By the time I'm ready to go to bed it's like 85 in there. It means I have a difficult time dropping off because it's too effing warm. Â And then I don't sleep well because first I throw off most of the covers and then I get chilled and my back muscles cramp up and then my neck hurts and I dream I'm slogging through the winter setting in Â GAME OF THRONES. Â I swear I am going to spend the rest of the winter in the guest room because it's nice and cool in there.

Â 

Then to add insult to injury, this morning he gets up early (for him) and I keep sleeping. Â He's up for a good solid 2 hours before I stagger out of bed, brush my teeth, empty the litter pans and check in here. Â Yes I know I shouldn't but I did. Â Shoot me. Â 

Â 

So now I"m at the 30 minute mark and need to get my breakfast done. Â HE saunters into the kitchen while I'm fishing out a ground beef patty and now I get to wait at least another 30 minutes while he dithers around fixing some eggs and bacon. Â A task I could accomplish in about 10 minutes but now I'm so pissed I'd likely crack his skull with the frying pan.

Â 

What a way to start the day.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-29T14:52:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-65856>

> **Shijin13 said**
> Its definitely easier when all family members are on board.... I still struggle w/my mom - even though she's told me point blank she won't change... I'm thinking that if I could get her to read the dopamine RX she might consider changing. Â I think I'll print it out and mail it to her via snail mail...Â 

I wish you the best of luck. Â I have given up. Â Family tunes me out, friends diss me. Â I've learned just to shut my yap.

Â 

As I lay in bed this morning pondering away I realized that the majority of people are content to live underwater. Â It makes me crazy. Â How can you NOT be curious about the world around you and the world IN you?

Â 

I think I was born asking WHY? Â and will probably go out the same way. Â It's my favorite word. Â WHY????? Â I want to cram all the knowledge of the world into my head. I want to KNOW. Â How can you live and not ask questions? Â How can you not search and seek and try to understand? Â It baffles me.

Â 

It's part of why I chase around Jack's blog and this forum. Â It's a real big WHY? Â I keep pounding the information into my head in the hopes the light will one of these days break and understanding will descend to a chorus of hallelujahs. Â How can everyone around me be so complacent? Â Especially where your health is concerned?

Â 

(insert sound of me banging my head on the wall in frustration)

Like you Darleen - I have always wanted to know why. I think I have always been left of centre and nothing was ever too weird. I sometimes worry that I drive people crazy with all the questions. We had a restaurant for 35 years and I soon discovered - there was so many amazing people passing thru and if I didn't talk to them and ask questions I would never know.
Having said that - friends and family seem to be really wary like you are on some kind of crusade for something really weird and crazy and totally stupid. I certainly don't push anything - I barely say anything really. If they knew that I ate raw meat and MHS they would probably have me committed!
It really makes me stop and think........If JK's FIL didn't listen to him - and he has all the education and knowledge and only his best interests at heart - what chance do we have to make an impression on anyone? Although I am understanding more and more -I still can't really explain it in any kind of technical terms. Thankfully I have a great new Doc but I think I am going to drive him crazy as I try to understand all this and his time is so limited.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-29T20:14:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-65897>

Then answer is in this blog post........modern life causes diminished brain dopamine from many avenues. Â So far I have given you three. Â When your dopamine levels are low you cant perceive the level of complacency of your decisions because your brain is working inefficiently.........and when someone points it out to you, you fly off the handle and attack instead of just thinking..........WHY might this be correct.

Â 

It's pretty simple. Â Bad choices are a sign of a modern sort of brain damage.

### Inger — 2012-11-30T06:31:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-65925>

LOL.. I must laugh when reading all your stories about it beeing to warm! Must be the CT..huh! Me too feels so warm everywhere I do not like to have it warm around me anymore! I like cool.. And before Jacks cold thermogenesis posts I was the opposite.. always loved to have it hot!

Darleen I suggest to go sleep in the guest room too! Sleep really is important. Maybe you hubby starts missing you so much he will turn the heat down..lol amazing things might happend! But I believe we need to care for us first. Yes.

I tell everyone (when it comes/fits naturally) that I eat raw fishheads and raw meat and anything. It is not hard to explain / understand for anyone how a fresh raw fishheadsmoothie is so much healthier than some rancid processed cod liver oil everyone is choking down withouth a second thought... Or how vitamins and stuff gets destroyed by cooking and what benefit foods have in their raw state.

I am not one bit ashamed. Not ONE bit. Cause I know what good they do me. They save me my sanity and my health. I do not care one bit if someone thinks I am weird because of that. I have nothing to loose anymore anyways, that broken is this girl. How could anyone ever see, if I do not show/tell what treasures I know about..??? That is what I wish from anyone, to share their treasures with me! So I do it too. As long as someone is not forbidding me to speak about it. I am always respecting that people do different choises though, and I am not pushing anything on anyone. And guess what. There is so much love around.... that amazes me again and again.

### Inger — 2012-11-30T07:29:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-65937>

> Yes - I can see talking when it fits naturally ...maybe in Europe this is all more acceptable. People I know think it is crazy not to eat bread/grains etc. They actually are worried that I am harming myself. I have been told so may times that I will make myself very sick if I don't have carbs. They tell me that carbs are absolutely necessary. Their is no doubt in their mind that they are right and I am wrong - even tho they have all kinds of health issues.

You know what I do then? I tell them I go regularely to the doctor and test just in case my diet would be not good for me because who can be sure about anything in life.. and that my tests are awesome! (in their or my docs eyes..lol to me they can be still much better but that I leave out..ha) Now, that always works! It needs to be scientific, always. So that is why I go to the doc and test even if I need to pay out of pocket. And.. they see my energy, my moods.. they always tell how healthy I am.. And I tell them why that is. And that I am actually very weak so I need to pay very strict attention to what I put in my mouth or I will feel not good. I say stuff like;

" oh.. I see you are eating this ice cream. Good for you.. lucky you you are strong and healthy to be able to eat this stuff. I am not unfortunately I would get sick eating these.. But hey I take it because I feel so great with my fishheadsmoothies..lol"

And they all see it is true! That way I do not come over as someone pushing them to change, but I hope when they have a silent moment in pain they might consider that they are weak too... and maybe want to change little by little!
There might be a much better way to do it but this is how I do so far and it works fine.. people do not get offended.. and I have a change to tell about my lifestyle too..
Like I always have my sunglasses on when somewhere out at night (only at work I cannot wear them for sure but after) and people ask why and I tell I am optimizing my hormones. Sound good, or?

When they ask how the glasses do that, I explain it further. Nature rules. When it is dark out we should have it dark too, when it is light out we should have it light too. The rules in nature are there for a reason. And I explain the signaling and stuff, how our brain works. It is all very easy to understand!

### Shijin13 — 2012-11-30T08:49:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-65943>

It only takes one small nugget to plant the seed w/ppl who are hesitant. Yesterday my Lactation consultant who worked w/me for both Chaos & Havoc called me b/c of the last time we spoke I told her I'd figured out why I had problems w/lactation. That little nugget stuck in her mind & kept coming back to her while working with a present client. She wanted to find out how I made my discovery. I broke down hormones 101, ct 7 & the BG series down in 15 mins. She asked me for the links & wants to sit down to learn more.

### Shijin13 — 2012-11-30T11:41:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-65959>

In my job I've had to teach myself how to learn something in short order and be able to explain it back to the experts w/in days of starting a job. Its a learned skill, not a lack of tools or knowledge base. I'd say 100% of the time I'm thrown into job I know nothing about - but I can learn it. you're learning too...add that to your online courses you're taking you'll be able to explain this as well. I think in the end you're going to understand it at a far different level than I do b/c of the courses you're taking and be able to translate it to someone else's perspective...where I'm only good at the n=1. your journal is amazing at actually explaining a lot of things... and your BHRT knowledge is amazing - don't sell yourself short my dear

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-01T12:28:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66029>

AUGH Â our internet was down ALL DAY LONG yesterday. Â They finally got it back up some time during the night. Â Talk about withdrawal! Â Stupid phone company. Â Long distance was out as well. Â I'm surprised every single business in town didn't shut down.

Â 

OK favorite movie line EVER ... "Never give up! Â Never surrender!" Â That's kinda my mantra.

So thanks for the ego strokes -- giving up was never in my vocabulary. Â Oh wait, there's that mule headed stubbornness Â again.

Â 

CT was tough yesterday because I had just settled into the tub when the internet went down. Â Double augh. Â I knew that if I got out to go investigate I wouldn't get back in because our water has dropped to 49. Â Yes, I'm a wuss unlike certain neurosurgeons who trek off to the 63rd parallel, climb into a deep, dark hole and hop in a tub of 42Â° water. Â pfffbt.

Â 

anyhoo, I had to have hubster check the modem which, since he is technologically challenged, is always a PITA. Â I finally gave up and had him bring me my iPod. Â Rocked the rest of CT and am thinking about adding to the 'talk sex' thread because of it. Â 

Â 

BAB was usual. Â Sardines for lunch. Baked tilapia for dinner with 1/4 of a honker of a sweet potato. Â I just like a little something extra on my plate. Â The fish looked lonely.

Â 

finally, snagged a new quote off the telly for my sig line. Â I love pithy, short commentary as long as it isn't telling me to put all my troubles in a little pink box, tie a pink bow around it, attach it to a pink balloon and let them float away. Â

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-01T12:56:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66030>

somebody talk me down off this ledge.

Â 

I just got off the scale and I gained FOUR POUNDS this last week.

Â 

The only thing that has changed has been the addition of a bit more fat. Â I'm going through a stick of butter in my coffee(s) daily ... cause not even I could do 4 ounces of butter in one mug. Â Other than that it's been the same routine as always. Â I quit putting salt in my morning ice water about a week ago so it cannot be simple fluid retention. Â My measurements have gone UP so you can't claim it's added muscle.

I'm upping my morning cytomel to 1/2 tab with 2 more 1/4 tab doses. Â (runs screaming through the house)

### PaulaRichards — 2012-12-01T13:07:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66031>

Geez Darleen I wish I had words of wisdom for ya but with my history there is no rhyme nor reason for the numbers on the scale sometimes!Â  Do you seriously add 1/2 cup of butter to your coffee?Â  You must have a bubba mug!Â  I certainly am not a fat phobic and some dinners lately consist almost entirely of fat with some veggies.Â  I discovered I like asparagus (please don't tell me there is anything wrong with it lol!) and learned to make hollandaise sauce, yummy!Â  Not only am I drinking HWC with my bacon & eggs in the mornings, I'm covering it with hollandaise sauce...yup...no fear of fat here.Â

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-01T13:16:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66033>

I've given up on weighing... I've discovered its just too stressful... Â I go by how I feel. Â Did I get a good sleep ect.... if it makes Â you feel better I'm rocking the same butter/ghee consumption/day + Cream and CO in my coffee... Â not to mention the 1/4c of bacon fat I'm drinking at the end of my lunch each day too....Â 

Â 

is it possible you're not a fully efficient fat burner? Â I think I'm just waiting for everything to come on line and have things start humming.... back away from dropping fat. Â stay at the qty you're at...and just let it ride...Â

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-01T14:32:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66040>

WHOA! Â You actually drink bacon fat? Â WOW color me impressed. Â I actually had just increased the butter intake because of another thread. Â My mug holds about 1.75 cups. Â It's a "Stitch" mug done up a la Warhol. Â  { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Â  I LOVE Stitch and one year for xmas DH sent his sister to their local Disney store and she bought everything she could find that was Stitch-related. I have baby Stitches and his fellow critters all along the top of my computer desk, big Stitch with the ray guns peeking out of my CD basket, fuzzy blue Stitch looking at me from a cubby, a Stitch water globe that lights up and beeps and my Stitch mug. Â He sorta took over Cinderella's place in my chlid's heart. Â And yes, I have been gifted with a couple of Cinderella water globes as well along with the honkin' big Fantasia water globe. Â I'm spoiled. Â Yes I am.

I've been thinking all morning about this and trying to figure out what I've done that's different. Â The answer is ... wine. Â The last couple of weeks I have indulged in a glass or two a couple of times a week. Â So I guess that fantastic bottle of Wild Horse pinot noir I just cracked open is going to have to just sit there for a bit while I give up the grape to see if that was the problem.

I've also not really been working at upping the cytomel. Â I'm still only at half the daily dose of 25mcg. Â After those couple of days of going bonkers at the full dose I got a little scared of it. Â And even though I did take that 2nd dose of d-ribose yesterday I was a little trembly this morning. Â Don't like that either.

I wouldn't have gotten on the scale at all this morning but it actually feels as if I've dropped a few pounds. My core is feeling rock hard these days (when I can find it through the flab). Â I decided that CT must be doing something besides making me shiver for hours even at this early stage (because I just started actually getting in the tub just a couple of weeks ago). Â I've been determined about it and have been doing 30 minutes on my back with 10-15 seated for my legs. Â Small tub which I thought was bigger but (shrug). Â First week I was in the tub every single day. Â Now I'm doing every other day.

sigh

Â 

I try not to get impatient. Â I try to tell myself I have lots of stuff to fix. Â Some days the problem seems insurmountable. Â Especially when I start looking through clothing catalogs. Â I get tired of living in stretchy pants and big t-shirts. Â bleh.

Â 

Ok. Â Pity party's over.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-01T14:52:55+0200

I'm with Kathylu on this. Â Take it back ... somethings rotten.

Â 

Just had to share. Â My (now retired) chiropractor, for whom I play computer guru, just called to sing "happy birthday" to me. Â Just cracked me up! Â He is such a sweetheart and the story he told me of how he married the love of his life would bring tears to anyone's eyes. Â She died some years back from Huntington's and because she worried about getting the disease chose early on in life to not ever have children. Â He decided that he loved her so much he would give that up as well. Â They hosted exchange students over the years (she was also a school teacher) and they became their "children." Â A couple of years ago he met and married a lovely woman. Â Her granddaughter is now the apple of my friend's eye and he spoils that child rotten, LOL. Â He is the sweetest, kindest man and I just adore him. Â I am also so happy he has found love again and now has the family he denied himself for his first love.

Â

I have definitely been there Darleen - It is so frustrating to be trying so hard and your weight is going up instead of down. Â I am finding more and more that my system is more sensitive I think. Â I for sure can't eat bacon - salt, nitrates? Â This last week I have discovered that anything from a can really bothers me - bloating etc. and my weight goes up by 1 Kilo. Â So I have a cupboard full of sardines, salmon etc that I will have to leave for now. Â I tried a little bit of stilton cheese the other day and I was up 1 and a half Kilos - that is craziness. Â  I do so much better if I eat lightly and mostly raw and lots of fat. Â I also think I have food sensitivities that I didn't know I had. Â I use to love eggs and ate them all the time but they are a problem now too. Â I get severe reactions in restaurants now too - MSG. Â That never used to happen either. Â I don't know what has changed but I feel so much better now. Â  Don't know if anything here helps but I guess if things aren't working you have to look at all the possibilities. Â I feel better too when I somewhat avoid salt - I seem to be highly sensitive - although everyone says you need salt in your diet. Â

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-01T17:40:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66062>

so there I am, ranting away to DH about my weight and when I finish up go to the kitchen to find a dozen red roses and ... wait for it ... a small box of chocolates. Â LOL -- I shouldn't because his heart was, as usual, in the right place. Â I had one bite of one piece and had to put it down because it was so sweet it made my mouth hurt.

Â 

Which brings me to the reason for THIS post. Â I came up with a new recipe for coco crack which I've been mulling over for weeks now. Â I nuked 1/2 jar or so of Artisana coconut butter just enough to soften it up. Â dumped it in a bowl with 2 squares of unsweetened Ghiradelli chocolate (the really thin bar), about 1/4 cup of 60% semi-sweet chocolate chips and nuked it for another minute. Â Stirred it up real good. Â Let it sit a bit then tested it. Â WOOO too bitter, too dark. Â Added half a dozen packets of splenda (next time will use pure sucralose to avoid the dextrose) and covered it up. Â Fast forward to this afternoon. It's now firm again so I dug out a spoonful. Â OM. Â I mean, seriously OMG. Â Is it good! Â I was afraid it would be too grainy-textured but it isn't. Â It is just plain flat out yummy. Â Pardon me while I go back for a 3rd spoonful.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-02T13:25:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66144>

I don't think I could do that (drink bacon fat). Â Just thinking about it upsets my tummy. Â I guess that pretty much means I am not a fat burner. Â yet. Â I love fat but I can only do so much.

DH took me to dinner last night at the Rib & Chop House. Â We went early (5:30) thinking we'd get there before the dinner crowd. ahahahahaha boy were we surprised. Â The parking lot was full there and there weren't any tables available so we sat at the bar. Which was OK. Â The food was just as good. Â 

We had mussels to start and I had the Dover sole with a Louisiana shrimp stuffing (no bread in it) and a lovely sauce, and squash casserole. Â I only had a few bites of the squash. Â It was good but I was more interested in the fish. Â Had a B&B for dessert and this morning discovered why I should not do alcohol. Â My FBG was 130. Â sigh. Â Of course I also took 2 - 1/2 tab doses of cytomel yesterday as well.

CT this morning in about 20 minutes. Â Just finished breakfast. Â Yeah, slept until just after 9. Â BUT I also had trouble falling asleep AGAIN. Â I'm starting to think I need to be in bed by 9. Â I was yawning and nodding off about 8 and could barely keep my eyes open. Â Lights out at 10:28 and then lay there for an hour before dropping off. Â THEN woke at about 3:30 and lay there for about another hour or so before dropping off again.

Haven't a clue what I'm fixing for dinner. Â I may do canned salmon later. Â Might have to do baked pork chops since I have some left from the last Costco visit.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-02T19:00:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66172>

I've pretty much demolished half of the coco crack fudge stuff I Â made. Â It's hard to stay out of it! LOL. Â Made it pretty tough to eat that canned salmon for lunch. Â I had to force the last 4 bites down my throat. Â 

Â 

Just realized why I'm sort of in the dog house. Â We are supposed to be headed to Disney World. Â I got "yelled" at a week ago because I never made the reservations and it got kind of tense last night at dinner because it came up again.

I don't know how to tell him I can't bring myself to go because I've gained back almost all the weight I lost 5 years ago. Â My ass is the size of Montana for cripes sake. Â I know I know the bulk (and I do not use that term lightly) of folks who wander DW are so fat that a lot of them rent scooters to get around. Â That's why DW has invested in kneeling buses. Â I'm not THAT fat but I'm still pretty much, well, ashamed, to be seen out in public. Â Which is why I've started hiding again.

So in an attempt to mollify him I said maybe we could do a Viking river cruise next May. Â Maybe by then I'll be losing some of the flab. Â And we''ll do DW NEXT December. Â He doesn't want to go any other time because he thinks that between turkey day and the middle of Dec is the slowest time and the weather is the best. Â He seems to forget it was pretty crowded in '07 when we went and it was so hot the natives were all whining. Â Whatever.

Â 

I was thinking today about starting my "Y" membership again. Â We have a fantastic "Y" here ... they received a lot of money over the years from Whitney Benefits and the facilities are amazing. Â I know I should be out walking in the cold but (a) I worry about running into critters, specifically a mountain lion and (b) the ice and mud. Â right now we have a bit of both and the last thing I need to do is take a flying header on the ice. Â So I was thinking I could use the treadmill at the "Y" because I'm starting to feel the need to get off my fat butt and move around some. Â Of course, it IS December and they close for 10 days? at the end of every year to do major maintenance. Â But there's still 3 full weeks I could get in. Â That's a start. Â And it would MAKE me do it instead of thinking, oh I can go out tomorrow. Â We'll see.

Â 

ok

/whining

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-02T23:38:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66199>

Um. Â Caroline, mountain lions are not adorable. Â They are major predators with sharp claws and fangs and yes they WILL take a rather large bite out of you. Â There were several people killed near Boulder, CO a few years back by cougars. Â Although they are normally shy of humans I'd rather be armed.

and yes, we figured he was maybe a year old and mom had given him the boot. I felt really bad when he got shot but also relieved. Â This may be wild 'n wooly Wyoming but we live in a valley with a pretty good sized human population with kids and pets. Â Better the cat than a kid.

Krista, I figure walking on the treadmill at a moderate pace is probably a good way to start. Â Then if things improve, in January I can start up with my 30 minute/once a week, heavy weight lifting program. Â But I'm not starting it until I see some positive movement.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-03T14:36:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66238>

Where to start.

Last night's dinner was a big ol' steak which I cooked 'rare.' Â It was flat out raw in the middle and I relished every bite. Â It was a good 8-10 ounces and that was all I ate. Â Had dinner around 6:30.

Early to bed (9:30) and light's out at 10. Â Almost dropped off when hubby and the cats came in and woke me up. Â From there it was toss and turn for several hours because the wind was blowing like crazy and for about the 3rd night in a row I was HOT. Â Not like a hot flash. Â this was more like my skin was running a fever. Â I side sleep and every part of me that was touching the pillow or the bed just fired up something awful. Â I kept tossing and turning to cool off, finally kicked off everything but the sheet and was STILL uncomfortable.

I finally fell asleep but guess who woke me up around 4:45. Â Dropped back off and didn't wake again until nearly 10. Â Checked my FBG to find it was 222.

so my question is, is it the cytomel? Â Is my metabolism revving up too much during the night? Â Is it the CT? Â What the heck is going on? Â I'm hesitant to stop the ctyomel because I want the RT3 cleared and the sooner the better. Â But I'm not going to continue if it's going to shove my BG through the roof.

Other than that there's nothing new or exciting to report. Â Either hubby has been sneaking "my" coco crack 'fudge' or I've been eating more of it than I thought because it's nearly gone. Â I am really working on putting more fat in my diet. Â Not ready to drink bacon fat though. Â Maybe some cream ... I could do that.Â

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-03T15:36:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66242>

Oh... I get that HOT Thing... ughhh Â Its like your thyroid has turned on...but the rest of the body isn't quite listening... Best two analogies I've got for this... its like you flip a switch, but the relay doesn't connect properly giving off all this crazy heat. Â Â 

the other is the conductor to the orchestra is tapping away at the podium trying to get the attention of the orchestra - but they're not listening ...just making controlled chaos but even then they're making music even if it isn't all that good.

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-03T16:41:02+0200

there are nights I can't get warm enough and then there are nights I'm kicking off all the covers and getting undressed to get any sleep.... ugghhh its crazy.. its worse for me about 2hrs after I take my Armour in the morning... but I also get other bursts of HOT across the day/night as well....Â

here's a better analogy.... its like stoking a steam engine... you keep feeding it until it actually catches....Â

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-04T13:32:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66300>

I guess my pilot light is still sputtering. Â I didn't CT yesterday but I still warmed up a bit when I went to bed. Â Not nearly as hot as the night before. Â I'm about ready to go run the cold water so am curious how tonight will be.

Finally got to see "The Life of Pi" and, as usual, they put all the really best stuff in the trailers. Â Which isn't to say the entire film wasn't 2 hours of eye candy ... because it definitely was. Â The movie paralleled the book very well. Â It was most enjoyable. Â It was nice to be reminded I'm not the only one with a hidden tiger lurking inside.Â 

Took a friend and she bought the popcorn but -- major news flash -- I ate about a 1/3 of it and tossed the rest (it was a small bag ... and here we still have small, med and large not large, larger and OMG-that's-enough-to-feed-10-people-HUGE. Â I simply didn't want it. Â Let me repeat that. Â I. Â Didn't. Â Want. Â It. Â Color me amazed.

Remembering how sky high Â my FBG was yesterday I had 1/4 of a leftover sweet potato (about 1/3 cup as measurements go) with dinner (which was a hefty helping of Costco pulled pork) so this A.M. FBG was 107. Â I'm still not sure I completely understand why going NO-carb causes FBG to skyrocket. Â Anybody have any ideas? Â And I did go to a blog post someone mentioned in another thread. Â Still don't get it. Â Does this mean I am definitely LR (after all this time?) or my metabolism was so fired up it drained my liver of glycogen stores? Â Color me confused but curious.

Well it's now time to go fill the tub. Â oh joy.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-04T18:59:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66330>

I have decided to just pretty much stick to writing in here. Â The forum seems to have died since the big switchover. Â I keep hoping people will find their way back but it just isn't happening. Â It's a real pity. Â I miss all the chit chat etc.

Oh well.

Just got back from the cat food expedition. Â Our shopping choices here are pretty limited. Â Wally world, Kmart and a couple of big grocery chains. Â I hate spending money at WW but what're you gonna do? Â They have the best prices in town on the cat food my spoiled little darlings will eat so hi ho off we go.

And just let me add that at this time of year I HATE HATE HATE HATE going there. Â It was ridiculously crowded for mid-afternoon on Tuesday. Â Stupidly so. Â And now the junk season is in full swing. Â I swear I want to puke having to weave my way through the piles of crap they bring in. Â And then there are the shoppers. Â You've been to "people of walmart.com" I trust. Â Well, we got 'em here. Â And I swear everyone in the store looks as unhealthy as can be. Â One woman was on her way in as I was circling the lot looking for a parking space. Â I wanted to weep. Â She was beyond enormous and I don't know how she was even walking.

Oops, almost lost where I was going with this.

Once upon a time I used to loved December. Â I'd put up the tree, decorate like a mad-woman, sing holiday songs at the top of my lungs, dance around the house. Â Now I have become Scroogette. Â The commercialization really began to get to me 20 years ago. Â That was the year we donated money in everyone's names to charity. No gifts that year. Â I sort of came out of it for a while but the annual crap merchandising really wears on me plus the fact I HATE HATE HATE the fact that wallyworld insists on playing religious music while I'm shopping. Â If I want that I will go to church (and that would be a cold day indeed given I finally tossed religion out the window and admitted to my atheistic bent). Â 

So I grind my teeth as I blast around picking up the stuff I cannot get elsewhere and hope for the best. Â But the holiday feeling is gone. Â Two years ago I threatened to start celebrating Saturnalia -- at least THAT holiday isn't worn into the dirt by the merchants.

/rant

Â 

OK I posted a dream I had over on another thread with a challenge/request for people to give me their interpretations. Â One person sort of bit so I can only surmise nobody cared. Â But since I happen to believe that an unexamined life is NOT worth living I'm gonna interpret it here (and yes I know I said it was intensely personal but not in the way people might have thought.

Here's the dream:

> _A tall, blonde, nicely built man was smiling at me withÂ thatÂ twinkle in his eye. Â He approached me as if to kiss me but instead, just the tip of his tongue grazed the tip of MY tongue. Â Then he stepped back, smiled and went back to talking with another man. Â They were sitting on a rooftop, smiling and laughing. Â He kept glancing my way, though.
>
> _Then I was in a room trying to corral a couple of unruly kids. Â There was a large window overlooking the rooftop and I could see the blonde man sitting there still talking to the other man. Â He saw me looking at him and smiled. Â There was a door next to the window and it had come unlatched. Â I reached to open it and saw a screen door behind it and then a few steps leading up to the rooftop. Â As I reached to open the door â€¦._
>
> Â_ 

First of all, I hold to the belief that every element in a dream is a representation of the dreamer. Â The tongue thing? Â Represents verbal communication. Â The tall blonde guy? Â That's my male self. Â He's tall and blond and nicely built because I like that sort of thingÂ 

Â (Leonardo DeCaprio, either of the Hemsworth brothers but esp the one who played "Thor," Chis Evans, Brad Pitt -- the list is endless). Â 

ANYway. Â the twinkle in his eye? Â that's the challenge I keep posing for myself to put myself out there, to join in the fun, to stop hiding! Â The unruly kids? Â Me again. Â Undisciplined. Â Unrestrained. Â And I'm always trying to put on the ladlylike face and to hide the real me. Â That's the room with the big window which I forgot to mention was dirty ... like looking through a dirty aquarium. Â The door to that other self is not locked. Â All I had to do was push open the screen door, climb the steps and join in the conversation.

Hesitation comes from the part of me that lives in fear. Â Fear of being found out, fear of being reprimanded, punished, humiliated. Â 

The tigress paces in the background, waiting to be set free. Â The guardian keeps a close watch on the door. Â I'm caught between.

Up late again this morning because I woke up about 1:30 and couldn't get back to sleep for a while. Â I thought I had this little issue all ironed out but evidently not.

Only ate 2 meals yesterday ... not enough hours in the day(light) to get in any more. Â Fixed a quick ham salad for dinner and enjoyed a glass of that YUMMY Wild Horse Pinot Noir 2004 Central Coast. If you can find it ... it's very very tasty.

It's currently about 62 in here and I'm running around in my house coat and barefooted while hubby has just put on his vest although he hasn't yet started whining about being cold.Â 

time for a shower. Â Looks like it's going to be a boring, overcast day. Â Oh except it IS Wednesday which is drive hubby to the Legion for his weekly tÃªte-Ã -tÃªteÂ with the guys and dodge all the deer going and coming. Â That's plenty of excitement for today I guess.

The only thing I worry about when wandering around the house sans clothing is somebody coming to the door. Â Our house is a bit of a fishbowl -- good thing we live out in the country surrounded by ... country cause I wander around nekkid quite a bit. Â Wouldn't do to terrify the Schwan's driver or the UPS guy, now would it. Â I'll stick to running bare through the halls of the forum during the day. Â Less chance of causing someone to lose their last meal.

As to grounding, well my feet have been cramping again which is a sign they are really cold. Â Last night I had to put some footies on when I went to bed because they were so cold. Â So it's pretty unlikely I will be doing much barefootin' outside. Â Grounding will have to be done inside during meditation.

Don't have much to relate this morning. Â Am supposed to do CT but scheduled a haircut for 11 (duh) and just got up (lazy bones ... can't get up when the sun doesn't show up until about now either. Â We live behind a row of hills so the sun, even though it's LIGHT out, doesn't show up until about 8:30-9:00 this time of year. Â I'm taking full advantage of it. Â Might get the CT in later, we'll see.

One perplexing issue has cropped up. Â I broke out in a small cluster of ugly, red, itchy spots on my chest about 2 weeks ago. There were 5 of 'em ... yes large enough to count them.
