### DarleenMB — 2012-12-07T14:11:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66697>

Back again. Â Breakfast was inspired by Krista (I think) ... I chopped up 3 slices of thick bacon, sauteed them and then added in a tiny bit of minced onion. Â when the bacon was nice and crispy I added in a drained can of salmon. Â It was YUMMY. Â I also threw a few leftover shrimp on the plate and dumped 2oz of butter into my mug of decaf. Â 

You know, when the gas tank is full, trying to add more gas only dumps it on the ground. Â I could NOT finish it. Â could. Â not. Â Ate all the shrimp, am still working on the coffee but about 1/2 cup of the salmon/bacon went into the fridge for later.

Braved the scale this morning just for the hell of it and, no surprise, that huge jump the other day is long gone.

Cytomel ... day before yesterday I had a crash just before bedtime. Â It was weird. Â I had been in a GREAT mood all day long and it just evaporated. Â I think I must have run through all the T3. Â 

And today I realized that's why I used to get such a great feeling from drinking a mug of regular coffee. Â That caffeine high I would get did more than just wake me up. Â Since I'm back on full dose of 25mcg of the cytomel I'm feeling, not exactly euphoric, but just plain happy. Â I'm not grumpy or out of sorts and I have energy to do more than just sit in front of the computer all day. Â AAMOF I don't WANT to sit here at all. Â That's a real positive sign.

Random thoughts ... I know NO one on this forum will believe what I am about to say. Â But it is the truth. Â I am a very VERY private person. Â The fact I have been able to come in here and literally bare my heart and soul is astounding to me. Â It has not been easy. Â Giving anyone access to any information to my true self is like handing Jack the Ripper a scalpel and lying back on a bed and telling him to enjoy himself. Â It is terrifying like you cannot believe, painful in the extreme and something I simply cannot do out here on the other side of the computer screen.

Giving anyone access to the deepest parts of my personal being leaves the door wide open for them to hurt me, humiliate me, abuse me ... you name it. Â This is how it has been for me my whole life. Â Share a secret, get my throat cut while the person hurting me stands there and laughs at me.

so lest you think I am some freewheeling, let it all hang out, anything goes kinda person ... know that is actually the antithesis of who I really am.

And if I can do it ... why can't you?

Darleen - can't wait to see the haircut - I loved that movie. Â I took my grandsons and we had so much fun!

It is amazing Â - the things we are talking about - never would have dreamt that in a million years. Â Stuff that is kept inside is so hurtful/painful and kind of festers and does so much damage. ### DarleenMB — 2012-12-08T18:45:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66865>

Figured I'd better check in before the day is over with.

Last night was the annual UTU xmas party which I thought was NEXT weekend. Â And of course it freakin' snowed yesterday so we had the usual icy walk to get in the stupid building. Â They've been holding it at the Eagles out in the Commercial Park so the "street" is sorta paved. Â Add in a little snowfall and trucks pounding over it all day long and you have a skating rink. Â Last year I made the mistake of wearing heeled boots and almost didn't make it. Â 

I had wanted to wear my new twinkle toe shoes but since they are brand new had to wear my old(er) black flats. Â Nutz. Â  AND the top I had bought specifically to wear to the party was too low cut and those stupid red blotchy bumps are still there on my chest so ... oh well. Â And (almost done whining) my HAIR ... I had to wash it and discovered that my mousse wasn't enough to get it to spike like it should so it sorta just fell down and lay there limp as a cooked noodle. (HA thought I was gonna say something else, didn't ya!)

Fortunately I had the good sense to eat prior because the food is always atrocious. Lots of bread, overcooked beef, overcooked pork, mystery meat with some kinda barbecue sauce with probably an atrocious amount of sugar in it, pasta salad (getting the picture?) and two kinds of over sugared cake. Â shudder. Â Just cause it's "free" doesn't mean I have to eat it. Â Everyone had a fit because I wouldn't eat. Â I kept telling them I had had a meal earlier. Â sigh

Had a friend snap a pic of me and the hubby in front of the tree. Â Still haven't found where she left them on FB. Â IF her camera did it right (make me look 20 years younger and 50 pounds lighter) I'll post it.Â  { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

No more than fell asleep last night that DH woke me up because Sammy (the cat) was having a seizure. Â Why thank you dear. Â He's seizing, I can't do any more than you could which is pet him and try to calm him down but you HAD to wake me? Yes I love you. Â Good thing there aren't any heavy objects in the bedroom.

I lay there for at least 2 hours and finally FINALLY fell asleep again only to be awakened by a THUMP and thrashing sounds from the other bedroom. Â This time the seizure went full bore and I had to stay with the poor guy for about 5-10 minutes until his poor little brain reset itself and he was able to get up and walk. Â ANNNNNND I lay there for another 2 hours (until around 5:30) before I dozed off.

ANNNNND woke up about 7:30 because someone was running the VitaMix to make BPC. Â I'll give you a hint. Â It was not one of the cats. Â Since he had arranged with out of town friends to meet for breakfast before they began the trek back to Billings I hadda get up. Â good god.

I managed to make myself presentable and appear semi-lucid through breakfast. Â Forgot to take my cytomel before we left for town so was pretty well zombified. Â got home and tried to take a nap (stupid autocorrect!). Â No go. Â 2 hours and I finally gave up.

Am now listening to Mannheim Steamroller Christmas album and feeling tired but a bit festive.

Just had the last of the pork and sauerkraut so probably just some sort of light snack in lieu of dinner if I feel hungry.

And that's all I know.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-09T15:52:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66944>

Slept really well until about 4 a.m. ... crap on toast. Â Finally gave in and gave up and GOT up about 6-ish. Â Haven't been up that early in a couple of months. Â AND it was -2 out in the great outdoors. Â 61 inside. Â holy mother of pearl.

Could have just wandered out in the all-together and parked my bare backside on the deck for and hour and called it CT. Â Could have. Â Didn't.

BAB was the usual. Â along about 9:30 just as DH was rousting himself out of bed I decided it was time to go BACK. Â slept until nearly noon when he woke me talking on the speakerphone. Â DAMN WILL YOU GET YOUR HEARING CHECKED ALREADY?! Â I swear to god I am going to trade him in for a couple of 35-year olds.

Missed CT yesterday and had thought I'd do it this morning but after the second sleep decided no way -- I needed to shower so made it a cold one. Â Here's an interesting observation (which I'm sure all the rest of you already noticed) but when the cold (and our tap water is COLD!) hit my back it didn't feel cold at all. Â Silly little cold receptors musta seen it coming and went into hibernation mode.

Second observation ... the girls are getting out of control. Â Yes, my cups suddenly runneth over. Â since they are already too big in my inestimable opinion, I'm just a wee bit disconcerted over this development. Â On a scale of you-just-need-a-bandaid to Dolly-Parton-frankenboobs they fall more towards the latter but not THAT big. Â I'm thinking (always a dangerous activity when I've not had enough sleep) it must be all those fat cells releasing stored estrogen. Â I'm taking DIM and thinking I may need to take a little more. Â Also silymarin so my liver oughtta be clearing things out properly. Â anyway, thank god for the genie bra cause it s-t-r-e-t-c-h-e-s.Â

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-10T12:43:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-67006>

I dunno ... Â I used to be on the band-aid end as well. Â You're too young to remember the "pencil test." Â this was in the 70's ... you put a pencil under your breast, if it fell away you didn't need a bra. Â LOL. Â The only way I could have kept one in place then was if I used double stick tape. Â Then I gained weight and went from a AAA cup to a B to a C to a D. Â When I lost those 50 pounds starving myself on that stupid jenny-diet I was headed back into a B.

BUT I also know that the girls got WAY outta control from being estrogen dominant. Â Once I had the parts yanked and got onto just pitiful OTC progesterone cream I dropped nearly a full cup size. Â Which is why I'm wondering if I'm dumping stored estrogen. Â They're kinda getting in the way again.

I'm not really complaining too much though. Â Prior to getting on the BHRT they were pretty sad looking. Â Ick. Â Old lady boobs. Nothing like a boost to your hormones to get everybody awake again.

OK enough boob talk.

Here's something ELSE I've noticed of late that I keep forgetting to mention. Â My posture over the years has gotten really really BAD. Â You know that old lady slumping thing? Â My chiro told me it was common to women because of the weight of our breasts (thanks mother nature). Â I was having a lot neck pain and he told me I was hunching my shoulders. Â Straighten up, pull your elbows back and down and the pain stops. Â Who'da thunk.

Well, lately I find myself standing up really straight and tall with no effort or thought. Â It just is how I stand and walk. Â aMAzing. Â Who knows what's next. Â I'm feeling the need to dance so maybe I'll dust off that unopened set of ZUMBA dvd's.

CT this morning. Â Just about time to go fill the tub. Â It's overcast this morning, spitting snow, and about 33Â°. Â Winter has officially descended. Â Bother.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-10T17:32:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-67039>

CT was brutal this morning. Don't know why. Â My legs ached the whole 15 minutes. Â On my back wasn't much better. Â Water temp was still reading 48 so it's not gotten any colder. Â Oh well.

Just started to warm up and had to go to town. Â It was around 30Â° and the wind has been blowing for what seems like days so wind chill is anyone's guess. Â I was wearing a turtleneck, my vest (not down, either), slacks and my Uggs. Â Oh and my gloves because my hands get so cold. Â My teeth chattered and I shook all the way to town as I cursed Jack and his ancestors all the way back to **10\[sup\]12\[/sup\]**.

Fixing clam chowdah for dinner. Â Seems like the right weather for it. Â Lots of chopped clams and half and half. Â I'll have to throw in a couple of potatoes for hubby. Â 

speaking of whom. Â I meant to share his last commentary about my eating habits. Â It was the first day I fixed bacon and salmon for breakfast and couldn't finish it. Â Later he asked what was in the little container. Â I told him and also told him why I couldn't quite finish it. Â His comment? Â "You're going to gain more weight!"

Really?

Well how do you explain the fact I've been eating like this for over a year (any small weight gain I attribute to the stupid RT3 and lack of real CT) and I'm NOT GAINING? Hmmm?

In spite of all my attempts to indoctrinate him (aka educate him) he still clings to the idiotic notion that fat will make you fat.

At the xmas party I tried to explain to a friend (who has gotten such a pot belly in the last few years he could easily play Santa without any padding) that bacon is your friend, sugar and grains will kill you. Â I left it at that because the look on his face told me he thought I was out of my mind.

so much for being a starfish.

SO? Â Where is it? Â (what? Â You think I"m impatient? argh. Â No I'm not {shakes head emphatically} Â Not me. Â nuh-uh)

(puts away large, pointy stick)

Â 

Talked to my sister this morning. Â The good news is her "lump" is a result of multiple incisions along the same area. Â She finally had a doc explain it to her instead of just blowing her off. Â She did a lot of searching on WebMD and has finally relaxed about it BUT -- her weight, it seems, is making it worse so now she's talking about losing weight.

Want to take a guess what the next words were out of her mouth. Â G'wan. Â Guess.

ahem. Â "After looking at the fat content** in ground beef and turkey" (I'm paraphrasing here) she thinks she should get a grinder and do her own.

{insert sound of my head banging on my keyboard AGAIN}

Yeah, count your calories, limit your fat and you'll lose weight. Â unhuh.

She's also been "on a sugar binge" the last two weeks. Â sigh. Â AGAIN ... (paraphrasing) she thinks she's "trying to destroy her body" because she "hates" it because of all the abuse. Â But hey ... she's forgiven the evil monsters for what they did. Â yup. Â you betcha.

I JUST had this conversation with my friend yesterday (her childhood was another horror show). Â Maybe I'm explaining my position all wrong because they do. not. get. it.

Here's what I think. Â First off, in continually claiming she's subconsciously trying to destroy her body my sister is continuing to victimize herself (yes I mentioned the dopamine blog). Â claiming they have each "forgiven" their abusers both my friend and my sister set themselves up to continue to victimize themselves. Â It is also another version of the holier-than-thou-I'm-so-religious-you're-not set that it makes my teeth hurt. Â If you've forgiven them WHY THE HELL ARE YOU STILL TALKING ABOUT IT?

Forgiveness is such a loaded word. Â IMNSHO the ONLY person who can forgive you is ... YOU. Â True forgiveness does not come from the victim but the abuser who, once they face and accept what they did (and do it honestly) will be hard pressed to forgive themselves. Â But that is the only place true forgiveness can come from.

OK that probably doesn't make sense either.

Saying you "forgive" your abuser is a bandaid. Â It means nothing. Â Except that you are deluding yourself. Â Everything you do in Â life is a choice. Â YOUR choice. Â I wish I could get her to understand that.

OH well. Â I've urged her yet again to get over here and read the Leptin Rx quick start guide and the CT guide. Â You can lead a horse to water but you sure as HELL cannot make them drink.

Enough.

We had scrambled leftovers for dinner last night. Â I was lazy. Â Diced some ham, tossed in the last of the smoked pulled pork from Costco, diced some onion and red pepper and threw in some eggs. Â topped the whole thing with a bit of colby jack and part of an avocado that was languishing on the counter. Â Was actually pretty tasty.

BAB was bacon and onion and salmon. Â All but licked the plate, LOL. Â Seems I have not been eating nearly as much fat as I thought I was. Â mmmm mmmmm good.

Tossed a pot roast into the crock pot since DH and friends have moved their Wed. night bull session to thursday. Â Guess the dart teams got to be too much for them.

Up really REALLY late this morning ... only woke up once during the nightÂ 

Â last night and would probably still be in bed if sister hadn't called. Â Yes, thoughtful hubby brought me the phone and woke me up. Â Bless his pointed little head. Â Anyway, I'm still not showered or dressed so probably oughtta get off the 'puter and into the shower.

TTFN

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-15T23:48:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-67620>

LMAO ... yup, we're all definitely members of the Kruse's Krazies Klub. Â 

When the squatty potty arrived I handed the enclosed brochure to DH and just walked away. Â He has had major issues for YEARS (horrible job, horrible hours, horrible food ... it's taken its toll on him). Â He only mentioned it once but I know he's using it. Â hee hee. I bought him a pair of star shields from blu=blockers but he won't wear them. Â Says they pinch. Â Won't let me try and adjust them either. Â He actually had told me to get them but they interfere with his computer games. Â big baby.

He bellyaches about how cold the house is. Â Put on a sweater!!! Â I always turn the heat down at night so he cranks up his side of the electric blanket to "max sweat." Â I don't know how he stands it. Â My side is unplugged. Â And half the time I wind up kicking that blanket off.

Luckily, the van has separate controls for the heater so he leaves his cranked up to high and mine is either all the way over on Â cold or at the midpoint just between hot/cold. Â Hey. Â When I get chilled and it's 20Â° out and the car's been sitting in a cold garage all day I need a little help. Â I'm working on it!

So did you all see the price for the mov/nat thing next month? Â I figured (after I opened my big mouth) it was going to run at least two grand. I had already checked on airfare from Billings ... just a tidge over $500. Â Then there's hotel, etc. Â Bummer ... simply cannot do it. Â besides, I'm not cold water adapted enough for that. Â Again, maybe I can justify attendance at a Denver workshop. Â I can drive down (that'll save about $400), don't need a motel/hotel (I'll stay with the outlaws) which'll save another couple hundred and if I can actually sell a bead or two I may be able to scrape together the money for the workshop without touching the household funds. ### DarleenMB — 2012-12-17T13:03:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-67697>

goood morning! Â Feeling great this morning except for that damned rash thingy on my chest. Â Still there. Â Still itching. Â Going to the PA this afternoon and have HER look at it.

Decided to switch back to eggs for breakfast since I'm almost out of canned salmon and not looking like any trips to Costco in the near future so ... Â Cooked up the bacon in the skillet, the better to keep all the baconyfatty goodness for the eggs. Â Slapped on some commercial guac I found hiding in the back of the freezer the other day, added the usual HUMONGOUS hunka' butter to my decaf and I'm good to go for most of the rest of the day.

FBG was only 85 this morning where it's been around 105 - 110 recently. Â Thought it might be because I had some extra carbs last night and then remembered I'd added 1/2 tsp of cinnamon to my morning coffee yesterday. Â But would the effects last that long? Â dunno. Â Seems the fewer carbs I eat the higher my FBG goes. Â I don't get that.

Today is also massage day. Â THANK YOU! Â My upper back is tied up in knots from sleeping with so few covers. Â Since I eschew bed clothes (why the heck you would wear CLOTHES to bed is beyond me!) my bare shoulders get really chilled by early morning and the muscles tend to cramp up. Â So once a month I get to see my massage therapist and SHE gets to take a sledge hammer to them and try to get them to loosen up.Â 

You would think that all that lying about in near-freezing water would make them immune to cold air but, alas, not yet it seems.

WHOA thought I just lost this post because of my sometimes-you-irritate-the-\*\*\*\*-out-of-me Mighty Mouse. Â It works with finger swipes across the surface and sometimes just the slightest movement of my finger makes it go back a page when I don't want it to. Â whew.

Accomplished about NOTHING this weekend and the workload is piling up. Â Well, as much as a workload can when you're mostly retired. Â Although I'm feeling perky I'm also feeling low energy again today. Â Sure wish this would clear up.

oh well.

TTFN!

Just got up ... need to go fix breakfast but wanted to check in and see if anything new had developed. Â 

Am very VERY unhappy with hubby. Â He was watching tv in the bedroom last night with the door closed. Â I went in to get Missy so I could feed her before I went to bed. Â When I opened the door this BLAST of hot air smacked me in the face. Â He'd turned up the little heater to MAX WARP and it had to have been 85 in there. Â It's not that big of a room ... about 13 x 14 ... so it doesn't take much to overheat it. I have asked him before not to do that because I cannot sleep when the room is that hot.

sigh

so I grabbed my pillow, kindle, reading glasses and sleeping mask and went and slept in the guest room. Â I have always thought THAT room was really cold but last night it was perfect. Â Must be the CT. Â 

Speaking of which, yesterday I had to go to town and left wearing a VERY light, long sleeved cotton t shirt. Â No socks, just my summer canvas slip-ons. Â I didn't even feel the cold (it was 30Â°) until I got to town and even then it was just noticeable. Â Must be the CT.Â 

Today is my "little" brother's 59th birthday. Â Assuming he's still alive. Â I forgot to ask my sister if she'd heard from him recently. Â He and I haven't spoken for 2 years since he decided everything that was wrong in his life was my fault. Â sigh

I hear ya although I don't know what your brother is actually doing but, we, older sisters continue to worry even when they are adults.Â  Must be the mother in us.Â 

I have a whole family of bro's and sis's who are killing themselves.Â  Probably not quite like yours but none-the-less still doing everything they shouldn't be.Â  My one brother has a drinking problem and a very large belly to show for it; he also smokes.Â  He also has extreme inflammation and takes meds for it.Â  He told me he knows he shouldn't eat grains as they affect him but continues to anyway.Â  His fingernails look dead and disgusting and all he does is take drugs for it and his inflammation.Â  Another sis is a nurse who now has diabetes and takes premarin for her hot flashes.Â  Tell me she doesn't know better.Â  I am done teaching and if they ask, fine; otherwise, it is about me.Â  I am not angry at them anymore for not trying.Â  It is who they are and they have to make the change.Â Â  I have another brother who belittles me cuz I talk about all of this to anyone who will listen.Â  Unfortunately, if he is in the room, he makes a big deal about it even if I am not talking to him.Â  Go figure!Â  Some people are interested!!!

I sure hope you little brother wises up soon and realizes that big sisters usually know way more than they!

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-19T12:21:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-67960>

Vickie thanks for sharing. Â boy am I glad I only have one of each. Â My brother has been drinking himself to death for sometime. Â 2 years ago my sister and I went to his house which was left to him by our late evil aunt/adopted "mother." Â I use that word loosely because a rattlesnake has more mothering instincts that that woman did (how she could have been remotely related to my Mother \[her sister\] is beyond me). Â ANYway. Â It was a beautiful little place in a very nice neighborhood.

Have you ever watched "Hoarders" on A&E? Â It was worse. Â And not because my brother is a hoarder but because he is a drunk. Â I burst into tears when I saw the place. Â I was so angry! Â How could MY BROTHER be living in this cesspool?

It's a good thing he wasn't home (he had gone off to Denver and had left the door unlocked). Â Both my sister and I, and her husband and son were shocked beyond words. Â The STENCH alone was enough to knock you off your feet.

Last summer he wound up in the hospital. Â His liver is failing. Â I don't know how much longer he has but if he continues down this road (which I'm sure he is) he won't be around a lot longer. Â 

He found someone through his so-called girl friend who bought his house, cleaned it up and let him live there for 2 years (the rent was held back from the sale price). Â They finally had enough and tossed his sorry backside out into the street. Â He did find a place up near the Devil's Backbone West of Loveland. Â Out in the sticks so he can drink to his hearts content and not be bothered.

I cannot believe any of this, still. Â Both my sister and I have fought like mad to overcome everything we went through and to build decent lives. Â My brother is just as smart as we are but more lost, I guess.

There's nothing I can do. Â Nothing. Â Except ache for his loss.

### endless — 2012-12-19T22:18:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-68025>

I have an alcoholic brother as well. unfortunately he's a very 'functional' alcoholic and in complete denial so I have to stand by and watch his health slowly go downhill for now. He's already lost his marriage, and is now on the verge of losing another long term relationship over it. He's only 36. Pisses me off, but I can't do anything as long as he won't admit it.

I am tolerating cold better too, but only to a point. Yesterday I walked outside an hour or so, and took my coat off for most of it. Yet later I left the house in a windproof shell with the same t-shirt on underneath and was outside for an hour attending a guy having a heart attack in a chicken coop and I swear I could NOT warm up after that! Maybe I can only tolerate so much in one day!

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-21T15:09:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-68137>

Tune of the day ... we have like 3 radio stations here and until recently none of them were worth turning on the car radio. Â We now have one that plays oldie moldies for the likes of me. Â Last night as I was bringing hubby home from his weekly gabfest at the Legion they played one I had not heard in 30 years. Â I had forgotten how much I loved it. It is the most haunting song I have ever heard -- light on the lyrics but the guitar work just sends me flying.

Emerson, Lake & Palmer ... "From the Beginning"

Use Spotify to check it out. Â You won't be sorry. Â And while you're there, you might want to listen to "Father Christmas" -- a nice bit of music for the holidays to go along with ANYthing by Mannheim Steamroller.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-22T12:30:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-68182>

I"m seeing people here having such great success with this and here I am ... shouldn't have gotten on the scale. Â My face is all puffy this morning and I feel like crap. Â I know I'm still fighting off some cold virus. Â Weight is up even more. Â I'm closing in on my highest weight ever and I want to scream. Â This just isn't fair.

Yesterday I had my BAB, around 2 I was a tiny bit hungry so had the last 2 slices of braunschweiger and a glass of kombucha and a piece of dark chocolate. Â Dinner was a bowl of chicken soup and another mug of BPDC. Â That's been pretty much the norm for weeks. Â Little to no lunch because I'm seldom hungry. Â Small dinner because I'm not hungry. Â Yes to the blu-blockers from sundown on. Â Yes to bed early even if I'm not dropping off quickly like I was. Â I'm tired this morning and cranky and so disgusted and disappointed.

My attitude and my weight both stink.

Maybe I should just go back to bed and pull the covers over my head until June.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-12-22T13:14:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-68185>

I can't believe I just lost a post.Â  Will try again!Â  I feel your frustration.Â  I AM back to my highest weight.Â  It doesn't bother me too much when my mood is good because I love having a postive attitude even if I am overweight.Â  BUT when the mood sucks I get disgusted too.Â  Your moods don't stay bad for long and before you know it you'll be in the right frame of mind again!

I have to share a dream I had last night.Â  Where this came from I have NO idea!Â  I didn't remember it until I came to read your journal and saw your picture.Â  I dreamt I met you.Â  I don't know why but it seemed like I was outside your house, there was snow on the ground, it was cold, your were wearing a blue sweater like the color in your pic.Â  As you were rushing past me to get into the house I recognized you (we hadn't been introduced at that point) and said "Geez Darleen look at you! The weight you lost!Â  Why didn't you tell us how well you've been doing!"

So, there you go!Â  I'm seeing your success before you are!

### diane — 2012-12-22T13:34:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-68187>

Paulalynn - that was a cool dream!Â 

I'm trying to focus on how I feel rather than the weight. Because if I did focus on the weight, I would definitely feel more frustrated. But when I am focusing on weight, it's usually me judging myself, which I'm really working on changing. So by focusing on how I feel, it keeps me motivated and moving forward, and more likely to keep changes happening because I can definitely see changes in how I feel happen faster than weight.

I also think that having days here and there where your attitude stinks are perfectly acceptable and I try to embrace them! { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. I think my little pity party is done for the moment.

I also wonder if the meds I'm on for this stupid rash might not be causing some fluid retention. Â And I KNOW my chest hurts ... has for several days. Â Not a lot. Â Just enough to let me know a cold virus is lurking somewhere trying to wreak havoc and is fouling my mood.

Decided to try and up my daily cytomel by 1/2 a tab and see how it goes. ### Charlotte — 2012-12-23T09:20:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-68235>

Merry Christmas, Darlene. OMG I spent all morning, reading your post. I still have a lot of catching up to do. You made my Sunday. I cannot help myself but admire your attitude and persistence despite all the issues you had to face. I am a pretty persistent woman, but I am not sure I would have stayed on track as you did. Even though your husband might be in a bad mood (which hopefully has lifted by now) he must love you for your wits and humour. Here is an educated woman who knows how to think and reflect on herself and be experimental about her issues.

It is hard for me to put into words how I feel about your story, but the essence of it is that that you hold gifts that are rare and most precious. You never buckled or gave up, you are a fighter and that is something I truly admire. You will succeed, I am sure because defeat is something you can never accept. And thank you for sharing your story and letting us know who you are. That shows how couragious you are.

When I started reading your post I was totally sucked in. But I had some urgent chores to do, which I would have loved to postpone but could not. So in order to get back to your post quickly I stuffed my nightgown into my leggins, put on my jacket and drove to work to check on my freezedryer which was supposed to be finished yesterday. I couldn't bother to waste time on getting dressed. I snuck out of the house, pulled my car out of the garage which is pretty tight hoping to be back in 15 minutes and above all not to catch any attention from my neighbours as I was definitely a sight I did not want anybody to see. On my way out I hit the garbage bag I had deposited there yesterday and the garbage spread all over the place. Because it is quite windy I had to pick it up immediately, so I got out of the car and started collecting. And that was the end of plan. Just as I was bending over to pick up a disgustingly fishy smelling paper wrapping my neighbours all came out of their house. They must have been getting ready for a sunday outing. It was definitely not the church they were heading to, at least not the kind of church we think of for a sunday morning. The husband is a scrawny looking guy who has acrylic fingernails, long and with patterns on them (like they do them in nailshops), the wife is 300 pounds for sure, blond with streaks of green in them and the daughters, OMG, had pink and blue hair, with tight black clothes around a waistline that fits their mother's. What you also need to know is that I had a long tear in the seam of the leggin in the back, which I had forgotten to remember when "getting dressed". I immediately remembered. So here I was exposing my big, naked ass to my neighbours picking up garbage. My nightgown had loosened and came down from under my jacket and there was no place to hide. The cascade of emotions that went through my body you can imagine. They looked at me as if I was some nightmarish apparition and then the guy started grinning. I then realized that I was just another member of an ongoing freakshow in a world full of people with damages. Some, or maybe even many, cultivate them and some of us are doing their best to fix them. I tend to think that you and I are among those who fix them (which I will do today by mending the seam of my leggins as a reminder of an outward fix to the inner) and what the heck, I have been carrying my damage all my life with me for everybody to see which has also been a constant reminder for myself what needs fixing. And just this makes me grateful, because there is nothing to hide which cannot be seen by everybody anyway. It sets me free to live my life the way I chose. Same goes for anyone else. The difference? WE KNOW! Aren't we just really lucky? We win so many times over.

And then I jammed my kitchen door which holds the pots and pans last night and I just cannot get the damn thing open. I tried everything short of just breaking it. So I cannot cook and it is Christmas, no chance of getting a handyman. At first I was totally frustrated then I resigned and thought: ok, then it is raw. Fortunately I still have access to my fridge and all the other kitchen utensils, so it is going to be carpaccio, minced raw meat and even raw tiger prawns and salad. It is an opportunity not a catastrophy.

And since my freezedryer needs my attention through the holidays I cannot travel as I had planned to visit friends which means I have to stay home and go to the factory every few hours. My first Christmas all alone, as my children do not come home this year. Catastrophy? No, opportunity to rest, sleep, eat well and do some things I never got around doing so far.

So all is well here at my end and it is true that there are many things beyond our control but we definitely control the way we act and feel about it. (Just don't write another Readers Digest version of your story to soon and get me into trouble)

So I wish for you this Christmas that all is well at your end too and that you have a wonderful Christmas with your family and friends.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-12-23T10:21:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-68239>

What a wonderful post Charlotte. Touching and hilarious...after your description of the events of yesterday you should be writing sitcoms...priceless!Â

### Charlotte — 2012-12-23T10:52:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-68241>

that is very kind of you Paulalynn, thank you for appreciating. Life just holds the best stories. All we have to do is see the humor in it. Now I am off to another trip to the factory. I repaired the leggins, so no more embarrassment from there and I removed the garbage bag from the front of the garage. But I still refuse to put on "real" clothes. Why bother. It is 4 in the afternoon and no reason to get dressed now anymore. So lets see if I can stay out of of trouble despite.

I hope your Christmas is merry in spite of being by yourself (which is always the BEST company anyway) and that you have a spectacular new year. Â I'm looking forward to hearing more about YOU!

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-23T16:38:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-68257>

FINALLY. Â WHAT is going on with the forum? Â I've been unable to get it to allow me to post for hours now.

Still have 2 gifts to wrap, one to buy and waiting on one. Â Went on an archeological dig in the garage and found my OLD OLD aluminum tree. Â It's pretty forlorn ... think Charlie Brown and you've got it. Â So now we're "decorated." Â That was part of DH issue the other day.Â 

He's used to me doing all the work while he sits around and enjoys it. Â I'm tired of it and I'm done. Â Our garage is detached and it's a good 30 feet from door to door. Â YOU try dragging box after box after box of holiday ornaments across ice and snow, haul them indoors, unpack, decorate and then drag them back out to the garage only to rinse and repeat in a few weeks. Â Then there are the cards. Â I'm sick of it. Â So now he bellyaches about me not helping. Â Tough.

/grumpy

SOOOO

I'm reading another book on chakra meditations. Â Good thing. Â I decided to start doing my "color" meditations that I used to do all the time. Â Basically I breathe in white light and fill my body up from my toes to my head with light. Â When I exhale that breath takes out all the negative stuff. Â Weird things started happening when I did this regularly to the point I began to feel like I had lost the boundary between myself and everything else. Â It got scary so I quit.

So now I'm reading in the tantra book that this is a good thing. Â OOOOkay. Â If you all say so.

Yesterday I tried to get my mind back into the groove for meditating. Â Breathe white light in ... WTH??? Â Nothing but ash coming out. Â I'm serious here. Â Ashy gray tending towards brown. Â UGLY. Â I'm working on my first chakra which is closed up tight. Â Seems they're all shut down. Â Well beans. Â 

Looks like I have a lot of work ahead of me.

I'm thinking of trying to find my "buddy" the silversmith who found a way to "charge" water. Â He made the most interesting meditation tools. Â He'd come into my studio when I had it out of the house, wave his latest little concoction at me and laugh as my aura blew up. Â I'm very sensitive. Â That's me. Â A delicate little flower.

And as part of my new early resolutions for next year and the rest of my life I have decided to quit being so negative about my physical being. Â I stood nekkid in front of the mirror this morning with a new attitude. Â You know what? Â Not so bad. Â Not nearly as bad as I have been telling myself. Â Rubanesque, yes but not ugly. Â Being attractive starts in the mind. Â So I'm going to concentrate on one positive thing about my body each day. Â Today ... I have gorgeous skin. Â That's a plus, yes? Â Yes!

The only one who ever worries about what size my clothing is is me. Â So starting today I am stopping that. Â I am beautiful just as I am.

Merry Christmas everyone!

### Charlotte — 2012-12-24T05:51:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-68290>

Hi everybody, some day... not too far away I will tell my story. It is long, mind you, because I had a very busy and evenful life and I am looking forward to a lot more. I am, what you call a busybody, always moving, always doing something. What I want to say is that at one point in my life I realized that a lot of me being miserable came from judgement. Judging other people is easy and even holds very funny moments, but judging others and me is only two sides of the same coin. Judging me (I am so ugly, I am so fat, I am not lovable, and so on and on) makes you miserable. Sure, because whatever you do, you never match up to your own ideal. And even if you are objectively right, the pure comparison with something we perceive as ideal will pull you down. So I started questioning my own thinking. Thoughts come and go, our brain is geared to thinking and that always within its limits of conditioning, we have received from the day we are born. So I think that what Dr. Kruse means by thinking out of the box is putting aside the conditioning and start being innovative in our own thinking. To me that means to question my own thought processes and ask myself if it is true what I am thinking. And sure enough, most of the time it is not.

And the mind is always repetitive in thinking that our experiences from the past will repeat themselves in the future. Like: oh, if I do this then that is what is going to happen, or, I always failed at this so I never succeed. Is that true? Sometimes, but often we get surprised. And that is also how fear is created. Fear is also just a thought process. Of course there are situations, when fear must happen, like just before a car accident or when in a situation that is simply dangerous, so one can react adequately, but this is naturally embedded in our bodies and brain. But why does our mind keep repeating such situations, projecting them in the future and making us fearful, negative, undecisive, reluctant, etc.? We compare situations from the past with what might happen in the future. And our imagination goes wild.

What I mean by all this (boy am I philosophical this morning, must be because it is Christmas and I haven't talked to anybody in a few days) we should not waste time pondering about all the thoughts we have about us and the world but make up our minds about what we want to change and DO it. Make a plan and go by it. And if that turns out to be the wrong route, then take another one or make the necessary adjustments. The brain has only one function and that is being a TOOL to achieve what you want to achieve. The mind is just a blabbering thinking machine, very repetitive and not useful. So lets use our brain by making it optimal, for which Dr. Kruse is providing the tools, to guide us to do all the things we truly want to do keeping it focused on our goals.

Darleen, how could you ever be ugly? Maybe you have some more pounds on you, but does that make you ugly? NEVER.

You are so beautiful inside, you have shown so much of you and there is so much in you that is adorable it must show on the outside I am sure. And you know what? I just love intelligent people and I do not mean people who have an academic degree. I mean people who have the intelligence to think for themselves, come to their own conclusions and do something about it. That to me is true intelligence. And something else, these are the people which whom you can have the best laughs. It is rare. And I just love laughing, it is the best remedy I know and in these moments I am at ease the most. Hopefully one day we can all laugh together and have a good time.

I think that Dr. Kruse should have his own conference one day where we can all meet.

Okay, now I must clean up the mess, the wild cats did on my terrasse. I put out some raw meat last night and they dragged it all over the place. Funny, but the birds also seem to like it.

And it is beautiful outside this morning. The sun is shining.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-26T14:03:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-68434>

Thought I should check back in. Â I've been reading all the other journals, just don't have much to say today.

Slept like the proverbial rock last night. I started closing both sets of curtains in the bedroom and didn't wake up until 9:30 this morning! Â I feel like I'm hibernating. Â I don't want anything to eat except bacon and all the fat, butter and braunschweiger. Â I'll eat stuff but I'm just not into it. Â 

My BAB has devolved to 3 slices of thick sliced bacon plus all the fat, 2 eggs scrambled into the bacon and fat and a mug of BPC with 1/2 a stick of butter. Â Then I'm good till late afternoon when I might have a handful of raw almonds while I ponder what I want to eat which is generally nothing. Â So I have a bit of braunschweiger and another mug of BPDC with 1/2 a stick of butter. Â Maybe a few more raw almonds. Â meh. Â I'd rather sleep.

See what I mean?

I haven't been in the tub for a week now. Â In 15 minutes though I'm filling the tub. Â This should be interesting since it's been COLD here. Â 5 yesterday when we went to friends for dinner and -2 by the time we got home. I went without a coat but forgot my gloves so my hands got really cold and on the way home I wound up turning the heater on full blast. Â It was my hands that were really bothering me. Â The rest of me was ok. Â I was aware of the cold but, again, meh. Â No big deal.

Now if you knew how I have had to bundle up every winter since we moved here you would be stunned. Â I wasn't even wearing my boots last night! Â Just knee-highs and my dress loafers.

Hope everyone had a great christmas. Â In addition to the snow globe I got a meat/food grinder attachment for the Kitchen Aid ... yeehaw! Â Steak tartare here I come.

Final word of the day ... Â **WEBINAR**!!!!! Â

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-26T17:22:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-68446>

I couldn't decide if this should go here or in my personal journal. Â both places I guess. Â I was dreaming away this morning just before I fully woke up. Â It was, as usual, pretty bizarre but it relates to my recent commentary here. Patience. Â You'll see how and why.

_In the dream I had entered St. Joseph's church which is, or was, THE Catholic church in Fort Collins when I was a kid. I attended parochial school there for 7th and 8th grade as my Mom thought the public school boys would be, I dunno, too "rowdy" or something. Â If only she had known. (insert snarky little giggle).Â _I walked up to one of the pews, genuflected and made the sign of the cross and then knelt. Â As I looked around I saw a few people here and there and I kept asking myself WTH I was doing in the church since I am anti-religion and an atheist. Â Not exactly in those thoughts but that was the gist. I was watching the priest at the alter and he was getting ready to offer communion. Â I still didn't understand WHY I was there. Â then the thought came to me, clear as day. Â **Comfort**.

Ah. Â 

I debated about taking communion then remembered that even though IN THE DREAM I was baptized in the Church I hadn't Â been confirmedÂ_ (Just for clarification I am a fallen away Episcopalian which is often referred to as first cousin to the Catholics or a Catholic who forgot their Latin. Â Any and all that I learned about Catholicism came from 2 years of indoctrination at St. Joseph's school. Â Sr. Olympia NEVER forgave me for being a "heathen" or for knowing the prayers better than any of my classmates.)

Instead I walked back out of the church. Â But my car was no where to be found. Â It had been stolen._

That's where I woke up ... not the first time in a dream my car has been stolen. Â I'm still not sure what that means. Â But the rest of the dream ties back to my getting back to my meditative practice. Â I realized last night that I have fallen far away from any kind of spiritual practice. Â And the result has been the state I currently find myself in. Â Completely closed up and receding further and further from life.

I'm not sure how this happened exactly but it has been accelerating the last few years. Â So I think it is a positive sign that I finally realized I need to get back on The Path. Â Life, for me, lived without any kind of spiritual connection is no life at all. Â Eating a healthy diet, getting enough sleep, studying & learning, being creative ... it's not enough. Â Not for me. Â 

There is comfort in some kind of spiritual practice no matter how you go about it. Â Mind, body, spirit all need attention. Â In neglecting my spiritual side I have also lost my creativity. Wonder of wonders, in just focusing on my intention to open my chakras and heal them that creativity has come roaring back. Â I read a story on HuffPo the other day about "the world's sexiest grandmother." Â Seems some 76 year old woman is writing hot novels for a publisher called Ellora's Cave. Â That story sparked something in me and in just thinking about inspiration for writing anything I came up with half a dozen possible plot lines. Â We watched the last Harry Potter movie on xmas eve and here came the bead ideas ... Inspiration is everywhere but if you are shut down you don't see it.

My point to all this is, for me, opening up to your heart, your true self, is not easy but it is necessary to heal all aspects of your life. Â Mind. Â Body. Â Spirit. Â All three need to be healthy to make a whole person.

Still considering doing the (safe) tanning bed thing this winter.

Still feeling punk. Â Going to do CT in about 20 minutes anyway. Â At least the cold will take my mind off things for a bit. Â have I mentioned how much I LIKE the cold these days? Â I mean I seriously cannot take even warmish water in the shower anymore. Â I splash icy tap water on my face every single morning. Â It feels WONderful. ### DarleenMB — 2013-01-01T13:32:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-68911>

Well, now even Chrome is choking up. You may now insert 4 letter words of your own choosing along with fuming volcano smiley.

Krista, I'm actually finding the colder it is the less "painful" it is. Or maybe I'm finally making some forward progress.
I had a heckuvatime falling asleep last night. DH was watching football late into the night so I took a page out of Ealachan's book. Didn't help. Sure was fun though.

then the hubster woke me up somewhere in the middle of by yelling at the cats. They like to lie all over him because he turns his electric blanket up to max sweat. My side is unplugged and half the time I throw it off because I'm so warm. Like last night. I think I finally cooled down around 4A.M. with the rest of the house.

Somebody around the valley set off their annual NYD fireworks. I couldn't figure out what the noise was for about 10 minutes. Then I remembered. I miss Allie McDog who hated the noise and would set up barking so I always knew what was going on.
Feeling sorta OK this morning. Juries out. Speaking of which I drew jury pool from now until April so have to remember to check the website to see if I'm called in. Civic duty and all that jazz.

Got the laundry done yesterday but not the ironing. I wouldn't want to strain myself. So guess what I'm doing today. bleh.

Today's music recommend: Don Henley. "If Dirt Were Dollars" because he manages to work "uncomprehendingly" into the lyrics. As in "she stared at me uncomprehendingly like a cow at a passing train." bwahahahaha. Not only can he keep the beat he's smart! And "Building the Perfect Beast" with it's quick nod to evolution: "Ever since we crawled out of the ocean and stood upright on the land" ... DUDE! Who says rock 'n roll and intelligence are incompatible?!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-01T13:49:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-68915>

I just looked up & downloaded some music from Sons of Anarchy, helps with my withdrawal lol, might have to start re-watching!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-02T12:37:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69012>

Woke up around 3A.M. and lay there for what was probably close to 2 hours. Ever since I started the cytomel in earnest it's been like this.

Nothing exciting to report. Well except for the fact I think I may need to cut back on the fat. My stomach has really REALLY been bothering me lately. Last night it just flat out HURT. Since I started sucking up all the bacon fat for breakfast I added in a bile acid factors capsule which sort of helps. Lunch yesterday was sardines and a small avocado. No pill and upset tummy. Last night we had beef short ribs which were very fatty (and very yummy) and my stomach hurt so bad at bedtime I couldn't sleep. I wound up scrounging for the mylanta tabs ... relief! So I'm evidently still not digesting well. pooh.

And today is CT. I decided to switch things up and start on my back for a change. Even with the drain cover a lot of water escapes while I lie there so I decided it makes more sense to start on my back and get better coverage and more cold than just sitting to start. We'll see how it goes.

I keep waiting for the magic to happen. Then realized maybe if I stopped eating so much beef and started chowing down on the fish that's in the freezer then (a) my stomach might quit hurting and (b) something in the ol' biochemistry might shift. You know what they say. Shift happens.

### janagram — 2013-01-02T13:17:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69022>

lol...I sometimes need to take a full spectrum enzyme...I don't have my GB anymore but have had no problem eating tons of fat, except that I will take the enzymes if I feel indigestion coming on. I hope that doesn't sound like an oxymoron, the fact is I can eat quite a bit of fat without trouble. But why not help your digestion out? Bromelain is good too.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-02T14:15:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69028>

Janagram, no GB here either. Maybe I'll try some enzymes instead of the bile acid factors. One seems to be not quite enough lately and 2 will give me unrelenting heart burn. (sigh) And I just placed a BIG order with vitacost and then this morning realized I"m out of maca. DANG it!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-02T19:13:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69063>

Just a quick checkin. CT was only on my back and a goodly portion of my front. Except for the girls and they're quite happy they didn't get the big freeze, thank you very much. 40 minutes and woulda gone 45 but HBOGO pooped out on my and Netflix wouldn't load and Spotify sounds like crap straight out on the iPad so BOO. I gave up. Skin was BRIGHT cherry red -- gold star for me today. I'm still trying to warm up.

Off to see Django Unchained ... I absolutely LOVE Tarantino's stuff.

Music of the day ... Led Zep ... nothing like a little head banging to get the ol' heart started.

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-02T19:16:46+0200

### diane — 2013-01-02T19:20:34+0200

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-02T23:09:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69080>

Django Unchained was AWEsome! Jamie Fox was incredible. Not to mention sexy as hell. And unless they did some fancy computer stuff he can sit a horse and ride like nobody's business. WHO KNEW?! And, oh yeah. The scene where's bound, gagged and hanging upside down -- nekkid -- was a real eye opener.

The movie was a tad long but not boring at all. I really enjoyed it, DH was so-so. There was one scene where the whole audience (all 8 of us) were howling so loud I thought I'd wet myself. It reminded me of the campfire and beans scene in Blazing Saddles. See, there's my 5th grade sense of humor being tickled pink again. I'm SUCH a goofball.

So if you go ... DON'T DRINK anything before or during. LOL. Or you'll likely miss something when you go to pee.

### endless — 2013-01-03T00:17:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69092>

Oh, good. I'm looking forward to seeing that. Knowing me I won't see it till it's on video though...I hate sitting in theatres.

I've always loved that line about the cows too

Did you buy the drain cover, or just rig one up? I used a ziploc freezer bag and 'Tuck' tape (sheathing tape? It's red) to make one and it works pretty good!

### diane — 2013-01-03T10:56:16+0200

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-03T12:36:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69140>

Diane I'm not a fan of gore either but Tarantino splashes it around with such abandon you can only laugh. He has this thing for the color red and he uses it profligately in this flick. He does, as usual, put in an appearance and I was shocked at how much weight he has gained. Even Hollywood isn't immune to the fat factor.

Decided last night that maybe my tummy issues are being caused by the bile acid factors I've been taking. So I'm skipping it today and we'll see how it goes.

VERY interesting dream this morning. More later. Gotta run go brush my teeth. I'm really enjoying sleeping in.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-03T14:57:49+0200

The weather widget says it's 26 right now. I spent 19 minutes out in my chaise rockin' out with my iPod and it felt WONDERFUL. As long as the sun is shining and the wind isn't blowing I'll be doing this on non-CT days. It was GREAT!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69200>

You know- I was thinking earlier that this is something that I would probably do. Except that I live downtown with no privacy lol.

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-03T17:28:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69211>

OMG...nude sunbathing in the winter....awesome!!! That sounds more appealing than a tub of cold water...if only I had somewhere private I might join you! Now tell us about the dream!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-03T17:59:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69218>

Paulalynn that's the great thing about where we live. We have neighbors but no one can see me in my back yard unless they actually come INTO the yard.

OK, the dream. I was with a large group of people (I have just SO many facets to my personality ... sometimes the crowd is GINormous, LOL). Some stuff happened prior to this part but it isn't really relevant and I've lost that thread anyway. Big surprise ... a man came up to me. He was relatively nondescript which was kind of surprising. He took me in his arms and we began to dance. I closed my eyes and it was like I was floating. I felt my hand on his back as he held me close. All I could see was this bright golden light through my closed eyes. I sensed everyone around me was not very happy I was dancing with this guy. I didn't care. We danced and danced and at the end I wound up stepping in dog doo (WTH, LOL) so we stopped so I could clean things up. He went into the bathroom and I had words with some of the other people. When I went into the bathroom he was sitting in the tub, back to the shower head, with one of those large, light beach towels draped over his head and around his body.

More stuff ensued and the dream changed course. Not important.
\======================
And what do I make of this?

The male side of my/your brain is the logical, rational side ... the part that loves math but isn't very good verbally. Right side is creative as hell, verbal to the max, the exact opposite. Male and Female. Yin and Yang. My embracing of the man in my dream is me embracing and accepting the male (sexual) side of myself. the muttering was the peanut gallery trying to heap guilt on my head. The bright golden light? This is the second dream where that light has shown up and I still am not exactly sure what it represents. It's important, obviously, but what does it mean?!

My consult with Jack was very traumatic. But I asked for it. Yes, I did. I accept that. I needed to have someone in "authority" kick me in the butt and tell me to grow up and stop hiding from this very important aspect of myself. And here I am, a couple of months later, embracing that part of myself. It still is not easy. Maybe that was the dog doo-doo, LOL. and it's still scary. that sexy-as-hell rock goddess is still locked up in her gilded cage and she's rattling the bars on a daily basis. She still scares the crap out of me because who knows what she will do or what mischief she will get up to if I let her loose. She's uncontrollable. And she doesn't give a rat's backside, either.

So the work-in-progress continues. But the progress is remarkable. There is no WAY in hell I would have gone out into my yard before and sunbathed in the nude. NEVER EVER EVER. Who the heck IS this woman???

### diane — 2013-01-03T18:31:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69221>

Darleen it's just amazing how you're pushing yourself and facing even the scariest parts. You have been such an inspiration for us reading your journal and working alongside your journey. And just when you think you've reached your limits - you go further!

I would have never have gotten the same interpretation from your dream - that's so cool. In terms of the light - it's funny, when I do face dunks for a few days I start to see a light while I'm immersed in the water. I wonder if there's a connection?

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-03T20:02:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69229>

Hi Darleen...I lOVE the idea of nude sunbathing in winter...If you could work in grounding at the same time it would be ultra fantastic.

Here's my two cents on some of your dream....from a Jungian perspective...dog doo, basements, mud, getting sucked into dark tunnels etc...all can represent the primordial dark that we still carry with us...the old dark shadows and impulses that live in our unconscious. The while/golden light could be your integrated authentic self emerging...just as you said the male and female aspects of you are uniting for a quantum leap in being. The dog doo often shows up when we are shifting ourselves to a new level....it's the old stuff ready to try to suck us back down into the ooze. My thinking is that the ooze is important, but not as a controlling force...it's part of life...just like the light is. So when we are comfortable with all aspects of the light spectrum we are integrated. .... my humble opinion is that you are transforming in all ways!!!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-03T20:09:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69231>

OOOO SeaHorse!! thanks! that is really cool. I hadn't thought of either of those in that way but it makes a lot of sense.

OK Jack posted something on FB about mutant cucumbers causing baldness on the old Vajayjay. then me mentioned vajazzling. WTF? Of course I had to google it and have spent the last 5 minutes laughing hysterically at the definition and the PICTURES! OMG ... bedazzling taken to new heights. Or is that lows? Whatever, my only response, besides LMAO was OUCH!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-04T13:03:55+0200

Never heard of it but now have it on my Kindle ... thanks!

JUST as I was getting ready to go fill up the tub for today's CT DH crawled outta bed and into the shower. sounds like he's sitting on his little homemade shower seat and running hot water on his back. sigh. He refuses to listen to me about embracing the cold and how it would help with his pain. No one can diss you like loved ones.

Sun is shining and I"m thinking hmmmmm maybe a bit of nude sunbathing after CT? Seems I like running around (figuratively that is) outside sans clothing. WHO IS THIS WOMAN?

Actually was up about 8:15 this morning. Had tilapia for dinner last night and woke up about 7 with my stomach yelling at me for food but I was too lazy to get up. FBG was down to 92 (has been a little over 100 the last several day) so I think it was combo of the fish and no cytomel yesterday. I got my bottle of 50mg tabs of iodoral and took one of those yesterday instead. I know I do not eat nearly enough seafoods and was thinking this might be a better pathway than taking the synthetic T3 to help clear the RT3. We'll see.

Gonna whip up Charlotte's recipe for German Chocolate Mocha Torte today. Sounds divine!

Wow Darleen. When you embrace something, you really EMBRACE it! Look at you and the nude sunbathing!

Would love to hear how the torte turns out!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-04T14:43:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69323>

45 minutes of CT proved to be too much. I lasted all of 2 minutes outside. SHaking really hard rightnow. brrrrrrrrrr

### janagram — 2013-01-04T16:07:30+0200

### Charlotte — 2013-01-04T16:50:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69343>

Gosh Darleen, everytime I read your post, I roll around laughing. You are so funny. I have this vision of you all pink and rosy running naked outside having a ball.

Now about the cake. I baked it yesterday and decorated today. This was my first try at this without any sugar. It smelled heavenly but it had some big holes in the cake. I don't know exactly why, maybe I did not fold the egg whites carefully enough. So do not make the batter too firm, Rather use a little less eggyolks, meaning, just separate maybe 12 and do only 4 whole eggs. That way the batter is a little more liquid and comes out more fluffy. I need to try it again. (Good reason to make another one soon)
The cream was perfect. One suggestion there also. Use just a little (very little) bit of hot water to dissolve the coffee and let it cool before adding it. Tomorrow I make a picture and post it (if I am not too stupide to do this.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-04T17:01:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69345>

Naked in Wyoming. Hmmmmm. Cowboys, Indians, main character with no clothes on. Hmmmm.

Charlotte, thanks for the tip!

Now then, it's a helluva note when one cannot differentiate between nausea and hunger. Evidently I burned off my entire breakfast while lounging in the tub. About 1 I finally figured it out. so I had an avocado, some braunschweiger, a tiny bit of a lovely English cheddar, a dill pickle spear, some Kombucha and for "dessert" a paper thin itty bitty square of Gjetost -- my new fave cheese. I feel better but my neck still is tight and I still have a tiny headache from it.

So I guess the only thing to do is wait just a few more minutes then go hang out in the massage chair. Funny, we bought it because of hubby's neck and back injuries (he was headed to get on a locomotive and slipped on the ice which the railroad had thoughtfully neglected to clear off the pathway). But I'm the one who uses the darn thing daily. Same with the Teeter Hangup I bought for him. They both help him but he won't use them. MEN.

### endless — 2013-01-04T17:18:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69350>

I find 45 minutes is too much for me too, in the sense that it takes me forever to warm up after. I've been sticking to 30 minutes or less. I haven't SEEN any sun to sit out in here, too overcast. I might try the rolling in snow once we get some fresh...it's too crispy right now.

I would love to have a massage chair! I'd be in it all the time.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-05T13:14:52+0200

Woke about 8:30 when DH got up to leave for breakfast with the guys (his usual Sat. routine). Too tired for any oxytocin therapy. sigh.

Waiting for my bacon to finish crisping up so I can add in salmon and an egg. Groovin' to Stevie Ray Vaughn, again. TASTY tasty blues. With a rock edge, natch.

Music for the day .... ANYthing by Steely Dan. ### DarleenMB — 2013-01-05T15:45:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69472>

It's sunny and about 19Â° here right now. Nude sunbathing ... whoohooo. 20 minutes to toast the girls and about 10 for aunt fanny. Pesky breeze kept messing with me. I'd no more than warm up and here it came. Just enough to cool me right off.

My fingers are really cold, my feet chilled but the rest of me is ready to hit the showers.

Afternoon music recommends ... latin rhythms. Try out Bebel Giberto. Lovely Brazilian tunes.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-06T15:13:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69612>

gah. CT was BRUTAL today. Water same temp as always ... just under 43Â° but it took 2 minutes to get "acclimated" and I shivered the whole time. That was new. Toughed out the full 45 minutes though, skin bright cherry red. yay me.

Got dissed again about my breakfast. Hubby walked up, stuck his nose at my plate and sniffed. "What's THAT?"

"my usual .. bacon and eggs with the rest of yesterdays bacon & salmon."
"Bacon and FISH? EWWWWW."
"well why not ... you like bacon wrapped scallops."
tsk

My appetite yesterday just wasn't there. Left about 1/2 of cup of my breakfast on the plate (see above). Had a handful of nuts in the afternoon and yes YES I know I should NOT be eating them this time of year. I'm trying to stop. And yes, I know .. .I am VERY trying. Decided about 6 I might be a LITTLE hungry so had some braunschweiger, a couple/3 sweet potato chips, a dill pickle hunk, and a few slices of pepperoni sized hard salami. Or as it's spelled on the pkg ... salame. Was fine the rest of the evening.

Bed and asleep by about 10:30. Woke at midnight feeling so sick to my stomach I thought for minute there I was going to hurl. then decided maybe it was EXTREME hunger. Yeah, still have a little trouble telling the difference. Got up, went to the fridge (no lectures now ... I KNOW!) and rummaged around for a bite of anything to shut my stomach up. Finally settled on the tiny wedge of French brie lurking in the bottom drawer. Cut off 3 thin bits and inhaled them then drank a glass of water.

Oh dear, now it's even worse and I was about to get on my knees to the porcelain god. Finally decided everything was staying put so crawled back into bed in a position to make a sprint for the bathroom if needed. Fell sound asleep and didn't wake up until dawn.

Now it should be noted when I first went to bed it was like somebody turned my personal furnace on full blast. I don't know how to describe it ... it's not like the screwed-up=hormonal heat from menopause. My body just starts running hot. It heats up my entire side of the bed ... you can put your hand on the mattress where I have been lying and it is HOT. and then the twitches start. when my thighs heat up like that they get twitchy. It's NOT restless leg syndrome. OK I'm just weird, I already know that.

This morning I was STARVED. Ate my whole BAB ... 3 pieces of thick sliced bacon, all the fat, 2 eggs and the leftover 1/2 cup of BAB from the day before PLUS the usual BPDC made with 4 TBS of butter. Scarfed it right down.

Which makes me wonder about the effect the Iodoral is having on my metabolism already. 50mg coupled with regular CT and now nude sunbathing (when the sun is shining, that is which it is NOT today ... all overcast, boo hoo) in 20Â° weather. puhleeeeeeeeeeze let it be a positive sign.

This heat thing you're experiencing is amazing.....something is going on...

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-06T18:26:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69648>

Caroline, I am reading the book "Iodine, Why you need it, why you can't live without it" by David Brownstein, MD. I thought I'd try a big dose to see if it helped my thyroid as I'd rather not take synthetic anything. And I am not eating enough seafood. I'm working on it but am not where I should be at all. So I figured what the heck.

Was your iodine a urine test to see how much you've excreted overnight? According to the book, that's a good way to determine how much you need.

I'm just fumbling around here trying to see if I can fix things. And I'll tell you what ... I've only been on the Iodoral for a few days but today about an hour ago I checked my temp and it was 98!!!! I don't remember WHEN it has been that high. Somethings working.

Yeah yeah ... MORE FISH, thank you Jack. I got the message.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-06T21:43:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69678>

Just finished dinner ... 1/2 pound of mussels and 1/2 pound shrimp. MMMMMM. I had a packet of sardines for lunch with a couple bites of the French brie and a glass of kombucha. That was at 2 and by about 5 I was hungry again. Hopefully I got enough dinner that I'll sleep through the night tonight. I can't believe I'm suddenly eating this much!

Drank a liter of steeped green tea. Odd little thing ... I'm peeing a lot while I'm drinking it. Must have some kind of diuretic effect?

time for a mug of BPDC and then I'm done for the night. Adding in CO to this one. YAY fat!

I've never heard of Iodoral. Must look it up! Sounds like something has got you fired up!

### janagram — 2013-01-07T14:06:39+0200

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-07T14:07:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69758>

Saw a post on FB this morning that really explained a lot:

> On a very low carb diet one may experience higher than normal blood sugars, especially in the AM dawn phenomenon because you get simultaneously peripherally insulin resistant, (a normal healthy response to glucose deprivation) and more insulin sensitive to CNS tissue. The sugar arguably is higher for the CNS to have access to it. Thus you can have "high" fasting blood sugar but basement level A1Cs. I am not sure I accurately portrayed this process as I am no subject matter expert. If so, let me know how I am incorrect. I first read about it on Hyperlipid Peter's blog below after Brook Dubose informed me about it.

Last night, as I mentioned, well, last night, I had nothing but mussels and shrimp for dinner. My FBG this morning was up from 91 the day before to 123. EVERY time I skip or limit carbs at night, the next day my FBG is UP. Not that I understand AT ALL what the AM dawn phenomenon is but I'm working on it.

On another note, probably FA or it could be SO, the sun is shining and I need to ditch my clothes and go plop on the chaise for a little sun therapy.

Also have to pay the bills today and do laundry. snrrrkkkk. I hate mondays. Even in retirement they suck.

Oh yeah, had that heat thing again last night. Not quite as intense as the night before but it was there. I'm feeling ... mmm, how to describe it? Energetic is a bit too extreme cause I haven't felt that in about a decade. Slightly more HERE? More ready to get off my prodigious backside and DO something? Definitely a good sign. Must be all the fish and all the iodine.

Oh and my new sig line? Homage to Jack who not only stands alone but is head and shoulders above any crowd he might find himself in.

### endless — 2013-01-08T13:21:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69871>

I think I have the same problem with my FBG....it's almost always high in the morning. I should do a test and eat some carbs for dinner to see what happens.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-08T15:31:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69892>

OMFGHSMFDIATH ..... CT was beyond brutal this morning. I didn't bother to take the water temp before I hopped in but it has to have dropped another degree. If it's over 42 I'll eat my hat. It burned, it was so damned cold. I never did stop shivering ... 45 minutes and I'm feeling like a giant popsicle.

Yesterday I managed a whopping total of 20 minutes out in the sun. High cirrus clouds and 19Â° meant not a lot of sun warmth. I gritted my teeth and toughed it out. the last bit of open water on the creek has finally iced over. Must be January.

On the dream front, that little embrace with my higher self the other morning has resulted in issue ... I dreamed this morning that one of "us" gave birth to a baby girl. I immediately took her as my own, loving and cooing, Mommy's here. While I was busy getting dressed in a beautiful gown the rest of the crew took her outside to play where she was already growing by leaps and bounds. this should be interesting. I wonder what "she" is going to be like. Nothing like a little transformational change to brighten one's outlook.

Went to the movies yesterday afternoon ... please RUN away as fast as you can from Tom Cruise's new flick "Jack Reacher." That's 2 hours of my life I want back and right now. Got home late so fixed a quick tuna salad and had it on an avocado. done.

Still chugging that liter of steeped green tea daily. Really working on eating fish at least once, if not twice a day.

Got the chores done yesterday but now have to go to town to run errands. Big excitement out here in Wyoming. Yes indeed. My life is just the envy of all.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-08T15:39:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69896>

Hey Pink Popsicle, my doctor explained to me what happens when BG is up in the morning when not eating carbs. He said it is a counter reaction of the liver, producing glucose because it is low (something like that). He said it is normal and nothing to worry.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-08T15:54:57+0200

Have noticed something amazing today. My mood has been sky high! I have been happy and laughing and just enjoying everything including a dreaded trip to wallyworld. My energy feels better today. I ate BAB about 9:30 and wasn't hungry until about 4 when I got home from my foraging expedition. At THAT point I could have chewed off my arm and eaten it. HOLY CATFISH I was hungry!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-09T14:36:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70061>

Ashryn, You little dickens you! I wasn't really censoring anything. I just like to let people fill in their own choice vocab.

Sun is shining this morning so about ready to ditch the clothes and head out to see how long I can manage a little sunbathing.

I'm sure I'll get an eyeroll or two from DH as I stagger out the door with my cushion, iPod,speakers and timer.

Figured out this morning why I have probably felt so good recently. The coffee I have been drinking is NOT decaf. crap. I wasn't getting any buzz off it so no wonder I didn't realize. Or maybe it's because French Press brewed isn't as heavy on the caf as regular brew. shrug. Could also explain the difficulty I've had of late in sleeping. Nothing major just sometimes difficult to drop off and sometimes waking up. It was, however, beginning to bug me.

Rather than fill up my Optimal Journal with day-to-day stupid stuff, I'm MAKING myself post it on my own blog. I see all the fun HTML buttons are missing again so here's the addy:

www.darleenmb.wordpress.com

Off to catch a few rays.

Oh yeah, my new avatar ... taken 5 years ago at DisneyWorld. Nemo is hiding behind my back -- that is NOT my hair, LOL. 50 pounds lighter after starving myself on the jenny stupid SAD diet for a year. too bad it's all back. Even though my thighs were still big and I was still a bit thick through the middle I sure looked a lot better than I do right now.
