### DarleenMB — 2013-01-10T13:51:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70167>

You might want to cover your eyes as I'm sitting here nekkid as the proverbial jaybird. It's another gray, overcast day so no sunbathing. I may have to cave and go see how much the tanning beds charge. I have 4 more weeks of 50,000 iu Vit D to get through and then another week before testing again. I've been taking 10,000 iu's on the other days in the hopes of pushing those levels up to normal.

Crawled outta bed at about 9:40 this morning. I guess I'm going to have to start leaving one set of drapes open. I'm logging between 9 and 11 hours snooze time every night. Puts too big a dent in my day.

Today is Thursday (in case you all were wondering) which means about 90 minutes of uninterrupted, blissful ALONE time late this afternoon after I take hubby to the Legion for his weekly confab with the other vets.

Had a little trouble finishing my BAB this morning. OK it's not THAT big but it is FAT laden. Some days I can pound it down and lick the plate (that was yesterday) and some days, like today, I have to save part of it for lunch.

FBG was 81 this morning. Gotta remember to ask the new doc, when I find one, why the syn. T3 makes it go up.

Still miffed at myself for drinking regular coffee the last couple of weeks thinking it was decaf. I sure hope that wasn't the entire reason for the boost in mood and energy. Keeping my fingers crossed as I come back off the stuff. You'd think I could read. But noooooo.

Well I only have 30 minutes to CT. That screechy sound you'll likely hear followed by a string of undecipherable but blue words will be me as the cold water smacks me.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-11T14:39:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70311>

OK, I went back into my old photo albums and dug out some pics from a 1975 road trip to Taos. I weighed about 145 at that point in time:

New Year's Eve, 1979 I looked like this:

Well cannot post the link because of a stupid assed rule that says MAXIMUM NUMBER OF ALLOWED LINKS EXCEEDED: 2 Allowed. Who thought THAT up?

Now go look at my xmas pic again. Yes there is a definite difference which all the nice clothes and good skin and makeup cannot cover up. Ask Jack how he felt when he was at his highest weight. Ask anyone who is more than 20% overweight how they feel. I've said before it's hard NOT to let it get to me. 7 years ago I was an emotional mess. Today I am determined. No I don't like how I look, I never will while I'm at this weight. But this time around I don't hate myself. This time around I understand (pretty much) how and why this has happened. And instead of falling into the pity pool or worse yet, starting a restricted calorie diet, which was how I used to try and handle it, I'm working on repairing the damage.

While I appreciate all the kind words, please understand, I don't believe them. And yes, I know that is just me judging myself. Hard habit to break. But I have made some progress because previously I wouldn't have asked to have that picture taken in the first place let alone post it here for everyone to see. Now to press the post button and see if this looks ok. I sure wish we had that preview button or better yet, the "go advanced post" button. I like to check what I've written and am getting tired of having to edit posts because I typed too fast or spell something incorrectly (which I NEVER EVER used to do. WHERE did I put my brain?). anyhoo. Here goes.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-11T14:39:58+0200

Here's the link to the New Year's eve 1979 pic where I weighed in at about 132 pounds. THAT's where I want to be only healthy, not stupid.

### Inger — 2013-01-11T15:09:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70320>

Darleen, I thought you were much fatter than in the picture... You are no way looking obese at all..huh! You look so sweet you and your hubs!

Now.. If it was me suffering that much over those extra pounds.. I would go raw. 100%. I would bet my head you would soon have to worry about beeing too thin. To follow the epi paleo diet but raw, eat as much as you want.. raw fat.. lots of it, and protein too. You could still try it Darleen! Just for a week.. and then see..but I bet you would not stop.. you would not want to miss that feeling out.. and the pounds just disappearing.

And there are lots of delicious stuff you can make eating raw too!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-11T15:44:24+0200

### caroline — 2013-01-11T21:36:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70362>

Darleen - the thing is this....since you are quite tall .....I have always thought that your goal weight was WAY too low......I am saying this really,really gently and with the utmost kindness. I am 5"5"
and am pretty good at 132 - you probably need to weigh about 160+ or so????? for your height? Have you ever tried looking at someone you think is an okay size and then ask them what they weigh? It is very often a real eye opener! Are you fixated on a number because it was a happy time? to me \[expert that I am!\] You look excruitiatingly thin at 132 - plus you are just the teeniest bit older

What if you picked a reasonable number and then re evaluate? I think this could be an "ask Jack" I have always had this self image problem - I am fixated on a number for some reason which has no bearing on my bone structure etc. When I was young 125 was the magic number - don't remember why - makes no sense. I seem to not understand what size I am - comes from verbal abuse when younger I am sure - and when I see myself in a pic I think - is that what I look like???? holy crap - I don't look too bad - but I can't seem to assess this on my own - no matter how old I get!.
I think maybe JK has to give us a talking to about our own body image - after all - like you said - he has been there. I wonder if it is different for a man????

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-11T22:16:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70366>

Caroline, when I weighed 130 pounds it was definitely NOT a happy time. I weighed 142 for a long time before my body rebelled and started packing on the pounds. I'm very comfortable at that weight. Now where my body decides it's happiest is totally out of my control and I know that. 160 though is just too fat. That pic from 1979 was a result of starving myself unmercifully. I was living on 750 calories a day. that's why I didn't look so good. 130 might be dreaming but I know that's where my body wanted, at one time, to be.

I guess that bears a bit of an explanation.

While living with my grandparents before my Mom died, the three of us were left pretty much to our own devices. We weren't exactly starving but you show me an unsupervised, wild 9 year old who knows anything at all about nutrition. Plus there wasn't much to eat. We did have lots of eggs (grandparents raised chickens) and raw milk (they had a couple of cows) so we did have that.

When Mom died and we went to live with the evil aunt and her husband we suddenly found ourselves getting real meals 3 times a day. I dropped 10 pounds in one short month without even trying. A couple years later when I started skipping meals again my weight went back up. There's a pattern there. And a lesson.

Again, it's just a number and I know it means nothing. I'm looking more at those pictures now and wishing.

### caroline — 2013-01-12T02:30:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70375>

At the end of the day we seem to be such slaves to our emotions - I guess we got screwed up years ago and it is pretty hard to change no matter how much we want to.......we have shed a lot of things lately tho - you especially!! ..... it is that guy with the flashlight ........

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-12T09:23:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70383>

Caroline you are not going to like this post so ... sorry.

I woke up at 4 this morning feeling like I was in the middle of a heat wave. I'd left the furnace on a bit higher than usual because the wind and the temps yesterday had the house like a freezer. So now I cannot go back to sleep because I"m hot. I can feel the room air is chilly but forget it. Now I'm wide awake and I'm starting to think. Never a good thing this early.

I'm wondering WTH is wrong with me that I cannot get out the door and DO the things I'd like to do. We haven't been on a trip together for 2 years because I just can't make myself get with the program. Once you get me out the door and to whatever destination then I'm fine and have a great time. I just don't like all the prep work and the actual getting there. This time, though, I"m wondering what's wrong with me that I cannot get off dead center about the movnat thing in Santa Fe. Part of me really REALLY wants to go play outside. But there's that other part, the one that keeps me home with an endless list of why I can't.

Why? Two things are going on. One is I am content right where I am. We do not have some fancy schmancy house ... in real estate parlance it is a 2 bedroom "cottage" although at 2,000 sq feet that seems a bit of an understatement. What we do have is 4 acres, privacy, quiet, live water bordering the property (right in the backyard actually) and I am HAPPY here. that one is easy to deal with.

The second one, well, it's kind of difficult to lie in bed at 5A.M. crying and trying not to wake up hubby. So I got up, fixed my coffee and here I am spilling my guts. Before you all start chiding me you need to understand and accept that I am talking about my emotions. Logic has no place here. I can stand in the left side of my head and lecture myself from sunup to sundown and agree with everything I say. won't do any good. Emotions are what they are. Trying to wrestle them to the ground with logic is a total waste of time.

It started because I couldn't figure out why I'm doing the whole approach/avoidance dance with the movnat thing in Santa Fe. It isn't that we don't have the money. We do. It is because I am loathe to spend that much on myself. DH would tell me to buy the plane ticket, sign up for the class if I wanted and go have fun. \*I'M\* the one who's telling me NO, not him. So WTH is it?

Never open a door if you aren't prepared to deal with what's behind it. 5:00A.M. ... What's behind Door #3, Monty? biiiig mistake.

I'm right back where I was in 2006, sitting at the kitchen counter with hubby and bursting into tears because I didn't want to go to Seattle to be with friends because I am ashamed of how I look. I hate myself for being so fat. It doesn't matter that now I know WHY and that it isn't some personal failing on my part. I'm broken but it doesn't matter because every single time I look at myself in the mirror all I see is bloat, fat, ugly. I KNOW how I look and I hate it.

When I was in my late 20's and my sister was morbidly obese and pregnant and I was a thin 140 pounds I remember thinking I would NEVER let myself wind up getting that fat. Never say never.

I try not to go there because down that road are the same behaviors that got me here in the first place. I try to be mindful that I'm eating enough which part of me finds hysterical given how much I weigh. But that's my danger zone ... that stinkin' thinkin' that whispers in the back of my head "you're fat because you eat too much. Stop it." The hidden self-bully that tells me I won't ever be thin if I don't stop eating. If I really want to beat myself up I could always climb on the scale. Boy that always adds to the fun.

So there's the demon I thought I'd exorcised. Holding me prisoner because I hate being seen in public by strangers because I'm so fat. It doesn't matter that I \*KNOW\* I"m judging myself far more harshly than anyone else. What matters is this is how I feel back in the dark corners of my heart where I don't want to look.

Please don't try and tell me it's OK and that I'm something I know I'm not. Again, this isn't about logic. This is about emotion. this is how I feel not about what I know.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-12T10:41:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70387>

Darleen, my heart goes out to you and you are so right, there is no logic and it is not about logic, it is about the deepest secrets we have about ourselves, which nobody can understand, except one has been in that place. And there is nothing to say, nothing to console only pure raw pain. I am crying here with you because it sounded so familiar so very much like the same place I have been practically all my life. It is so BIG to where nothing anybody could or would say matters, as it just does not match your feelings about oneself. So, I have no advice for you as there is none. But I am there with you right now.
So cry your heart out, go to that place inside of you, take that pain and use it for you.
And Darleen, I admire you for this post, you are an honest to God person who is not afraid to show to us who she is and take us to her darkest place, the most fearful thoughts one can have about oneself. To me, that is greatness and true grace.

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-12T10:53:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70388>

Darleen....something to think about:......emotions, as you say, don't really respond to logic, so one way of working with them is to listen to them (like you did this morning) and just sit with them. Don't try to "make" them go away, but get the message that they hold for you and THEN see if they will move on. In the work I do, it has been very useful for people to understand that we carry a "false self" around with us, but that each of us also has a innate "authentic self." So where would all these emotions of self loathing come from? They don't come from your authentic self...that's for sure. They are the accumulation of outside messages, experiences, survival patterns and coping strategies. They were responses to the outside world....not a recognition of your true self. They are part of you....but they don't have to control you. So listen to them and try to counterbalance all the un -useful stuff with some activating of your authentic self. I'd say each time you get in a bath you are tipping the balance...each time you take care of yourself...you are strengthening the muscle of your authentic being. It's ALL in there, you are finding your way to uncovering it.

So your authentic self is yearning to go to Santa Fe, but way the false works is to throw a fit if you start to change the norm. (Like an emotional detox) The fact that you were able to work through all of these emotions and then write about them allows you to see how strong that belief is for you (I'm too fat and too ugly to be seen) This is FANTASTIC!!! Give yourself a real thumbs up. Your well fed brain is working to unclog all the thinking and feeling that has been the way you've worked in the past. Don't try to reason your way out of those beliefs...just say thank you very much, that might have worked in the past, but I've got a bath to get into and some raw meat to eat and as I continue to feel alive and strong I will add some wonderful explorations and trips to my life.

Ripping too many emotional blankets off at once, doesn't serve us....but neither does staying in bed. I think you're finding your balance as you heal.

### vkiernan — 2013-01-12T11:34:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70392>

Wow Seahorse, what a message. Darleen, I'm borrowing your message if you don't mind sharing! I have never thought about anything in that manner.

### Inger — 2013-01-12T11:49:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70394>

Darleen, I am taking my hat off to you, beeing willing to try more raw. I am thinking... for people in your age coming from a culture where raw meat and fish was seen as dangerous, it is not easy at all. I see it in my parents (my brothers.. they just chow down raw chicken hearts from fresh killed own chickens.. all kind of weird stuff I tell them to try..lol my younger sisters too.. they have no issues with raw meat at all). They really struggle with eating meat raw, the do eat my salmon tartare though and love it, and chevice and marinated herring and stuff. They tried oysters first time at new year and my mom had big difficulties but they did it! So I so much understand it is not easy. I think you are so bold Darleen. You tan naked too. To read about how you try new things all the time makes me so warm and happy in my heart because I know you will feel better and better all the time! It makes me just so happy.

Your last post could have been me. I have had the same issues Darleen. I still have it at times but it is so minor now in comaprison with how it was.. Thinking I am so ugly I have to hide.
I can tell you what help me acutely, when a bout come over me like that. I concentrate on the other people.. that are present, that I was/am about to meet. I just consciously forget about myself, as if I were transparent. I suck in the impressions from my surroundings.. I learn to get intrested in others.. I forget about ugly me.
Then.. to do me as pretty as I can before leaving help too. Put on my favorit clothes and colors, do a nice make up, use some nice jewerely, make my hair. And then after that I just forgot about me. I had done my best about improving my outlook so I leave the worrying behind. one can only do ones best at the very monet, and that is enough. Then.. to concentrate on my surroundings.

It always helps me to eat the good stuff too. When I cheat it makes all worse. It is weird but my eating habits helps me tremendousy, they help my mind the most. They makes me feel not so scared! Yeah I know it sounds weird..lol They kind of makes me open up, or to get more transparent to my surroundings, so I feel more one with everything. This oneness just leaves the suffering we talk about above, behind! It is magic..?!

IDK I hope it does not sound like I have lost my mind.. because it is for real. it is very real. I just do not know why exactly it works like that but I am sure Jack could explain.

### Inger — 2013-01-12T12:04:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70395>

What SeaHorse said about not ripping off too many blankets at once is very wise me thinks. Be good to you, do it in your pace. When you feel you are ready, step by step. That is how I do. I am by far not done yet. I know so much I still need to do better but I am not stressing, I give me time.
Too much ripped off at once might just cause trauma maybe... we need to grow with the changes so they stay.
The only important thing IMO is, that we go forwards and change...

I just want to thank you for being an amazing example. I tend to do the opposite, I 'hide' from people I know rather than ppl I don't know. ### Lyndra — 2013-01-12T16:15:02+0200

### caroline — 2013-01-12T17:18:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70436>

I am so very sorry Darleen if you thought I was judging you in any way ...I definitely am not .... that is my life story as well. So much of my life passed me by because I didn't want to go anywhere being the size that I was. I was an obese teenager \[with tons of pimples\] and ugly glasses and an even uglier haircut because my mother took me to a barber...... and carry a whole array of scars from that. Teenagers can be so cruel but nothing compared to the things we say to ourselves. I can remember so clearly - not wanting to live \[I wouldn't have done anything to myself - coward here\] but on a day to day basis my life was such crap. The very ugliest of clothes ... my mother thought you should have 2 sets of clothes - one to wear while the other is being washed. I guess in retrospect - I was doing C/T as a kid ...my mother made me wear an old men's coat to school....so I wouldn't wear it ...I would just hide it in my bag!! see - every cloud has a silver lining

I really think all of our terrible stories - give us definition and complexity and understanding and kindness. I think all of our stories make it hard for the people who love us - we aren't easy .....but we are real.....

### Inger — 2013-01-13T03:16:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70481>

The guys in school said to me I was ugly too. Such things you just never forget.
I had to use ugly clothes too all my childhood and never was allowed to keep my hair open, but always in two braids that was so thightly braided my head almost hurt.
I had pimples too and still have scars. I always had ugly skin. I always got read as a tomato because I was so ashamed for the smallest thing. I have weird white spots on my body (on parts that mostly are covered with a bikini) that I always was so ashamed to show.. no pigmentation at all and they got easily burnt too. I always wished I could be normal like others but hey.. I finally came to accept better and better who I am with my weird things and all. It really helps to focus more on others, how can I help and make their life easier and happier.. and then think.. oh my body is what it is because my mom ate almost only potatoes as she was pregnant with me.

and I stop comparing myself with the more beautiful ones and just concentrate on how I feel instead. To do the good things to me.
When I feel great everything is just beautiful! Every other person around is beautiful and I think I look so okay to when I check in the mirror and I really think it is all in the brain/heart!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-13T12:23:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70503>

You guys are just the BEST. thank you.

I was going to come in and claim my cats had snuck in and written yesterday's post without my knowledge but they claim they don't know my password.

ahem

Holy falling estrogen levels, batman. I haven't been THAT weepy in ages. Those of you who are certain age will understand. THAT kind of weepy meant I would sob uncontrollably at toilet paper commercials. Seriously. It would get that bad.

thank goodness Vickie mentioned that my complaint elsewhere about sore nipples meant too much progesterone (or the converse, too little estrogen which is what the OD who prescribed the Wiley Protocol BHRT for me told me). She reminded me (because I know I ran across this somewhere ... damn Jack do you have to be so cotton pickin' prolific? ... that CT will lower estrogen. It's a darn good thing that the night before the meltdown began I added an extra line of estrogen to my normal dose (which is what the OD told me to do previously when I complained about it). So extra 2 lines yesterday and extra one this morning. I skipped CT all together yesterday. I really wasn't in the mood anyway but somewhere in my poorly functioning brain it occurred to me that might not be the best thing to do until I got myself leveled back out.

Still a little sore this morning, physically and emotionally but feeling a bit more normal. god what a trip down the rabbit hole THAT was.

Speaking of the rabbit hole, every single time Jack mentions oysters my brain goes click click click:

"The time has come," the Walrus said,
"To talk of many things:
Of shoes--and ships--and sealing-wax--
Of cabbages--and kings--
And why the sea is boiling hot--
And whether pigs have wings."

"But wait a bit," the Oysters cried,
"Before we have our chat;
For some of us are out of breath,
And all of us are fat!"
"No hurry!" said the Carpenter.
They thanked him much for that.

EEEEEK. MAKE IT STOP, LOL

Back here in the real world, my new concern is that I am not eating enough. Seriously. Yesterday I had 3 strips of thick sliced bacon cooked in the pan then added in the other 5-6 oz of steak tartare that I hadn't finished the night before (I checked and I'd used about 10 ounces of meat but then couldn't eat it all) and an egg and then the usual BPDC with way too much butter. I"m thinking I'm probably tossing about 6 TBS or so because I'm getting the handrolled stuff in the 2 LB block and I just whack off a chunk in the morning. FAT I want FAT. Note that does not mean I want to BE fat, it means I want to EAT fat. Left about a half cup of the BAB or so because I couldn't finish THAT. During the day I had a couple of handfuls of raw almonds (yes yes I know ... NUTS OUT OF SEASON, make it stop!) but for dinner I had another mug of BPDC and then what looked like about 3-4 ounces of Costco's pulled pork. That was it.

I had no hunger other than just before dinner.

OK gotta run Sounds like the bacon is done cooking so need to finish up the BAB and EAT.

Later tomaters.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-13T14:13:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70520>

while I was in the midst of my little tantrum I did what any good, red-blooded woman would do.

I bought SHOES. TWO pair!

I've been jonesing for a pair of red shoes all winter long.

SQUEEEEEE ... I just hope I can WALK in them when I get them. We'll see.

And for giggles:

although I just spotted another pair I may have to get instead. But I like the ankle strap. The only part of my body that is skinny is my ankles. so maybe I should just stick with my first choice.

### ashryn — 2013-01-13T17:57:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70576>

Caroline.. No! You should always point yourself at the one you're attracted to.. Judging whether you'e going to speak to someone based on what you assume he will think of your place on the 'hotness' scale is doomed madness! People are attracted to each other for all kinds of reasons and physical beauty is way down in the scale of importance for all but the shallowest of beings.

Imnsho..

Darleen, I would love to be the person with the awesome words that will change how you feel about yourself, but i suspect even the most perfect, inspirational ones would be useless. So i just wanted to chime in and let you know i feel for you, and know what you're going through, and about myself at least, i have come to realise i am never going to look at myself and like what i see. Yes, when im fat i jump on the easiest thing and think everything would be awesomer if i was thin, but i remember being thin and actually, i didnt think i was thin, i still found something to dislike..

Perhaps it is the thing that stops us being 'read-only' people.. We are constantly striving for self improvement... Unfortunately sometimes the striving gets sabotaged by the evil emotional imp that makes us unreasonable and has an odd idea about what needs improvement the most.

I dont have an answer, most days i just go with it and do the best i can knowing with my intellectual brain that my emotional brain isnt really all that clever and maybe needs to just be smiled and nodded at as i continue on my way...

Hope youre feeling better today anyway..

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-13T19:22:23+0200

Oh yeah. Poor body image, I really get that.

Years ago, eons actually, I starve myself down to 130 pounds by eating 4 cartons of yogurt a day. That was it. 250 calories each. One day I was walking down the street and saw the reflection of a really REALLY slender woman in the window. I remember thinking, wow, she looks great! Yes. It was me.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-14T13:29:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70680>

Vickie that's definitely one way to see it.

Eye candy is fun to look at but that's about it. Most people who are THAT good looking tend (TEND) to be in love with themselves. Unless the guy is Mick Jagger or Keith Richards ugly (and who's to say they aren't swell guys when you get to know them) why would you make that kind of judgement?

When the evil aunt met my hubby (back when he and I were first dating) the bitch dragged me into my kitchen and in a PRONOUNCED stage whisper told me that "he was the ugliest man she'd ever seen" while laughing at me. Granted, at the time he weighed about 155 pounds (he's 6'2") and had long hair. So no, he was definitely not looking like Brad Pitt. But if I had only seen him the way she did we wouldn't have gotten past "hello."

What's on the inside is always ALWAYS far more important than what's on the outside. Remember never judge a book by its cover. And everyone is beautiful when seen from the right angle.

### caroline — 2013-01-14T17:38:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70728>

So true Darleen - It is funny developing relationships with people on line when you have no clue what they look like - mostly. So we are seeing someone's true essence I think. It is great to see pictures tho - makes us have a better connection I think. I have been trying to put up a pic - but as usual - can't really figure it out. It can't be reduced enough or something? The picture of you at Disney is lovely - how many pounds ago was that? you look perfect! You have an hour glass shape?
I think the weight gets dispersed more evenly when you do . When I am fat It all goes to my middle and I look like a beach ball - my legs and arms stay skinny.
Have you ever tried to buy pants to fit a beach ball?
That reminds me of something I was thinking about the other day - in regard to you. You have been talking about wearing stretch pants - have you tried on pants from before that weren't stretch? Maybe your body comp has changed and you aren't noticing.....Honestly - when my body comp changed I couldn't understand why my clothes were so big. I think when we have been fat and ugly and abused - everything is always our fault and I just thought I had bought stuff that was too big - stupid!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-14T18:35:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70741>

Caroline, pictures are easy. You can use Preview to change the size. Open your pic, then go to Tools and chose Adjust Size. You need to make your avatar about 70 pixels x 70 pixels. You can just drag across what you want to crop ... be sure and hold down the shift key to keep it square.

The picture at DW was taken about 15 months into the jenny diet ... it was Dec 2007. And that was about 50 pounds less of me or halfway to where I want to go. And yeah, on the hourglass. At 130 pounds I was 36-26-36. We will not talk about what those measurements are today.

My body comp really has not changed at all. Fat is still fat in my case. sigh.

On another note, we're going to go see Gangster Squad which should make Jack have a nice hearty laugh. I need a tongue sticking out emoticon to aim his way. pfbbbt will have to do. Ryan Gosling is OK looking but there is something decidedly and deliciously MALE about him. shrug. My tastes never cease to amaze me.

My stomach is upset again today. This time it really wasn't hunger. I do NOT get this. I'm starting to wonder if I've picked up some h.pylori somewhere again. It feels a bit ulcerish.

On a positive note, just got back from the massage therapist. She hurt me but good today. I'm thinking it's probably because of the contorted position I have to be in in the tub when I do CT. 45 minutes all crumped up. I'm gonna start looking at salt water hot tubs (which I would keep at 50Â°... wouldn't DH just LOVE that, LOL). Don't think I'll spend the money but it's free to look. Sure wish he hadn't gotten a while hair a couple of years ago and sold our old one. Of course we were using bromine to keep it clean so maybe it's best it's gone.

You just have to keep making it smaller.

Jack, thanks for the tip about Staind... great tune! Now added to my spotify faves.

Checked the cherry angiomas on my belly this morning and they're almost all gone. Just the 2 larger ones are left. I'm now thinking they may have been an indicator of chlorine or bromine toxicity. I spent a couple of years in the chlorine laden pool at the Y, twice a week for an hour, doing water aerobics while I was on that stupid diet. I finally had to quit because when I'd get out of the pool my entire body would be covered with tiny red dots where the water had touched my skin. And our hot tub, which I never used all that much, was bromine laden. So I'm sticking on the iodoral for now. I think the upset stomach of late may well have been detoxing all that gunk.

At least that's my story for now until someone in authority changes my mind with facts that I don't have.

Woke up to another ocular migraine so I guess it's time to get my eyes checked. Eye strain sucks.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-17T14:49:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-71250>

Kirsta, I took a short break at about the 2 1/2 hour mark but came back for the finale.

Caroline, I can always use a hug but it is definitely detoxing symptoms. Yesterday my brain finally clicked into gear and I had a small aha moment. If I'm detoxing, DRINK MORE WATER you big dummy. I chugged a mug of green tea and 2 big glasses of water and felt amazingly better in a short time. Went to bed later and boom! Started feeling crappy again. Felt great this morning, took the iodoral and am waiting to see what transpires. I"m drinking a mug of green tea and big glass of water as I write this. I foresee a day spent running in and out of the bathroom.

Learned some very interesting tidbits during the Q&A but am now wondering what happened to the links and/or the optimal program.

On another note, the cats woke me up (AGAIN) at 3AM doing the bug tussle on the bed. Two (neutered) males and there's always a dominance play going on at some point during the day or night. BAD boys. So I lay there until about 5:30 while my mind did its usual monkey dance when that happens. I rehashed some of yesterdays events and came up with some more insights about moi. Here's hoping I remembered the HTML code for some things.

**The Art of Invisibility**

Somewhere about the time I uncovered all my memories of the abuse (I sometimes think I should capitalize that ... kind of like in "The Girl With the Dragon Tattoo" where the narrator talks about "When All the Evil Happened"and you automatically use a voice thick with drama as you say it to yourself ... but I wander. Again.) About that time I also started gaining weight. And it was at that exact moment I became invisible to men. I'm not saying I ever had to fight them off with a large club but there would be moments when a man would look my way and there'd be that little bt of crackling energy -- the stuff that makes you smile, makes you feel like you're made of light and air and are wholly the most desirable woman on the planet. Just for that moment. It stopped. Totally dead in its tracks. I remember going out for drinks one evening with a girlfriend. Some guy came over to our table and hit on her for like 20 minutes and it was literally as if I weren't even there.

Ok so it's slightly damaging to the ego but nothing I couldn't live with. Until you factor in the ego demolishing I was getting at home. When you love someone, you share a life, a home and a bed with them, and they spurn you (literally and figuratively) at every turn; when you have cried a river or two or three of tears; when you are told you are no longer desirable because you're too fat (and that was like 50 pounds less ago), the invisibility factor hardens up to the consistency of concrete and you stop caring that no one sees you. It becomes comforting and comfortable.

Then you get your parts yanked and your libido goes with them and peace at last. No more humiliating hints at intimacy that are soundly and roundly ignored. You are completely and totally phase shifted out of reality. My own little world.

And before you ask why I stayed I will simply say that the answer is as simple and as complex as a sigh. And not something I want to share.

Where is all this leading? Why am I, once again, baring my soul for all to see? Well something rather odd happened yesterday. Nothing earth shattering but it has left me wondering if my Phase Shifter needs to be sent to the quantum mechanic for a tuneup.

I met my friend for coffee at Starbucks yesterday. I was on time, she was late, as usual. This is a very small place with room for about half a dozen tables and a couple of overstuffed chairs. At some point last year they removed 3 of the tables and replaced them with one large table that seats 8. If all other tables are taken you wind up sharing the big one with strangers. And that was the scenario yesterday. A nice looking man had taken up residence with his laptop on one end of the table (I don't know how old he was .. probably somewhere between 25 and 35 -- these days anyone under 40 looks like a teenager to me). I took a seat at the other end, straddling the table leg so as to keep a goodly distance. Phase shifter ON.

When he got up to get his coffee, he came around behind me instead of going around the other way. He bumped into my purse which I had hung off the back of the chair. He made some funny little comment about it as he excused himself and I didn't think too much about it. (Checked the Phase Shifter again to make sure it was working. Huh. Lights are all green. Shake it a couple of times. Shrug and put it back in the holster.)

My friend showed up about then and we sat there for nearly 3 hours. She is a dear woman who is even bigger than I am (both taller and wider). She is a bit loud, LOL but we have the best times. She is bright and funny and at one point in her life she was one of 2 women engineers on the railroad. Yup. She drove those big ol' locomotives. The other woman was as petite as this one is oversized. They earned a lot of respect from the men because it is not easy to move a mile-long coal train up that hill out of Sheridan. It's not like stepping on the accelerator and just going.

ANYway. We laughed and told silly stories and she asked more about CT and is thinking this summer she might use the creek, or it might just be a ditch, behind her house to soak in. I told her I'd be up and we could CT together while dodging ticks, bears and mountain lions.

Right about then the guy with the laptop got up and started putting his stuff away. Now there had been this invisible wall between us the entire time. At least I thought it was there. But he made a point of saying something to me as he got ready to leave .. something about solving all the world's problems at this table. I don't know. It was just ... startling.

I think my Phase Shifter is definitely broken.

A friend wants me to go to India later this year with her -so I have to start thinking about what my one trip this year is actually going to be??? I would love to go to India but It is hard to get the food you want/need on a group trip - still want to go to Disney tho too ..and be a big kid and have fun

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-18T14:17:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-71424>

Caroline, the short answer to your first question is "no." I starved myself for over a decade in an attempt to keep thin. In the Fall of '79 I lived on 750 calories a day for several months and got my weight down to about 132 pounds. The MINUTE I ate any amount over that I started packing on the pounds. Reverse T3 at work.

No, no one every gave details about Santa Fe other than where to sign up which has the dates. And no one ever said "boo" about if they were going although I think I read in the original thread that a couple of the quieter members had signed up. I'm still toying with the idea of driving down but as Jack said, I'm a wuss.

It looks like the weather will be sunny and dry but it's two long days of driving down and then two back. Since I'm not much good a traveling I'd "have" to spend a day at the out-laws recovering from the drive. I swear we have driven all over the West in the last 15 years and have been in every single state. Of course when I say that you need to know that means \*I\* did ALL the driving. Now it takes an act of Congress to get me to drive any further than Billings.

I really love Santa Fe and basically all of NM. Although these days I'd guess SF is pretty much even more of a tourist trap than when I was there the last time. Taos used to be a sleepy little bump in the road. So was Albuquerque. Man, not any more. Being a stick in the mud I liked it better before when nobody even knew where it was.

OK, enough of the chit chat. I thought I had done pretty good yesterday with my eating. Had what passes for me as a BAB. And in case you missed it that means that I cook up 3 pieces of thick sliced CHOPPED bacon in the pan so I can keep all the fat, stir in a couple of eggs and call it good. Now I have a mug of decaf green tea and then a mug of BPDC with an obscene amount of butter in it. That was about 10:30? About 3 I realized I needed to eat so I had a packet of water packed sardines, a dill pickle and a few sweet potato chips. Hubby had fixed paleo stew so I had a bowl of that around 6-ish which OK was too close to lunch but I was now HUNGRY. I also had half an avocado and another mug of BPDC.

I woke up about 1:30 thinking I was in the tropics. Missy (my almost-20 year old cat) loves to snuggle up under the covers. She's only about 7 pounds but she is as hot as an atomic furnace. Three was snugged up against my chest and Sammy kept wandering up and over my head. Now you know why DH has taken to sleeping in the guest room this week with the door SHUT. ARGH. Nothing like being on the bottom of a cat pile.

I lay wide awake for about 3 hours when I couldn't take the complaining from my stomach another second. I snagged a couple of pieces of cold meat out of the stew and washed them down with half a glass of water. AH peace at last. Woke up about 9 when I heard the propane truck rumbling out in the driveway. got on here intending to do a quick update when my sister calls. AH it doesn't rain but it pours. So halfway through my post I spend a good 45 minutes on the phone and finally got to my breakfast. Yes, I was way late. Now back here for a quick finish and then CT! Haven't been in the tub for a week because of low estrogen issues ... we do NOT need another meltdown. But I miss it and today I'm getting back at it.

soooo. That's all I know for now. Waiting for updates on the Optimal challenge or whatever it is they're calling it.

I'm going to write this on my steering wheel......I can go all day without drinking if I get super busy at work!

### Charlotte — 2013-01-19T03:24:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-71565>

Caroline, you don't need a snowsuit to rob a bank.. LOL
My suggestion would be to take a big jar of High meat, drop it on the counter and open it.

Darleen, thats a story I can relate to very well. My Dad used to look at me like an artist viewing his painting in progress and say: you could be so beautiful if you weren't so fat. Years later, I married someone like my Dad who used to say: I could love you so much if you weren't so fat. Needless to comment,.
But... the message I got was, it was all my fault.

In 1995 I was working in Melbourne calling the family every evening to see how they were doing and my husband was telling me again some story (I dont remember what it was) and something clicked in my head, as simple as that and I knrew that I was going to end this drama. First thing when I got back to Germany was calling the lawyer and filing for divorce. Then I went home and told my husband. I never forget his face. ME? Leaving HIM? If anything it should be him leaving me. (he did that a lot but always came back and I had told him before that one day I would be leaving, but I would not come back)
He even left me with a 2 month old baby all by myself in America and I still joined him again in Germany 2 years later, just so you know how screwed up I was. We had another child 8 years after the first and he was six, when I finally left.
Then for years, my youngest son told me that I should lose weight so his Dad would come back. That was heartbreaking, as it seemed that this story was repeating itself endlessly from generation to generation and that even my child should suffer from this distortion of truth. Fact was, I gained weight very early to protect myself from the physical and mental abuse and to create the selffullfilling prophecy that I was not lovable and ugly, to mention one of many ideas I had about myself. (including of feeling invisible for men who were a lot of cause for pain for me)

But like you, I am a rebel, and something (I realized only much later) kept me from losing as I wanted to be loved and accepted for the person I was and not because I looked as I did.
And now, something funny has happened. Because I lost all that weight, someone said to me: your sons must be so proud of you. And no... they didn't even notice (we talk over Skype a lot and they can see me). I told my oldest son this a few days ago and he said: you are my Mom and I see your person, I never see your weight. And when I told my youngest son, that I had lost all that weight he said: Mom, I didn't notice but I am so happy for you. Thats when I realized that healing has taken place for him too.

This is simply a little history of Charlotte and I do not want to imply anything, but only to let you know how very well I can relate.

I personally think that you are doing really great because you follow Dr. Kruses advice and hang in there patiently. Patience has never been a trait of mine and I learn a lot from your posts. Thank you for that.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-19T14:08:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-71615>

Caroline, you don't need a snowsuit! silly woman. Although going straight from high summer to high winter might be a little challenging. My car has a good heater as well.

Charlotte thank you so much for sharing. You sound just like my sister on the weight thing. But weight gain is not so simple as saying you gained to hide your emotions. Sorry. I won't let her get away with that any more either. When you have run out of reasons why you gain weight that one seems to be the most obvious ... but that's just blaming the victim. Weight gain is a signal that something has gone awry with your biochemistry. If I've learned NOTHING else from Jack's posts I have learned that.

I'm glad you finally dumped the baggage. sounds like he deserved it. And thank you for sharing all of that!

BAB this morning was, as usual, about 30 minutes late. I pan fried 2 pieces of thick sliced bacon then cooked two salmon filets in the fat. They were about 4oz each or thereabouts. I ate almost all of it. Green tea and now sipping my BPDC.

45 minutes of CT yesterday and last night when I went to bed my nipples were, once again, REALLY sore. Crap. I stayed out of the tub an entire week, upped my estrogen by 2ml per day, had just gotten rid of the soreness and now it's back. Guess it's time to Ask Jack. My sleep was also affected.

It's taken me a week to realize that the sleep issues I've suddenly been having the last several weeks HAVE to be related to dropping estrogen levels. for the longest time my sleep was GREAT. I'd lie down, close my eyes, roll over and it would be morning. Lately I'm having trouble falling asleep, am waking at least once a night and many times I cannot get back to sleep for several hours. Sounds like menopause all over again.

Huh. One step forward, six back.

Oh, and Charlotte, patience comes easy when you have hit the end of the line. I have NO where else to go. Nothing else has worked or only worked for like a minute or two. I've said it before ... I trust Jack because HE HAS BEEN THERE. He knows what it's like and he fixed himself. THAT is why I follow him. It isn't blind faith either. He backs up what he teaches us with science. And he knows how I crave knowledge so keep feeding us the good stuff!

### endless — 2013-01-19T14:44:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-71626>

Sore nipples is low estrogen? I didn't know that....

I get general breat tenderness from mid cycle onward, but I always thought it was rising progesterone.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-19T15:30:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-71634>

Rising progesterone or low estrogen. Same thing. The OD who prescribed my WP told me to up my estrogen when I complained about sore nipples. I was quite surprised. But it makes sense. If your estrogen levels are low and your progesterone rises it's going to lower your estrogen even more.

So it's not the CT according to Ask Jack. Something else is going on. DAMN it. Now I have to try and figure out what happened about 2 months ago when all this nonsense started.

Guess the first step is to really tighten up the leptin RX and maybe cut back on the fat and eat more protein. I cannot believe when Jack said the other night that it can take up to 24 months to convert from being a sugar burner to being a fat burner. I'm pretty sure I heard him right. So maybe I need more protein for now. I guess I'll try that and see what happens. I sure miss my good sleep though.

I had a funny dream last night - I dreamt I was at Disney with all you guys and brought all the wrong shoes!!! How dreadful is that? Not just wrong shoes - but REALLY WRONG STUPID shoes. I was mortified! When we finally get to disney you will have to help me with a packing list ... ### DarleenMB — 2013-01-20T14:14:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-71856>

Krista ... the image that brings to mind makes want to alternately weep with frustration and LMAO.

Caroline, I haven't danced for, well as long as I haven't _danced_ if you get my drift.

Charlotte that is TOO funny! The town I live in has a ton of artists and lot of them working in bronze. So, emulating Loveland, CO bronzes have been appearing all over town the last 8-10 years. Last year the life-size bronze of a fisherman (old world) was placed on the corner of the main intersection downtown. EVERY single time I went to town I'd be startled by it because that's where I turn right to go to the store and I always thought "he" was someone getting ready to cross the street, LOL. It was changed out a few months ago to a totem pole.

And Charlotte ... bit of advice ... put the scale in the closet and close the door. This isn't about that number on the scale and yes I know ... I still fall prey to that every now and then. I work on keeping to my own advice but I"m only human.

Lyndra ... I had the answer to the sleep thing then promptly spaced it off. I asked Jack about CT and falling estrogen levels and was told (if you missed it) that I'm losing electrons. See post above. So yesterday I decided to put the protein BACK full bore. BAB was 2 filets of salmon (about 7-8 oz) and some bacon and BPDC. "Lunch" was a Quelson protein shake with 2 scoops of WPI (40g protein) and dinner was the last of the paleo stew.

I slept really good, only waking up a couple of times to go pee and promptly fell back asleep. Woke about 9:30? but didn't get my lazy ass out of bed and would still be there except hubby got up and Three (my youngest and mouthiest cat) kept walking all over me yelling at me to GET OUT OF BED!

Soreness has lessened appreciably so am headed for the tub in about 20 minutes. BAB this morning was 2 pieces of thick sliced bacon, 4.5 oz of ground GF beef, 2 eggs and yes I poked it all down and am now STUFFED. A mug of green tea and now sipping a mug of BPDC with less butter than I usually dump in.

My current plan is to alternate the protein shakes with some kind of fishy little midday meal/snack. Today I'll probably just eat some sardines since I ate breakfast so late.

Also, even though it's frickin' cold today and snow is forecast I intend, once I'm outta the tub and dressed, to go stand outside my studio (fully dressed, however) in my bare feet on the patch of dirt next to the house that doesn't have snow on it. THAT will be a daily occurrence until/unless I can't get to the dirt. I'm not standing on icy snow. No way. No how.

One last thing. Some years ago a good friend from Toronto sent me magnetic mug. He swore drinking water that had been in the mug gave him more energy. I thought he was full of it. And I don't mean energy. So the mug has traveled from cupboard to pantry until I went hunting for it last night. I thought I'd thrown it out but I finally found it. Washed it and filled it with water. I let it sit overnight and put my D-ribose and MSM in it this morning. I figured WTH. If it does anything then I'm up one, if it doesn't, I needed the water anyway.

Would you all think I'm way strange if I go fondle my new shoes? LOL

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-21T13:19:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-72057>

OKEYDOKEY Just polished off EMF2 (membership has it's privileges) and all I can say is holy shit.

I watched most of that video that Thor posted ... and Jack ... from the German doctor. Sent it off to all my cell phone loving friends and relatives, not that they will get it. When DH came home yesterday afternoon I announced I was getting rid of all the cordless phones (he got emailed the link to the vid as well). I got a snort and some snarky comment about what crazy thing I was up to now.

And from the same vid ... I know we have a smart meter. Now to figure out how to force MDU to remove it short of sabotage. Anyone have any bright ideas on shielding the damn thing if I can't make them remove it?

WiFi turned off at night but starting to think about hunting down the Cat3 cables and going back to direct hookup on the modem.

I will say I am now finally and happily planted forever in Wyoming. The nearest cell phone antennae are at least 1/2 a mile away from my house, there are NO cell towers anywhere near us, the radiation map says NONE anywhere near us. whew. There's more but I'll wait until the big unveiling to continue the discussion. This stuff came from the german docs video and some of Thor's links.

BAB choked down ... every last bite. Feeling WAY better, thanks, since I put the protein back in. I guess I am not nearly ready to dine primarily on fat no matter what I was craving. And wasn't that just weird? Craving FAT?! I don't usually crave things. I haven't eaten chocolate in months although yesterday I did finally open up that bar of 85% and had a couple of bites. It was ok. (did I just say that?!) Take it or leave it. Probably leave it again.

I had some canned mussels for "lunch" ... ewwwww. Sounded good, tasted blech. Too overdone. So I polished off a double handful of shrimp instead

Dinner was a hunk of the last of the pulled pork from Costco with a handful of lettuce w/homemade olive oil/balsamic vinegrette.

Slept pretty good .... hubs went back to the guest room and closed the door so I was at the bottom of the cat pile again. You wouldn't think a 7 pound cat could force a fully grown and overweight woman to wake up and let her under the blankets. 3 or 4 times. You'd be wrong. I'd lock them out of the bedroom but they just sit outside, whine and cry and scratch at the door. It's easier to let 'em snuggle.

ANNNND it's Monday. oh boy. Even in retirement monday's suck.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-22T14:35:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-72325>

Woke up at 2AM to find myself the filling in a cat sandwich. How is it that two 7 pound cats can defy Newtonian physics? How do they manage to increase their mass to the point where they can pin me to the bed? Ever try to pick up a cat that didn't want to go? That's what I mean.

so I lay there for 3 hours pondering things and managed to write an entire post in my head. A LONG post. Came back in here this morning and decided not to post it. I will, however, share some of my insights from all that thinking.

Allow me to quote from an Eagles song to get the ball rolling ... "things in this life change very slowly if they ever change at all."

Words of wisdom when one is trying to change the world. Which all led to me to thinking about a book by Marilyn Ferguson titled "The Aquarian Conspiracy." I read it in the late 70's and it's now out of print. But the one take away I got was a phrase she borrowed from biology. She applied it to psychology and it is a very interesting concept. Paradigm shift. See the above quote.

If you aren't familiar with the concept or the book, I'll do my best to explain. In a closed system, as new thoughts and ideas are introduced it starts to get a little crowded. The more new information that comes in the more uncomfortable it gets. It's like your skin gets too tight or you fell back into your body while you were sleeping and didn't get it on quite right. This is where cognitive dissonance comes into play which only makes one even more uncomfortable.

Eventually the system has to expand to encompass all the new information. When that happens there is a _shift_ that takes place as all the information rearranges itself. I like to shorthand it by calling it the AHA moment.

I believe this is the uphill battle now facing Jack. Scientists (and I include physicians in that lot) tend to suffer from a hardening of the attitudes. It's easy to see why when you take into consideration all the years spent in education learning that things are one way. Their work lives are spent reinforcing this dogma. It is almost impossible for anyone caught in this mindset to accept new and untested ideas without a putting up a big fight. See the above quote again.

As an example I'm going to point you to the use of statins and low fat diets as a means to "cure" heart disease. How many of you buy into that? Now how many doctors out there believe this is the proper course of treatment. See. The "conventional wisdom" is nothing more than brainwashing and we, as a society including physicians and scientists and people who should KNOW better, have all bought into this nonsense. My husband is a perfect example. He has been on statins for 30+ years (I'll wait while some of you put 2 and 2 together and come up with AHA).

To say his job was stressful is an understatement. Rail freight does not have a schedule. He was ON CALL 24/7/50. He could get home, tie up (that's RR jargon for park the train and file the paperwork), get home and get called BACK to work 8 hours from the time he tied up. He was gone more than he was home living in motel rooms at the other end of the road and eating crap from local diners. Way back when we first moved here many of the guys self medicated to the point where management finally had to do something. Railroading is one of the most dangerous jobs there are. There's a reason for the 8 hour rest rule and it's not enough. ANYWAY. STRESS and hereditary factors had pushed his "cholesterol" up over 400. So he's been given what I consider to be a poison for 30 years and we have both suffered.

He won't quit. He's 71, retired, out of all that stress and home resting and enjoying life and eating mostly OK. I can't control his every movement. But he's still on the damned statins.

Hardening of the attitudes. It's almost impossible to fight until someone goes through a paradigm shift. Good luck with that. Closed minds don't take easily to new information if they take it in at all.

I hope that all made some sense. It kept me awake for a good 3 hours when I should have been sleeping.

BAB ... 8 ounces of salmon and a fistful of rice-paper-thin bacon, green tea, and a waiting mug of BPDC with CO in it.

Skipped lunch yesterday because of a late start and will be doing the same again today. No idea what to fix for dinner. Hubby is sick with a cold ... smacked him in the face yesterday. Good thing he's sleeping the guest room.

Time for CT and I need to read EMF 1 and 2 again. Later!

### diane — 2013-01-22T14:48:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-72329>

AHA!

Very interesting post Darleen. And I agree with you about changing attitudes and fixed ideas. But someone has to do it! LOL. I work with someone who is doing the same in trying to change attitudes and behaviours towards dogs, and it is an uphill battle for sure. And the poison and vitriol that people spit out is shocking - and getting worse. I think I have more of an insight into why now. But he does it because he has a passion for it and has made it his mission so he doesn't let small minded people stop him. I assume that Jack will do the same.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-22T16:26:12+0200

CT ... damn I still can't get that overflow drain stoppered up. The water level dropped 4" again in 45 minutes. Boy am I shaking. hard to type. Why does my temp drop? it was 95 when I got out of the water. sheesh. I thought it was supposed to go UP???

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-22T18:39:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-72373>

Caroline I'm sorry to hear about your daughter and SIL. I've said it before, no one can diss you like your family. Doesn't matter if you're a particle physicist, a neurosurgeon or the President of the US. Your family KNOWS you so you get no respect.

I have finally reached the point (rising dopamine levels?) where I can just casually hand off a bit of info "just FYI" and let it go. It isn't that I don't care ... of course I still do, but rather there's no point in trying to force the issue or explain why whatever it is you've recommended might be helpful. give 'em the info and change the subject, that's my new mantra.

Honestly I'm tired of trying to help my family. They tell me on the one hand "you are so smart" while on the other they dismiss everything I say with eye rolls and nasty comments. OKAY fine. Here ya go and I'll visit you in the hospital.

### diane — 2013-01-22T19:33:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-72388>

Well, to both of you "twisted sisters" - many of us listen to you, and respect your opinion!

I don't try with my family. My mom believes that doctors know everything and worries about the supplements etc that I take. My response is have you ever seen the list of side effects in drugs? And you think that is better? Or her doctor who hasn't taken severe stomach problems (and bringing up foam in her mouth) seriously for 10 years. Then both she and my dad have colon cancer. But I try. I even suggested she tried giving up wheat for just 2 weeks to see if there is a difference - but she has no idea what she will eat. LOL. Oh well - I will continue to try different approaches. Maybe I'm wrong, and maybe I'm right. But I know enough to know that doing the same thing ain't working.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-23T13:52:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-72529>

Diane you are definitely one smart woman!

Slept really well last night; only woke up once? Maybe twice but I'm not sure. So getting back to plenty of protein seems to be the right direction. How easy it is to lose my way.

Dinner last night was 7 oz of steak, half an avocado and some sweet potato. BAB this morning was a 5oz packet of pink salmon, heated in CO with 2 FF eggs scrambled in and a mug of green tea. Enjoying my mug of BPDC with less butter and a TBS of CO.

Hubby is still sick but is at least ambulatory this morning. So I know he's still alive.

No deep thoughts to share this morning (lucky you). Have more errands to run since Monday was a holiday and I didn't get to finish them then. I'm headed to Home Depot for a new light fixture for our computer room. It's the only room in the house with no windows. Yeah. Great, eh? And we have a fluorescent fixture in here which I have always hated. You know, I've all but lived in this room for the last 2.5 years and all that time kept thinking it was NOT healthy because of those two things PLUS the computer. Turns out I shoulda listened to my intuition.

I've thought about moving my computer out to my little studio because it's full of light. However, the ceiling needs work and I've been told I will have to move a lot of stuff out so DH can do it. So I think I'll wait until that gets done. Or maybe not. He won't be getting to it for several months and, come on DARLEEN, it's not like it's a major effort to move the damned computer a few feet. geez.

Off to buy that tri-meter from Amazon and wincing at the cost.

### Inger — 2013-01-23T14:32:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-72541>

> **DarleenMB said**
>
> Woke up at 2AM to find myself the filling in a cat sandwich. How is it that two 7 pound cats can defy Newtonian physics? How do they manage to increase their mass to the point where they can pin me to the bed? Ever try to pick up a cat that didn't want to go? That's what I mean.
> so I lay there for 3 hours pondering things and managed to write an entire post in my head. A LONG post. Came back in here this morning and decided not to post it. I will, however, share some of my insights from all that thinking.
> Allow me to quote from an Eagles song to get the ball rolling ... "things in this life change very slowly if they ever change at all."
> Words of wisdom when one is trying to change the world. Which all led to me to thinking about a book by Marilyn Ferguson titled "The Aquarian Conspiracy." I read it in the late 70's and it's now out of print. But the one take away I got was a phrase she borrowed from biology. She applied it to psychology and it is a very interesting concept. Paradigm shift. See the above quote.
> If you aren't familiar with the concept or the book, I'll do my best to explain. In a closed system, as new thoughts and ideas are introduced it starts to get a little crowded. The more new information that comes in the more uncomfortable it gets. It's like your skin gets too tight or you fell back into your body while you were sleeping and didn't get it on quite right. This is where cognitive dissonance comes into play which only makes one even more uncomfortable.
> Eventually the system has to expand to encompass all the new information. When that happens there is a _shift_ that takes place as all the information rearranges itself. I like to shorthand it by calling it the AHA moment.
> I believe this is the uphill battle now facing Jack. Scientists (and I include physicians in that lot) tend to suffer from a hardening of the attitudes. It's easy to see why when you take into consideration all the years spent in education learning that things are one way. Their work lives are spent reinforcing this dogma. It is almost impossible for anyone caught in this mindset to accept new and untested ideas without a putting up a big fight. See the above quote again.
> As an example I'm going to point you to the use of statins and low fat diets as a means to "cure" heart disease. How many of you buy into that? Now how many doctors out there believe this is the proper course of treatment. See. The "conventional wisdom" is nothing more than brainwashing and we, as a society including physicians and scientists and people who should KNOW better, have all bought into this nonsense. My husband is a perfect example. He has been on statins for 30+ years (I'll wait while some of you put 2 and 2 together and come up with AHA).
> To say his job was stressful is an understatement. Rail freight does not have a schedule. He was ON CALL 24/7/50. He could get home, tie up (that's RR jargon for park the train and file the paperwork), get home and get called BACK to work 8 hours from the time he tied up. He was gone more than he was home living in motel rooms at the other end of the road and eating crap from local diners. Way back when we first moved here many of the guys self medicated to the point where management finally had to do something. Railroading is one of the most dangerous jobs there are. There's a reason for the 8 hour rest rule and it's not enough. ANYWAY. STRESS and hereditary factors had pushed his "cholesterol" up over 400. So he's been given what I consider to be a poison for 30 years and we have both suffered.
> He won't quit. He's 71, retired, out of all that stress and home resting and enjoying life and eating mostly OK. I can't control his every movement. But he's still on the damned statins.
> Hardening of the attitudes. It's almost impossible to fight until someone goes through a paradigm shift. Good luck with that. Closed minds don't take easily to new information if they take it in at all.
> I hope that all made some sense. It kept me awake for a good 3 hours when I should have been sleeping.
> BAB ... 8 ounces of salmon and a fistful of rice-paper-thin bacon, green tea, and a waiting mug of BPDC with CO in it.
> Skipped lunch yesterday because of a late start and will be doing the same again today. No idea what to fix for dinner. Hubby is sick with a cold ... smacked him in the face yesterday. Good thing he's sleeping the guest room.
> Time for CT and I need to read EMF 1 and 2 again. Later!

It made a lot of sense to me Darleen! Wow!

BAB was 5+oz of GF ground beef, 2 pieces thick bacon, 2 eggs and my mug of BPDC with CO and small amt of butter. Trying to cut back on the dairy.

I was expecting the \*GASP\* and not being able to breathe for 2 minutes. I did shiver a lot though. When I got out at the 45 minute mark I checked the temp and it was 63Â° so must have started about 55Â°. I also tried duct taping over the edges of the over=flow cover. didn't work. ### DarleenMB — 2013-01-27T19:27:06+0200

I keep thinking I'm losing a little weight and when I get on the scale ... nope. Just your imagination. Nothing has budged. Then I get frustrated and want to yell 'WHY AREN'T I LOSING?!" and then I remember the RT3 and just want to bang my head on the nearest wall. sigh

Dinner last night was orange roughy again (I had forgotten just how yummy it is ... all it needs is a bit of S&P) and some shrimp and this time I remember the half of an avocado. And a glass of kombucha.

Slept fine after I finally fell asleep. I had dozed off when the cats decided to rumble. Took another hour after that to finally drift off. Woke up about 6-ish but lazed in bed for another couple of hours until Missy finally had her way and shoved me out of bed. Cats.

BAB as usual. It's overcast this morning so no sunbathing. boo. Going to the tanning salon tomorrow. 6 minutes and don't forget to ask about the ballast, Darleen!

Hubby is still fighting the cold. I hate to say it but having the bedroom all to myself (well, except for the cats) has been kind of nice. I'm sure he's thinking the same thing since they tend to pile on him otherwise.

Still bummed about going back to this format. I'm having trouble navigating again. I'd just finally gotten the hang of the other format ... sure wish they could have straightened it out.

Ennui. word of the day.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-28T18:09:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-73010>

Boy today is definitely MONDAY. Got into a dustup over on FB with someone I do not know ... she said something hurtful and mean and I reacted. Nice little blood pressure spike there. I hate being laughed at. In my book, people who poke you with a sharp stick are bullies. ESPECIALLY people you don't know and who don't know you. Can you tell I'm still pissed?

ANYway. I'm now feeling low=estrogeny again. I was thinking maybe my pharmacist had screwed up my estrogen prescription but then I got to thinking that all this began right about the time I started supplementing with pregnenolone. My DHEA test had come back high and the preg. was flatlined. So I figured I probably need to supplement. Now I'm thinking that may not have been such a good idea. My sleep has suffered, I've been emotional (jeebus I have been emotional), parts have hurt, in short I feel like a small train wreck today. I guess I'll stop taking the stuff for a while and see if things improve.

Managed to choke down my BAB in spite of weeping into the bacon fat. See what I mean about being cranky? Last night's dinner was tuna salad and half an avocado. The 2 meals a day has become routine. Nothing I had planned, it's just sorta happened.

Still doing 25mcg of cytomel daily and 50mg of iodine + 300mg of selenium. Still need to find a doc to get my thyroid rechecked to see if there's been any improvement on the RT3 issue. My massage therapist gave me the name of a nurse practitioner she knows. I'm hesitant to call her, don't know why.

It snowed yesterday and everything is all pretty and clean and white again. We're due to have snow all week (YAY!) and temps in the low 20's during the day.

It's CT today and then the tanning salon for 6 minutes of toasting.

Boy I wish things would go back to where they were before I took this unexpected detour.

I know I need more Pg... my E went up big time, even though we increased my Pg back at the end of OCt. I am a serious estrogen collector - would you like me syphon off my extra and send it in the mail to you...you can have it...

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-28T21:23:30+0200

Yes please. To both statements.

You know I was estrogen dominant for so many years (evidence? a kilo's worth of uterine fibroid tumors. don't grow those babies overnight) that I'm scared to go in that direction again. Too much estrogen = bad sleep, and clown parts. My breasts shrank by a good 25% when I started using OTC progesterone cream, that's how bad it was.

Guess I need to put on my big girl panties and stop dithering.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-30T18:40:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-73354>

You don't know who Patrick Stewart is??? Seriously? Most famous for Star Trek: The Next Generation tv series and movies. YOU MUST watch!

speaking of SF ... watched "The Fifth Element" last night for about the 6th time. It is a pretty silly little flick but the subtext is rich and thought provoking. I have to admit that while watching it I kept having this rather heated reaction to Bruce Willis ... and I am not a fan believe me. Must be the progesterone, LOL, cannot think of any other reason for it.

Went to bed last night with a headache and woke up with a worse one that is getting worse by the minute. Not sure if it's a sympathy reaction to Gretchen's 2 day migraine (which I sincerely hope has left her by now), muscle tension in my lower back (I'm thinking of having DH hammer at those muscles with the meat tenderizer), or if I have succumbed to his bug. crap. On toast.

Speaking of that, I wonder if my favorite fictional witch has a new adventure out. I'll have to go check Amazon. I hate paying the same frickkin' price for an ebook that those greedy assed publishers charge for a PRINT book but I may have to cave in and just do it. Charlaine Harris should have a new Sookie Stackhouse out by now. There goes my monthly book allowance. I just bought "The Joy of Sexus" which is an historical look at sex 2,000 years ago. Looks to be ... hmmm ... interesting if nothing else. DAMN I have sex on the brain lately.

ANYhoo. In talking to my sister the other day she mentioned our brother had called her and she was upset, as usual. I had to remind her ... he lies. About everything. He is a master manipulator as well. But the bottom line is he lies. And once again she admitted that was true but she didn't understand it. So once again I had to explain it to her. Denial is a tough nut to crack.

She and I had the benefit of our Mom during those crucial formative years. Our brother did not. She divorced that that that ... I don't know what to call him ... lazy shiftless bum of a husband (and my father) before our brother was born. We were living with her parents and she had to work. She HAD to work. So the three of us were left pretty much on our own on a daily basis. No one taught our brother the difference between truth and lies (or make believe shit). Every word he uttered was taken as absolute gospel. So here he is ... 59 years old and he just makes shit up. He's wily enough to figure out which of your buttons to push to get you on his side so he does that too.

I'm not saying he lies with malicious intent. I just do not believe he knows the difference. The manipulation is another story. I'm neither justifying nor condoning his behavior. It is what it is. And it's difficult to accept. But in the end, he is our blood and as angry as he makes me I still feel love for him. I just have to step waaaay back and try and keep proper perspective.

Ah the soap opera that is my life. Ain't it fun.

Dentist appt in 90 minutes to get my teeth cleaned. Oh boy. Hope I got all the salmon out from between my teeth.

Weighed myself this morning just to punish myself. Just kidding. I'm not a masochist but sometimes I have to wonder. Here's the thing. My weight has not budged so much as a tenth of a pound in weeks. My pants are feeling loose through the thighs and the waistband feels loose as well. hmmm.

Hope springs eternal or some such bullshit.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-02T03:33:55+0200

Wandered over to Mark's Daily Apple and see there's a primalCon event the end of Sept at Lake Tahoe. Limited to 100 and it's already sold out but I asked to be put on the waiting list. It's expensive (well it's at Tahoe, after all) but it looks like it would be a great event to attend. Probably will NOT get a ticket but you never know.

I'm going to start posting more over on my blog ... link is in my siggie line.

Something seems to be changing ... you know how you have fat clothes, medium clothes, thin clothes? And underwear to match? Well I have medium underwear that's not quite bikini but sort of ... low enough to be annoying when you have even a bit of a belly because it pushes the band down it drives me nuts. Since I still haven't done the laundry yet (hey, there's only TWO of us and it can take 10 days to get enough to make a couple of decent loads ... not counting sheets and stuff) I ran out of clean undies. So I was forced to grab a pair of the medium ones. I was prepared to fling them off in disgust but, HUH. they fit. Well that's just weird.
