### DarleenMB — 2013-03-25T16:58:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81691>

LOL, Martin that was almost as funny as the cartoon!

Definite mood improvement since I stepped down off the reset. Small amounts of protein 3x a day and limiting carbs (but not eliminating them) and bumping the fat seems to be having a positive effect.

Yesterday:

B: 1 piece thick sliced bacon, 2 eggs scrambled in the bacon fat, 1 mug BPDC with 1oz of butter and 2TBS upgraded MCT oil
L: leftover roast beast with a few bites of potato (!yes potato!), 3 or 4 baby carrots, a bite or 2 of onion and the broth, handful of raw almonds for dessert
S: apple and a few more raw almonds.
D: small tin of mackerel fillets (packed in sunflower oil ... ick but that's all they had), sweet potato chips (about an ounce)

Lots of spring water (which has hubby in a snit as he thinks our tap water is just fine).

Slept ok except for the damned cats. Between Missy pawing at my neck to be let under the covers, Sammy hopping up and down off the bed (and walking over me, of course ... 16 pound cat who is NOT at all graceful), and Three trying to snuggle without disturbing Missy ... well you get the idea. Woke early (like about 5:30) and lay there about an hour before giving up and getting up. Sunrise always is 20 minutes AFTER actual sunrise because of that ridge we're behind but it was spectacular nonetheless.

Weighed in ... will weigh once a week from now on. Scale was up 2 pounds (probably from getting rehydrated) but I'm not worried as I am now eating WAY less than I was before.

### endless — 2013-03-25T17:19:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81698>

Ah yes, sleeping with cats. I have three on my bed as well, needless to say I don't change position much!

How on earth can you handle eating only ONE piece of bacon?? It's like teasing...I can't stop at just one.

### janagram — 2013-03-25T17:21:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81700>

trying a new approach is a damn good idea. I've tried a potatohack...(last wk...1 day)...its hard to do but I do lose about a lb a day if I stick to it. No fat.
Hell, Darleen...I just don't know anymore. Just want a diet that lets you live your life! Went out to dinner last night and gained a pound! why? I had ahi tuna!
Oh, sorry, this is your journal!!

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-25T18:44:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81714>

Krista ... look at all the fat I'm getting in the morning. Makes it real easy to stop at just one piece.

Jana, I hear ya. I really like the force fat approach. Honestly 60g of protein is plenty. I'm getting enough carbs to keep my FBG in a good range and I am NOT hungry. As I said, my mood has improved -- the one marker I've been waiting a whole damned year to see and here it is! I also firmly believe that paleo is a good template. I will continue to add as much fish as I can and keep starches to a minimum. Doesn't get much easier than that. And I think there's room for an occasional treat. I also think that weighing every day is a big mistake. This from the woman who used to start every day by doing just that. Your weight can vary so much in a 24 hour period it isn't even funny.

That's all I know. Not much. But I'm working on it.

### endless — 2013-03-25T18:46:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81715>

I wonder if your mood has improved from adding the carbs in...

I don't weigh myself even every week anymore. waiting till I see/feel some difference in my clothes.

### janagram — 2013-03-31T19:11:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-82358>

Darleen,
I, maybe like you, find myself at square one....N-1, perhaps. I notice you are taking a new approach to eating. newish....I feel like I just need to find my very own N-1 and I haven't found it yet.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-31T19:50:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-82362>

Jana, I'm back on low carb, modest protein, plenty of good fats and on a regular schedule. No more BAB. I'm using livestrong.com to track my calories and attempting to keep them around 12-1400. And btw I do NOT count the calories in my BPDC ... they come from fat and if I count them then I don't have much left for food-food. I know that sounds kind of stupid but the MCT oil gets used immediately and I'm now very strict on the butter. Looks something like this:

B: 2 regular slices pan fried bacon, 2 eggs scrambled in the bacon fat, mug of BPDC, 2 ounces pomegranate juice
L: water packed sardines, 1 ounce sweet potato chips, dill pickle spear, handful raw almonds
S: apple and more raw almonds if I feel hungry
D: baked tilapia, green salad with homemade balsamic vinaigrette (usually 2TBS or less)

### Hope — 2013-04-01T20:10:38+0300

### lioness7 — 2013-04-01T22:07:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-82509>

> [diane said:](/goto/post?id=82238)
>
> Oh man. Someone posted a link to the sun's distance from the earth based on your lat and long. You could enter in a day, then it had degrees from the sun by hour. It's in one of the threads here!

The link is [http://aa.usno.navy.mil/data/docs/AltAz.php](http://aa.usno.navy.mil/data/docs/AltAz.php)

But, I always had a problem using the international form (though have not tried it for a while), so I used to use the U.S. form and entered Niagara Falls N.Y. to get a pretty good approximation for Toronto.

The other site is [http://nadir.nilu.no/~olaeng/fastrt/VitD\_quartMEDandMED\_v2.html](http://nadir.nilu.no/~olaeng/fastrt/VitD_quartMEDandMED_v2.html) where you can input more variables.

### DarleenMB — 2013-04-02T04:56:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-82562>

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=82493)
>
> Darleen, that is awesome about your new T labs!!!!! Way to go!! I have to check out your new site as well!
>
> And....did you ever start red light therapy? If so do you like it?

I've done 10 sessions so far. And my lips are back! My upper lip had disappeared quite a while back. Age sucks. But I noticed a couple of weeks ago it felt like I had a fat lip and sure enough, they're plumping up. Well, as much as mine ever do. So yeah, I'm really liking it.

> [Lanie said:](/goto/post?id=82515)
>
> Darleen, can you say more about this? Even if the MCT oil gets used right away as opposed to being stored, shouldn't those calories be counted since your body is NOT burning off other calories since the MCT oil is being burned? I mean, if you're going to count calories why wouldn't you count all of them?

Lanie I want to make sure I get enough protein so I decided to ignore those two counts. It's a game I play with my head like entering deductions in the checkbook that never actually exist. I've managed to build up quite a nice little cushion doing that so now I never ever worry about having enough money to pay the bills or bouncing a check. It's silly, I know but it works for me.

and I forgot ... about the RT3 ... the NP told me wrong. That number is 157 NOT 1.57. WOW what a difference. It means my ratio IS 22.3!! I stopped the cytomel Saturday and just take the iodoral + selenium now. I did notice a little bit of trembling in my left hand again about 3pm. Gotta remember to take some d-ribose at lunch. That stops it. So YAY for me on the thyroid. On the right track.

### DarleenMB — 2013-04-03T04:46:55+0300

Lanie, good to know. I thought I was just being weird. Something new is I now pay for everything (except the monthly bills) with the cc. We're racking up air miles for our next trip. yay! Of course I have to be careful not to spend too much because the house rule is always pay the cc in full every month. We spent way too many years giving too much money to the frackkin' banks. ### DarleenMB — 2013-04-08T01:36:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-83290>

Since things are not going anywhere I've decided to do a once-weekly checkin starting today.

My weight has not budged in over a year which, in and of itself, is actually a very good thing. But being stuck at 231 sucks. However, I have decided that I've set my goals way too high (or low, depending on your viewpoint). Why? Well, my age for one thing. For another, there was an article in one of the fashion magazines I read that showed real women getting their jean-thing on.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/jeans-jpg.85/) [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/jeans2-jpg.86/)

On the left, 5'8", 162 pounds. On the right, 5'10", 200 pounds.

I'm 5'9.5" and was aiming for 130. Well, not gonna happen. So I'm thinking 160-165 might be a better goal. Especially since I'm starting back to doing heavy lifting. Muscle and all that.

On another note I was steered to a book by Ridha Arem entitled "The thyroid solution diet." Dr. Arem is an endocrinologist in Houston with a spectacular website and a forum which no one posts in. Too bad. He pretty much lost me when he mentioned "healthy grains" and he DID lose me at insisting on cardio. Um, no thanks doc. I may yet buy the book (I just downloaded a sample to my Kindle, that's as far as I've gotten) but after reading some of the review, probably not. It seems EVERYone has an answer but I've yet to find one that really works. And before the howls of protest begin, let me point out the only reason I started on THIS journey with Jack was because I want/need to LOSE WEIGHT.

My health is pretty decent given how much extra weight I'm packing around.

Well anyway. That's all I have for this week. See ya around the water cooler.

### fitness@home — 2013-04-15T00:28:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-84098>

> [DarleenMB said:](/goto/post?id=83290)
>
> I'm 5'9.5" and was aiming for 130. Well, not gonna happen. So I'm thinking 160-165 might be a better goal. Especially since I'm starting back to doing heavy lifting. Muscle and all that.

Darlene, I am 5'7.5" and have never been considered petite. Have been this height since beginning of 7th grade when I towered over the rest of my peers, especially the boys . I would love to see 130 again but it is also not likely...my weight doesn't go down that far unless I am dealing with IBS and/or high anxiety.

Right now I am just barely under 145 and carry it well. I feel good. Not going to beat myself up over it. I think your goal is good for your height. Would LOVE to be be 5'9"!

### DarleenMB — 2013-05-06T23:42:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-85536>

It's been an interesting several weeks. The sun finally decided to quit playing hide and go seek with Mother Nature and I've spent a couple of blissful afternoons (well, OK really only 15-20 minutes per side each time but still) getting solar powered vitamin D therapy! Man it was HOT out there today. I am a pansy? or is it candy? ass when it comes to summer heat. I wilt. Pansy it is.

Sleep has been pretty good but when you're sharing your bed (even if it IS king sized) with three cats it can be problematic when the little darlings want the warm spot, or want you to get your ass outta bed because the SUN IS UP DAMMIT AND YOU SHOULD BE AS WELL! Or maybe they think you should get their breakfast ready because, you know, dry kibble is just not what they wanted.

I finally managed to get my big ol' ass to the gym last week. The owner took me through all the machines and wrote down the settings for me. I think he was trying to kill me. The next day was fine but the day after that I was a hurtin' unit. And the next. But I went today when my massage therapist changed my appointment on me and I got through all of them. Want to know what I hate the most? The wall to wall frickin' mirrors. I've gotten very adept at only looking at my face but when you're sitting 3 feet from the mirror it's kinda hard not to see your/my fat belly, the batwings on my arms, ugh ugh ugh. Well, that's not exactly what I said to myself.

I may yet hit the treadmills he has. The place is pretty well empty or it has been each time I've been there. Much nicer than the crowds at the Y and no kids. yay!

And finally, a little something to get everyone stirred up. We WERE talking about oxytocin weren't we?

I still haven't done the piercings. Doesn't seem to be much point since I'm the only one playing with my parts. Oh well. I'm still thinking about it though.

I have discovered, quite unintentionally and totally by accident (drum roll) that female ejaculation is a real deal. See it all started when I spotted an interesting blog entitled "[aroused woman](http://arousedwomanblog.com)" and naturally had to go check it out. Well she just doesn't pull any punches. She recommended [this](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00893XJMQ/ref=oh_details_o03_s00_i00?ie=UTF8&psc=1) so naturally I ran right over to Amazon and bought it. Let me just add here that glass is wonderful. She recommended another toy but I haven't gotten the nerve up yet to order it. I'm sure you'll understand when you find it.

short pause while I ponder the wonder that is glass

OK.

So anyhoo I was giving it a good workout the other day when I finally FOUND the damned spot. Leave it to me to have things not be where they are supposed to be. Did I ever mention that when I had my hyst I decided to do a spinal block but after 30 minutes of trying the anesthesiologist had to give up because he couldn't block me? So OF COURSE something as important as the OMG-spot isn't where it should be. That explains a lot.

But it's there.

Pause for a while as I ponder the wonders of the female operating system.

Back on track. There I was enjoying the sensation when ... WTF was THAT?

[This is what THAT was.](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Female_ejaculation)

I have been wandering around like a hormone amped 16 year old since then. I've caught myself eyeballing every male who crosses my path. holy macaroni. Nothing like being in your mid-60's before you finally discover WHAT is what.

# Thread: you know how i feel
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/>

### .lina — 2012-04-03T18:28:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/post-10806>

_fish in the sea - you know how i feel

river runnin' free - you know how i feel

blossom on the tree - you know how I feel

it's a new dawn

it's a new day

its a new life

for me

and i'm feeling good

THIS. this has become my theme song. what a YEAR it has been. oh my oh my oh my.

please excuse me, i am feeling very shy and quite n00bish here amongst all you experts,

but i shall jump in the deep end of the cold pool and carry on introducing myself:

i am a swedish female of 33 years. january last year i weighed 111 kilos / 245 lbs.

that was after 15 years dedicated to low fat regime and exercise. i dieted myself fat.

an online friend in Iceland inspired me to do the opposite - her results with LCHF, which

is what we call it here in Sweden, Low Carb High Fat - gave amazing results. her weight

and her winter depression (welcome to the cold dark north) both vanished. i wanted me

some of that! so off carbs i went in march.

after some amazing success with my lifelong stomach problems instantly vanishing, my

weight still felt a bit stubborn so i searched around for possible ways to budge it, and i

came across the Leptin Reset. so THIS was why i always had been constantly hungry!?

i could literally have dinner and stuff myself to bursting point and then shortly after be

ravenous. it had eased off a bit with with LCHF but not entirely.

so on the reset I went in june.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

(my weight chart for the last few years)

things have gone so nicely - a bit up and down, sure, but overall down. and my health up.

**so** why wouldn't i try CT as well!  it's an adventure and i am full of trust. and so _inspired.

i am following the discussions here, Jack's facebook and blog, and consuming all the links

to other blogs, articles and research that i can lay my eyes upon. my eyes are wider open

than ever before, i see red threads going all over_ the place. connecting the dots. and being

considered all kinds of crazy by people around me  but i will let my results speak for me.

i still have a long_ way to go, probably 20kg / 44lbs more, but i am starting to feel like it can

be done, and people are commenting on my amazing energy and mood and improving figure.

thank you all for bringing this new paradigm on, and thank you Jack for changing my life.

### .lina — 2012-04-04T18:29:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/post-11326>

oh wow, that's a lot of sweetness all at once - i'm blushing!

**Jack, thank you kindly! will keep posting :}

**Marsha**, thanks! oh the image posting thing is a debacle isn't it! hehe. i just use the \[/img\*\] (sans the \*) and it always works. also no need for any elaborate answer to the photography lighting thing - all natural daylight (Jack would approve, non? hehe)

AbetterME\[/b\], you know, i can only take credit for taking pics of it and eating it up ;D i am an outright horrible cook! the one who stands for the magic in the kitchen is my better half. he just gave me permission to post his success-story with lowcarbing and his own first steps into CT, so that will come in a future blog post. but omg, his before/after photos are \[i\]something else\[/i\].

my own CT:ing is all babysteps - i have done the facedunking and the cold showers and even today i have sat here with ice packs strapped to my body, haha. which makes me wonder - if they are applied to areas you want to slim down a little extra, would it work to have one strapped to your chin? \[img\]http://4um.fromamouth.com/images/smilies/icon\_shifty.gif

i am working up courage to do the baths, but am a bit hesitant as i have felt a little off these last few days - i guess it could be the detox thingy from the CT maybe? i feel a little lightheaded and like my heart races. maybe just a cold coming on. right now i feel pretty awesome

i am longing for when the weather gets better so we can start enjoying the outdoors a bit more, and head to the lake for refreshing cool dips in the setting sun like last year!

**

### .lina — 2012-04-05T12:13:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/post-11659>

**davek, thanks  it's a magical photo indeed, hope to take many more this summer!

**ealachan, i agree!

elin**, thank you. just ask for recipes and thou shall receive ;D

kiwilauren**, hehehe thank you, i shall lead you into temptation

last night i cooked because my chef was out at a dinner with coworkers. i decided to make

it hard for myself and test new recipes - also to cook fish since he's allergic to all seafood.

so i started out by baking something that people had written about as a perfect pasta substitute.

not sure if i did it correctly, but it turned out... well, like a cross between pasta and omelet, hehe.

it was pretty simple to do - mix 6 eggs with 200g philadelphia and 0.75 deciliter psyllium husk,

let it set for 10 minutes, pour thinly in a baking pan, bake for 10 minutes then take out, let chill

and start cutting strips. i'm not sure if paleo people eat cream cheese though? LCHF allows it

then i ovenbaked a fillet of cod (wild-caught, i was looking for salmon but they only had farmed

ones and i read they get fed chickenpoop and liquidized feathers, which if true is just _so_ sad & gross.)

with some lemon- and parsley butter.

to add another level of chef-i-ness, i fried up some shrimp in olive oil and lime zest. et voilÃ !

though i have discovered that i am no longer a fan of olive oil (i tried 3 different), it tastes oddly

like candle wax!? haha. i guess my tastebuds have gotten more sensitive since i quit eating junk...

i confess, i've been quite naughty! my parents are visiting for easter and that

means wine, lots of wine. i've had mostly red delicous wine, but also some

glasses of rosÃ©. i usually don't snack inbetween meals but they brought out

the roasted almonds and dark chocolate. we haven't had any proper easter

food so nothing to gorge on, which is a relief. so. my weight?

**thursday -500 gram / -1.1 lbs

**friday -400 gram / -0.9 lbs

**saturday -100 gram / -0.2 lbs

sunday** -600 gram / -1.3 lbs

what kind of black magic is this? :O hehe. well i don't complain... i guess it'll bounce

up soon, probably tomorrow as we are tasting some variations wedding cakes today.

(we're getting hitched in june on our 16-year anniversary together).

and some food pics:

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

roast with fresh mushroom sauce and cauliflower mash

taco seasoned minced meat with fake pasta, avocado, creme fraiche, shredded cheese and tacosauce

oh by the way, my mom tasted the fake pasta and burst out "but it tastes just

like regular pasta!", so here's the recipe for those of you who want to try it, or

when you have friends over who are the kind that say "i could NEVER give up

pasta!", to show them there are viable alternatives sans gluten & carbs

> FAKE

**

> PASTA

**

> for 2 oven pans
>
>
>
> \- 6 eggs
>
> \- 200 grams / 1 cup of cream cheese (f.x Philadelphia)
>
> \- 0,75 deciliter / almost half a cup of psyllium husk
>
> \- a pinch of salt
>
>
>
> Put the oven on 150Â°c / 302 f.
>
> Whisk the eggs fluffy, add cream cheese and psyllium husk and whisk it all together.
>
> Let the "dough" set for 10 minutes.
>
> Cover an oven pan in a cookie dough sheet and pour in the dough, make sure it is as thick as you want the pasta to be.
>
> Bake it for 10 minutes.
>
> Take it out and let it cool off. Then you can either slice it thinly with a pizza cutter, cut out bigger pieces for lasagna or canelloni. You could also try to roll it up and cut or slice it for easier strips. For me, it was a bit tricky to get it off the baking sheet, but i think mine were too thin.
>
>
>
> Finished! You can eat it immediately, keep it in the fridge for a few days or freeze it.
>
>
>

### .lina — 2012-04-14T15:02:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/post-15036>

ok! this sauce is made with button mushrooms, this kind:

dice half of the mushrooms finely, slice the others in 2-4 slices.

start by panfrying the diced ones in butter,

then add the sliced ones and keep panfrying.

season with black pepper and the herb savory.

if you want, you can add some stock now.

add heavy cream and let it storm-boil until it thickens.

then take off the stove and let it settle.

this should do it

### .lina — 2012-04-14T23:47:47+0300

my pleasure, **AbetterME**!

so hey, i was looking through photos on my harddrive and stumbled across these pics from 2 years ago taken at my niece's baptism, that made me absolutely cringe when i saw them! so naturally, i wanted to post them online for everyone to see  and pasted it together with some newer pics from yesterday and today to compare progress since i cut carbs.

i still have a loooong way to go but what a _shock_ i got from seeing how much it showed on my face! :O you know, from day to day looking in the mirror, it's so gradual that i didn't pay much notice to it - but like this side by side it's quite a difference. i can hardly imagine what i'll look like 10 more kilos down the road, let alone 20 or 30!

oh! and! haha, i got a comment at work yesterday that i looked "fit", bless 'er!

i still weigh twice as much as the girl who said it, but it still warmed ye ol' cockles ;D

### .lina — 2012-04-16T17:26:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/post-15578>

ok so, the moussaka recipe, just approximately:

first, slice up two aubergine in fingerthick slices that you place on paper

and add salt generously on top to make them sweat out excess liquid.

then fry up 500 grams of minced meat with cinnamon, oregano, cumin,

and black pepper to taste. add a dash of cream to make it sauce-y.

when that is done, wipe the salt off the aubergines, (panfry them if you want

to but it's a matter of taste, we used them raw) and then you layer the following

in an oven pan: aubergine, meat sauce, cheese, aubergine, meat sauce, **cheese**.

bake in oven about as hot and long as you would a lasagna  it's pretty simple.

you can also just do the meatsauce, stuff it in a pitted paprika, cheese ontop, bake. yum!

### .lina — 2012-04-17T09:09:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/post-15830>

by the way, a miracle happened on sunday afternoon.

my mother phoned me up, positively _chirping_ with delight, to tell me that now that my dad has finished his VLCD thing and lost 10 kilos, their natural follow-up is to start eating the way i do, cutting the carbs and eating good healthy fats.

she said she used to have a completely distorted picture of what this meant and assumed it was only bacon and butter all day long, but after me having been eating like this for a year now, and showing her photos of what my meals look like, and treating her to delicious dinners, she's super enthusiastic about low carbing! she had even lost weight while visiting me for easter, and we ate GOOD and drank a lot of delicious wines - she was so impressed she kept lowcarbing whn she got home, and lost 2 more kilos on her own.

so she said she had baked a really delicous carb-free bread, and was about to make a batch of that fake pasta i posted the recipe of here earlier  she was so cute in her gleefulness, you could tell she was really happy about this commitment, which made ME super happy

AND she said that my older sister, who has always been unable to gain weight and always had horrible stomach problems, has started talking really well about lowcarbing to her friends after having seen the change in me, and might be open to testing it herself to finally be able to eat without stomach pains. THIS impressed me the most, because she too has been very suspicious towards this "fat diet" before, but my mom said she's gonna bake some extra bread for her and introduce her to some good dishes to show her it's all good.

it feels like i kinda won the lottery!!! i couldn't be more proud of my dear ones

### FF_BC — 2012-04-17T11:59:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/post-15841>

> **.lina;15636 wrote:**
>
> i just did this weekend as my very first time, and i am not sure if i did everything right because i felt kinda crap
>
>
>
> i went from harmonic, happy, energized, "regular" (ahem), and satiated
>
> to brainfogged, lethargic, headachey, constipated, grumpy and craving junkfood.
>
>
>
> and to crown it all, i _gained weight! XD
>
>
>
> did i do it right? i had my last meal at 8pm and first meal the next day at noon.
>
> ate regular sized portions of lunch and dinner, no snacking inbetween (despite gruesome cravings).
>
> _

_

It can be hard the first times, all that ghrelin telling you to eat just because you use to. Did you drink any coffee or tea? That really helps to keep away the hunger  It's just the first few times that the fasting is hard, keep trying! Once you've done a 24h fast, a 16h is nothing ;D Also, physical activity is great to keep hunger away, if I get very hungry I will drop down and do some pushups, that helps

For me the real breakthrough was when I realized that hunger is _not_ the body telling you to eat, it's the body telling you to start looking_ for food, there's a BIG difference!

### .lina — 2012-04-17T14:19:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/post-15849>

i did have coffee for "breakfast" and found that i actually CAN drink it without adding

some milk substitute, which was good to know, i've been gagging at black coffee before.

but yeah i'll give it another go sometime, it's just hard to pep myself for it when i've gotten

my best weightloss results from regular 3 lowcarb meals a day and a glass or two of wine,

all while feeling absolutely awesome and energetic  the choice seems easier then, hehe.

still, i am gonna stay openminded towards it and maybe it'll feel better at a later stage.

thanks for the support though!

haha! thank you all. i have been a bit tied up to make more serious efforts at snapping pics of food,

but i will have some new soon. all in all i have had a good week, and stuck to my food, workouts and CT.

infact, i started my day today (had t get up at 5am!) with ice strapped to the small of my back after

some all too vigorous gardening yesterday  the cold felt good and relieving. last night i did my facedunks

with one cat sticking his head next to mine to drink the cold water, and one on my shoulders, purring.

lovely to see the "family" engaging themselves in my activities... ;D

until i have more food photos, here are pics i snapped of my boyfriend, first pics from two years ago at a

standard swedish diet, and then pics in the same clothes from a few weeks ago, after a year of (non-strict)

LCHF and since a few months he's started doing the cold showers thing as well.

he went from 87 kilos (192 lbs) in february 2011 to 65 (143 lbs) in february 2012, and has started

working on regaining some weight in form of muscles. which also seems to come easy to him,

i'm not jealous at all yes i am

but hey, i'm at a new record low weight again, i had to replace my favourite jeans with a size smaller, yay!

thank you!  i am doing well, just have so much time outdoors during the summer,

and less computer time - which is also nice. my weight had a bit of a plateau but i

manage my diet pretty well and things seem to be budging again. i am working way

more seafood into my diet, and we are gonna build a new wooden deck for the back

yard and then i will set up our big inflatable pool and resume CT:ing properly.

is shining but i have to sit in an office all day. but the days are long and we get some

nice summery evenings and dinners in the garden.

speaking of garden, my zucchinis are finally coming in after i gave them more dirt! YAY!

such pleasure in growing your own food. maybe i should dig a pond and fish my own dinners...

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

and the recipe for the fish soup / stew, my dears:

\- whatever fish/seafood you want - i had salmon, cod, mussles and crayfish-tails.

\- some veggies of choice - i had leek, squash, fresh mushrooms, half an onion and some yellow bellpepper.

\- a lemon.

\- sambal oelek (or curry).

\- lime- or lemonpepper.

\- 100g philadelphia or other cream cheese (optional if paleo, i guess).

\- 3dl heavy cream or coconut milk.

first fry up the onion, mushrooms and leek in butter.

then add the diced bellpepper and squash.

add the cream and cream cheese (or just coconutmilk).

add the spices, sambal oelek (or curry), and the juice of half a lemon.

dice and add the fish, let stew. (i added the mussles now)

when the fish has gotten whiter in the flesh, take the pot off the stove and stir in the shrimp or crayfish.

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-07T10:38:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/post-49942>

Hi .lina I also grow zucchini in my garden - they are a never fail produce! They grow so prolifically and like you say one plant will be growing 5 at once and in different sizes. I try not let them grow too big as the skin gets tough when they are big. But they are a great vegetable to have chopped into stir frys, grated in quiche (and hidden from the children who don't like them much!), and they are delicious fried up with onions and tomatoes - chunky style. But also something I tried recently - slice them lengthways and use as a substitute for lasagne sheets in lasagne.

I have just read through you blog for the first time and its so inspiring! I will copy some of the recipes out. Thank you so much!

### .lina — 2013-04-29T15:08:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/post-85119>

hi everybody! i've been in working mode all winter and haven't been very active online (save for facebook, heh).
i'm gonna try and kick into gear with my food photography again, looking through my thread here has inspired me

last summer was really crappy in regards of growing my own vegetables, but you wouldn't believe what a winter
we had here in Sweden - we were waist-deep in snow for months upon months, and only first NOW we're seeing signs
of spring arriving... finally! some vitamin D \*not\* in pill-shape ;D

my seedlings are sprouting like mad at home, and the champions of the garden will once again be zucchini,
followed by brussel sprouts and kale. fingers crossed the frost and bugs and "killer slugs" (yeah) will stay away.
but it'll still be about a month before it's hot enough outside to plant it out - right now the only things growing
in my garden are moss and tadpoles...

anyway, nice to be back  see ya around!

### MrPinkies — 2013-07-12T15:37:23+0300

### Inger — 2013-07-12T22:21:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/post-89992>

Yes Lina, awesome!!!! I am not far from you, and we have had an awesome summer too! Just a bit on the dry side here..huh!

### .lina — 2013-07-31T17:30:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/post-91270>

thank you all sooo much  you're too sweet!!!

some updates!

i have started physiotherapy for my back, been at the gym about 2-3 times a week for about 45 minutes,
doing muscle strengthening exercises. it's really fun to see yourself getting stronger! i started in may and
back then i could do 20 reps of 31,5 kilos on one leg on the leg press machine, and now i do 58,5 : D

last week i started experimenting with the (in Sweden very) popular 5:2 fasting, and found i could breeze
by a whole day on just water and coffee with coconut oil. i even work out more efficiently during fasting,
which is a nice revelation, as i usually feel kind of tired and stuffed with food. so now i will synchronize my
fasting days with my workout days.

some pics!

people at work ooh and aaah over my lunchboxes lately, when i harvest whatever grows in my garden, to add to my proteins:

the rooftop garden delivers! colleagues were really happy to have a taste of our own produce:

my kale is going craaaazy!

### fitness@home — 2013-07-31T20:16:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/post-91280>

Lina, wonderful pictures as always!

You should ask if this thread can be moved to the Journals area. I think it belongs there  .

Thanks for sharing

### caroline — 2013-08-01T01:10:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/post-91294>

So happy to see you back with an update Lina - you are an inspired photographer! what is the meat on a stick? What is in your lunchbox? Your commitment to lovely food is wonderful.......

# Thread: Do you feel hungry for breakfast?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-feel-hungry-for-breakfast.629/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### agatha — 2012-04-03T18:56:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-feel-hungry-for-breakfast.629/post-10819>

This morning I woke up and was HUNGRY!!! This has never happened to me before, even as a child. Sluggish, cold and mildly nauseous best describes my usual morning feelings. Does this mean my cortisol rhythm is beginning to normalise? Does everyone else feel hungry when they wake?

### Birdy — 2012-04-03T19:01:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-feel-hungry-for-breakfast.629/post-10825>

For me, it usually depends on what I ate the night before. I think that since I have added CT after the leptin reset, I am not as hungry in the morning.

### fitness@home — 2012-04-03T19:54:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-feel-hungry-for-breakfast.629/post-10844>

If I wake up at my normal time of 4 AM (weekdays), I am not hungry but do make sure I eat a BAB within 30 minutes per the Leptin Reset protocol.

Oddly enough, if I am sleeping poorly and wake up between 1-3 AM my appetite is off the charts. No problem getting a good breakfast in then

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-04-03T20:00:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-feel-hungry-for-breakfast.629/post-10852>

It comes and it goes. Some mornings I am ravenous - other mornings not so much.

I'm chalking it up to detox from estrogen stored in the adipose tissue.

Prior to doing CT, I had been IF for 3+ years - and was never hungry in the morning.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-03T22:28:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-feel-hungry-for-breakfast.629/post-10916>

Some mornings, yes, most mornings, no.

### PaleoSue — 2012-04-04T00:24:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-feel-hungry-for-breakfast.629/post-10993>

Most mornings, yes. I'm also routinely skipping dinner because I get home most nights after 7 and I'm generally not hungry.

But last night I ended up quite hungry and ate a large dinner. In that case, this morning I was not hungry at ALL. I munched a bit, but just couldn't get down my normal BAB.

I was wondering how I can post an actual picture in the text of a post. When I select the "Insert Image" icon I am only able to post the link to the image. I see others have somehow figured this out. Can you share the procedure?

### .lina — 2012-04-03T19:23:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/posting-pictures.630/post-10837>

there is supposed to be a clickable tab reading "from computer" next to the "from url" headline in the popup

### admin — 2012-04-03T20:39:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/posting-pictures.630/post-10871>

Inside the text editor you'll see a little icon that looks like a frame with a tree inside. Click on that. The popup will default to select an image from your computer or from a URL. Please note here if you have any issues. Thanks!

So there must be a way to do it. Thanks.

### .lina — 2012-04-03T22:28:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/posting-pictures.630/post-10915>

ah you are _attaching_ an image, whereas when you post pics that show up immediately,

you take the image's URL and put and \[/IMG\*\] tags around them (without the\*), like this:

\[img\]http://forum.jackkruse.com/attachment.php?attachmentid=87&d=1333476384\[/IMG\*\]

\[img\]http://forum.jackkruse.com/attachment.php?attachmentid=87&d=1333476384

this can only be done if it's already uploaded somewhere though  i guess you can first attach it,

copy the URL and then edit the post to insert it in IMG tags, a little more work though ;D

You are almost there MamaGrok! When you are in your list of attachments, right click on the image name and a pop up box will appear. Select the "Copy Link Location" option. Then go back to your post and paste in the link. You can just do "Ctrl V" if you have a PC and the full link will appear. Then do the IMG tagging thing.

If you are currently doing P3/P4 phases of HCG diet and following Leptin Reset RX protocol, please join me here to share your issues, challenges. Let's support each other.

I am at R4P3D9 and trying to figure out what supplements to take, the amount of CO to consume, see if just 45 min - 1 hour walking a day is going to slow down the reset process.

I am not ready to try CT, not sure if I need it.

I didn't do any blood work, but positive that I am LR based on the symptoms described by Dr. Kruse and Mastering Leptin.

# Thread: Are Leaky Gut, Silent Reflux, and Toe Nail Fungus Related?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-leaky-gut-silent-reflux-and-toe-nail-fungus-related.634/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 37
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### curves64 — 2012-04-03T23:34:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-leaky-gut-silent-reflux-and-toe-nail-fungus-related.634/post-10960>

Dr. Kruse. I am new here and am amazed at the plethora of relevant information. Every post seems to lead me to a deeper understanding of how to best optimize my health. By way of background, i am a 54-year-old female, 5'4", 128 pounds, and gain weight easily. I can't say I'm new to paleo as I was diagnosed with a wheat allergy in my 30s, and have limited (not eliminated) carbs ever since. I recently saw an ENT for a low level chronic sore throat, and was diagnosed with **Silent Reflux. I have also had persistent **toe nail fungus** for over 20 years. After reading your blog, I am wondering if both the Silent Reflux and toe nail fungus are related to Leaky Gut**? I did start the LR and CT a week ago and already notice I am not hungry between meals and sleep more soundly. I would be most appreciative if you would let me know your thoughts on the toe nail fungus. Thanks again!

### Jim — 2012-04-04T00:05:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-leaky-gut-silent-reflux-and-toe-nail-fungus-related.634/post-10971>

curves64,

I have a leaky gut and toe nail fungus. I believe my leaky gut is much better (cured?) and the toe nail fungus seems to be getting better. I believe that eating paleo (i.e. staying away from grains) and CT have helped both conditions. I don't have reflux so I can't comment on that personally but my wife had it bad for the longest time. She took Prilosec all the time. We didn't know anything about paleo or CT at the time but she lost weight via a low carb diet and that seemed to do the trick. So given her history I would say yes to the reflux too.

Good luck!

### curves64 — 2012-04-04T00:23:33+0300

### Jim — 2012-04-04T20:38:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-leaky-gut-silent-reflux-and-toe-nail-fungus-related.634/post-11392>

Well it ain't over yet. I have it on two toes on my right foot. I use an anti-fungal on it more regularly now and it seems as though it is not regenerating in the lower part of the nail. So I am thinking that as the nail grows out, the remaining fungus will move with it. Not sure if it's the lack of carbs, the topical anti-fungal or the cold that is working but something is preventing expansion.

Chris Kresser has some info on it.

How to prevent spending the last 10 years of your life in a diaper and a wheelchair [http://chriskresser.com/how-to-prevent-spending-the-last-10-years-of-your-life-in-a-diaper-and-a-wheelchair](http://http://chriskresser.com/how-to-prevent-spending-the-last-10-years-of-your-life-in-a-diaper-and-a-wheelchair)

### Beepsilver — 2023-08-28T20:13:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-leaky-gut-silent-reflux-and-toe-nail-fungus-related.634/post-325647>

I've made some progress on my toenail fungus that I'd like to share--the remedy? Not sure what that is exactly because I've done several things and the fungus appears to be going away, so you decide.

I've had a toenail fungus on my big toe for several decades. I've taken lamosil pills two different times and the fungus goes away, but comes right back. I've tried soaking in vinegar, using Vicks vapor rub and dermatology-provided topicals. I replaced socks/shoes but none of it worked. Every week or so I'd try my best to trim the ugly distal end of the nail back which was an effort because the affected part of the nail was still attached to the nail bed. The fungus also liked to eat away at the skin around the nail--it was a poor sight.

I always wore boots/shoes because I was still in the military and always stationed in cold climates. I avoided the sun and wore sunscreen because several dermatologists I'd seen told me that's what I needed to do after having having no less than 10 skin cancers removed (basal and squamous cell) over the past 20 years.

Two years ago I moved from Nebraska to the Florida panhandle near the Gulf of Mexico. Toenail problem persisted. However about a year ago I discovered Dr Kruse and became hooked after watching the Nourish Vermont series on youtube (PS, Uncle Jack, it'd be nice if you'd make the 2018 Nourish Vermont video accessible again...at least to paying members, which I'm not, but would be if you unlocked the video ). Since then I've watched, listened and read as much as I possibly could to optimize my health. I wear the blue blocking glasses, turned off circuit breakers, use candles at night, etc. But what have I done to make toenail progress?

I can count on one hand how many times I've put shoes and socks on in the past 3-4 months. I either go barefoot, wear flip flops or Earth Runner sandals. I see the sunrise practically every morning in a speedo and over the past several months that includes feet in the surf at the beach 4-5 days a week. I usually spend two to three hours out there--75% of the time my feet are wet in one or two inches of water. Some days if I get up early or if there's a thunderstorm with lightning I use a Ruby 250W reptile lamp from Amazon on the front of my shins close to the ankle (be careful, you can and will burn yourself if you try to tolerate the heat...two feet away is good). Other days I'll use the EMR Tek light I recently got instead. I probably use the red light 3 or 4 days a week. Also I started doing CT in a cold outdoor pool last year, but the water temperature only dipped below 55 a few times, so even though I was technically becoming cold adapted, it wasn't the ideal exposure. However 3 months ago I was able to get into a water-filled freezer a handful of times, but the owner was hard to schedule with (and I don't think he appreciated that I wanted to use it every day). Not only that, I couldn't handle temps in the high 30s for anything more than 5 minutes. So I've recently started adding ice to the water in my bathtub and consistently stay 20 minutes at temps between 50-55 degrees. My tub is smallish, so after 20 minutes I roll over and do a couple of minutes of face dunking (shaking your head "no" under water speeds up the brain freeze ). (I pay for the ice until I get a freezer of my own)--I'm lean and weigh around 145ish so I'm a shivering mess afterward. Finally, the last couple of things I do is eat local seafood daily, gulf oysters/shrimp/scallps/fish which is loaded with iodine among other things. And every day over the past 6 months I've been having one serving of the sea vegetable: Maine Dulse Flakes (Amazon) which is also high in iodine. For what it's worth I've been eating mostly high fat carnivore since Dec 2020. I don't take supplements of any kind and am on no medications.

So why am I more excited about my toenail progress this time versus when I saw my toenail grow out normally after dosing with Lamosil? Because this time the fungus portion of the nail has completely pulled away from the nail bed and I was able to cut it off completely, exposing half the nail bed. Also the skin around the nail has completely healed and looks normal. So while I wait for my nail to grow out I'm keeping my fingers crossed that I staying my current course my nail will eventually grow out normally.

As an aside and maybe Dr Jack can chime in here with an explanation, but I had real difficulty tanning my face vs my body until recently. My body could take an hour or more of Florida July solar noon sun, but my face would burn after 5 minutes. I only recently discovered that if immediately after doing CT (I'd shiver-walk outside and lay face up on a cotton towel during midday sun for 40 minutes before I rolled over)--no facial burn! Also no sweating--granted it takes me 10 minutes just to stop shivering, so it's easy to understand the no sweating. I begin to sweat after I flip over, so often I will go get back in the tub (having left the cold water in it) to cool off again before going back outside to finish UVB exposure. Just FYI, I've tried face dunking only on a non-CT day and it didn't seem to have the same effect with regard to facial exposure to solar noon sun. BTW, I had my Vitamin D 25OH tested in June and it was at a whopping 116! I can only imagine what it is now.

>
> I've had a toenail fungus on my big toe for several decades. I've taken lamosil pills two different times and the fungus goes away, but comes right back. I've tried soaking in vinegar, using Vicks vapor rub and dermatology-provided topicals. I replaced socks/shoes but none of it worked. Every week or so I'd try my best to trim the ugly distal end of the nail back which was an effort because the affected part of the nail was still attached to the nail bed. The fungus also liked to eat away at the skin around the nail--it was a poor sight.
>
> I always wore boots/shoes because I was still in the military and always stationed in cold climates. I avoided the sun and wore sunscreen because several dermatologists I'd seen told me that's what I needed to do after having having no less than 10 skin cancers removed (basal and squamous cell) over the past 20 years.
>
> Two years ago I moved from Nebraska to the Florida panhandle near the Gulf of Mexico. Toenail problem persisted. However about a year ago I discovered Dr Kruse and became hooked after watching the Nourish Vermont series on youtube (PS, Uncle Jack, it'd be nice if you'd make the 2018 Nourish Vermont video accessible again...at least to paying members, which I'm not, but would be if you unlocked the video ). Since then I've watched, listened and read as much as I possibly could to optimize my health. I wear the blue blocking glasses, turned off circuit breakers, use candles at night, etc. But what have I done to make toenail progress?
>
> I can count on one hand how many times I've put shoes and socks on in the past 3-4 months. I either go barefoot, wear flip flops or Earth Runner sandals. I see the sunrise practically every morning in a speedo and over the past several months that includes feet in the surf at the beach 4-5 days a week. I usually spend two to three hours out there--75% of the time my feet are wet in one or two inches of water. Some days if I get up early or if there's a thunderstorm with lightning I use a Ruby 250W reptile lamp from Amazon on the front of my shins close to the ankle (be careful, you can and will burn yourself if you try to tolerate the heat...two feet away is good). Other days I'll use the EMR Tek light I recently got instead. I probably use the red light 3 or 4 days a week. Also I started doing CT in a cold outdoor pool last year, but the water temperature only dipped below 55 a few times, so even though I was technically becoming cold adapted, it wasn't the ideal exposure. However 3 months ago I was able to get into a water-filled freezer a handful of times, but the owner was hard to schedule with (and I don't think he appreciated that I wanted to use it every day). Not only that, I couldn't handle temps in the high 30s for anything more than 5 minutes. So I've recently started adding ice to the water in my bathtub and consistently stay 20 minutes at temps between 50-55 degrees. My tub is smallish, so after 20 minutes I roll over and do a couple of minutes of face dunking (shaking your head "no" under water speeds up the brain freeze ). (I pay for the ice until I get a freezer of my own)--I'm lean and weigh around 145ish so I'm a shivering mess afterward. Finally, the last couple of things I do is eat local seafood daily, gulf oysters/shrimp/scallps/fish which is loaded with iodine among other things. And every day over the past 6 months I've been having one serving of the sea vegetable: Maine Dulse Flakes (Amazon) which is also high in iodine. For what it's worth I've been eating mostly high fat carnivore since Dec 2020. I don't take supplements of any kind and am on no medications.
>
> So why am I more excited about my toenail progress this time versus when I saw my toenail grow out normally after dosing with Lamosil? Because this time the fungus portion of the nail has completely pulled away from the nail bed and I was able to cut it off completely, exposing half the nail bed. Also the skin around the nail has completely healed and looks normal. So while I wait for my nail to grow out I'm keeping my fingers crossed that I staying my current course my nail will eventually grow out normally.
>
> As an aside and maybe Dr Jack can chime in here with an explanation, but I had real difficulty tanning my face vs my body until recently. My body could take an hour or more of Florida July solar noon sun, but my face would burn after 5 minutes. I only recently discovered that if immediately after doing CT (I'd shiver-walk outside and lay face up on a cotton towel during midday sun for 40 minutes before I rolled over)--no facial burn! Also no sweating--granted it takes me 10 minutes just to stop shivering, so it's easy to understand the no sweating. I begin to sweat after I flip over, so often I will go get back in the tub (having left the cold water in it) to cool off again before going back outside to finish UVB exposure. Just FYI, I've tried face dunking only on a non-CT day and it didn't seem to have the same effect with regard to facial exposure to solar noon sun. BTW, I had my Vitamin D 25OH tested in June and it was at a whopping 116! I can only imagine what it is now.

Forgot to add, no basal or squamous cell carcinomas since seeing the "real" light

### Catherine Drapeau — 2025-01-06T02:32:06+0200

W
