# Thread: Swollen, painful toes WITHOUT CT??
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2012-04-04T03:24:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-11067>

Since I've been increasing my CT lately and doing about 5-6 baths a week I'm finding my toes are swollen and painful all the time. Strange thing is that I don't even put my toes in the water at all. I keep them out and even have them covered with a warm pair of socks. It's happening to toes on both feet and they are super swollen to the point that wearing shoes is very uncomfortable as is walking and putting any pressure on them.

Any ideas what this is or what would be causing it?

### shilohman — 2012-04-04T03:48:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-11084>

> **BJK77;11136 wrote:** Since I've been increasing my CT lately and doing about 5-6 baths a week I'm finding my toes are swollen and painful all the time. Strange thing is that I don't even put my toes in the water at all. I keep them out and even have them covered with a warm pair of socks. It's happening to toes on both feet and they are super swollen to the point that wearing shoes is very uncomfortable as is walking and putting any pressure on them.
>
>
>
> Any ideas what this is or what would be causing it?

BJK77, Don't have a clue, but it could be results from detox from the CT. Swelling indicates inflammation which points to excess omega 6. Have you had your omega 6/3 tested? If not get tested. Do you take krill oil?

### Adriana — 2012-04-05T17:21:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-11737>

Is there a chance you might have gout?

### Aili — 2012-04-05T17:27:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-11741>

> **Adriana;11806 wrote:** Is there a chance you might have gout?

This seems a definite possibility.

### BJK77 — 2012-04-11T03:06:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-13540>

I never really considered gout. I don't know! I just read an article linking gout to high consumption of red meat and shellfish though, so it has me wondering since my diet is pretty much 100% red meant and shellfish! I wonder if Dr. K would have any insight on this.

### Adriana — 2012-04-11T03:40:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-13567>

> **BJK77;13609 wrote:** I never really considered gout. I don't know! I just read an article linking gout to high consumption of red meat and shellfish though, so it has me wondering since my diet is pretty much 100% red meant and shellfish! I wonder if Dr. K would have any insight on this.

You need to go to a doctor and have lab work done to get a definitive diagnosis. Not something a doctor can do over the internet.

### shilohman — 2012-04-11T03:49:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-13575>

I use to have gout in my big toe, very painful. You need to get your omega 6/3 test done or like Adriana said, see a doctor.

### jenn — 2012-04-13T21:58:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-14831>

I've been experiencing a similar type of thing -- my small toes get red, swollen, and shiny on top, and the bottoms feel almost like they have blisters. Definitely not gout, which tends to be in a joint and is excruciating (DH has had it in the past). I've been mostly keeping my feet out of the water too, and encased in socks. I also take ~ 1500mg krill oil and eat fish daily, so I don't think I have excess Omega 6 (though I've got quite a macadamia habit, so who knows?) It could be some weird transitional stage, as my feet and hands will typically get whitish in a Raynaud-type way after my daily baths, and are almost never immersed in the water. If I figure it out, I'll let you know!

### BJK77 — 2012-04-14T03:19:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-14968>

> **Jenn;14900 wrote:** I've been experiencing a similar type of thing -- my small toes get red, swollen, and shiny on top, and the bottoms feel almost like they have blisters. Definitely not gout, which tends to be in a joint and is excruciating (DH has had it in the past). I've been mostly keeping my feet out of the water too, and encased in socks. I also take ~ 1500mg krill oil and eat fish daily, so I don't think I have excess Omega 6 (though I've got quite a macadamia habit, so who knows?) It could be some weird transitional stage, as my feet and hands will typically get whitish in a Raynaud-type way after my daily baths, and are almost never immersed in the water. If I figure it out, I'll let you know!

Yes, definitely keep me posted on anything you find out. From what I've read, gout is excruciating as you said. This can be painful, but not unbearable so I assume it's not gout. I just ordered another omega test so it will be interesting to see what it shows. I do eat seafood at least once a day and have just started krill oil too, but I may still be paying for past transgressions! I have read others have had similar symptoms though and they have low O6:O3, so I'm not sure what to think.

### jenn — 2012-04-14T03:20:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-14970>

I've done some Googling of the symptoms I just described and have come to the reluctant conclusion that I have chilblains! My symptoms match the descriptions and photos I've seen pretty exactly, although they're a bit milder in presentation. Not sure what to do now, as all the recommended treatments I've read say to avoid cold and bundle up...pretty much the opposite of what I've been doing....

Check out chilblains online and see if it matches what you're experiencing.

### shilohman — 2012-04-14T03:24:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-14973>

I forgot to mention this in my previous post. When I started drinking 70-80 oz of water my gout never came back. How hydrated are you?

### Greg rx — 2012-05-25T07:17:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swollen-painful-toes-without-ct.640/post-27278>

Sounds like "the gout" except it is not usually bilateral. I have isolated my trigger to processed meats but I have had bacon almost every day since starting the reset 3 months ago. Usually I get little warnings so I just increase fluid intake and have had great success, but not this week. Went boating on Saturday, had great ct session in Lake St.Claire, lots of water to drink, way too much sun. Tuesday I thought I had stubbed my toe and Wednesday I could barely walk.

Maybe it was the"on sale" bacon or unusual amount of poultry, the point is that gout is a mystery even to most doctors. The good news is that just a short time of ice SEEMS to be clearing it up.

### Koofster — 2012-04-04T03:53:21+0300

### colleencoble — 2012-04-04T04:32:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-buy-hcg.641/post-11101>

It's gotten harder.  You can but it at [www.reliablerxpharmacy.com](http://http://www.reliablerxpharmacy.com) and [www.escrorefills.com](http://http://www.escrorefills.com). With escrow you need a code and you can use 150095. Any of the hcg brands are fine. You would need 2 5000 iu vials for a long round or 4 2000 iu vials (which will allow you to mix more to keep it fresher) and you'll need insulin needles, mixing vials, a mixing needle and bacteriostatic water. You can get the bac water at Amazon and the other things at [www.gpzservices.com](http://http://www.gpzservices.com).

### colleencoble — 2012-04-12T18:43:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-buy-hcg.641/post-14258>

With hucog, you would totally fill it up to the very brim and that's 4 cc total fluid. I would start at 150 iu which is 30 units when mixed this way.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-12T19:10:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-buy-hcg.641/post-14283>

It comes both ways. If yours is liquid, it already contains 1 cc so you would add 3 more.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-12T19:44:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-buy-hcg.641/post-14297>

I've seen hucog and Corion. Hucog is the smallest of the two but will still hold a total of 4cc--just barely. Corion is a bit larger. I haven't seen the other brands though. Definitely needs to be the 2000 iu though. Mixing only 4 cc of fluid with 5000 iu would be super concentrated so I wouldn't do it that way. I don't like the concentrated doses and neither did Dr. Simeon.  I actually usually mix mine with 8 cc in 2000 iu but most do the lesser water. I do 8 cc because that's the way Simeon mixed it.

### colleencoble — 2012-06-05T15:46:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-buy-hcg.641/post-29526>

Is the vacation just with your family? If so, it's totally doable to take your food along and do it. The good thing about pushing through anyway is that it teaches you how to resist the pressure to eat things you shouldn't. The temptations are always going to be there--peer pressure, parties, good times that seem to always be associated with food--and you have to learn that food is fuel only. This is as good a time as any to learn that.  Without learning that, ultimate success doesn't come. It's too easy to fall back into emotional eating until you break the back of that monster.

Okay, lecture over.  This is just something I see over and over again. We get in social situations and don't know how to deal with it. I was proud of one gal over on the hcg forum who went to a bridal shower over the weekend and stayed strong. Doing things like that really develops your character. LOL

### colleencoble — 2012-06-05T15:47:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-buy-hcg.641/post-29528>

Oh and if you skip a day a week, you should have enough hcg for about 2 40 days rounds. 3 vials will go 36 days plus skipping a day a week gives you another 4 days so 40 days.

No kidding, This last year has been a training round for this type of thing. I have to watch out for when I'm alone more than with others. Interestingly enough, the social stuff doesn't bother me nearly as much at the other people who can't stand it when I'm not participating. I've always been a nibbler in public and drive through in private so that part doesn't bug me. It's the practicality of finding the no-fat protien while on the road that is my worry.

So I think I'll just start when I get it, and let the chips fall where they may. Plan Plan Plan. The nice thing is I won't be spending NEARLY as much money on fancy restaurants so I can get a steak and salad/veg and not eating the rest. Upon reflection, it will probably pay for itself on that alone.

In reality, it's not eating much so how hard can it be? I'm thinking just get the veggies I can eat raw and bring grilled meats that I froze.

I can tell I'm not quite there on fixing the brain - as part of my excitement is having the free range "load days".

Next question for you guys  From what I can tell it's good to go into the RX + CT for P3? Is this what you are seeing?

You guys rock!!!

### [email redacted] — 2012-11-03T17:25:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-buy-hcg.641/post-61785>

just a note, if you have bought 1x before from escrow you cannot use another code to get a further discount, they will have sent you an account code and you have to use that to purchase after the first time

### AmieSue — 2012-11-17T06:23:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-buy-hcg.641/post-64895>

How does Ecrow ship? UPS or USPS, I can't find it on their site. It says they don't ship to POBOX but the only way we get mail at our home is via UPS or Fed-X... thoughts?

### AmieSue — 2012-11-18T08:22:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-buy-hcg.641/post-65108>

EEEP! I have been trying to order as well from daynightchemist and my CC won't go through and when I send emails, I get weird, very unprofessional responses. Any suggestions?

### AmieSue — 2012-11-18T20:53:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-buy-hcg.641/post-65189>

Colleen, What do you mean when you say "special delivery"? So, I have it mailed to a physical address? Sorry for bothering you, just still a tad confused.

### colleencoble — 2012-11-18T21:40:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-buy-hcg.641/post-65200>

It has to come to a physical address. They will not send to a PO box. A special person comes and you have to sign for it.

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-01-01T19:36:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-buy-hcg.641/post-68945>

Does anyone have a new code for escrowrefills?

Also - does anyone know how to get a fake bank account number to use to purchase it online? Would bank of america issue that to me - like an account number that would only work once? My debit card got hacked shortly after purchasing it online a while ago so I'd like to avoid that again { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Lyndra — 2013-01-01T22:29:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-buy-hcg.641/post-68951>

Bank of America will let you create another credit card number to use for online shopping. You specify the maximum charge and the expiration date of the card. They call it ShopSafe. Discover has something similar. In my experience, it's good peace of mind but not a guarantee you won't have issues.

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-01-03T18:00:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-buy-hcg.641/post-69219>

Thanks for the help!

If anyone is looking for a code at escrowrefills to get $30 off their first order, use this:
CID293606

Thanks!

# Thread: So what are you up to for Easter?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Glamorama — 2012-04-04T06:37:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/post-11155>

Personally ill be watching the extended family binge on chocolate and fish ruined by gluten-rich batter,beer and wine. Then I'll spend the weekend painting our lounge-room and hopefully squeeze in a few hikes and a gym-session.

### caroline — 2012-04-04T10:06:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/post-11191>

> **Glamorama;11224 wrote:** Personally ill be watching the extended family binge on chocolate and fish ruined by gluten-rich batter,beer and wine. If I'm lucky I might get some nasty glares from my SIL (she's gotten big while I lost and she is angry about it). Then I'll spend the weekend painting our lounge-room and hopefully squeeze in a few hikes and a gym-session.

You look so fantastic I am positive you will get lots of nasty glares!! Rock on Glam!! Your family will benefit big time from what you are doing for yourself. Happy Easter

### caroline — 2012-04-04T10:08:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/post-11192>

I think I am going to go to the Opera in Sydney Harbour. I never get tired of hanging out at the harbour. Happy Easter everyone. We are all amazing!

### JustinB — 2012-04-04T13:41:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/post-11203>

I hope that I'll gonna join you there at opera in Sydney Harbor.

Happy Eater to you as well.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-04-04T23:14:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/post-11461>

I'm going to my MIL's (joy....) Friday-Sunday. Going to pack my BABs but otherwise I'm kind of at the mercy of what's served for food, which totally stresses me out. So I guess I will eat the ham and the veggie tray...even though the ham is going to have some unpleasant msg, hopefully it's not covered in brown-sugar sauce, too. If that's the case, I guess I'm doing a weekend IF.

Happy Easter to all of you!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-04T23:29:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/post-11470>

Im making my daughter Kalifornia burgers from the e cookbook and my Sean Series bacon crust pizza's from the cook book for the family get together......9 in total. All pizza will be in spring update of e cookbook

### Shijin13 — 2012-04-05T15:53:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/post-11705>

6lbs of grass fed/finished leg of lamb will be slow roasted to perfection in my garlic and herb ghee blend (B/c I was raised its only acceptable to have lamb at Easter) if asparagus is on sale (since its in season here in VA) we'll have that other wise we'll just slow cook some collards or broccoli greens.

we'll have an easter egg hunt for the kids sunday morning. we'll also finish planting the garden. get the cover crops down for the part of the garden we're going to leave fallow, and get our salad greens, summer greens, summer squash, snap peas, heirloom beans, and tomatoes planted.

Saturday we'll zip out to the farm to pick up our Meat CSA! see all the baby goats, chickens and calfs... then head home for some play!

### Shijin13 — 2012-04-05T15:58:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/post-11707>

> **Jack Kruse;11539 wrote:** Im making my daughter Kalifornia burgers from the e cookbook and my Sean Series bacon crust pizza's from the cook book for the family get together......9 in total. All pizza will be in spring update of e cookbook

Kalifornia burgers were on the menu for this week but the grass fed pork shoulder roasted in the rosemary recipe has lasted us 3 meals + lunches!

FWIW DH saw the Kalifornia burgers and decided to take them to the next level - he plans to wilt cabbage, and wrap the burgers in the wilted cabbage leaves and bake!!!!

### vlynnb — 2012-04-05T18:48:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/post-11780>

Going to dinner at my daughter's. She will be trying to accommodate my diet with the meat she serves at least. I am hoping she will get to the point of studying and applying the Leptin Rx soon as she struggles so with her weight. She is on Weight Watchers now after using many diets including hhcg successfully but always gaining back. She is afraid to do anything more right now as she slips easily into anorexia. I'm hoping she will be open to Dr. K opinions on that soon.

On another note, another daughter who has been a highly placed Div I collegiate athlete is now doing cross fit at an alarming pace and is trying to get me to join her. She hasn't read Dr. K. but is sure her exercise schedule will benefit me more. We are a family with a strong background in medicine and athletics and have been well steeped in CW so it is proving difficult to change their views. Hope my determination and improving health will convince them.

Happy Easter without chocolate to all of you, well without milk chocolate anyway!

### Croak — 2012-04-05T20:32:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/post-11816>

Some well meaning asshat put a pile of Hersey's Kisses and Cadbury Cream Eggs on my desk this morning, it ambushed me when I got in. I fought it off, forced it to retreat to another part of the office. I won that skirmish, but the war continues.

### ealachan — 2012-04-05T20:52:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/post-11826>

> **Croak;11885 wrote:** Some well meaning asshat put a pile of Hersey's Kisses and Cadbury Cream Eggs on my desk this morning, it ambushed me when I got in. I fought it off, forced it to retreat to another part of the office. I won that skirmish, but the war continues.

Dude, I feel you. I had to re-home a little bag of jelly beans and a pile of Hershey Kisses today, too!

### vlynnb — 2012-04-06T00:14:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/post-11945>

I'm happy to say that chocolate in general and jelly beans do not tempt me anymore. It is just a good thing that salt and vinegar potato chips or any other potato chips are not part of the Easter tradition or I'd be having to exercise a good deal of self control.

### TerrierMom — 2012-04-06T17:07:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/post-12131>

> **Jack Kruse;11539 wrote:** Im making my daughter Kalifornia burgers from the e cookbook and my Sean Series bacon crust pizza's from the cook book for the family get together......9 in total. All pizza will be in spring update of e cookbook

Anxiously awaiting the spring update!!! Love the cookbook! Got my folks cooking from it too and hope one day to get them off statins, HBP meds, metformin.... Resolved I will not take my "morning" and "nighttime" Rx meds like them. They think I'm crazy to come use their 64degree pool for my CT but are happy to feed me fat and protein before I jump in, God bless 'em (and God bless Jack Kruse... Changing lives!)

### TerrierMom — 2012-04-06T17:12:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/post-12136>

> **Croak;11885 wrote:** Some well meaning asshat put a pile of Hersey's Kisses and Cadbury Cream Eggs on my desk this morning, it ambushed me when I got in. I fought it off, forced it to retreat to another part of the office. I won that skirmish, but the war continues.

That made me laugh!! My office is full of such asshats. Every holiday is a new flavor for the zillion candy jars and dishes.

My fun this Easter weekend is a baby shower... Hoping my BAB will help that morning as I doubt there will be food I can eat there. I'll also have to forcefully decline cake (my weakness).... But at 7-8 weeks on the reset (and feeling the signs it's working) I am NOT ABOUT to blow it....!!!!

Best part about Easter is no more meatless Fridays. Fish (even slathered in fat) just doesnt satisfy me like meat does!! Yay Easter!!

### Shijin13 — 2012-04-06T17:30:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/post-12147>

You know what's funny about offices and co-workers - when some one at HQ sends an email out to all employees about "There's Xfood at so and so's desk - stop buy to get your piece!" the off-site employees - some who are Overseas - send nasty emails back - saying "Thanks for sharing, can you take an picture, and send it to me so I can see what I can't have" makes me LOL all the time...

I remember one place where I worked - there was this candy bowl - one day when the owner was on vacation - the office conspired against him, confiscated the bowl & candy and threw it out so it was no longer a temptation! That lasted until the next halloween when he brought in a new bowl and candy... and the same thing happened on his next vacation... eventually he got the hint...

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-06T17:32:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/post-12148>

I'll be eating lamb and nothing else, despite being asked to bring a vegetable I can eat, because I don't eat carbs after breakfast and no one else likes the veggies I can eat, lol.

And probably doing maybe a little resisting, at least of my kids' easter eggs (which I fill with "yogurt" covered raisins, dried fruit, organic gummies, etc.), judging by the dream I had last night (of eating half a Kroger cake).

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-08T12:52:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/post-12617>

We'll be eating a grass-fed 3-rib of beef roast today, I'll be doing some roast potatoes for the children, and other vegetables will be roasted cauliflower and some sauteed broccoli. I've made 90% dark chocolate Easter eggs for all (mini-ones), and will have some baked chocolate custard from The Primal Cookbook for the children for pudding. We'll also be going for an extra-long walk with the dogs down to our pond (which I might just get into!) and back through the woods. Racing Demon by candlelight will be the order of the evening, rather than any computers or television.

### David — 2012-04-08T13:11:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/post-12620>

I colored hard-boiled eggs for the first time in decades for in the baskets. No candy this year, not even a jelly bean; but I did include a surprise: brightly colored Primal T-shirts from Mark's Daily Apple still in their neat little cellophane packages.

We will soon see how well this substitutes ...

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-08T16:30:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/post-12649>

we are unleashing the Sean series of ketogenic pizza's on our family.......this should be epic. And I am doing Jack attack video's on them all.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-04-08T17:24:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/post-12676>

> **Krusing\_tptimal\_in\_CA;12200 wrote:** Anxiously awaiting the spring update!!! Love the cookbook! Got my folks cooking from it too and hope one day to get them off statins, HBP meds, metformin.... Resolved I will not take my "morning" and "nighttime" Rx meds like them. They think I'm crazy to come use their 64degree pool for my CT but are happy to feed me fat and protein before I jump in, God bless 'em (and God bless Jack Kruse... Changing lives!)

I got rid of my BP meds after two weeks of the leptin reset. I have been on them for 10 years prior to the reset. Now it's been 3 months and the BP has settled at around 120-70. No doubt food choices were causing my HBP and I have been a "healthy" eater all my life. I hope your parents have the same results with their BP.

### BJK77 — 2012-04-09T01:23:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/post-12815>

This was my first full Paleo Easter. Although I was so sick last year I barely ate anything, so I guess that could have passed for paleo too

\- Pastured, organic ham

\- Zucchini noodles sauteed in bacon fat and topped with all the yummy crispy bits

\- Asparagus wrapped in prosciutto

\- And of course sauerkraut because what's a meal without some ferments?!?!

The kids and hubby had some fried sweet potatoes too, but I opted out of those to keep my carbs in check. Dessert was some cocoa nibs . . . and I was perfectly content and happy with my meal! My family, on the other hand (parents/siblings), thought I was crazy. They did decent too, but added in some mashed potatoes and of course dessert - fruit salad and carrot cake. Could have been worse I guess!

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-04-11T14:49:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-what-are-you-up-to-for-easter.643/post-13698>

I worked on modifying all our family traditions this year. It wasn't perfect, but very good. Roast Leg of Lamb, swiss chard, broccoli, sweet potatoes. There was also bread on the table. I am a hard core Catholic and realize that I can either entice them like Jesus did or ram it down their throats. Right now, I'm evangelizing by my actions and not so much my words. It just works better with college kids who question everything.

I did bake my Brown Bag Cookie Art cookies, but it was only one a piece. The rest were given away to neighbors who supplied the roaster when the oven died! Hopefully by next year I will be better apt at primal baking to create something truly worthy of this feast. Since I have college age kids who expected the usual Easter basket fare, it was minimal. I think they got the hint... especially the oldest who made her first visit home since January... with a "Mom you look really good".

To fill out Easter baskets, I purchased an appropriate book for the little ones ... one was an exquisite art book about the importance of the Mass.

[http://www.gingerbreadbooks.com/Gingerbread\_House\_Site/The\_Weight\_of\_a\_Mass.html](http://http://www.gingerbreadbooks.com/Gingerbread_House_Site/The_Weight_of_a_Mass.html)

The other was about being a good and virtuous friend.

Another neighbor, originally from Texas shared their custom of filling colored eggs with birdseed and pasting the hole at the end with tissue. We had over 200 eggs "hidden" for 5 kids. When found, the fun is to crack them on each other heads expelling the seed and sharing it with birds.

I did drink wine, and nibbled the naughty stuff, but probably the biggest change was sleep. Granted all the liturgical services of the Triduum can be alot, but what got my sleep really was having college kids home.... staring with a midnight arrival of their flight on Thurs night. My 10pm bedtime was shot to HE double toothpicks the whole weekend and I GAINED weight for the first time since I started the LRx.

Overall, it was a beautiful Lent and Easter. Very illuminating on so many levels. Nothing like reading the Imitation of Christ sitting in a tub of cold water. It was good balance to keep my desire to look better out of the vanity category and more ordered on a virtuous approach to temperance and prudent good health. A priest friend said "That's a tough penance". I laughed. "You don't feel it after the first 5 minutes".

### MJ* — 2012-04-04T07:17:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-you-are-becoming-optimized-when.644/post-11169>

### Glamorama — 2012-04-04T07:57:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-you-are-becoming-optimized-when.644/post-11180>

When you suggest sex for the husband while still wearing yellow lens goggles and ice packs, and don't get why he's not keen.

### caroline — 2012-04-04T10:01:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-you-are-becoming-optimized-when.644/post-11190>

when you have no room in your fridge or freezer for food. I would rather have ice and cold water!

### Honey Crisp — 2012-04-04T13:30:21+0300

### KiwiLauren — 2012-04-05T01:20:47+0300

Hey, I'm back!

I've been in the UK for weeks, but thought I'd post an update picture. Tough staying primal living in a hotel surrounded by restaurants, and frankly I didn't sweat it too much. Stayed away from carbs as much as possible, stayed with a big fat-heavy breakfast, but also had more than a few pints of lager (it's London, I had to be sociable!).

Just to recap, here's how I started in November:

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

And here's where I'm at as of this evening. I'm svelting! From 306 to 221 pounds for those of you keeping score.

Also, I'm wearing the same pair of underwear in both pictures. Pay attention to the "Hanes" tag for a good sense of perspective.

# Thread: interested Docs need bullet points I think
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interested-docs-need-bullet-points-i-think.646/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2012-04-04T10:51:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interested-docs-need-bullet-points-i-think.646/post-11193>

I have been thinking about this a lot lately. Those of us who have a hard time with all the science like to have bullet points so I think a busy Doc might appreciate their own bullet points prepared by Dr. Kruse. then they could go back for more info when ready. Over the last number of years I have been lucky enough to have friendships with some amazing, caring Docs - but they are so swamped. They adore their families and love their patients and contribute to their community but time is so limited. Just my 2 cents worth.

### Shijin13 — 2012-04-04T15:54:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interested-docs-need-bullet-points-i-think.646/post-11239>

Having lugged Dr K's work w/me to dr appointments - here's what I did... I picked the 1 blog I thought would be most valuable. when I went to the VA med ctr last august - that was the VAP; b/c I new my cholesterol would be high.

When you're working w/CW docs - you need to talk about things that can directly impact how they work today.

so lets say you've got high cholesterol - look at the VAP, and the Hormones blogs. Also consider bringing in some of Dr. Davis's blogs from track your plaque - he's a cardiologist - so why not include those things.

also make sure they understand you want a partner in your search for optimal health- find the things they have interest to lead them tow where Dr K is. some will say - don't trust everything you read on the internet - then ask them about the medical journals you've read etc on line - and their thoughts on the new findings etc.

Let them know you're trying to educate your self, you understand the recommendations they're making, and why,a s well as how it will help/hurt your condition/. Docs will be more open when you talk w/them about everything. granted you will run into docs who will tell you to just accept the way you are and you can't change anything... they are hopeless - and you should fire them.

If you're lucky you'll get a doc who's open, who also teaches young docs, and you'll have the opportunity to change not only your healthcare, but how your doctor not only treats you, but impact that future doc as well...

medical care isn't just about fixing whats wrong, but listening (both doctor and patient) its an exchange of ideas.... If you have to buy a copy of fathead or the perfect health diet and share that with them... ask them to watch the videos - so you can discuss the pros/cons of them at your next appointment...

### Tizawee — 2012-04-06T14:49:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interested-docs-need-bullet-points-i-think.646/post-12086>

I would like to see a page called "MD References" or something similar. It could contain points from Dr. Kruse specifically addressing the nuances of treating or operating on a cold-adapted human. I agree that we need to let them know we're looking for a partner, but it would be far easier to say "Go to jackkruse.com and look in the MD References section. If you still have questions after looking through the information from one doctor to another, let me know what they are, and I'll do some research."

I got this idea from [www.johnleemd.com](http://http://www.johnleemd.com). Dr. John Lee was the father of BHRT. He used progesterone cream in his clinical practice for 30 years, when everyone else thought he was nuts. He began writing books and one of his first was specifically directed at clinicians. So if I want to go to a doc who is unfamiliar with physiologic doses of progesterone to alleviate estrogen dominance symptoms, all I have to do is order the book or tell the doc to order it from the web site.

Just my two cents...

Elin


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Cautionary tales:  severe CT reactions and how you've dealt with them
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 46
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-04-04T14:16:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11214>

Okay, so I've whined and whinged and complained here, there, and everywhere. I promise after starting this thread I'll DROP it if I'm on my own!

After nearly 3 weeks of severe scalp rash, itch, scabbing, red and painful scalp/nape SWELLING, and lumpy head/neck lymph nodes, I've completely quit anything to do with cold. I finally clued in that it might be mis-located cold urticaria (googled it, it can happen!) and HMMM... maybe even the cool water I rinse my hair with might be aggravating it! So I'm on day two of naturopath's neem oil suggestion and the worst swelling has moved from my left side to the right and hopefully from there, GONE \*crossed fingers\*. There's \*some\* improvement, anyway, though the little bumps are still moving down my neck. Her spin on it was from chinese medicine - too much yang (cold) can turn into yin (heat, inflammation). My hsCRP is 1.1 (.1 away from "low), and the $130 for o3/o6 testing is out of reach at the moment (ontario health care won't pay for it), though til now i've been supplementing pretty high doses of fish oil (1-2T of high potency oil daily since the new year). Oh yes, and the naturopath also stopped my BHRT til this settles down. Except for 10 days or so in the middle, March has been one long, super light TOM, so my hormones are in a whack as well, though in fairness that could be perimenopause.

Has anyone else encountered any other "extreme" symptoms we haven't been warned about or advised how the blazes to treat?? Perhaps we need a cautionary thread for enthusiastic newbies. I haven't given up on CT, but you wanna bet I'm gonna get back into it CAREFULLY!! This feeling of being given a powerful tool and left to our own devices is a little frightening now

### Adria — 2012-04-04T17:35:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11281>

meyoolia I'm sorry you're having extreme symptoms. I'm glad you posted. We could be the first women in our age group taking a sudden dose of CT. We're all unique as to where our body health is currently, to me this means approach CT at your own individual pace and listen to your body. If I were you I'd work on diet and light until you're healthier. To me heathier means normal weight sustained for long time, your skin is clear, you're sleeping great, no diseases, little to no meds or supplements needed. And I would be cautious of any advice from a neuropath. I would't use neem on my scalp or skin as neem has shown to be toxic to children if ingested. So for me I'd consider it unsafe. Be as natural in all areas of your life as possible. I'd stay at little to no CT until you're at optimal health. Even then go very slow and be aware of your body at all times, be ready to back off or stop. This may not be the path DrK recommends, or your neuropath, or what any others on this forum are doing.

All that matters is that you become healthier in the long run.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-04T17:49:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11298>

We're all unique as to where our body health is currently, to me this means approach CT at your own individual pace and listen to your body. If I were you I'd work on diet and light until you're healthier. To me heathier means normal weight sustained for long time, your skin is clear, you're sleeping great, no diseases, little to no meds or supplements needed. And I would be cautious of any advice from a neuropath. I would't use neem on my scalp or skin as neem has shown to be toxic to children if ingested. So for me I'd consider it unsafe. Be as natural in all areas of your life as possible. I'd stay at little to no CT until you're at optimal health. Even then go very slow and be aware of your body at all times, be ready to back off or stop. This may not be the path DrK recommends, or your neuropath, or what any others on this forum are doing.
>
>
>
> All that matters is that you become healthier in the long run.

Agreed, re working on diet & light and getting candida & allergy tested next week. I've had my amber "sunglasses" for a week now and LOVE them....even my son says they make him feel mellow in the evening. I'll keep your neem alerts in mind, but so far it's the only thing that's provided some relief/healing, aside from doctor's cortisone liquid, which I quit after 2-3 applications.

Is CT working alright with you? how far down the CT rabbit hole are you?

### Pardus — 2012-04-04T18:10:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11316>

Hi Meyoolia,

21 yo male suffering with chronic eczema/atopic dermatitis + seb derm since birth.

CT so far is really drying out my skin, and at the moment I have rashes + eczema returning to areas where the skin had been clear for almost 2 years.

Apparently it is some kind of detox, can't say for sure... But I'm sticking through it because I've seen darker days in the past.

It's not all bad though. My face cleared up and looks better and more moist than it ever did in years. I think it's because I expose it to the cold the most, as I go for 1 hr cold walks every day without a coat. During this walk, my face gets the coldest, and is completely exposed to the wind the whole time.

Take CT as slow as you need to go. Whether it's lowering the room temp in your house by a few degrees, or cutting it out entirely for a period of time...

### colleencoble — 2012-04-04T18:31:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11327>

Sorry you're going through this. Doc has said cold urticaria is from poor omega 3/6 ratio so it's probably good you've backed off. How much were you doing and what were you doing? If full immersion, then that was probably too fast. Though I jumped right into immersion too. I'm 60 though and have been on BHRT for 5 years, regular hormones before that. And my naturopath got me in pretty good shape with omega 3 about 15 years ago. It's good you have a naturopath to help you. They usually try to treat underlying causes than regular doctors. They don't just throw drugs at the problem but try to fix the cause.

Neem is good. It's a natural shampoo my daughter uses on dogs in her groom shop when they have skin problems. Hopefully that will really fix it.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-04T22:14:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11434>

> **colleencoble;11396 wrote:** Sorry you're going through this. Doc has said cold urticaria is from poor omega 3/6 ratio so it's probably good you've backed off. How much were you doing and what were you doing? If full immersion, then that was probably too fast. Though I jumped right into immersion too. I'm 60 though and have been on BHRT for 5 years, regular hormones before that. And my naturopath got me in pretty good shape with omega 3 about 15 years ago. It's good you have a naturopath to help you. They usually try to treat underlying causes than regular doctors. They don't just throw drugs at the problem but try to fix the cause.
>
>
>
> Neem is good. It's a natural shampoo my daughter uses on dogs in her groom shop when they have skin problems. Hopefully that will really fix it.

thanks for your feedback, each of you! Like several other folks, i had high hopes and few concerns. I just wanted to start a thread with a less rosy picture, more realistic perhaps. I didn't leap straight to the tub, actually - did a couple of super easy face immersions, then moved to working up to 60 minutes with the cold packs on my whole torso over the course of 7-10 days, THEN started with Dr. K's 50-55F water for 10, 15, 20...up to 40 minutes over the same amount of time. When that seemed relatively easy, I lowered the water temps to 47 or 48F a couple of times and that seems to have "done" it  no warning, really, I just rashed up on my head, didn't know what it was and so quit tubbing but kept rinsing my shampoo out with cool/cold water. BAD idea in retrospect.

What we REALLY need from Dr. K, or each other, is a list of warning signs for when to slow down/stop CT, including bold lettering that cold urticaria doesn't HAVE to appear on the parts we've chilled. That's sorta why I started this thread, not to belabour my own "sufferings"

### colleencoble — 2012-04-04T22:19:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11438>

Yes that would be great! I think he has said you shouldn't put cold on your head though. I think he wears a hat when he's in the water.

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-04T22:42:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11447>

Are the rashes/itching/etc. exclusively in areas you're using cold?

If so, could be urticaria. Best path may be to lay off a while, or use only very gentle CT (like 85 degree baths), fix your omega ratio, and try again down the line, maybe 6 months or a year.

If not, it's likely detox (this ESPECIALLY likely for the one who said old eczema is returning). Slow up instead of quitting, if the detox is too much for you to take, and on the other side of this you will be well on your path to healthy you never imagined. Voice of experience here.

### Kathy — 2012-04-05T00:11:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11495>

> **meyoolia;11283 wrote:** Has anyone else encountered any other "extreme" symptoms we haven't been warned about or advised how the blazes to treat?? Perhaps we need a cautionary thread for enthusiastic newbies. I haven't given up on CT, but you wanna bet I'm gonna get back into it CAREFULLY!! This feeling of being given a powerful tool and left to our own devices is a little frightening now

After 2 weeks of face dunking, wanting to go slow and follow protocol exactly, I proceeded to 5 minutes of abdomen icing wearing a compression shirt & using a therapy gel-pack that came with a fabric covering. The gel-pack NEVER touched my skin. After 5 minutes my skin temperature was 49Âºf, so the next day, I went for 8 minutes, instead of increasing to 10 - thinking: "go slow". Skin temp was 50Âºf & tummy felt numb.

A few hours later, warm to touch, bright red marks appeared on belly where I'd iced. These began to hurt the next day & over a few days time darkened to eventually look like bruises. Next skin got itchy and coloring faded. They aren't completely gone yet & it's been about a week and a half. I've read from others that peeling skin may come next.

In [this thread](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?551), Dr K warns against using alcohol filled gel-packs for spot icing, too late for me, but perhaps helpful to someone else.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-05T00:28:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11503>

> **MamaGrok;11516 wrote:** Are the rashes/itching/etc. exclusively in areas you're using cold?
>
>
>
> If so, could be urticaria. Best path may be to lay off a while, or use only very gentle CT (like 85 degree baths), fix your omega ratio, and try again down the line, maybe 6 months or a year.
>
>
>
> If not, it's likely detox (this ESPECIALLY likely for the one who said old eczema is returning). Slow up instead of quitting, if the detox is too much for you to take, and on the other side of this you will be well on your path to healthy you never imagined. Voice of experience here.

Nope. That's what confused me for so long after it broke out. It's JUST on my head and neck. I CT immersed just up to my neck, with arms & legs out. The rash area was several inches above the water line, so I didn't initially guess urticaria! I've never had skin issues in my life, never eczema. I actually \*did\* slow up while I researched head/neck rashes, and only did ice packs. It got worse, I wised up and quit any cold for now.

And the cold-water shampoo rinses were done after I'd quit tubbing, with just a hand-held shower head, bent over the tub. Only made things worse, I suspect. Now I'm warm rinsing. The neem is actually a godsend - I'm neem oiling (cut 50/50 with almond oil) once or twice a day (2nd time in the evening and I 'marinade' in it overnight), washed twice a day with neem shampoo/conditioner today cuz I can't be seen by piano students all greased up, lol. It's unbelievable how swollen and really painful the more affected side has gotten...urff!... thankfully, slowly subsiding....hopefully gone by this time next week!!

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-05T00:33:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11505>

>
> A few hours later, warm to touch, bright red marks appeared on belly where I'd iced. These began to hurt the next day & over a few days time darkened to eventually look like bruises. Next skin got itchy and coloring faded. They aren't completely gone yet & it's been about a week and a half. I've read from others that peeling skin may come next.
>
>
>
> In [this thread](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?551), Dr K warns against using alcohol filled gel-packs for spot icing, too late for me, but perhaps helpful to someone else.

Well done on going slow. It's frustrating that one person's slow is another's too fast! I never reacted at the ice stage. Just happy cherry red. I iced with home made gel packs made of 1 part rubbing alcohol to 2 parts water. I suspect they get less cold than other mixtures I've found that use 1:1 mixtures or the commercial blue gel packs. I never had a problem with the 1:2 packs.... maybe give 1:2 or even 1/2:2-1/2 mixtures, to "warm" them up a bit but still avoid freezing solid?

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-05T00:57:24+0300

> **colleencoble;11396 wrote:** Neem is good. It's a natural shampoo my daughter uses on dogs in her groom shop when they have skin problems. Hopefully that will really fix it.

Warning: This stuff stinks to the point of being almost unbearable straight from the bottle.

The gel-pack NEVER touched my skin. After 5 minutes my skin temperature was 49Âºf, so the next day, I went for 8 minutes, instead of increasing to 10 - thinking: "go slow". Skin temp was 50Âºf & tummy felt numb.

> A few hours later, warm to touch, bright red marks appeared on belly where I'd iced. These began to hurt the next day & over a few days time darkened to eventually look like bruises. Next skin got itchy and coloring faded. They aren't completely gone yet & it's been about a week and a half. I've read from others that peeling skin may come next.

Thanks for reiterating that warning. For my own learning process, did have red or white skin the time you got the later soreness.

From my perspective, this kind of reaction is like a minor sunburn - not gonna cause any permanent harm at all, just don't make a habit of it. I'd like to know if Dr. Kruse agrees.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-05T06:37:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11632>

Several have posted about sinus issues and coughs etc.. with CTing. I've had sinus drainage and a cough for weeks.. this can't be just detoxing, can it? It's so bad, I can't sleep at night. This is pretty much my only detox symptom, but it's knocking me out. Dr. K says his family does CT when sick.. I did a 50 deg soak last night for 30 minutes and was feeling kind of clumsy and out of it afterward.. and shivered for a long time.. then coughed most of the night. (I did follow protocol exactly).

On the positive side, my muscles are rock hard and I don't have any pain anymore. I like the cold tub soaks, but not the bad symptoms.

### LisaAPB — 2012-04-05T07:02:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11634>

I have had a minor rash since starting CT. The calves of my legs itch, but I've had itchy legs off and on for years. The rash is on my back. It started at the tailbone area and moved up my spine to mid back. Actually, it started in my butt cheeks, it's the first thing I asked Dr K about after I started CT, ..."was it normal to experience itchy buttocks?" I hope it's just detox for me. I've also had a few pimples, which is not common for me and indicates something hormonal going on.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-05T11:20:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11654>

I have come out in an itchy rash up the back of my thighs and in the small of my back - it appeared last night. It is NOT cold urticaria, more like a bad case of eczema. I have also broken out in boils on my legs (3 at the last count) - most unpleasant! I'm hoping that these are all detox symptoms, and that they will pass.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-05T15:11:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11686>

LisaAPB & oliviascotland - ya see, this is what I mean. Powerful tools, and we have three choices: "it's detox, what do you expect" and we tough it out until some undetermined time when it settles down, which I'm gathering the majority of folks opt for.... or "it's detox, slow down" and we do.... or, door number three, we've caused some sort of autoimmune issue or illness and should likely STOP! But how to know which? Detox or illness or crashing immune system?? (yeah, the 1st 2 are arguably the same thing perhaps) Should some people \*not\* be doing parts or any of the CT? And how long should we wait ('suffer') before opting to stop? When does it become as productive as treating headache with a rock to the head? that said, I do plan to ease back into the CT eventually, but it really does make me wonder....

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-05T15:19:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11689>

> **PaleoNana;11701 wrote:** Several have posted about sinus issues and coughs etc.. with CTing. I've had sinus drainage and a cough for weeks.. this can't be just detoxing, can it? It's so bad, I can't sleep at night. This is pretty much my only detox symptom, but it's knocking me out. Dr. K says his family does CT when sick.. I did a 50 deg soak last night for 30 minutes and was feeling kind of clumsy and out of it afterward.. and shivered for a long time.. then coughed most of the night. (I did follow protocol exactly).
>
> On the positive side, my muscles are rock hard and I don't have any pain anymore. I like the cold tub soaks, but not the bad symptoms.

Have you tried easing up the intensity, either with less time or - arguably better - warmer soaks? Sleep is likely more important than hardcore CT. I love my neti pot - ever tried one? great for rinsing the sinuses.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-05T15:26:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11692>

Based on eczema-like rash, I've decided to keep the daily cold showers, keep the daily spot-icing, but move on to baths every second day until it gets better. I will also monitor the progress and slow down further if it gets worse. I really don't want to stop completely as so far it's helping me avoid major knee surgery and I'll put up with a lot to avoid that!

PaleoNana - I'd also suggest backing off a little; maybe raising the temperature of your bath a bit, and shortening the amount of time you spend in it. If that doesn't help, then reducing frequency as well may be the way to go, and maybe you need to stop altogether for a while.

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-05T15:43:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11701>

> **PaleoNana;11701 wrote: Several have posted about sinus issues and coughs etc.. with CTing. I've had sinus drainage and a cough for weeks.. this can't be just detoxing, can it? It's so bad, I can't sleep at night. This is pretty much my only detox symptom, but it's knocking me out. Dr. K says his family does CT when sick.. I did a 50 deg soak last night for 30 minutes and was feeling kind of clumsy and out of it afterward.. and shivered for a long time.. then coughed most of the night. (I did follow protocol exactly).
>
> On the positive side, my muscles are rock hard and I don't have any pain anymore. I like the cold tub soaks, but not the bad symptoms.
>
>
>
> > **LisaAPB;11703 wrote:** I have had a minor rash since starting CT. The calves of my legs itch, but I've had itchy legs off and on for years. The rash is on my back. It started at the tailbone area and moved up my spine to mid back. Actually, it started in my butt cheeks, it's the first thing I asked Dr K about after I started CT, ..."was it normal to experience itchy buttocks?" I hope it's just detox for me. I've also had a few pimples, which is not common for me and indicates something hormonal going on.
>
>
>
> oliviascotland;11723 wrote:** I have come out in an itchy rash up the back of my thighs and in the small of my back - it appeared last night. It is NOT cold urticaria, more like a bad case of eczema. I have also broken out in boils on my legs (3 at the last count) - most unpleasant! I'm hoping that these are all detox symptoms, and that they will pass.

I am not saying this is detox in any of your cases, but yes, by your descriptions, these are all symptoms that \*could\* be detox. On GAPS, it is not unusual to have eczema, drainage, itching, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, a huge gamut of symptoms.

Basically, what these symptoms all have in common is that they are all ways (or symptoms of ways) the body evacuates toxic crap. Why do you get pox from varicella, or vomit with Rotovirus? So the body can get the crap out!

There are two reasons for crap coming out of the body - a) crap is coming in (like when you're still on a terrible diet and/or catch a contagious illness) and needs to go out or b) crap that has been stored (usually b/c there was too much for the body to deal with it) is on its way out. Either way, it comes out.

Anonther kind of detox is where the crap that is coming in or going out is going through the bloodstream and you get non-evacuation symptoms. Examples from my family are tics & leg pain. Two of my children had bad tics & one of them had random nighttime severe leg pain (just like dh had as a child, but never since), but none had had them in the last few months since our diet got much better. Our first week of GAPS, both my son and my husband had the leg pains (remember, first time dh had ever had them as an adult), then _never had them again_. I can only guess the pains were not from sitting on a cold floor as he had always been told (something my son had never done), but from toxins long stored and finally released.

With the tics, they got worse on GAPS. For several months. Then disappeared. Detox - poison on the way in, poison on the way out, but better out than in.

The fact that CT also causes this tells me it is doing something powerful. It's not just burning fat (although burning fat itself releases toxins. I've seen mainstream medical articles discussing whether it was "worth it" for the obese to lose weight because of the detox symptoms (they called it "disease," although obviously it's just temporary) they would have to go through as toxins were released from the fat cells that had so long protected the body from toxic overload.).

> **meyoolia;11755 wrote:** Powerful tools, and we have three choices: "it's detox, what do you expect" and we tough it out until some undetermined time when it settles down, which I'm gathering the majority of folks opt for.... or "it's detox, slow down" and we do.... or, door number three, we've caused some sort of autoimmune issue or illness and should likely STOP!

Seriously - autoimmune disease does not come from cold or from detox. Seriously.

My rule of thumb is that if the illness is enough to make you quit CT or fall off the dietary wagon, slow down instead.

Of course, my threshold is high when I'm certain I'm on the right path. It took six weeks of a different serious die-off symptom every week before I fell off the GAPS wagon.

\- First week, felt like I had the flu for two days. Absolutely run over by a truck.

\- Second week had a sinus infection for the _first time in my life_ (shows that I had been storing up crap for a long time, but my body was too overloaded to detox it properly through an actual discernible illness) Three days, ending in a huge black blob coming out of my nose that I never knew was in there.

\- Third week had terrible abdominal pains that kept me on the toilet for literally four hours (not kidding, felt like labor, in intensity, and in waves), then continued abating over four days and nearly sent me running to the urgent care when I had one episode of black, tarry stools (indicates upper intestinal bleeding).

\- Fourth - Sixth weeks I can't remember (they may be in my MDA journal), but finally fell apart in a jar of JIF mixed with white chocolate chips b/c I couldn't stand it anymore! That huge craving should have been my signal that I needed to back off, STAT!

I personally welcome detox symptoms like rashes & eczema & pimples, as long as they don't itch me absolutely to death (despite comfort measures, like topical CO, ACV, etc.), b/c they're relatively painless compared to the 2-3 days of absolute physical misery I went through for the first 6 weeks of GAPS.

After GAPS, my health has been better than ever before. I needed that detox bad. And I truly believe that b/c I went through it then, that's why my CT detox has been limited to zits.

Everyone's detox will look different, depending on what crap they have stored. Heavy metals will be worse than some things, but better than others.

### LisaAPB — 2012-04-05T16:52:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11725>

I'd like to narrow down some other factors, if there are any, that might contribute to the rash & itch symptom. Although, this week is better than last week, which was better than the week before. I thought it was strange that the rash moved up my body and not down? Other than this, I feel fantastic, and I mean really really good!

One, are we are all women?

I am post menopausal, 54 yr old, live in the Pacific NW. Using cold showers, ice packs, and one weekend cold bath.

Former health issues: hypothyroid, fatty liver, hypertriglyceridemia, pre-diabetic, anemia (after 18 months paleo, 4 months Leptin Rx, and 2 weeks of CT I had my annual physical with labs and all of those were gone! Labs were better than they've been in over a decade!)

+1000. Thanks Mamagrok, your words of wisdom really encourage me. I never want to suffer through eczema again like I did in my SAD days... If CT is the way to fix it, I am more than willing to tolerate any bad side-effects because I feel I'm on the right track.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-04-05T17:13:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11733>

Joining in late here, but I started fast, went straight to the tub and experienced lots of increased hunger, out of control emotions and a 6 lb. loss on the scales in the first three weeks. I started to despise doing it and quit for a few days, but then started spot-CT and ending my showers with cold water. I never get my head wet, but I notice a lot of new hair growth, on my hairline and my face even, as well as scabs on my scalp that seem to come from nowhere. My face is also dotted with light red spots. I'm assuming I have a lot of toxins clearing out. I've experienced extreme fatigue and headaches, too. Some days are harder than others; I just take it slow, knowing that it's more sustainable for me in the long-term than pushing things to the max.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-05T17:49:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11754>

I have a question, too. I've been doing CT cold baths for 3 1/2 weeks now. I'm having feet pain in the tub for the last 15 min of my 45 min 50-55F bath. For the first 30 minutes I'm good to go, some shivering sometimes but then my feet hurt from just being cold. When I get out everything is a lovely cherry red. I've had cold hands & feet since childhood-so I'm assuming it's related to that but I'm wondering do I push through or back off? Anyone else experiencing this?

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-05T18:11:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11764>

Although, this week is better than last week, which was better than the week before. I thought it was strange that the rash moved up my body and not down? Other than this, I feel fantastic, and I mean really really good!

My husband had eczema for years in the winter (his father has it year-round now after a lifetime of winter eczema). Last year, it was dramatically diminished (a few years into going TRaditional Foods with our diet, and in the midst of our GAPS experience (no wheat, etc.)). This year, none at all!

Until he did his first CT bath. Eczema right away afterward. This is clearly the way he is detoxing this. 40, southeast, one bath, long-time naturally low-carb & high fat eater, very lean, no maor health issues at all, but two very unhealthy parents.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-05T18:37:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11775>

Thanks for the help MamaGrok. I always enjoy reading all your posts, here and on MDA. You are a wonderful part of this community!

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-05T20:39:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11820>

I think it was on this thread, someone mentioned getting clumsy after CT. Hypothermia can lead to mental confusion, and that's why we shouldn't extend the time or cold of our CT'ing without a spotter. Now, simple clumsiness b/c fingers are a little cold, you probably should just be sure to keep them warmer next time (out of water and covered with gloves), but if you get really really cold and clumsiness and/or confusion sets in, that's a warning sign - don't go that far next time. Or anywhere close. Just take it way slower.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-05T21:22:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11840>

> **MamaGrok;11770 wrote:**
>
> Seriously - autoimmune disease does not come from cold or from detox. Seriously.
>
>
>
> My rule of thumb is that if the illness is enough to make you quit CT or fall off the dietary wagon, slow down instead.

Oh good gracious no - I never meant to imply that CT will CAUSE autoimmune issues. Seriously! (lol). Some folks including me already \*have\* them (T2D, hashi's, etc), and wondered if the intensity of the tubbing kicked it into overdrive, which is also what my naturopath wondered when I told her what i'd been doing. lol, I'm not a \*total\* idiot (note the qualifier

I actually did back off to just cold packing and the rash/lymph swelling still got worse, so I've FULL stopped CT til it's clear for a couple of weeks. I think with HEAVY detox, it might still be safer to do it in several stages, a period of CT detox - full or mild - followed by a regroup, rather than one intense and stressful ordeal. At least, that's what I'm gonna try...work my way back to 50-55F tubbing over a few \*months\* rather than days/weeks.

Not knocking Dr. Kruse or the whole LR/CT idea.... It's just so easy to get swept up into the group enthusiasm and lose perspective about individual needs; we really are furry little lab rats, here - with the wide demographic, caution is prudent so individuals who discover themselves out of the norm don't get hurt by trying to \*keep up\* with the norm (whatever that is)

...and I also appreciate your input, btw

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-05T22:27:39+0300

### Sherry — 2012-04-06T00:42:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11961>

I know it's been stated before but because this forum is so jam packed with LOTS of different threads and lots of folks chiming in I don't think it ever hurts to repeat. The body does have to release toxins in some way and many of these symptoms are just that. I totally agree that if it gets too much - back off. One of my teachers from years ago said that what happens when we ignore Mother Nature's plan for us is that the symptoms we notice and then ignore (or treat with drugs) will get buried inside our body. They eventually morph into one disease state or another depending upon our own individual history and circumstances and eventually this will kill us.

My belief of what is happening with these detox symptoms is that we've already experienced them in the past. Perhaps it was for a day or perhaps longer or perhaps chronically but we're probably all familiar with them from our past. They got buried and now that we are treating them with a "natural" method instead of being stuffed inside with drugs or from ignoring them long enough they are releasing their clutch on us. We're re-experiencing them and with the other symptoms we've got (that brought us to this point) that haven't been stuffed it sometimes becomes too much for us to deal with.

I know that I've had some symptoms that I remember having had years ago. For example, I had some psoriasis or eczema-type rash on my elbow about 15 years ago. At the time I put some coconut oil on it, drank more water. I don't remember what all I did but after a few weeks it went away. Well, I doubt that it went away. It got buried within. Now, I am having that same symptom on the same elbow. It's been there about a week. It changes daily. Yesterday it got worse. Today it is better. I don't know what tomorrow will bring but I do trust that it is releasing from my body.

Someone else mentioned on here that perhaps the itchy scabby scalp symptoms were candida being released. I had candida years ago. I treated it similarly to suggestions that have been made on this forum and was then tested after a few years (it is hard to get rid of) and it was found to be gone. Well, maybe not. I too have this same type of rash on my head. It started about 6-7 weeks ago shortly after starting CT. It's much better now but just yesterday another small patch popped up.

I guess what I'm trying to say is that it's nice to know that we're all in this together and we will get through this together. Your story is my story and my story is your story. The connection is quite helpful. And it's helpful for me to remember that even with the symptoms I'm having I am also seeing some improvements. Not in NY-minute type of time but slowly and surely.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-06T01:12:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-11973>

> **PaleoCowgirl;11802 wrote:** Joining in late here, but I started fast, went straight to the tub and experienced lots of increased hunger, out of control emotions and a 6 lb. loss on the scales in the first three weeks. I started to despise doing it and quit for a few days, but then started spot-CT and ending my showers with cold water. I never get my head wet, but I notice a lot of new hair growth, on my hairline and my face even, as well as scabs on my scalp that seem to come from nowhere. My face is also dotted with light red spots. I'm assuming I have a lot of toxins clearing out. I've experienced extreme fatigue and headaches, too. Some days are harder than others; I just take it slow, knowing that it's more sustainable for me in the long-term than pushing things to the max.

Hello PaleoCowgirl, I have pretty well the same symptoms, scabs on my scalp, and my face is dotted too. The hair growth thing too, hairline and my face. I am really hoping that symptom clears on its own as I am growing some darker chin hairs. I am not stressing over it, it sure wont make it better. I know I have tons of toxins. I expect detox, but not a beard

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-06T17:17:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-12140>

Yesterday was a terrible day - I felt fantastic after a first thing in the morning cold shower, but after about an hour I started to feel bone-deep cold, and couldn't warm myself up at all. Spent the whole day shivering, my temperature went down to 35.4C/95.72F, and even when I went to bed I couldn't get warm enough to sleep. This lasted until lunch-time today. I had a warmish shower this morning rather than a cold one, but otherwise haven't added extra layers of clothing (didn't yesterday, either), but now I am feeling really glowingly warm and comfortable ... I don't know what it was, but my cold soak planned for this afternoon can wait until tomorrow!

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-08T04:35:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-12574>

I've been worrying about using the ice vest, when the ice has been in a deep freeze, because that's at 0 degrees F, extremely cold, especially for someone working into CT gradually. I considered putting the ice packs into the freezer for a shorter time, or leaving them out for a time before using them, figuring out how much it would take by experimenting and using a temperature gauge.

Today I had another thought -- buy the ice vest's replacement packs, but make my own garments, with wide velcro closures. I could make a vest, and I could make wraps for the backs and inner parts of my upper arms. And I could make them reversible. I see several benefits: (1) as I change size, the garments can change with me. (2) if I bond denim with something plush and insulating, like velour, I could have two sides of the garments: the thick side with the two layers bonded; and a thin side which would just be a denim layer with the slots for the ice packs in it. Sort out the velcro right, and one could choose which layer would be next to the skin.

So, the protocol would work like this: take the ice packs out of the deep freeze (0 deg. F), put them into the garment, turn it so that the plush thick layer is between the skin and the ice, then wear it awhile. As it warms up and one gets used to it, take the garment off and reverse it, so that the (now warmer) ice would have more contact.

Anybody else want some variations on the ice vest ideas, by means of a sewing machine?

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-09T20:58:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-13084>

so after a week of neem oiling/shampooing, the rash from HELL is 95% GONE - yay! - and a normal TOM is back (yay?) I'm gonna give the lymph's a chance to come down more, then it's back to cold packing before bed for a week or two, and gentler cold baths... like 65F... awhile. Yeeshk. "Sorry Dr. Ordanis" (my naturopath) I kinda promised her I'd be "hers" to treat with \*just\* BHRT again once this cleared up. \*shrug\*

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-09T21:15:23+0300

### Lyndra — 2012-04-09T22:21:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cautionary-tales-severe-ct-reactions-and-how-youve-dealt-with-them.647/post-13120>

### Queen of Elves — 2016-02-10T19:17:34+0200

# Thread: Seasonal Recipes

**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fitness@home — 2012-04-04T15:47:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seasonal-recipes.648/post-11237>

Summer is just around the corner. A thread with some of our favorite recipes that utilize seasonal produce would be great. This is my very favorite way to eat fresh zucchini from the garden:

**Zucchini Pancakes (Savory)**

1 Â½ C grated zucchini (drain between two paper towels)

Â¼ C coconut flour

Â¼ C Parmesan cheese

2 TBL chopped onion

2 TBL Mayo (real mayo, not Miracle Whip)

2 pastured eggs

Â¼ tsp oregano

Salt & Pepper to taste

Pastured butter or ghee for cooking (could also use coconut oil...will have to experiment!)

Combine ingredients. Let stand together in fridge a couple of hours or overnight. Cook in melted butter/ghee until brown. _Use no more than Â¼ C of mixture per pancake (experience has taught me that)._ These â€œpancakesâ€￾ smell and taste wonderful. Great side dish with meat & fish, but I also love them as part of a breakfast.

Enjoy!

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-11T21:31:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seasonal-recipes.648/post-13861>

sounds good. I'll omit the cheese though.
