**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-think-i-hacked-some-unpleasantness-of-the-cold-tub.676/post-11932>

Great idea, Cavemam!

I've noticed a time or two where I was a bit reluctant. I just put on some slightly warmer clothing, and half an hour later my attitude had reversed. I think with time I'll have better control over my internal temp.

Glad you found a work-around.

\-Sally

### AKMan — 2012-04-06T00:06:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-think-i-hacked-some-unpleasantness-of-the-cold-tub.676/post-11936>

If your end goal is to swim 300 yards under the ice, you would probably want to work your way down to 32.5 degree water for as long as you could, but if your goal is 'to get cold adapted so as to evoke an ancient pathway to longevity and miraculous health' then what we are all doing is probably fine.

### hazyjane — 2012-04-06T00:19:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-think-i-hacked-some-unpleasantness-of-the-cold-tub.676/post-11948>

Cavemam, I did a similar thing- I used a space heater to warm my bathroom and left my feet/hands out of the water (55Âº) and I have a big cold pack on my belly. I still got lots of results (including detox results!) Now that it's warmer out and I'm more cold adapted, the water doesn't feel so bad. I'm also experimenting with warmer water (65Âº) with more immersion, since BAT is still activated at this temp. It feels pretty good!

Now that my water temps are higher, I can even go straight into a slightly cool shower and then almost immediately go full cold and actually enjoy it

I should clarify: I'm also doing ice wraps all the way around my torso on my skin, so I'm def. still getting some major cold. In fact, the coldest I feel is after taking off the ice!

### quelsen — 2012-04-06T17:03:34+0300

i have no idea what i would do for an hour that way. i mean i cant read while on my stomach or sides.

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-06T17:30:05+0300

I turned the house air way up when I was taking baths in the winter (60 degrees or less). Now the tap water is closer to 75 and the outdoor air is 80 and i relish the cold baths!

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-06T18:57:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-think-i-hacked-some-unpleasantness-of-the-cold-tub.676/post-12184>

Careful staying frosty! (LOVED my cold bath yesterday!)

# Thread: Why is my face hot?

### agatha — 2012-04-06T00:30:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-my-face-hot.679/post-11952>

The last few days my face feels very hot - a little like mild sunburn (but I haven't been in the sun). It's not unpleasant but I'm wondering what's going on. Anybody got any thoughts?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-my-face-hot.679/post-11960>

### MonteD — 2012-04-08T05:15:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-my-face-hot.679/post-12581>

I've noticed both stomach and face flushing in response to eating. I think its great. Because weight is dropping too. Happens most when I eat and then "anticipate" the cold. It's as reliable as zippo.... Besides, heat is how we dispose of excess calories when leptin sensitive.

[http://wholehealthsource.blogspot.com/2011/07/weight-gain-and-weight-loss-in.html](http://http://wholehealthsource.blogspot.com/2011/07/weight-gain-and-weight-loss-in.html)

The above article discusses how researchers overfed The Massas, a traditional African culture (read: leptin sensitive) upwards of 8,000 calories per day in an overfeeding experiment. Accounting for fecal losses they actually took in about 7,000 calories a day. This is with a traditional baseline expenditure of 2500 calories a day.

Doing calculations for the amount of weight gained and lost... **there internal pilot lights turned into raging furnaces that burned roughly 3000 _additional_ calories per day as heat...**

# Thread: Awesome Wim Hof Video

**Posts:** 12

### AKMan — 2012-04-06T01:16:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/awesome-wim-hof-video.680/post-11975>

You guys gotta watch this. [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nRsNh0eB-Io\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;nRsNh0eB-Io\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nRsNh0eB-Io\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;nRsNh0eB-Io)

Scientists injected him with a toxin and he was able to control his immune system to where he had almost no effects. They are going to let Wim teach 10 people his techniques and do the toxin test on those 10 and 10 others with no training...I can't wait to see the results!

I'd bet this research facility would be interested in what Dr. Kruse has been doing.

If you want to know Wim Hof's breathing technique, it's simple:

1\. Take in 30 deep breaths and blow completely out, like blowing up a baloon.

2\. On the last breath, breathe it all out and hold as long as you can without taking in more air.

3\. When you can't hold it, take a deep breath and hold for 10 seconds.

4\. Do this often (several times a day), recording your times as you go.

5\. There also seems to be a variation, where on the 30th breath, you fill your lungs and hold it in as long as you can.

I have been working on this technique for a few weeks, when I started I could hold with no air in my lungs for about 30 seconds and with air about 1 minute. Today I am up to 1 min 20 sec w/o air and 2 min 45 sec with air.

Hope you can watch the video, it's in Dutch w/english subtitles.

### AKMan — 2012-04-06T03:03:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/awesome-wim-hof-video.680/post-12017>

> That is a great video. Thank you for sharing your research. Do you have a source where someone could get more information about his techniques? I just beat pneumonia in February without antibiotics. I would have liked to know a technique to increase my immune reaction at first and then reduce my cytokine storm afterwards.
>

> One more question about your breathing experiment. Do you have any insight as to the significance of increased time for empty lung and full lung? Are you doing anything else?

Wim has a website; [http://www.innerfire.nl/en-home](http://http://www.innerfire.nl/en-home) He has a lot of info there, but I got hold of his breathing technique from the book "Becoming the Iceman" by Wim Hof and Justin Rosales.

I'm not sure what increasing your time for breath holding actually does for you. From [http://gettingstronger.org/tag/justin-rosales/](http://http://gettingstronger.org/tag/justin-rosales/) I got this:

...the key adaptation occurs within the brain during meditation–specifically the yoga and controlled breathing exercises that Wim and the tumo practitioners follow. Of these exercises, breath retention exercises are key. As a result, there is a significant activation of blood flow and electrical activity in his frontal cortex and hypothalamus — areas that regulate peripheral nerves and veins involved in the regulation of body temperature. Normally, the circuit between the hypothalamus and these temperature control circuits is involuntary, governed by the autonomic nervous system. Kamler reasonably speculates that, through meditation, Wim is able to override the normal function of the hypothalamus, allowing the peripheral veins to remain open and heat the extremities, preventing injury. He points out that Wim must be generating heat and distributing it more efficiently, but he admits having no idea mechanistically how Wim’s meditative techniques accomplish this.

If you read the article and comments you will see some familiar names!

### chocolate — 2012-04-14T18:28:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cortisol-cytokines-and-the-brain-gut-axis.681/post-15073>

avatar Jack SaysCT-9 #235)

April 13th, 2012 at 5:20 pm

@Mark read this too…….From a summary of Nora Gegaudas talk at Paleo FX (and more insight into stress and inflammation and the importance of continuing on this path for our mental health as well as our physical!): (from: [http://roosclues.blogspot.co.nz/2012…-function.html)…](http://http://roosclues.blogspot.co.nz/2012…-function.html\)…) (thanks to Jennifer for posting on the Leptin RX thread)

These notes are for one section of a talk given by Nora Gedgaudas during the Paleo Summit. She was refuting the idea of safe starches, put forward by Paul Jaminet (author of The Perfect Health Diet), and discussing hypoglycemia. She explains that hypoglycemia only occurs in people who are (unnaturally) temporarily adapted to getting glucose from dietary sugars and starches, and that for people who are fat burners- which is what the human body is adapted for- mood and cognitive functioning are not dependent on blood sugar levels. The human body can make all of the glucose it needs in the liver from amino acids, there is no need to get it from the diet.

The exception to this, she explains, is when people have chronically depressed cortisol levels. What really caught my attention is that she says that this low cortisol production is not because the adrenals themselves have been “exhausted” by stress, which she says is an outdated idea. She says that cortisol output is not controlled by the glad itself, but by the brain. It is mitigated by the HPA (Hypothalamus Pituitary Axis), specifically by a group of cells inside the hypothalamus called the Paraventricular Nuclear cells (PVN). The level of cortisol that we produce depends on how these cells are stimulated- whether the balance of neurochemicals leans towards excitatory or inhibitory, but in particular by the presence of inflammatory cytokines. To rephrase that, low cortisol levels are caused by inflammation in the brain. Various stressors on the body result in the release of these cytokines, including chronic infection. This process can also impact our balance of neurotransmitters.

### chocolate — 2012-05-08T18:20:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cortisol-cytokines-and-the-brain-gut-axis.681/post-22247>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21629741](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21629741)

Melatonin in mitochondrial dysfunction and related disorders.

Srinivasan V, Spence DW, Pandi-Perumal SR, Brown GM, Cardinali DP.

Source

Sri Sathya Sai Medical, Educational and Research Foundation, Prashanthi Nilayam 40, Kovai Thirunagar Coimbatore 641014, India.

Abstract

Mitochondrial dysfunction is considered one of the major causative factors in the aging process, ischemia/reperfusion (I/R), septic shock, and neurodegenerative disorders like Parkinson's disease (PD), Alzheimer's disease (AD), and Huntington's disease (HD). Increased free radical generation, enhanced mitochondrial inducible nitric oxide (NO) synthase activity, enhanced NO production, decreased respiratory complex activity, impaired electron transport system, and opening of mitochondrial permeability transition pore all have been suggested as factors responsible for impaired mitochondrial function. Melatonin, the major hormone of the pineal gland, also acts as an antioxidant and as a regulator of mitochondrial bioenergetic function. Both in vitro and in vivo, melatonin was effective for preventing oxidative stress/nitrosative stress-induced mitochondrial dysfunction seen in experimental models of PD, AD, and HD. In addition, melatonin is known to retard aging and to inhibit the lethal effects of septic shock or I/R lesions by maintaining respiratory complex activities, electron transport chain, and ATP production in mitochondria. **Melatonin is selectively taken up by mitochondrial membranes, a function not shared by other antioxidants. Melatonin has thus emerged as a major potential therapeutic tool for treating neurodegenerative disorders such as PD or AD, and for preventing the lethal effects of septic shock or I/R.**

### chocolate — 2012-05-09T01:29:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cortisol-cytokines-and-the-brain-gut-axis.681/post-22412>

[http://www.medscape.com/viewarticle/548359](http://http://www.medscape.com/viewarticle/548359)

Sleep Deprivation Blocks Hippocampal Neurogenesis Through Steroid Effect

NEW YORK (Reuters Health) Nov 28 - Sleep deprivation causes a rise in glucocorticoid levels that works to inhibit neurogenesis in the hippocampus, results of an animal study suggest.

This finding may help explain the adverse cognitive effects associated with sleep deprivation, according to the report in the November 28th Early Edition of the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences.

In the new study, Dr. Elizabeth Gould and colleagues, from Princeton University in New Jersey, show that 72 hours of sleep deprivation in rats causes a drop in hippocampal neurogenesis, which coincides with an elevation in circulating levels of corticosterone.

When corticosteroid levels were maintained at a normal level, the sleep-deprived animals no longer showed a reduction in hippocampal neurogenesis, the report indicates.

### chocolate — 2012-05-09T01:42:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cortisol-cytokines-and-the-brain-gut-axis.681/post-22419>

[http://jcem.endojournals.org/content/88/1/450.full](http://http://jcem.endojournals.org/content/88/1/450.full)

mt1 Melatonin Receptor in the Primate Adrenal Gland: Inhibition of Adrenocorticotropin-Stimulated Cortisol Production by Melatonin

The pineal hormone melatonin participates in circadian, seasonal, and reproductive physiology. The presence of melatonin binding sites in human brain and peripheral tissues is well documented. However, in the mammalian adrenal gland, low-affinity melatonin binding sites have been detected only in the rat by some but not all authors. Conflicting evidence for a regulatory role of melatonin on adrenal cortisol production, prompted us to investigate this possibility in a New World primate, the capuchin monkey. Expression of melatonin receptors in the adrenal cortex was demonstrated through pharmacological characterization and autoradiographic localization of 2-\[125I\]iodomelatonin binding sites (dissociation constant = 96.9 Â± 15 pm; maximal binding capacity = 3.8 Â± 0.4 fmol/mg protein). The mt1 identity of these receptors was established by cDNA sequencing. **Melatonin treatment of dispersed cells and explants from adrenal gland did not affect basal cortisol production. However, cortisol production stimulated by 100 nm ACTH was significantly inhibited by low melatonin concentrations (0.1–100 nm); this inhibitory effect was reversed by the mt1/MT2 melatonin antagonist luzindole. Melatonin also inhibited dibutyril-cAMP-stimulated cortisol production, suggesting that melatonin acts through a cAMP-independent signaling pathway.** The present data demonstrate that the primate adrenal gland cortex expresses functional mt1 melatonin receptors and shows that melatonin inhibits ACTH-stimulated cortisol production.

### chocolate — 2012-05-09T16:49:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cortisol-cytokines-and-the-brain-gut-axis.681/post-22537>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22288898](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22288898)

Melatonin enhances endogenous heme oxygenase-1 and represses immune responses to ameliorate experimental murine membranous nephropathy.

Idiopathic membranous nephropathy (MN), an autoimmune-mediated glomerulonephritis, is one of the most common causes of nephrotic syndrome in adults. Therapeutic agents for MN remain ill defined. We assessed the efficacy of melatonin therapy for MN. Experimental murine MN was induced with cationic bovine serum albumin, and the mice were immediately administered 20 mg/kg melatonin or phosphate-buffered saline subcutaneously once a day. Disease severity was verified by examining serum and urine metabolic profiles and renal histopathology. The expression of cytokines and oxidative stress markers, cell apoptosis, and the associated mechanisms were also determined. Mice treated with melatonin displayed a significant reduction in proteinuria and a marked amelioration of glomerular lesions, with attenuated immunocomplex deposition. **The subpopulations of T cells were not altered, but the CD19(+) B-cell subpopulation was significantly reduced in the MN mice treated with melatonin. The expression of cytokine mRNAs in splenocytes indicated that melatonin reduced the expression of proinflammatory cytokines and increased the expression of anti-inflammatory cytokines (interleukin 10). The production of reactive oxygen species and TUNEL-positive apoptotic cells in the kidney were also significantly reduced in the melatonin-treated MN mice. Melatonin also upregulated heme oxygenase 1 (HO1) and ameliorated MN. The blockade of HO1 expression with SnPP, a HO1 inhibitor, attenuated HO1 induction by melatonin and thus mitigated its renoprotective effects during MN. Our results suggest that melatonin treatment ameliorates experimental MN via multiple pathways, including by its antioxidative, antiapoptotic, and immunomodulatory effects. Melatonin should be considered a potential therapeutic intervention for MN in the future**

# Thread: Turbinado sugar???
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turbinado-sugar.682/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ineedanLR — 2012-04-06T02:12:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turbinado-sugar.682/post-11995>

II'm trying to start my LR from food I have in the house already-primarily in the interest of time....I want to get the process started asap.

In the freezer, I found some frozen all natural 'al fresco' brand country style chicken sausauge. It says no artificial ingredients, MSG or preservatives.

Here's where it gets difficult::>

Under Total Carbs, it says 0%. In a sub category under total carbs, it says 'Sugars, 0g'.

I do notice the serving size is very small, less than 1 ounce each serving. The exact wording in the ingredients says 'contains 2% or less of sea salt, turbinado sugar, spices sage, thyme'.

How can it have sugar in it and have zero carbs???? Is this stuff ok to get me started on my BAB until I can get to a store to buy some more appropriate protein?

TY all.

Art

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-04-06T02:28:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turbinado-sugar.682/post-12004>

Most bacons do have some sort of sugar in them. Some would refrain, but others do just fine with that limited amount. I think you will be okay to eat it, imho, but ymmv.

I stopped bacon and switched to uncured side pork...then I season it how I want. It's one of my fave things to eat!

# Thread: Putting it all Together
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/putting-it-all-together.683/>
**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### saben — 2012-04-06T03:32:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/putting-it-all-together.683/post-12029>

Hi Everyone,

I just started a second round of HCG, short cycle this time to lose the last 15+ pounds. I'm on day 3 after loading for days 1 and 2. I'm excited to be integrating the HCG, CT and the circadian eating. I'm planning on Leptin RX and CT when done with the HCG. For this round, P2 I'm eating a 3.5 oz organic grass fed beef patty within the first 1/2 hour of waking or as close to that as I can. Eating lunch-vegie and chicken or beef(all organic, grass fed) 4-5 hours later and dinner(4-5 hours later) with vegie and protein-fish, crab meat or chicken. I did well with a lot of beef last round and will adjust as necessary. I'm not planning to add any MCT's at this time. I feel really good about what and when to eat.

I want to add the CT with cold packs and cold showers. I'm wondering if I'm planning the CT right. I will take a cold shower as close to after dinner(or breakfast on the weekend) and then use the cold packs up to 45 minutes. How many times a week should I do CT to be effective? Any advice on how to start CT as I'm hesitant about feeling that cold and uncomfortable? Has anyone used the bittermelon to help with adjusting to CT?

Thanks!

Sara

Weight today-155.4 lbs.

### rocketmom — 2012-04-11T04:41:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/putting-it-all-together.683/post-13599>

I am looking forward to hear of your progress also. I have given up the starch and fruit also. I am in R2P2 and struggling with slow losses. I have almost hit the average of .5 per day (.49) but it has been a rough ride. I have also increased my protein calories to sub for the starch and fruit, and still eating mostly fish and chicken never hit the 500 calories. I had tried CT by face dunking but really didn't like it, so I just did spot icing. I found that I was inconsistant in my efforts and just dropped it. I was having issues with the minute losses and frequent gains I was in the virtual dumps and wanted to quit entirely.

Keep us in the loop and good luck!

dennise

ennise

### Honey Crisp — 2012-04-11T15:45:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/putting-it-all-together.683/post-13714>

I'm just about down with my hcg round. I'm a few pounds from goal at this point and hope to see it by the time I take my last injection. I've been doing CT and eating fats. I started with coconut oil and switched to animal fats. I did cut out fats when I cheated and gained. I went strict protocol until the scale started moving again. I stopped CT a few days when the detox (?) symptoms became to much for me. I restarted the face dunks.

Wishing you lots of success on your journey to getting healthy!

# Thread: My teenager saw the sunrise!!!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-teenager-saw-the-sunrise.684/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kiera2 — 2012-04-06T04:43:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-teenager-saw-the-sunrise.684/post-12040>

This amuses me no end. My 13 year old daughter will stay in bed until lunchtime, any day she can. We've just finished the first week of a two week school break, and normally she would have wasted every single morning. That's with going to bed at 9 - 9.30. I deliberately have no curtains on her window, and the sun rises and shines right on her bed.

She wanted a pair of blue blocker glasses because 'they look cool' and has been wearing them every evening from sunset, for four nights now. Still going to bed at the same time, and getting up around 10.

This morning, she appeared at 8.30. Unheard of.

"I didn't know what was going on. I woke up, and the sky was pink! It was weird, and changed colours and stuff. Then I realised I was watching the sun rise. What's up with that?!"

She tried to go back to sleep, but with birds chirping and sunlight streaming in, she just had to get up. And has been in a great mood ever since! Magic.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-04-06T05:36:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-teenager-saw-the-sunrise.684/post-12055>

What a great story! Thanks for making my evening.

### Evo Mama — 2012-04-06T14:06:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-teenager-saw-the-sunrise.684/post-12084>

Cool story - love the description of her epiphany that she was watching a sunrise. I was kinda looking forward to my son sleeping in when he's a teen, but I can look forward to other things now, like sharing a sunrise with him!

\---

I am here: [http://tapatalk.com/map.php?n150cm](http://http://tapatalk.com/map.php?n150cm)

### texasmom4 — 2012-04-06T15:25:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-teenager-saw-the-sunrise.684/post-12090>

That's so cool, Coriander! My four have been sleeping later and later, and I've been letting them since we homeschool and can start whenever we want. I'm going to order more glasses from Amazon right now and initiate a science experiment in my house, tracking sleep time and quality. (Too bad we JUST MISSED the science fair!)

### Kiera2 — 2012-04-07T02:49:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-teenager-saw-the-sunrise.684/post-12334>

Ooops, she did it again! I love this! And we had a huge storm last night that dislodged some roofing. Dh and I were out repairing it at 11pm, and of course she was still awake. Even so, she saw the sunrise again this morning. And still in such a great mood!

Dr Kruse, you have made so many fantastic differences in my family's life. A simple 'thankyou' seems so inadequate; but _thankyou_ anyway.

### nonchalant — 2012-04-08T04:35:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-teenager-saw-the-sunrise.684/post-12575>

Looks like even the kids can recognize the benefits of resetting leptin.

I wish I knew, decades ago, the things I know now.

Don't we all..

# Thread: GUINEA PIGS - DEXA scan, HCG / CT and OIL
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/guinea-pigs-dexa-scan-hcg-ct-and-oil.686/>
**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ste colombe — 2012-04-06T05:36:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/guinea-pigs-dexa-scan-hcg-ct-and-oil.686/post-12054>

I thought as the original guinea pig thread was getting long, it might be a good idea for those of us doing HCG / CT/ OIL to put what we actually ate in terms of the added oil component, as this seems to be the big "?" with regards our experiment.

I've done HCG before and lost 21lbs in 30 days - I also vacillated between 500 cal and 700 cal during this first round. Presently, I dont have much weight to lose.

Thus far, with the addition of CT and oil, I am losing at a slightly faster rate than the first round (1lb steadily a day compared to .5 - .75 lb a day) - I am more hungry but its controllable, the CT makes my skin tighter and on a DEXA scan, I have lost much more fat than the scales show and added muscle to the tune of .75lb a day...). I do 30 minutes in a 50F bath 5 x a week

Instead of adding MCT, which I gather is still a big "?" due to its "winter or not" component, I am adding oil in the form of omega 3 fish - (Salmon, sea urchin, mackerel, anchovies....).

So, theres the back ground, heres what I eat....

30 min upon upon waking

250g salmon poached in homemade fish stock - I do not eat the stock (440 cal and 49g protein)

around 4pm

4 caps of Thorne krill oil

100g chicken white poached in homemade chicken stock

1 cup of that stock eaten with the chicken (110 cal and 23g protein)

1 cup of greens

TOTAL FOR THE DAY

596 Cal

29g fat

75g protein ( leptin Rx style 50g breakfast - 25g dinner)

3g Carb - (Winter wonderland....)

I will redo a DEXA scan in another 2 weeks and compare again and let you know if its of interest to you.

I am 43 yrs old, have 4 children and do not take any BIO HRT. I take the usual suspects of supplements in their wholefood form (Mega foods or Innate).

I hope that helps.......

### MaggieC — 2012-04-06T09:55:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/guinea-pigs-dexa-scan-hcg-ct-and-oil.686/post-12070>

I can't wait to see your update! Please let us know if your losses continue at this pace!

### nuttmegs17 — 2012-04-06T16:15:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/guinea-pigs-dexa-scan-hcg-ct-and-oil.686/post-12109>

super interesting - perhaps Chimpchick should post here as well

### Honey Crisp — 2012-04-06T16:54:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/guinea-pigs-dexa-scan-hcg-ct-and-oil.686/post-12125>

I started this round using coconut oil. I quit after a cheat and stalled. I started losing again and added animal fats. I like the added animal fat. I feel more satisified with the animal fat over the coconut oi.

### mistigi — 2012-04-06T22:28:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/guinea-pigs-dexa-scan-hcg-ct-and-oil.686/post-12253>

@ste colombe

can you tell us more about your dexa scan ? Is it the same that is being used for bone densitometry or the one that shows detailed body composition ? If the latter do you need your doctor's referral to get it done and how much does it cost if you pay out of pocket for it ?

My concern with doing X-ray scans so often would be radiation impact on the thyroid. I am not sure if you can request "thyroid guard" to mitigate the impact or consume iodine before the exam, something to think about.

### Ane — 2012-04-06T22:49:53+0300

>
>

Hi Mistigi

I did the body comp DEXA scan. I live in Hong Kong, so I dont need a referral and its not too expensive. For me its the only real way of assessing exactly whats going on with regards fat loss, muscle gain (in this case due to CT). I know the concerns over radiation - but I think its a good idea to get it done before, during and after - not sure about protecting your thyroid though. Good luck.

Hi Alicia

Im taking Pregnyl injections and am on day 16 of P2.

My results are better this round as there is a consistent loss and as I said earlier, I dont really have much to lose so that takes some doing. I think adding the CT has boosted the loss - not necessarily always apparent for people on the scales (we are way too attached to those stupid things) hence the DEXA scan showing the added muscle and fat loss.

I hope that helps. I intend stopping at the end of the month if not before, but its good to know.

By the way, I intend seriously cheating this weekend for Easter and I will see how that affects things, but my regular food is very clean as I'm french. I think the CT will protect me - I will keep you posted!

I intend doing another DEXA when I am finished that way I will know exactly what went on. Good luck to you

### Ane — 2012-04-07T16:13:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/guinea-pigs-dexa-scan-hcg-ct-and-oil.686/post-12438>

### colleencoble — 2012-04-09T01:54:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/guinea-pigs-dexa-scan-hcg-ct-and-oil.686/post-12826>

I would try skipping a day of your shots if I were you to see if the hunger is better. "Controllable" doesn't mean it's okay. Hunger is always a bad sign that you're losing the wrong kind of fat and those who are hungry or who have weakness are more apt to regain their weight. So I would try to fix that if you can.

### ste colombe — 2012-04-10T04:28:39+0300

Thanks coleen - I have always been a bit hungry - even during the first round I only had a few days when I really couldnt eat anything. Most days I get hungry in the mid afternoon....

Hi coleen

I have been away, so sorry for getting back to you so late. I have very rarely felt that "never hungry" feeling - I am always a bit hungry in the day, but as I said its totally managable. I always wondered whether I was slightly immune.... Easter has come and gone and I ate like everyone, enjoyed myself and did put on 4lbs, and I'm back on track and all seems to be going well but I would love to have that never want to eat feeling. As I said, I take injections, 1500 pregnyl divided into 10 doses of 150 each. I have lowered the dose to 120 and 130 but it never made a difference.... Thanks so much for your expert thoughts

ok I will try the lowered dose for 3 days and let you now. I partly think the problem may lie in the fact that my HCG doses are frozen. It is hard to get it here, so I froze the 150 syringes..... I am trying to order a fresh dose but theres no stock in HK so have to wait . Coleen, thank you

### colleencoble — 2012-04-16T04:16:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/guinea-pigs-dexa-scan-hcg-ct-and-oil.686/post-15481>

### Claudia — 2012-04-19T04:27:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/guinea-pigs-dexa-scan-hcg-ct-and-oil.686/post-16448>

> **colleencoble;15550 wrote:** Many freeze their syringes with no trouble. It's most likely just the dose and you'll be feeling better soon. To get there faster, you could skip tomorrow's dose.

Colleen, can you explain why skipping an injection would be better? I was planning on going straight through 28 days with injections every day. Thank you/.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-19T04:59:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/guinea-pigs-dexa-scan-hcg-ct-and-oil.686/post-16457>

When I've been saying skip a day, it's to get your dose adjusted more quickly. If you just lower the dose, it will take several days for the levels in the blood to fall. The protocol calls for a skip day though if you are going longer than 23 days. It's to avoid immunity issues though you shouldn't have trouble with 28 days. But if you decide to lengthen your round, you might wish you had skipped.

# Thread: Hi, Jazz here with a client's query
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-jazz-here-with-a-clients-query.688/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jasbinder — 2012-04-06T07:35:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-jazz-here-with-a-clients-query.688/post-12067>

Hi Dr. Jack,

Great to be a part of the community here and to learn more about your work. This is Jazz, from Melbourne, Australia. I work as a Personal Trainer and have been following Sean Croxton's work from a while now. That's how, I came to learn about you.

I need a help re a Client's condition. She recently wrote this to me:

"Thank you for helping me with my food allergies.

I'm sure you already know all this, just in case you don't I'll quickly explain it.

So a stage 4 is a strong positive and a stage one is just a positive. My doctor said I can eat stage 1's and 2's. 2's in moderation though.

Barley 3+

Durum Wheat 3+

Rice 3+

Rye 3+

Wheat 4+

Cow's Milk 4+

Egg White and Yolk 4+

Sheep Milk 3+

Goat's Milk 4+

Harocit Bean 1+

Kidney Bean 3+

Peas 1+

Potato 1+

Tomato 2+

Orange 1+

Plum 1+

Almond 2+

Cashew Nut 2+

Hazel Nut 1+

Peanut 3+

Chilli Pepper 2+

Mint 1+

Mushroom 1+

Brewers/Bakers Yeast 2+

My DHEA levels are at 7.8 and they are suppose to be between 0.95 and 11.7umol/L. I need to get my prescription renewed, my natropath doctor said I need to take the supplement every day of the rest of my life.

I want to look into this further, he told me a lot of things like if I continue to eat the food I am allergic to I will get cancer and alzheimer's disease. "

Dr. Jack, it will be awesome if you can guide me so as to help out this young girl (22 yrs old).

Many Thanks.

Regards,

Jazz (Jasbinder Gulati)

### Kiera2 — 2012-04-06T10:16:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-jazz-here-with-a-clients-query.688/post-12073>

Hi Jazz, Jack recommends the paleo diet, either Mark Sisson, or Robb Wolf would be a good guide. There are only three things on your list that I eat - cows milk, eggs and mint. I don't think it would be at all difficult to follow Mark Sissons dietary guidelines whilst leaving out those three.

I think your client would do well to follow Robb Wolf's autoimmune diet, which restricts eggs, tomato, eggplant, capsicum, paprika and chilli powder.

There are no meats on your list; is your client a vegetarian?

# Thread: Chocolate !
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 77
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KiwiLauren — 2012-04-06T08:36:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-12069>

Help from all you nutrition gurus....

I have been eating lots of 100% chocolate (can't get 99% here - I've been eating fermented, raw powder, as well as teh roasted) for the resveratrol and magnesium (and upon Dr K's recommendation). I have just, however, read a post by Chris Kressler regarding phytic acid and how chocolate (in any form) should NOT be a regular part of our diet.

So... for all your chocolate eaters out there... what do you think?

Is chocolate on your go-to list? Or your no-no list? In what form? Quantities? If you eat it, do you supplement with zinc to deal with the potential mineral depletion?

If anyone knows if there is a specific form of chocolate that reduces phytic acid and is best for health, please chime in. Or with any opinions about chocolate

Thanks!!

### TerrierMom — 2012-04-06T17:23:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-12144>

Dr K has a chocolate bark recipe in his book made from 100% ... So must not be keeping him from optimal?

Others have posted they're using the 85 percent from Green and Blacks organic during the reset (as have I). I've noticed meant brands include soy lecithin... Bad? I go for soy and rice free chocolate. I'm still losing 2-4 lbs a week so I'm happy....

### Patty Cakes — 2012-04-06T18:13:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-12169>

I eat about 1 oz the 99% chocolate about 3-4 times a week. It doesn't seem like much. I stay away from anything with soy in it in any form. I also make the doc's bark and eat that instead of the chocolate. Either way I only eat a bit for taste and then I am done. I don't crave it or need to have more after I eat it.

### wyldeman — 2012-04-06T18:22:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-12172>

Currently I am eating 1/2 oz of the 100% cacao Baker's chocolate with a brazil nut twice a day.

It's 100% cacao, so it will most likely not trigger binges ( I consider myself blessed to not have really any struggle with those)

### ealachan — 2012-04-06T20:47:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-12219>

I mix a TBSP of CO, a TBSP of unsweetened cocoa, a tsp of good cinnamon, and a tiny pinch of sea salt into a paste and eat it as-is. Sometimes I add a tablespoon or two of unsweetened flaked coconut too, for funsies. It's very tasty.

### AKMan — 2012-04-06T21:31:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-12230>

I gnaw on a square of unsweetened baker's chocolate every night after dinner. I used to think there was no way in the world anyone could eat that stuff straight, but now I love it!

### Shijin13 — 2012-04-06T21:36:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-12232>

I gave up chocolate for lent to support DH who I challenged to give up wheat/gluten - I get it back on SUNDAY!!! WHOOT...

### nonchalant — 2012-04-06T21:37:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-12233>

I eat 90% Lindt. No soy. Hard to believe a couple years ago I thought any non-milk chocolate was horridly bitter.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-04-06T22:05:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-12246>

So it sounds like no one, including Dr K, is worried about the _phytic acid content of chocolate. It's not in the additives in chocolate, but in the cocoa itself according to Chris Kresser, it's as bad as nuts and grains. I am not eating anything but 100%. I have a choice between roasted and unroasted (both fermented).

from the article:

_One of the main principles of the Paleo diet is to avoid eating grains and legumes because of the food toxins they contain. One of those toxins, phytic acid (a.k.a. phytate), is emphasized as one of the greatest offenders.

But what is often not mentioned in books or websites about the Paleo diet is that nuts are often as high or even higher in phytic acid than grains.

Disappointing side note for chocolate lovers: Raw unfermented cocoa beans and normal cocoa powder are extremely high in phytic acid. Processed chocolate may also contain significant levels.\]_

He then lists a table to show it has more phytic acid per 100g than things like almonds, brown rice, peanuts, and lentils.

In the comments/Q&A people repeatedly asked him about chocolate and he kept saying to never have it as a daily part of your diet.

**Do you think it's just plain wrong?**_ I'm hoping so, but am not convinced.

(By the way, I'm also amazed with my changing tastebuds. I love the 100%.)

### ginastarke — 2012-04-07T18:13:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-12470>

I eat either cocoa nibs (The ones from Navitas are available online, and have a very nice crunch) or unsweetened baking chocolate. Ghirridelli is good ,and a lot more affordable, but Schaffen Berger is the best. I also melt down some coconut oil and manna with cinnamon and anise seeds and stir in 3-4 tablespoons of cocoa.

With me it depends on what part of the month it is, around "that time" I gotta have it, the rest of the time, I don't care that much.

### Casey — 2012-04-08T02:40:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-12557>

Hmm, I have the same reaction to dark chocolate and raw cacao as I do nuts and grains - I haven't heard of chocolate containing phytic acid. I might look into this further. Thanks Lauren.

### hazyjane — 2012-04-08T02:55:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-12560>

This is interesting! I do eat a small amount on a weekly basis (Valhrona 85%).

### healthnut — 2012-04-11T04:50:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-13606>

Sharffenberger chocolate is primo! Gotta watch the Lindt 90, I've seen a blurb on it lately about it being alkalized. How do we really know which bars are alkalized and which are not, I don't think they are required to disclose this information?

If we were to avoid phytates, we'd have to go on an air and water diet.....they are in everything.

### Glamorama — 2012-04-11T04:57:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-13610>

I developed extreme pains in my gut thanks to phytic acid (leaky gut) and dark chocolate never caused me any issues, however, last month I noticed that 85% gave me a weird feeling in the gut... like the leaky gut pain I used to have, but less. I couldn't firgure out why, but this makes sense. I take zinc picciolate every 2nd night... The gut discomfort is gone again now. Personally I love my dark chocolate/barks etc!

### healthnut — 2012-04-11T05:09:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-13618>

Glam~ Coffee and cocoa are very acid. Both also contain a lot of copper, maybe something to do with that too?

I purchase raw cocao powder from Wilderness Family Naturals: [http://www.wildernessfamilynaturals.com/category/chocolate-products.php](http://http://www.wildernessfamilynaturals.com/category/chocolate-products.php)

I noticed it a lot less mild than natural cocoa powder. It is more fruity and a lot stronger.

I make 'homemade' chocolate with organic cocoa butter and the raw cocao powder. That way I can make it even stronger than store bought. I melt 1/2 cup of cocao butter and let it cool a bit. Then whisk in about 1 1/4 cup of the raw cocao powder and a little vanilla. Pour it on some wax paper on a baking sheet and freeze it to harden. You could add other things like crushed green tea leafes, nibs, sea salt....

### indigogirl — 2012-04-25T02:04:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-18525>

I just made some chocolate "candy" with a 4 oz. 100% Ghiradellis chocolate bar, 3 oz. of Nutiva coconut manna, about 4 tbls. of finely shredded unsweetened coconut, some crushed macadamia nuts, and about 8 drops of English Toffee flavored stevia. I poured it in a silicone muffin pan and put in the freezer for about 10 minutes.

Yummy...not really sweet even with the stevia. The finely shredded unsweetened coconut makes it chewy.

Nutiva coconut manna is like crack!!!

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-05-29T17:07:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-28170>

So, here' my chocolate story. Since I've been on a low carb paleo diet for as long as I can remember (15 years or so) I've written off chocolate. I picked up a few 100% dark chocolate bars after reading about it here. i added a little stevia for a treat, and the first bite was wonderful. I've since ate it without a problem - I maybe ate one bar without issue.

However, the last two times I've eaten just one square, it has made me feel ill. Very nauseous and that feeling you get when you drink too much. What is going on here? Anyone?

### chocolate — 2012-05-29T19:12:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-28200>

I can't eat it either.... I think its a gut problem.... its all the yummy stuff for bad gut bacteria to grow. I kind of finally figured out all the vitamin b deficiencies that overlap the digestive issues are caused by candida needing them for ethanol production. Anything candida needs I try to avoid or have it in the am, even with the biotin. The candida makes ethanol at night when we sleep. I'm going to get some chocolate nibs and try the water soak and make a tea or extract. The epicatechin is the stuff that heals mitochondria. Its probably the hot water that lets loose whatever the good stuff is anyway and we might not be getting the full benefits from the bars, even if it is 100 percent.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-05-30T15:24:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-28335>

> **Joann;28352 wrote:** anyone have an idea why stevia causes stinky gas and bloating?

I've never seen this with pure 100% stevia. However, there's several brands out there that blend the stevia with polyols or inulin/FOS. those are notorious for causing gas, bloating, and other digestive problems.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-04T01:08:00+0300

### [email redacted] — 2012-06-05T15:48:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-29529>

Souldanzer the reason you might be sick with Lindt is that it is not gluten free. Lindt admits they use barley malt to sweeten all chocolate under 70% and they do not change or even clean machines for the 70%+ brands.

fwiw, be careful with Green & Black as well, if you are sensitive you may react to G&B

> Although some of our products do not use cereal ingredients, they are produced in a factory that does handle them and therefore there are risks of cross contact.

you have to check G&B country sites for your country to see if there is cross contamination

[http://www.greenandblacks.com/us/our-chocolates/bars.html](http://http://www.greenandblacks.com/us/our-chocolates/bars.html) (the US site)

if you click on the 85% bar it does have the cross contamination warning. not important if you are not gluten intolerant but helpful if you are.

I\`m one of the super sensitives and the only two chocolates I can eat at this point are Endangered Species 88% and CÃ´te D\`Or 86% as both are certified gluten free. (in case you are wondering Jack\`s 99% bars have the cross contamination warning  )

### kathylu — 2012-12-06T22:52:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-66633>

Coconut Chocolate "Crack"

Â 

1 jar of Nutiva coconut manna

2 squares (1 oz) of baking chocolate (unsweetened, I use Callebaut)

1/4 cup coconut oil

sweetener to taste (I use a mix of stevia and z-sweet)

Â 

Melt the chocolate in the coconut oil (1 minute in the microwave), stir thoroughly. Soften jar of coconut manna by setting in a pan of hot water for 10 minutes or microwave the open jar for 1 minute, stir until mixed. Add the chocolate-oil mix to the jar of manna, sweeten to taste, mix well. Eat anytime the urge for chocolate strikes!

Â 

Remixes:

Add cinnamon and nutmeg, maybe ginger too

Add a bit of sea salt (for you salted chocolate fans)

Mix 50/50 with butter, use as a "nutella" type spread on coconut flour scones

Try adding flavoring extracts (banana, cherry, vanilla, maybe peppermint?)

Have fun...this is delicious!

### Charlotte — 2012-12-07T16:09:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-66720>

I just gave the receipe in another post but here itis again:

Melt your favorite dark chocolate in a bowl over a pan of boiling water. Add some almond milk or other milk of your choice. When warm, add Mascarpone. It is an Italian cream which is almost pure fat. Mix until creamy and add any spice (like vanilla) you like. I add Stevia as well. Pore in little dessert cups and put in the fridge for one hr. Then you have a delicious mousse au chocolat for dessert or when the chocolate craving comes. I also crush raspberries in it occasionally. Real good and a great dessert.

### Charlotte — 2012-12-07T16:21:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.689/post-66723>

I also put macadamia nuts and chocolate pieces in my Champion juicer or in blender and grind or blend until it is a chocolate-nutbutter. It is great on coconut or almond cookies. These I make with coconut flour, coconutÂ  coconut oil, Ghee. With eggs they stick better together but I cannot eat eggs so I make them without and keep them in the fridge.

### vkiernan — 2012-04-06T15:56:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hc-dhea-pregnenolone.691/post-12098>

Hi all. Time to get this adrenal and leaky gut thread going. I have soooo many questions since I have stage 7 adrenal fatigue and leaky gut, both confirmed, along with candida and hormone issues but the latter 2 are for another thread.

For starters, my first question to getting to optimal is when is the appropriate time to take HC, DHEA & pregnenolone?

I have read to take the DHEA and preg. first thing with fat and protein. But I have also read to take DHEA and preg. on any empty stomach first thing. Which is correct?

I was also doing some reading on HC and it said it needs to be taken with food or it might cause stomach issues. I have enough stomach issues going on so I really need some insight into this. If HC is to be taken with food, how should I take it at my 3 pm time when lunch was 3 hours ago and supper isn't for 3 more hour since no snacks allowed? I cannot take it too late in the day since it is supposed to be mimicking my natural cortisol rise and fall.

Thanks for any input!

as for HC... I'm no help for you on that...

You know so much and any advice you offer me is greatly appreciated. I don't want to take something that can really benefit me knowing I might be taking it incorrectly. I, too, take it 2x a day. I am just not sure whether I should be taking it before my BAB or after. I could always do my own n=1.

### hazyjane — 2012-04-08T02:44:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hc-dhea-pregnenolone.691/post-12558>

> **vkiernan;12412 wrote:** Thanks Hazyjane. Can I ask how long have you taken the preg? How much? What have you noticed from it? So you are taking it without food? You know so much and any advice you offer me is greatly appreciated. I don't want to take something that can really benefit me knowing I might be taking it incorrectly. I, too, take it 2x a day. I am just not sure whether I should be taking it before my BAB or after. I could always do my own n=1.

I take 20 mgs. 2 x/day. I started out with Apex Energetics brand, which is a liquid form and I seemed to need a lower dose of that (according to muscle testing by my doc ) than what I'm taking now (Source Naturals brand). I think I may go back to Apex.

I had low DHEA and progesterone, so my holistic doc wanted to try pregenolone. I've been on it for a few months- maybe 3-4 (I can't remember!) It's hard to say what I've noticed- I do think it's helped my adrenals and hormone balance, though right now I can tell I still have low morning cortisol going on. My head feels heavy in the a.m., but at least I can think clearly! My cognition has been pretty good, so it may be helping with that.

I need to do another ASI to see how my adrenal hormones are faring.

### Destiny — 2012-04-09T02:44:41+0300

### vkiernan — 2012-04-09T03:41:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hc-dhea-pregnenolone.691/post-12867>

You have opposite of what you want. You need to turn the lights way down at night and let your body naturally do it's thing. If you haven't installed f-lux on your computer, that would be a great start.

We could give you more tips if we knew more about you such as what is your life like and what are you currently taking and what time of day? Are you battling anything else in your life besides high nighttime cortisol?

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-09T04:14:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hc-dhea-pregnenolone.691/post-12876>

I was on HC for two years. Luckily was able to get off of it recently. I took it 4 x a day and did not take it with food. I had no issues. If you do have issues then take it with a little tea and cream or coffee and cream. Unless of course you are sensitive to dairy. Anyway you shouldn't need much cream to tolerate the HC.

I've taken DHEA first thing in the morning on an empty stomach and have never taken preg. I'm happy to say that I am completely off of all adrenal support after having severe adrenal fatigue!!! Even though I was off of the adrenal meds a few months prior to starting CT I do feel like doing the Leptin Rx and CT have helped immensely.

\--Steph

\--Steph

Plus like I said I think getting off of the HC has also changed my hormones quite a bit.

\--Steph

Hi Steph. Thanks for the input. Yep, been on DIM since last fall along with silymarin. Estrogen was being reduced according to labs but pms and sore breasts tell another story. ### [email redacted] — 2012-04-12T23:48:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hc-dhea-pregnenolone.691/post-14416>

> **vkiernan;14155 wrote:** Hi Steph. Thanks for the input. Yep, been on DIM since last fall along with silymarin. Estrogen was being reduced according to labs but pms and sore breasts tell another story. Sounds like you are dealing with everything I am attempting to deal with. The CT has really messed me up hormonal too but I am not quitting. I am going to keep letting it do it's thing and hope for the best. I cannot believe what cold water truly does to your body. Seems like most of us females and a few males are really getting the detox. It is crazy and I am floored when I read all the stuff that is happening to all of us like hormones, old injuries flaring up, nausea, headaches, etc. I have experienced all those I mentioned.
>
>
>
> How much HC and what time of day did you take it? From some stuff I've read, I am taking too much at once. One book talks about taking no more than 10 mg per dose to not shut the adrenals down and I am on 12.5 morning and noon then 7.5 at 3pm. Do you think this is an issue? I don't know anyone that takes it to even begin to know good from bad etc.

Check out the Yahoo NaturalThyroidHormonesADRENALS forum ([http://health.groups.yahoo.com/group/NaturalThyroidHormonesADRENALS/)...it](http://http://health.groups.yahoo.com/group/NaturalThyroidHormonesADRENALS/\)...it) is a great resource and they helped me heal my adrenals. They do suggest you stay under 10 mg for the reason you mentioned. I was at one point taking 10 mg of HC and 4 Isocort first thing in the AM and I had no issues. I think I also was on 12.5 mg on HC at one point, but can't remember. Do what works for you. I was on 25 mg of HC for about 2 years. There were times I had to go up and I found out I had low aldosterone so was also on Florinef for a while...that stuff is no fun and causes major issues for many including me. Anyway, if I remember my schedule was 7:00 am 10 mg, 11:00 am 7.5 mg, 4:00 pm 5 mg, right before bed 2.5 mg. The one at bed has to be taken right when your head hits the pillow so that it doesn't cause any sleep issues. I can tell you that I did a lot of experimenting with my doses. I journaled everything I was taking and how I was feeling, so it was easy to see if somethign wasn't working. You do need to stick with a certain protocol though long enough to know if it is working or not, but don't be afraid to experiment a bit. I think the hardest thing was knowing when it was time to wean off of the HC. I felt like after two years I should go ahead and try and thank goodness I was able to do it without any issues. I did it very slowly...going down 2.5 mg a week. On a week when I wasn't feeling great I wouldn't go down at all. It took some time, but I finally got off of it. I just got the saliva test kit from my doc, so can't wait to see what's going on now!

The moderators on the adrenal forum can help a lot. Unfortunately that forum gets very busy, so a lot of times I found it easier to read through the old posts for information versus trying to ask my own questions. Tons of great info and they can help you interprut your labs. Hope that helps.

\--Steph

### vkiernan — 2012-04-15T02:25:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hc-dhea-pregnenolone.691/post-15171>

Steph, Thanks for the info and link. Good info out there.

### hazyjane — 2012-04-15T07:34:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hc-dhea-pregnenolone.691/post-15250>

> **vkiernan;15237 wrote:** You lost me on that one. I thought DIM helped remove it from the system; but, if it is only blocking it then what good is it if I am not actually removing it? I need help on this one.

Ultimately, your liver is what removes the estrogen, but it gets metabolized along 2 pathways- one makes harmful metabolites and the other makes beneficial estrogen metabolites. From what I've read, DIM helps it to be metabolized along the beneficial pathway, to balance the ratio of the estrogen metabolites. It also reduces estrogen receptor activity, which is why I don't understand the breast pain thing...

### Clabbergirl — 2012-04-18T19:32:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hc-dhea-pregnenolone.691/post-16248>

I've been trying to understand the benefits of DIM also. This is something I've found [here](http://http://www.naturodoc.com/library/hormones/diindolymethane.htm) that might shed some light, and backs up what hazyjane spoke of above:

**Q. What benefits can DIM offer?

A. Supplementing our diets with DIM can shift the production of estrogen metabolites away from dangerous 16-hydroxy in favor of beneficial 2-hydroxy metabolites. _Taking DIM in an absorbable formulation encourages active and healthy estrogen metabolism. DIM supports estrogen balance by increasing beneficial 2-hydroxy estrogens and reducing the unwanted 16-hydroxy variety. This improves

estrogen metabolism and helps resolve all three forms of estrogen dominance.

NaturoDoc Note: Remember, 2-hydroxy is good; 16-hydroxy is bad._**

### hazyjane — 2012-04-23T03:22:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hc-dhea-pregnenolone.691/post-17944>

> **tweety;17279 wrote:** hazyjane- why not raw maca? I take that in the powder form. I thought that was a good product.

Maca is never used raw in traditional Incan culture and there's probably a good reason for that. It's in the turnip family and I believe it might be goitrogenic when eaten raw. When you think about it, tubers in general are not consumed raw, except for carrots.

The gelatanizing process removes more of the starch and makes it more digestible, so that would be a good thing.

### tweety — 2012-04-23T20:54:07+0300

>
> The gelatanizing process removes more of the starch and makes it more digestible, so that would be a good thing.

### hazyjane — 2012-04-30T21:41:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hc-dhea-pregnenolone.691/post-20163>

> **BJK77;20181 wrote:** Would maca be beneficial for someone who is low on all sex hormones? Most of what I've been reading indicates it helps deal with ED, but I don't think that's an issue for me. I just pretty much have nothing going on in the hormone department!

I think it would. It can raise testosterone. I believe it works on the level of hormone synthesis and adrenal health, hence its usefulness for general low hormones or specific low hormones.

### hazyjane — 2012-04-30T21:42:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hc-dhea-pregnenolone.691/post-20164>

> **janagram;20200 wrote:** I'm 64, should I still try maca or progesterone?

I would try maca first and then add progesterone later if you're still low. Give it 3-4 months. This is generally how practitioners use it

### [email redacted] — 2012-05-01T04:23:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hc-dhea-pregnenolone.691/post-20276>

Hi everyone. Hope you are all feeling well. I have to say I feel like I've turned a corner. Just when I thought my poor adrenals were beyond help, I've gotten some relief. Not sure what to point to. I have been taking Pregnenolone 50 mg along with my other vitamins and the estradiol. I just added progesterone. I have not been able to take Maca or Astragalus because I'd get a low blood sugar response. I have had an occassional coffee but always regret it afterward. Today I had a cup and felt very low blood sugar like after. But 99% chocolate seems fine. Of course I eat too much of it! Dr K has posted that he eats the 99% choc when he wants to lose weight! Not sure what he meant by that but Here's hoping!

But any way. I feel a lot better lately even though I have been eating more chocolate and coconut manna. Ive kind of cut back on my veggies because im trying to get back to ketosis but i keep overdoing the manna. Not in control yet. And I've gained at least 7 pounds Since Easter, which on the one hand, I am not hapPy about but on the other hand, I am happy to feel better. Go figure!

But now I have some heart palpitations but only in the evening. I take all my supplements (except magnesium and progesterone) in the morning, so I have no idea about the Palpitations. Maybe I should take the Pregnenolone every other day?? Any suggestions?

I, too, have just started having the heart palps again but mine are w/BAB. I just upped my preg. in the am to 100 from 40 w/BAB and 40/lunch because I ran out of the lower dose. I was thinking it was my thyroid med. I switched taking it at 9:30 am to right away on an empty stomach in the am with my dhea and preg. So, I made 2 changes without thinking and now I don't know the cause. But it is interesting that you are having the same thing. I have to mention to that I am also feeling like I've turned a corner for the better. Are you on thyroid meds too? Perhaps our thyroids are turning on and we don't need as much? So, we both feel better and we both are taking more preg. Hmmm. We will figure this out.

I am surprised you are not taking some of progesterone in the evening at bed time to help you sleep.

Did you back off the preg today? If so, let us know how you fare this evening. I would consider getting a smaller dose of the preg and taking it daily instead of every other day. This keeps your brain going daily. I am not sure but I don't think it is one of those things that stays with you per se. From what I've read, it helps you focus and stimulates your creative thinking. As Uzzi Reiss says, helps you swim in the crowd with people. Makes you more social. He says he has never seen it build and turn into other hormones like they say it does.

### [email redacted] — 2012-05-01T15:39:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hc-dhea-pregnenolone.691/post-20347>

> **vkiernan;20409 wrote:**
>
> I am surprised you are not taking some of progesterone in the evening at bed time to help you sleep.
>
>
>
> Did you back off the preg today? If so, let us know how you fare this evening. I would consider getting a smaller dose of the preg and taking it daily instead of every other day. This keeps your brain going daily. I am not sure but I don't think it is one of those things that stays with you per se. From what I've read, it helps you focus and stimulates your creative thinking. As Uzzi Reiss says, helps you swim in the crowd with people. Makes you more social. He says he has never seen it build and turn into other hormones like they say it does.

I have NO TROUBLE sleeping. Which is a blessing. I had that whole thing where you lie in bed and are SO TIRED but your body feels ramped up, like it is about to ran a marathan. I would lie there and say, "Body, go to sleep!" LOL But IDK how that resolved. After I bought my house in Dec and moved in and got settled, I started sleeping well. When I first started Leptin Rest I also started sleeping like a champ...until daylight savings hit. Then it was bad, then good again. Now, I've been traveling and living life out there in the world (as in spending time with friends, going OUT, theatre, etc) and I'm not going to bed as early and getting up is harder. I want to find a balance. I have also now gained 10 pounds since Easter. I could shoot myself. It's all me, eating too much

But, back to the subject at hand, yes, I take synthroid for Hashimoto's. Not the best, I know. I hope to switch to Armour or T3 soon. I did skip the pregnenolone today. So, we'll see. I feel very edgy and cranky, but we are being audited at work and I've been out of the office and have WAY too much to do, so I am stressed. My temp is still ridiculously low, so I don't think it's thyroid kicking in.

That is interesting what you say about Dr Reiss. I wonder do I need the 7 Keto DHEA. I am going to have my estrogen drawn now. I have a feeling its still low. I can't stand the dry eyes. I didn't realize that was an estrogen thing. I really should not make any more changes until I see the endo and functional med doctor. I"m gonna make myself nuts! LOL

The only problem is early morning when I first get up, sometimes my eyes water and it can't go anywhere. But, this is really nothing compared to what I was going thru. Hello!

i have gotten my meds from dzugan...progesterone cream plus testosterone cream (super low: .1 )...i have only started the progesterone cream as I am wanting to be sure that is the right dose before adding another cream. Since doing so, I experienced extreme fatigue so i'm still getting the dose right...recently they made the change to half the dose and take some in the morning and some in the evening so we'll see if that helps! i have suffered from extreme dry eyes lately which has never been a prob in the past. they are thinking they may add a bit of estrogen (i am estrogen dominant only by relation - my levels are actually normal to low) but want to wait a bit to see.

colleen said her daughter benefitted from testosterone for her dry eye so i'm anxious to see if that will help. i do know my thyroid levels have been dropping so that could be it too (have hashis). i looked like both my T4 levels and T3 levels could use a boost so i was expecting to have my dr recommend an armour increase, but instead he just bumped up the T3. i did ok yesterday morning with the new dose but i pretty much crashed last night. ...**is that a sign of adrenal fatigue?** ...would i be better off upping the armour instead or is it ok to ignore the T4 (assuming it stresses the adrenals more to conver T4 to T3) i'm tempted to ask my doc for a low dose of HC bc my cortisol levels have been depressed for a while. i did a saliva test and they looked a bit better but i was on a bunch of adapatogens at the time so i'm wondering if they are truly "better". I know in particular my morning levels have PLUMMENTED.

did anyone suffer bad side affects form hc?

### nuttmegs17 — 2012-05-15T15:32:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hc-dhea-pregnenolone.691/post-24180>

> **tweety;24241 wrote: That is similar to my day, but I haven't done anything about thyroid. I am trying to treat the adrenals and hormones in an attempt to straighten it all out. I was surprised Dzugan said no salivary too but she said it was bcs they feel it is unreliable.

I suppose its all anecdotal, but I still like to see other's experiences.**

**

i take the gel, 5% 50mg/ml....They had me on a higher dose but I did not tolerate it well - it made me VERY sleepy - not so good when you are trying to work. i can see how for some woman who are anxious that might be a welcome gift, but for a person still struggling with the thyroid, i was almost catatonic! haha. luckily, the weeks right after my period are always my best so being on a low dose right now is fine. i still have some tinkering to do as my period approaches.

i had very little pms symptons this month while on progesterone which was promising - bloat was almost nonexistent and my boobs did not get sore...also, for the first time in a year, my period started exactly on day 28 (its been on Day 31-34 for while). i think i can do more progesterone towards the end of the month but we are going to experiment with splitting the dose up (am and then taking the other half before bed to minimize the fatigue during the day). the only downside is that i was a bit depressed/anxious right around my period and am hoping some hormone tweaking will fix that and for some reason I always have a day or 2 where it feels like i'm coming down with the flu - cannot get out of bed, muscles weak, light headed....this always happens around my period so trying to figure that out as well**.....wondering if i might need a little estrogen as well. i guess my body is out of everything!

he also gave me testosterone (.1) but i am waiting until the progesterone is straightened out before trying that.

i know my cortisol is low and while i get being so conservative about HC, i think it might be time to try. for whatever reason, i have a really hard time tolerating thyroid dose increases and adrenals is about the only thing left we havnet addressed. despite my clean primal diet, leptin reset etc...i unfortunately need more thyroid hormone  yet my body fights it. not good. i desperately need it.

### vkiernan — 2012-05-15T20:47:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hc-dhea-pregnenolone.691/post-24320>

Nuttmegs, Is it going fairly well with the Chicago doc? I might be switching very soon. It is either them or Dr. K. in Tennessee. I have a phone consult with him and I will go from there. My current(soon to be old) is helping me with the adrenals but she doesn't believe in bio's. I need hormone help to help the adrenals as well and she just won't go there. So, I am looking again. But at least I have an option now. If Dr. K. says it is ok to use them and not him, then I am going to try to get my old doc on board and maybe she will become one of their docs. I have the paperwork, just need to figure out the details.

Just an fyi, Dr. K. does like the salivary cortisol tests for the adrenals. You could do this on your own and will give you a clearer picture. As far as I know, you only want to use HC in the worst case scenarios; Dr. K. doesn't advocate the use of HC either. Is your dhea tanked? From what I remember it wasn't. If it is tanked and you have low cortisol all day, then I would say "think" about the use of HC but not necessarily. If you just have wonky cortisol, then I would use CT and the morning sun and no light at night to help straighten your circadian rhythm out.

I did the salvia tests and i came out low on adrenals...we tried adaptogens (through chris kressor and my reg thyroid doc) but if anything my adrenals aren't really responding. i follow the leptin reset, go to bed appropriatly, get a ton of sun...nothing. I know my DHEA is fine - not tanked - not super high, but i'm ready to try soemthing else to give my adrenals a rest and time to heal I realize i gotta stay on top of fixing them, but they keep preventing my thyroid meds to work properly. I'm going to go slow. Ifi have an adverse reaction I'll stop.

my next step is to get in with this lyme disease doc over the summer and be checked out of lyme - i'd love tohave it ruled out bc its always in the back of my mind - perhaps that is what is stressing my adrenals out

My doc just perscribed 15 mg of which I'll work up to...they want me to start like this

1st week: 2.5 mg in the morning

2nd week, 2.5 in the morning, 2.5 at noon

3rd week: 5 mg in the morning, 2.5 at noon

4th week: 5 mg inthe morning, 5 at noon

5th week: 5 mg in the morning, 5 at noon, 2.5 at 3 or so.

Does this sound about right? I read at stop the thyroid madness that I should probably start with the 5th week recommendations instead of slowly ramping up -how did you do it? what was your response? just curious.

### vkiernan — 2012-05-18T01:30:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hc-dhea-pregnenolone.691/post-25018>

Nutmeggs,

I take 12.5 at 7, 12.5 at 11 and 7.5 at 3. She had me drop the 3rd dose as I wasn't getting tired by 10. I dropped from 10 to 7.5. On good days, it is enough. On stressful days, I can tell it isn't. This is what she gave me from the start. No ramp up but it hasn't done anything bad that I can tell. Between the HC, the dhea and preg. I am doing better. Not great, but better. I bet if my hormones were balanced, I would be doing much better. All in good time though.

I have my phone consult with Dr. K this coming Tuesday and I am going to do what he tells me. I am very excited. He probably won't have enough labs and I will have to figure that out but at least it is a start.

Good, you are digging deeper. Sucks to have tanked cortisol. Glad your dhea wasn't tanked. That means adrenal fatigue and not just adrenal issues. When your dhea tanks, you are pretty much at the last stage and thank God you are not there yet.

### vkiernan — 2012-05-19T15:46:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hc-dhea-pregnenolone.691/post-25466>

Not sure if this is why but I had lost a lb or so, my stomach was down but now I seem to have gained a lb or 2. Stomach is more bloated but I also put a little cream (just a little in my morning coffee w/lots of co) and hard cheese (into BAB 1 oz) back into my diet and that could be the reason; seems like it happened around the same time. I keep telling myself to ditch the dairy completely. I do seem flabbier than ever though. OTOH, my stomach is also starting to work as I have been constipated my whole life. Now whether that is due the CT'ing, HC, etc or combination I don't know. I do not take the HCL like I use to; maybe 1 or 2 a day and that is all I can take. Oddly enough, I also started sleeping thru the night when this all happened. This all started happening iirc after my 2 week detox period that I went thru from CT. Did Ct'ing get rid of enough estrogen to help me sleep? There are many questions to be answered and maybe some will be answered Tuesday morn.

Nuttmegs, have you thought about a 1/2 phone consult with Dr. K.? He might just open up some avenues for you that no one else is considering. If he says I should come see him then I am going to Tennessee. With my family history and my own, I don't want any more guesses. I am too old for any more messing around and life is PASSINGGGG me by. Even from a supplement cost stand point, spending $150 on a consult might even save me money in the long run. What am I taking that isn't being absorbed or not needed.

i'm hoping that this hc is small enough to not cause any long term effects but will help my adrenals heal so i can take more thyroid hormone (its been such a hard process getting my thyroid levels up, bc i tend to not tolerate thyroid meds very well....another sign of adrenal probs). i'm losing weight and pretty much at my normal weight now, yet still have hashi face which annoys the hell out of me (hashi can cause facial edema, and the only cure is adequate thyroid hormone..which i've been working on for over a year...sigh). hcg has been good bc its made me super strict on my diet and i think the rest has been good for digestion. i'm looking forward to adding things in slowly and pinpointing further what might be exasperating probs (assuming its foods i dont suspect)

i understand the frustration with "watching life pass you by" it sux. i'm tired of it as well. i'd love to focus on other things besides health and supplements...i mean, i know i'll always be a health nut now, but i'd like to not be so obsessive and worried about it all. we will get there! you really have come far and made so much progess since i met you...you are on the right path

DC= Doctor of Chiropractic and CCN= certified clinical nutritionist.

Some DC's have pretty advanced endocrinology training and are usually more up on hormone stuff than most endos are.

Yeah, my Navitas gelatinized maca is kind of sweet. I've used others that weren't and tasted more like turnips.

I always look for the short term but I think when one is this broke, I will have to look at the long term. What is the chinese herbal formula? Last time I saw my doc she told me if have acupuncture that I should not take any chinese herbs. She was very adament about that. I think she doesn't want me on any stimulants other than the HC and dhea.

Yes, I've had adrenal fatigue on and off for years.

If you have stage 7 adrenal fatigue, anything you do is going to take awhile to work- that's just the nature of the beast, so to speak. In fact, at the height of my adrenal fatigue, my acupuncturist told me it could take up to 2 years to fully recover.

I'm on a classic formula called Gui Pi Tang. It has some ginseng but it's not a stimulant. It's actually a sleep/anemia formula. One of my issues (which makes it super hard to totally recover) is _excessive_ dreaming. When you're stuck in excessive REM cycling, you don't get as much deep, slow-wave sleep, which is when the body/brain repairs itself, so you wake up very physically tired even after 9-10 hours of sleep. If the dreams are intense enough, you wake up exhausted. The Gui Pi Tang does seem to be helping, although it takes awhile to have the full effect.

### hazyjane — 2012-05-30T00:39:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hc-dhea-pregnenolone.691/post-28249>

> **vkiernan;28258 wrote:** HazyJane, I need your help again. What do the initials ND stand for? Can they prescribe? Still on the hunt for a doc. Thanks so much for your help.

ND is a naturopathic doctor and they can't prescribe (unless they're also an MD or NP).

I would check out [http://www.functionalmedicine.org/](http://http://www.functionalmedicine.org/) and [http://thyroidbook.com/practitioner-locator.html](http://http://thyroidbook.com/practitioner-locator.html) for practitioners who can treat the endocrine system.
